It is simply so, that of her broader paces, that they are set and made by that settlement. "Where the treats came from the scent and flavor which our liberated being ordained; or at least fashioned us to be drawn to in the sprouts of our liberty."
 ---Liberty sprouts from the soil of harmony. As life bears us down, naturally rooting us into the ground. While liberty is a result, of our actions being commiserate with such dynamics, as to overcome the pull, and without mere remedy or removal, but opposing force. Nature has given us plenty, and I am gratefull for the strenght she's given us. In fact, of good and evil, all and gained, I am truly at peace with the one who's fashioned the world: for whatever pull she's put upon us, so she gave us the lever to lift. "It's as if she knew that for us to be, life would have to be accordingly; but only fairly, were we given the means to be."
Our aim in life is wellness, well-being and well-living. The source for this, or, more or less, means, is of pleasure, be it of the happy or sensual kind.
 Because many things can produce such, along with other effects, we say that something is good, in tha the pleasure it creates brings wellness. The end of being well-off or gluttonous, tends to have goods, or at least means, that are not so akin to the goods of well-being, and may in fact run contrary.
 We don't say that things which don't bring out well-being are bad, in that they are not fully, or at least as good as those which do. But we do make note of our ends, and how whatever is bringing out something besides wellness and well-being, is not effecting us in the best way. This is why being well-off is looked at as something beyond us, and not so rich and dear. Its aim is one of novelty and expensive delight; but nothing which is lasting or meaningfull.
 Of means then, you have those which provide a variety of pleasures. But we distinguish these into those of sense, be it of flavor and sight, or of happiness, in the sense of grace and security. Health for instance, is something we may experience during play; but it is generally something were aware of via understanding; and similar to all sorts of security, we may simple be pleased that we are so well, 'as the future feels bright'. But, despite enjoying the taste of certain things, you may not be happy and thereby pleased with them, due to the contradicting effect upon your health, and otherwise stable pleasure. "The moment of good feels outweighed by its susbquent erosion."
 When I acquire granite countertops, we tend to like the look of them. But, it seems the pleasure comes from the excitment of having them, 'as if some ecstatic delight in it's prescence': in other words, a concentrated dose of happiness, 'for how great do I feel with this being hear'. Yet, this feeling tends to whither away, and over a relatively short period of time, the care you have for their prescence is absent. No longer does it make you that happy, nor was it ever that tasty of a sight. Indeed, such a thing was a novelty, and is now a floating accessory. Yet, our effort for such thing was much more than this end would suggest, in worth. As such, if it brought us pleasure consisently, of sight and or ease, then we'd say that it was producing an adornment to our living. But since its pleasure more or less ceases, and soon --considering the mountainous effort-- then so are we fair to say, that as a good, granite is not so for well-being.
 The notion of being well-off may then be but a remnant of confusion; as being somewhat murky on the truth of our aims, so did we hold a spot for alternatives. Yet it seems unneccasary. There is simply that which creates good living, in that, as a good, its effects bear qualities to our life which we truly deem swell. "Any notion of bad seems more of a confusion of what is good; for things are merely so or not, but not something if the latter is so."
 Ultimately, what we care for are effects with certain qualities. In the case of granite, it produced a short experience of real good, but then died off. In the case of Kingship, it does hold riches; but you must move mountains, 'and perhaps overt your humanity' to acquire; and we thus say it is not that accessible or near. The matter of flavor is perhaps the trickiest to explain, but, we can rest on the fact that moments of pleasure, however sweet and fine, are not so encompassing to your day. Indeed, past pleasure brings with it a sort of ease, but along the lines of comfort. While appreciation is more of a drifting pleasure as it feels supported and or warmed by things around. This takes a natrually calm yet active notice. "I delight in the prescence of the moon, as it makes even my darkest moments find. But I must see it."
 Our inquiry though, into the tastes of our pleasure, needs some sort of binding. As it is in fact hard to say why chocolate and wine is not as good as veggies and water. Indeed, the balance of satiating pleasures with those of the happy sort, makes this easier. But so do we end up in a sort of concession, 'as if we must merely be wise in avoiding that which, if constantly and without contrariety, wuold be the best'. In part this is true, for fried chicken does bring with it a sort of delight that carrots never will. But again, consider our aim in living well. This accounts for all of our life, its demands, our finer interests and all their lasting appeal. In a way, there are brambles and thicket around; and for the sake of argument, fairly. And so, as one born of the water, it pays much peace and comfort knowing their able, 'and actaully being so during the time of need'. But so, and perhaps this is the fruit of this portion, that of the warmer goods we seek, 'beyond the abasement of duty and effort, but joy and comfort', that so do these have a mould, a figure if you will, and as such, we can judge the quality of this statues on how well they stand together, the quality in each, 'as a dense muffine, albeit sugary and sweet, may not hold as much favor as a mountain of fruit'; too, we may consider whether the required materials take away from our sheares and pesticide, or, upon consumption, make is insolent and unable to such deeds. "Remarking upon the other statues, so can we consider them equally, in that building and (more or less) consuming one statue, drains and or mitigates the prodcution and or use of another." For the good of work, or money rather, so doth the contrariety of a hangover lay --as it quells our capacity.
 /////Room of Statues which represent generosity, interest and craft. Food and what not compare, in that you work for them and consume them. But they may be demanding, and short lived in their good. Nor so congruent with our otherwise deeds.
 ///Porcelain, figure, mould, statue, Davids Delight, Dear Gems, Room of Mine, Hall of Craft,
When I had my thing for Simron, I recall making a deliberate choice to not pursue, but let things carry out. This was so that things did not blow up in my face and become awkward; and, in tandum with that, I felt that the thing which we were otherwise acting on, was not so solid or true. There was merely a tenure of care; and be it from her I do not know. But, in being human, she could more or less become as inclined towards me. But my resolve was to the fact that this took time. And our trust was in the fact that if she went somewhere else, or us, that we'd be fine. "It was better to do right and fail, then more than likely attempt something and not succeed." We could more or less accomplish something; but our reservation came from the fact that besides any potential enamure between us, so didn't we care.
 I feel the same way about Amon, and now Mikala. There is this human desire, this longing for the heart. And I saw hers and now another. What I'd say, is that considering we still let things be, 'and live happily commonly, and otherwise deeply on some far end', that we proceed in ways that allow any natural repor to establish something at a greater pace. If were together, quibling, but with that sense of enamure, bring more to the conversation; so it is a less passing fancy and delight, but one that is deeper and more connected. The passive part is about the day, one's shift, whether their tired or not. But to bring more, it comes down to where one is from and what they like to do. Their home, family, school, interests, hobbies, delight. This is the esscence of a date; but you can simply be communal when the time is fair.
 I must note this senitment, whereby in listening to music, we feel detached from those we care for. The thing is, that in those moments, where song brings our stride right, we are not, more or less, able to dance with another, and otherwise care and or like them as much as our heart sings. We only wish for them to like us more, so that our candor is well-recieved.
 What I notice though, is that much of our interest comes down to sex. Or at least, that fashioning of our heart, and the extension of that into our loins. This is the sort of relational warmth and treat, which all other things of duty and interest are but adjoining. The truth of the matter, is that as we age, and those real matters hold precadence, that the mere interest of our heart becomes less so; and those other things must also be present. There is room to associate on more emotional levels, but the workplace is not so. And again, to bring more connection and feeling, we ought be able to associate on more substantial things. This is what weaves us together in the world, rather than our floating yearning.
 I mentioned statues, or figures rather, as remarkd the form of a joy and or delight. And when I listen to music, read Plutarch, or avoid even dramas and twitter, I feel ingratiated into life and the world in a certain way, 'as if my move and groove is to an according beat'. I only see though, how others are with other motions; which draws me to fret over not being near them, as to bear their attention towards me. "As there is less natural fitting for us to intertwine." And thus, I overly hope that they see me, being special in some sense, 'so that they forget their material interest, and become drawn to us in a merely heartfull manner'. The irony being, that I dont lack my interest; and so much as they live by theres, then there is no real comparability between us. And thus I merely hope that I can continue being me, while being emotional with them. "Yet this will never happen; becaue they are more than heart."
So, we direct ourselves on the basis of work; and to some extent chores. But, of what I really need to set aside as something which is moulded around another, is gaming. For we need to eat, stretch, walk, more or less prepare. And games and what not ought surround this; not the other way around.
 I see some value in corn and tomatoe sauce, along with lighter beans. This can add protein and eatability. The beans may be too rought; but with the sauce and corn, we can make our grain more tasty and thus worth an extra bowl. In fact, the biggest thing is in extending our time for grain, or eating more during the day. I've landed on the morning veg then fruit, afternoon dried fruit, nuts, milk and protein. In a moment like this, where were off from work, have to work in the monring, and thus turn things off at 10, and like finish eatin closer to 9, so am I looking for a good amount of a food, a MereFlow sesh, maybe a game or two of breakthrough, a little time on video making; Im gonna walk now, around dinner, and maybe a stroll around 8. Indeed, between 8 and 9 is a great food period. We've made the deliberate call that gaming before bed is poor. At least the cost of it ought not be underestimated. Our time for sleep is clear, but, gaming is similar to heavy food, in that it ought finish prior to that, leaving our last moments for roughage and tv.
 The insight I have regarding food is similar to exercise, that, although the kind of thing is important, it is more about the general practice of exercise and eating; and thus, doing these often, through this or that manner, is whats important. We should be eating food through the day, from kale and tomatoes, to an orange, milk and cranberries, to smoothies, peanuts and cucumber, to bowls of grain, bread and potato. Popcorn, crackers and spingrolls may come; but better for days like this coming Friday, where we've had our day off, and have two to come. Perfect. Exercise is equal, as is yoga, in that whats nearly required, if not best, is to do things through the day, and small as is fitting for such. We ought do standing rise, strolls, breif aerobics and or running, with pushups and juggling to boot; leg stretch, brief lifts, seated stretches for neck and legs, on your back, rinsing of feet and hands.
Theres nothing like friendship, as you get one another. Love on the other hand, seems to come, perhaps by mere difference in sex, but, as such, by the motive of a comparable beat in one's heart; the remedy of which amounts to romance, relations, such love.
 This is indeed one of the dearest feelings. Of all things in the world, the tributes, or at least figures reminiscent of Aphrodite, are many's favorite.
 They are one though, at least of the bunch. And as such, our whole life is not so keen to be entirely focused for the sake of that. Fair belief suggests that all may find love for eachother, and that it is more about facilitating such means. Culture has come about this way as to make things more accessible. While in modern times, there is not real custom; that is, the only rule is how others communicate, and what they want is all that matters. Such communication though is a blessed curse. For those who do not know themselves, and are not otherwise unquestioning of who they are, then so is this open time but reminder of such: that of what we could want, we don't actually know.
There are these fallacies, akin to heuristics, whereby you compare your life to anothers, and attribute something novel or different (from yours) as some mighty esteem, of which all are aware of and flaunting over; while you sit, alone, unseen, pathetic. This is never true; and in fact, only comes by your insolidity of your own goods ands wears.
 Of that, it seems that Mikaela, or Simron, even Bella, are an abscence to my heart, something which would seem so nice. I have felt this for some time, that these feelings would suffice, if all else was well, and we were pleased with only this. This sentiment of flourishing and joy, reason, grace and delight, so would something other be fine. "Akin to one esteemed by tavel, and with a friend at that"
 It is important though not to let your life get brought away, where you gaze at things, with desire and or envy, of all but what you have. The good is here, often I feel it. But there is actually alot which, in a finer mind, you'd see. Our boss is quite nice, as are most our collegue rather fair. I get along with Joey, Andy, Tristan, Shanoa, Alex, Kim, Tera, Teresa, Brielle, Andre, Carli, Carry, Will, Mikala. There is so much. And I don't have a great deal of friends, nor any friendship or relation. But my life doesn't swelch for that. Were in fact fine, and making a grand ado with the circumstance. Normally, I'd feel threathened or bethrothed to far off things, planning our commute. I recognize that what our life is truly outside those dreams and ventures is one thing. But indeed, I don't feel it is in much more than further tasting what we have. This past evening, seeing Baker deep in the blue, what a sight. And this notion of sights and security, what great sense and rigor this has instilled in me. And our remedy of rancour and garulity, not even the news is a bother. And that Red Cross, how it brings me to a calm exclaim. Perhaps, in truth, I don't know much. And more that certaintly am I missing a friend. But the resolve might be best in doing what you know to be missed; yoga, online dating, 'as we for a moment experience', conversing with people in more meaningfull ways.
 I find, as all things old and honest, that these warm motions around me are suitable amidst our swell of near effort. Making our yoga, working on our health, tenuring the ryhtm of work and leisure. All is fine in these regards. I just cant wait for Ark, playing Bf5 is so fine, even AOE4 is fun, along with Derek. "Our halls are pure and solid, and I fear little of caring for nothing else."
I want an html website, scripts using jquerry.
 Simply, I want to paste in a long piece of text which is segmented into pieces --ill wrap these in appropriate divs on notepad++. Then, in such divs, I want a small button that opens an overlay/modal, which several options. These will allow me to input a title, and categories. The categories are Philo, World, Reflection, joy. In the top of the webpage, these categories shoudd be placed, and when I click them, they bring up the text associated with such categories. Also, at the top of each piece and its div, when I input a name via the modal, it should display that in small italic font (in said place at the top of the div). Of the categories and titles, these should be saved upon reload. I want the font of the main body of text to be centaur. Also, above the row of cateogires --near the top of the page-- there should be several square buttons that change the background color --and keep it after reload. Also, I will be reading through this body of text, and so if i could return to the part I was at when I closed, or after reopening, that would be great
So, roughage wise, theres plenty of oranges, kale, tomatoe --we may need more sunflower seeds.
 Enrrgy wise, theres cranberries, no sardines, a little bit of tofu, but some edamame.
 Smoothie wise, theres full soy, blueberries, alot of frozen kale, flax, several bananas ---frozen ones too.
 Grain wise, theres a big bowl of barley, lentils and kidney beans. We need quinoa, lentils. Theres corn --which we might grab more of, since its on sale-- and we've got a few days worth of tomato sauce.
 Starch wise, theres a moutain of springrolls, good amount of potatoes, popcorn, but no bread.
Sincerely, if some girl, of any sort, 'and perhaps one who I know', came to me, and with a gratuitious or sweet end in mind, spoke to me, 'and sought, or at least bode for actions as such', I'd amend in a heartbead. I just wouldn't say know. And, surely, in my dreams, 'and otherwise, upon friction, doth lie sparks of trouble', I see us asking for something similar, and recieving commisery.
 But, I wonder if romance, or at least physical interaction, were not allowed, what would I seek. To talk and play? Well, what? What do you even know of such things, in which you'd relate. It seems you be bored.
 Then, there is this notion of fantasy, or at least rot expectation; as perhaps sprung by your own youthfull whims and confidence, or confided and provoked by others around, who exlcaim to you, 'to just hit it, or, of all these things of the sort, its easy'; to which I then feel envy and or shame, 'as if I am truly missing out'.
 But I see clearly that we are dealing with people.
 I look at Mikala, she smiles, asks me how I'm doing. This seems different than the average person. Normally, you get a hey, and not much else. But I am fooling myself, or letting my self be subject to feelings quicker then they actually reflect, to think she says this, and in such manner, because she likes you, has feelings, thinks of you, 'has you in sight, and on her mind'. The truth of the matter is that I want more, to not be alone, to see someone and be seen. But this does not make it so others speaking to you, as our general repor elicits, means that they are feeling something more. Were too keen to believe otherwise, because we want it to be so. Perhaps it means so much for her to do as she does, that my deeper wants and 'dreams' are sprung, causing exageration. For we don't actually want her, relate to her, know her even. Its just like Holly, and Simron even, where we want her to be part of our life, but not the other way around. There is not truth that says she hasn't her own life and way. Don't despair as if vanity is her aim, or motivation; for which you will pathetic be devoid from. But, the ideal, or truth, is that were brought together as people: romance and love being a part of that. "There ought be substance beneath; otherwise were children at liberty to care only for such, and have not interests or needs beneath, 'for which our love must form around'."
These younger girls are not inherently going to like me. For one, they are not that young, and as such, prone to pure attraction and laundering. Second, is that they are still people, and not so interested to brazenly hook up or hang out. At this point, the idea that we'd have to spend time with them, talking and or chatting, is of no interest to me. So we ought not feel this envy as if were not achieving what is so easy and in front of us.
 I realize that young people come and go; as their life is ahead of them. Chanoya however, Andre, Carry and Joey evem, are hard in people; and thus I fear not missing them or saying good bye, because I know I'll see them. And so we may rest on what we have, and let any lingering notion of oppertunity and shame drift away. Mikalae may leave, and that is alright. I am not so inclined to see her as someone more than one who has a life which excites me; and this, like many, brings much joy and commradery to our workday.
 
 I understand that more coudl be done, and that my disinterest is not so whole and founded. But I find it completely reeaosnable, and almost as equally fair to regard contraiety, that work always comes, and that all things life are in such balance. As such, I appreciatr what I have, in the same way I did at school. I may not be missing things at home, but whatever truth were hiding about this, so can we rest on those at work we have; as they are apart of the balance and as such, the whole. "Those in one part of the rythm effect parts of the other."
 I must also note this erraneous thought, that, understandibly, you just want a girl to like you, to be there and do as you seek, sexually or in fun. Even nicer would be if she saw you, garnered your wit and granduer. Yet this does not so rightfully come. Even if Socrates himself came to town, my gaze upon him would be on my own accounts. People around you are no different; you cannot just have them. And were past that age, and like so for others, where we just simply pursue and recieve romance and love. Were people after all, as our age and duty reflect; and thus more is needed to come together. Friends may be had, in that you share drinks. But for sex, and farther relations to occur, as much as each person is formiddable, in needs and interest, so ought harmony lie.
My purpose is to live well. Having haughty images and or grief about young girls, or women even, is nothing pleasing to me. What matters most is the heart of things, where the truth and bulk lie. Chanoya for instance, is someone who I cannot fool or expect anything from, as she is bound in herself. But in being there, we more or less support eachother. We may learn more about her, and share about ourselves. But then even, were in the motions of understanding, where we see eachother coming and going, familiar to the others plight. My sight of Mikala is nothing but a dream of her being there for me, by my mere virtue; or at least hoping that we can meaningfully communicate. But only because you are drawn to someone; as it was Vienna then Amon, Bella and now her. Its just a fancy, a want, a desire; and overly bent mind you by porn. We can masterbate, and may even find good in doing so. But it is merely that. Our real relations come from real things, and this takes time and proper placement. We are not ill or rot to not have such things; nor to be ashamed or with envy at not priortizting it. Nor should we let pithy judgements and or concepts fool you, into thinking everyone is having a grand old time; and otherwise, in doing so, see you striclty as ill fit for much.
 You would benefit from not watching porn, jerking off normally, with your toy, but naturally, 'as it creates less fear and dreams'. Rejoice in the calm you experience with others, be it Josh, Joey, Andre or Carry. Were all one big group, mingled together. Don't let your hope and fear throttle this, 'as if theres some cool kids going off, ignoring you, and otherwise experiencing ectasy'. It is likely not that special nor private.
Another weird thing, is that if I have an urge, or desire, the pleasure comes from the thing which relieves this. Thats one thing.
 Its another to actually want the desire, or at least wish for something pleasing. For, what would seem pleasurable brings with it a sort of move towards such pleasure, and having the motive for this feels good.
 So when I think of Mikala, I like to like her more than I actually care to like her. The pleasure would be good, but on all accounts, is jumbled amongst other parts of the experience. In many ways, our grievence comes not because we lack her, but our interest in her. Yet, how do we like her, but not begrieve not having her. Is the wanting enough. What if we think of her while touching ourself? Does this not make us want her more. Yet, we know that, to the extent we do want her, and how, 'depsite our blind eye', the blend of other experiences within this certain one, does not make our liking her as good. The more we do, despite how harmless, has no real end. And ironically, the possibility of there being so, which we perhaps stand by, makes it so the exeragted want, of which we wont be fufilled, makes our otherwise finer interest unsteady; for were above such a want, whose possibility is there. "someone other at least."
I can feel it, this pity and shame, and more or less depression, and not wanting, seeking, anticipating: being excited.
 There ought be good though in our aim. We may appreciate all the things we have. As may we recognize that as kids, we like willy nilly. While adults have more duty and form. But all the more do you seek those fitting for such: be it in general, that they have money and manners, or in particular, that they compare in some way. Perhaps this isn't even an issue, and pure like is there; but, being formed as an adult, the tasks within such need only be met, 'and love then rains'.
 It is obvious though, that, of a relationship which is not like a crush, but is an actual relation, 'as if between to grown people', that so is our need in line with that. Everyone then may have found or be on route to finding; but it is a sitch, a knot, a binding. Our jeoulousy is never of who is bound, but that they are bound. But this is not a frivilous attachment which you are missing, but merely on route for your own. "Were all moving or settled."
I like Mikala. I have a crush on her. But, if I had to, I would not wish to spend time with her. Indeed, I'd be excited, but only for what it would bring, or at least the meaning of it all. Not so much in the actual pleasure of being with her. Obviously, there is a warmth from people, but in love and romance, or intamacy at least. This is to be expected, as good. But, in terms of how we compare, I know nothing about her, nor she of me. Were just colleuges who get along somewhat. She merely springs my heart.
 So I dont know the root of our sexual concern. It lies within us that such things are pleasing, and any sense we have of those who may provide such, brings a sort of interest in them. But so does film exasterbat ethis. And, I submitt, our atrocious use of porn. We began in grade 9, watched it everyday, while jerking off. I reckon some five years went by before we stopped doing it at that rate. It was so interesting, and orgasms were the best, but our view of people, as perhaps built on a natrual form ,is eroded and or distorted. Now even as our consumption of such things has gone away, so do we still have this view of woman as if their greatest worth is in sex. I expeirencd much delight in being with them and am drawn by such. But much of our shame, fear and worry comes from those haughty conceptions, lying near the outside of us; which makes it hard to rest and settle. We are aware of our friends sexual ventures, but with no real detail. And our own sexual forray is short, and so our imagination is left to pitifull believe. "As we musnt't deny who got a blowjob; but we also don't want to see is as big and wet. As if it weren't normal nor spectacular." That is what I see then, with these girls I like. I really don't like them but for their inherent humanity and charm. But our motivating factor is such forrays. Indeed, this interest seems deep within me, 'as if I can't help it, from having no real memory and taste of it'. On one hand, the exagerations of our mind make this want a drainer, as your excitement is engorged; your aware though and this rouses disent. I am aware that we could communicate beeter, and that our life has bade me a complexion which is not so easy to share; and I could better do so. But, in all the good of this, and considering what we'd be better to feel open about, this does not mean a) that others will suddenly become attracted or enamured with you, or b) that your feeling towards them is that sensible, as your really just being yourself, around people and friends. I have no longing to go out as I did as boy. I want to live and play as I have grown to care about, and in accordance with our work to come. What I really seek is someone to rest with me in this stride; and embrace me while doing so. Your not thinking of them though, as you just want something for yourself, 'while their to give'. It is not that they have something better to do, or more elaborate wants, but general cares, as people do.
Lets reflect.
 We've got some yoga to do, a few Skyrim pieces, Arranging the display of our pdf viewer, remarking on the transition into the collection, as well as fitting that for 'edit'. Skyrim is swell, all things considered. The trees have a little flicker, but this is moreso from Obsidian than any mod. "Though the lighting overhaul could be causig it."
 Ark is out tomorrow night, and I'd like to reflect on that, 'as if I were coming to the island, from sea.'
 Allies is good at the moment, and 2021 remains. But Gt time is just so good; and honeslty, more whole and usefull. Bar rescue, despite draining my as reality tv, is really good. Life With Derek remains good, Df clips is a great source, BF5 is wonderfull, and AOE is great for a random game.
 I mean to do some range of motion tomorrow. The limit is our cardio, or ability to run. We can bring a bin and our ball, warm up, short push, properly pull, a few squats and juggle. Easy legs is wonderful as is Quads --which we can combine as not to go through lunges then quad stretch. A bigger smoothie would help me, while dried fruit and nuts are good for the day. I am not for the moment sure when we should switch to grain, but we do need to make some at any rate. Quinoa and corn blend well, and we've got a serving of bean mix. There is butter but no margarine. We have no bread or oranges
So i'll be exhaustive for the sake of clarity, but otherwise reflect on what we need right now.
 SImply, we've decided that our scope is double sided: the collection, and viewer. The collection is similar to what it is now, only that it has an edit button. Practically though, because the items in the collection are already named, we can have a general button that is always visible, and have it serve is the input for whichver item is up. In our viewer, because there is merely a list of 'content', you need buttons per piece. The issue being that if you wish to write something early on, you have to scroll to the bottom. This could be overted by mirroring that button with one on top, and at the very least, scroll up rather down.
 I have more thoughts on this, but lets keep going. For the viewer, the font and width is good, on our new tablet it is. But, for it and our collection, we need a black and white mode, for when were outside. In regards to the final binding of these two, it is likely simple that we add the edit section that the viewer items have to the collection itmes. The difference being how you trigger it --local to the one you click, or via hte general button, which marks whichever item is loaded.
 For Yoga, we have done much, and fundementally were nearly done. All we really need is our neck stretch and we'll be good. But there is much more I'd like to add. Here is a list.
 Combine lunge with quad stretch, going through Downward dog when you go from right to left.
 Morning salutation can remain as is, being mere flow but with chaturanga.
 Morning Rise is fine, but the arm part is a bit too long, and the rise part is also a bit extra. I'd prefer a shorter routine, focusing even on the standing, while another session has a fold then arm behind back. These sequences need not be long, as their meant to get me to breathe and stand up right.
 A mere back session would be nice, when I just want to lie down. Similar to our seated twised, we may just want to bring knees to chest, lightly twist then settle.
 And we ought add Lifts to the flow section.
 As for music, were relatively good, but I see a missing area: classics. 3 hour classic has at least a few pieces, maybe one or two for slow. Mozart is begging me, as were there a few more when we looked at it a few months ago. I would like to go through the annals of Zimmer, James, Shore, Newman. And there is that chemistry one by Rivala
So this is important: our categories. There is, simply, things of the world, be it questioning media or parlaying history. There is Philo, which is pretty self explanatory and by itself.
 Then there is living, which can have many varieties. For one, you have the techincal briefings of your health and ways. This is seperate mind you from personal inquiries, where you think of work and things; which differs from thinking of life in general. By that, you have Grub, Reflections, Principle and Joy. These are easy to follow. Little things beside this, such as violence or civ, may be unparticular. But you can put them their no less. Frankly, violence is not so common in this part, and Civ is about the only other specific, so lets just have that.
So we've gopt our pff viewer formed. Each piece is within content. As viewed though, theres the piece and note above it, which will just act as another paragraph in notepad. You could widen the view as to show where that first paragraph is, and seeing the difference may be easier.
 Ultiamtley, I wish to make another 'collection' if you will, and have all the pieces per category be the sub headers. I would like to add notes to our PWA, but the noting is not cross platform. There is a json/online means to do so, but we'll have to look at that then.
 When were done with the PDFviewer, we'll take the text file, and reformat it in notepad. I'm assuming we can do this. At heart, the notes are there to familiarize me with the pieces so I can better name them and organize them. BUt, like those already in our collection, i;d like to further note, and thus bring over the notes I already have. They may not be as important.
 I think the easiest approach is to add a title-display section in all the pieces within PWA. That way, we can more easily wrap our PDF notes. We will have to script the fact that of those already in the PWA, they have no notes, and so the function for those were adding from the PDf ought be maintained. This can more or less be done. So long as we have the base file, which we'll get from copying the presented form of our notes and pieces, then we can format and wrap as we please; and worse case scenario, just manually add the notes to the more important pieces; or just accept that any new notes will erase them.
 I mean to add a work section, to distinguish from health and life.
 Yoga wise, we have edits and new vids. For one, you've got seated, refresh, short lunge, short supine, and short standing. This requires some arrangment, for more casual edits.
 For edits, we have the Morning Salutation, condensing our quad stretch, shortening our monring rise slightly
So I'm on a raft, heading for the island ahead. I once was similarly headed, but this current destination seems different. There are more mountains in the centre, with yellow sands in the east. There seems to be a great plain stretching down the west, and a volcanic stretch in the north.
 Our aim is to survive but also flourish. There are great tames and riches about, and the sort of security, pride and strenght in the means to reach so. "There's even the simple pleasure of painting a house."
 To commiserate our time here, I'll give some account of the world and its ways.
 Each person begins small, and upon their actions, grows into someone of stature. Some of the creatures are worth taming, but if they are of a high level, and dangerous class, will yield such growth --upon defeat. Many things can be made, and some are particularly generous in their reward. Bows for instance, which cost a moderate amount of wood and fibre, or rafts, which do require hide, but give much. "We ought tame a moschops in order to succeed."
 The land is quite harsh and surivial is really up to us. Tames make this easier, as do ceratain tools, namely the hourglass and jar: each requiring a bit of crystal. "There seems to be a bay were landing on, and up on its cliffside I see a sparkle."
 Seeing as we are with someone, we will share our experience, and thus combine our growth --though the further we are from one another, the less this is so.
 Lastly, there are rumours of pesky birds in the sky, who take your things. In our pouch, we ought place useless things in the most accessible position, so they may take that. If one gets sick they may traverse the swamp in hope for antiobiotic ingrediants. It can be harsh in the lands, and an otter --who sits in ponds and streams-- may serve as protection. A small stone or thatch hut may insulate you from the cold or heat, respectively.
So theres two things about our Viewer. One, is that updating it removes our notes. I felt we were good enough to ignore this, but alas there are changes I'd like to make. What we can do, is be mindfull of how it currently is and use it as we see fit, or we can copy what is there, paste in in whole or per category into notepad folder, then make the change we want; and likely to the cache as well.
 Right now though, what has me unsure, is how certain things should be. Some pieces, for instance, are about our dad, knowledge fortifying us, and what knowledge actually is. This is not enough of a reflection, but perhaps an inquiry, or of philo. We can note the clear differences and one part can go in its own section. But another instance, were talking about discourse and its effect on us; is this about the world, or inquiry? In its own right, inquiry has become philo and principle.
 At any rate, the goal is to make something similar to our current collection. There need not be one soul section for each subject, but at least a batch of things --as we have for the Deep and Linens. It is obviuos that we have some remarks about Civ and Ark, and this will go under games --and Civ right now. Grub is about our health and fitness; while work is literally about work, and perhaps moving. These both are about numbers and accounting, while reflection is about our thoughts on these. A great deal could just go into that. Joy is different, as we talk about virtue for instance, and how she comes about, then we speak about the weather and finer times to move. I put that into joy and the former into principle. Which is fine; yet part of me wants to put the happier sentimetns of virtue into joy.
 Ulitmatley, the issue is one of relevance. For we cannot see things cateogries less we onyl display said categfory, or click a things button. Perhaps we can look at our notes, and say, 'well we clearly have this batch of things, lets put them together'. And maybe a certain thing is so in line with work that we put it with more techincal parts. The point being, is that our end goal may simply require us to scroll through our list, see the notes we've made and orgainze them then. We mean to do this with titles. It seems our mistake is in finely categorizing things as we go through, when in fact, our purpose here is to make it easier to organize --formeost, by knowing what something is. We might simplify by putting things in general categories, as to have a clear set of things to manage when were titling/organizing. Because were going to do food and work, some civ. But as regards joy and living, maybe put it together. I do see things of a reflective sort being on their onw, 'as any subject could fit this but still be a reflection'. But a piece about Laws and discourse seem fit to go together.
 In that, I'd make the brief effort to go through what we have, and simplify the sorting process. No doubles, just Philo, life/joy, reflection, work and or grub --of which techincal things regarding family can go into. I'd say all things non fitness and health go into work. I recognize that some things may not be so deep or of linens, but principle and inquiry. But most things are never so far apart that they can go together. And of those which are clear and unique --such as our gentlmens discussion about the century, make room on your PWA for longer titles, as to be more specific --in the kind or nature of a talk, or what identifies it, ie. Our trip to the green planet
So, just this tricky little thing on my mind, but Bella and I are in no way friends. We pass by, sometimes exchange nods and hello, but seldom more than that. We can speak, and if we were alone we could talk; but less were talking about work, there isn't much to bring up. We just don't connect in any meaningfull way.
 Yet, this is so, for instance, with Sierra, Lomolita, Nargus, Ybraj and a few others. Yet I don't fret about them. I think this is in part an ethinicity thing; but, more particular to the fact, that Bella is a well-liked person, and talks with many people. And so I feel odd not being one of those folk, as I feel less able to enter conversations when shes there, even if someone I spoke to alot elsewhere was present. This relates to my highschool years, where, be it girls or some guy, I would not talk as I would with another, even if they were there.
I would like to speak about this in lenght, similar to our time during grade 5. But, for now, lets establish a baseline, and relate it to the present if we please.
 I began work at Superstore October 18th. Brittany called me, met me for an interview, and we were hired. I was a little nervous, but experienced that simple relief upon entering the building. I was introduced to several people, namely Evan, and Charlese, 'who I awkwardly remembered from Walmart some time ago'. I also remeber meeting Andy, who I thought I'd hit it off with, and we sure did about games. For some reason, I never played with him, and my feeling is similar now. Were just not friends beyond that, and so getting together happens less naturally.
 Anyways, I recall meeting this younger girl, who was staging in the back fridge; I made some humourous remarks, and felt connected. But it was the sort of self-centered connection, where I felt more or less established, or confident at least, amidst the group around me. "Like I was above and beyond there common ways, and thus notable; and to some extent attractive."
 This early part of work, through the holidays, held too struggles. One, was in regards to my speech, where I noticed that peopel would not always hear me well, and I would more or less notice myself being unable to speak clearly. It is the molassusy state that I still recognize. But it more or less stilted me back then; and I recall walking to work with this grave sense that I was not going to be able to speak. "There was an assocaited thinness to our body, or at least spirit; similar to what happens following a heated or lenghty arguyment with your family, where theres this clammy sensation in your heart and chest." So in my nerves did I recognize a sort of furriness, of which my muddled speaking was mingled to.
 The Second issue was about the habit of working. I did not like it at first, and often felt these sentiments of releif and or yearning. I would see some promise ahead, and heed it as some thing to come and free us from our load. The labor was not the issue so much, but the mental apathy and depravation. "I fought quite hard to not return to work because of this." Some live in a balance of 'getting through work to live lively', while I had always tended to overcome work and live more wholly and pure, 'as if we were on the side of reason as opposed to fun or relief'. I have this balance now, or rythm rather, but back then, it felt like a great weight to not be sucked into work. Much of our grief and apprhension came from our dissharmony with people. For I would not, nor would I give in to the nature of the effort, and live as though it were some inordinate problem, of which we needed more elaborte passtimes to warrant. I have always felt that you could ameloiraite yourself to the job and live in peace and harmony. "The excess of this being the fruit of liberty."
 As it were, the new year saw our mentality of work coming to fruition. As determing the worth of it made our amends to it both worthwhile and clear. "Plus, we saw the encumbency it it and thus worth of amending well: the sentiment that it was something you'd always return to, and thus was worth being a scale for contraiety, came about here." Early in the year we were still adjusting naturally, having more or less calmed down from our initial misgivnings and strife. But, into the spring, we began our 'spring writings'; which compared to the writtne notes we'd done through the prior year, was the first thing of worth we'd ever concieved. To this day, these writings are of heart and meaning. "Similar to the eternal worth of Skyrim, even though it measures less than the Witcher or Red Dead." I wrote about 50 tomes, centering on duty, adjustment, contentedness and worth. Mind you the seed was planted, and the beginning scents were there, but this idea of liberty, or habition to the common excess' in it, were not then know; and so our time was spent with our condition in mind. Which is to say, that our condition was given weight as something prominent, but overcombale. While our current fruits are one where the condition is barely regarded --thats how far we've come.
So, our goal ought be to exist without futile effort; which is to say, that upon death, we don't cripple ourselves. Such would be the result if we lost our ptera saddle, or Argie. We have benefited from it so far, and made the neccessary steps from it; but so will we struggle to reinstate ourselves upon a loss.
 By that, as with many things, there is merrit in establishing solid foundations. We may lose means to cementing paste, but lets use what we have no smartly. Down the pass we can build wells for water- these will not break. On our own, there are some trees infront of our house, but many down the hill. There are foes there, but we can use Ronda for protection. But we may also put in a little defensive hut, likely a 1x1 with two doors. We can store our wood in here, and have a sleeping bag or two; just so we can fall back while were gathering. I reckon something similar can happen in the front woods, and further up as we close in on the mountain and Red Wood.
 Far east of us lies a desert; the nearer part is rocky while the further part is sandy. Megatherium, or the sloth, excell at killing insects, and would serve wonderfully for harvesting them. I believe I saw such a mega in the rocky portion; though there are surley ones in the nearby redwoods.
 An argie would serve us the most, as it can haul loot and pick up moderate sized tames. I am sure it cant pick up trikes and stegos; but it can pick up ankies and doedricus for harvesting; which can then support making traps. Wood is quite easy to gather, but is limited only in scope. We ought bring several argies, load em up in a dense forest; while the doedricus removes the ardor of getting stone.
 In terms of taming, the issue is one of time. We have no means of kibble, nor the steady ability to kill large creatures for prime. We have our Allo, but another fighter would do. Likely a rex. At any rate, Jimmy is servicable, while a trike is likely all we'll need. We may make many narvotics then, and arrows, and remove that as a bottleneck for taming. The issue then is softened by surrounding the tame in progress; though, knocking them out in a decent spot is the trouble.
 Guns are also a needed addition. We've got 300 powder, but this will run through. We've got much charcol, and sparkpoweder is not hard to make. Metal is quite accessible too. We need tranq darts for doedricus, and shotguns for defense. My understanding is that 8 poweder is needed to make two bullets, plus another bunch to make a shotgun shell.
So I am trying to balance a life of work and leisure. We seem to have much of the latter, and the former, albeit strong in form, is less so in proportion. Our yoga could use an ironing out, as we doing less than we should when it comes to the morning and night. Our training is well, but it feels the slack of our yoga. Eating is also good, but the amount feels short when were off in other regards. Sleep is paramount, as it lets us rise and thrive during our early shifts. And otherwise, tenure our off days, as to be both good in their own right, but also for the days of work to come. I am glad to be turning things off earlier and getting more wholesome rest. We could stretch more; but our adjustment to eating and games, per the coming threshold is pretty swell.
 Our feelings of sloth and rancour are not absent, but qwelled by both posture and sense. Nor do I see a lack of fruitfull insights coming, as to understand what in the moment, may feel irritating. Our notion of liberty is spreading her wings otherwise, and that balance gives great delight in both our work and leisure.
 There was something I though of earlier, that I felt showed much insight regarding our worldy affair. For one, belidgerence is what it is, and identifying something as so removes a great deal of this defensive stance, 'whereby we overlook the fact that something is in such belidgerence bad'. The nature of our regard as that we don't ordain ourselves to things as if we were unsettled and or fascinated. Memory does holster us down, and remvoes the childish chimeras which otherwise innur us with fright; but our mind right now is less so about invalidating such scares, and giving more credence to what they actually are. As a matter of duty, I am not truly inducted to the belidgerences of Russia, China or Iran. There is a cosm of folk more local to such affairs; and as much as people abroad are invovled, it is moreso from state officials and or literaly public donars --in funds or thought. But I am not averse to be unaware. By such standard I would be unprivy to alot. Which indeed is the point. Besides that, my interest and or care is not burdened by far off happenings; and however clear, are not as so for even close matters and or effects around. "The world quickly beoomes a haze."
 As regards Ark, there is much good news. For one, it is clear and fair that we'd transfer our tames, selves and items. This makes all our progress un-superfluous. We'll have to rebuild, but with full means; and the true servers will be less full. It is quite clear that playing official has certain needs. For one, you need a Doedricus, as getting stone is an ardor. Wood is similar, though requires less hits to break a tree. A beaver or Thorny Dragon helps. Pteranadons help with scouting, getting cement from beaver dams and Crystal from hard to reach places. Chitin is not so accessible, and indeed is one of the hardest bottlenecks to break. But, considering the mechanics in which tames can be left, we are able to tame some Sloths, and put them in the eastern desert. This ought do. Trikes are an overlooked boon, as they gather narcos much quicker; and these are needed in ample supply. Taming is not so much an issue, but rather a nervous affair. Protecting them is a small concern, but rather their food and torpor. On one hand, carnivores can be more than serviced by mutton; and making such a farm is relatively easy considering what it provides. Otherwise, if you can get a vericous means to defeat large creatures, then you can do so for the sake of prime. Herbivores need some 200 mojoberries, plus another bunch in case your calculations are off. I learned this the hard way, but the lesson was one of sense rather than hefty adjustment. The torpor is literally the real drain; though, you do have some 30 minutes or so, and 50 narcos refills most bars. Still, you may need a 100 or so narcos, sometimes double for high tier tames. And so the trike is a beacon for that.
One can tell that their mind is off, when they concieve of things, of a negative sort in particular, and all that appears is a harrowing and or verocious sense. These hues are not absent from the garment, but are not a) what constitute the garment, at least entirely, nor b) the defining feature of the experience, as there are many moments and thus features which make up time.
 I mean to say though, that we may not be so well read or absent of ignorance, 'whereupon impressions make our view of things sulk, as if a rope that was drooping upon the meagerest way (in the middle)'. Or we may simply be irritable and or flushed, 'as to make even our deepest foundations whither away'. "I'd remark on this notion of maturity, where you feel even the slightest of errors, for you are so otherwise well', and that much of our resistance and or rancoue comes from feeling the slighest sense of fear or ignornance, 'as our mighty roots hold no room for any weakness in their sway'." Something like that, more or less.
 But I find that there is a truth to all matters, and one which is in fact not so servicable in being clear, but rather sound yourself.
 I would like to spend less time considering the complexion of our thoughts and how certain things feel like harsh smells that trigger it; but instead, actually think about, deliberate and amend to who you are and how you should be. Much of our duty as a citizen and a man will come to solidity then, 'and be it our poor choices or through a passing mark, so will bumps and or friction slide on by'. For too much time has been spent trying to define problems and or symptoms, as to give them less hold on me. That, or when we were younger, we sought things far and about; for your meager self becomes the subject for indolence and credulity without any comfort and dreams. Yet, having an un-meager self, but instead one which is in its foundations formatted and emboldened by action, makes for a sort of piercing progression into the world. "Where you cut the sediment and wind." Let us save our adjustments to things of a fair sort, rather than those prickly obituaries and or critiques of authority and belidgerence. We can understand these things, but in adjacence, or harmony at least, with the whole of the world and ourselves. In most ways, the basis for our flourishing comes from our experience as so; and thus upon a lapse, we defend what we believe to be rot and or inferior expression. "But from a place of recession, where your lack of movement and thus sense makes you vulnerable to all things that penetrate those who are not otherwise in the motion of a yearnfull and or relieving kind. Our position is one of wisdom and care, as motivated by the delight it brings and burdenless peace. But so does our steadyness here, beside acquating us with a prefeence for its flavor, that so we lack any of the motives for yearning ---of lack-- nor any accpetence for comforts which run contrary. It is only clear then, how upon your cease of fidelity and joy, that you have not. For if a man were not, nor a child and or boy at that, then we are nothing, empty, spurred by reaction and foresight."
 What is the truth then? Has it to do with the Gods, whether gratuity exists, and if so, in a begrieved fashion? Doth liberty lay in the palm of our hands, but rot away by distraction; in that if we hold no guilt or shame, that we in fact pursue other worldy things? Is such purity only a defence against things, who upon moments of freedom seek awe and comfort?
The way to live this life of abundance, is not to fret over every slip and fall, and thereby grind every coming minute down to its last detail, but to rather use principle to carry you, and reflection to guide you.
 In one, you have all you know, akin to a habit or disposition. We have no question about walking twice before we sit down and play. The requirment of this pulls on other tasks, but ought those be attended.
 Of such tasks, ought we reflect, as to simply relaign and adjust. A few that come to mind are mid-day rests, as to unwind and declout. Sleep is something which has been a struggle, and even in recent memory, our minor change has seen great effect.
 I tend to rest on the fact of nature, or at least our anthropic condition. For we need food, and thus whatever attaining that requires, so our direction lay. Of the techinqiue and or ability that serves us there, so is that our mean. In an economy, our mean is of itinerary; and so far as our post is contemporary, being present and hard working is enough. "You will not loose your mean without flagrant shortcoming." And so feel well about this, that of your deed, so is it mere and manageble.
 But it is one no less; and this orients all posts, be it of Kings or creation. My feeling over the years, is of those things which are not born of neccessity but interest, that so are these varied beyond content; while those things which yield provision, so are these particular and exact. One can see the moral of the story, in that, of the work one ought to do, despite it being work their fine to do, and passionate for even, that so is it work. And that experience of it being less a burden but a deed, or at least light amend, is liberty. There may be different sides to things, in terms of what gives them more flavor and levity. But so as a deed it lays, or at least amend. And as such, it sits in our life; as something amongst other factors, such as broader duty, general reflection and inquiry, breadth of leisure, and overal propiety.
 I wish not to have a childish view of things. In most cases, our aversion, or begrudgement rather, comes not from the source but of peoples reactions. By that, I don't see the news as some feeble output, but rather peoples consumption and response as tired and old. "This is a sort of childishness, where things are seen in the same manor, but responded to with the sort of brevity and huff that age brings."
Why does palpatine want the treaty signed? We know he met with the Gunray to get him to set up the blockade. He was peeved by the chancelor sending the Jedi, as he felt they'd interfer, and thus wished them removed. He then ushered the order to invade, and promsied he would make the occupation legal.
 Where I'm confused, is we learn later that Amidala wants a new chancelor, of which Palpatine conives to make himself. But this proposal comes from the senates inability to act quickly. Yet, if Palpatines wish came through, which we have little reason to suspect is not his intention, then the invasion would be made legal. But why does he seek this?
So I have a fair measure of the factor of children. It rests on the dynamic of whats contemporary or not, and what we can trust in. For in an older age, our sensibilities, had they arrived as they did during this age, would have come sooner; and this is so, for of the tradition which we'd overcome, or sense which we'd imparted, it would have to be earlier as to enact against the encumbant action of the more traditioanl force: there'd be no choice sooner than we had in our age, as the pressure was so great, that you either resolved to it or changed. By that, the fact of when and how a woman could give birth would be more relevant at an earlier age. In many ways, the likely less able means for this would correlate with our earlier amends to it; be it from common or enlightened grounds. While in the current day, our direction was developed under less pressure and thus occured later. But manifesting this into a relatively later age than would be in times prior, correlates with an ability for woman to have children later. It is not so eradict to suggest that a woman can have children in her 30s, late even. As far as I'm concerned, then end of our youth is the point where our maturity began, and thus the formation of that has taken peak hold, and from there is set for use. This does not bring us outside the age when we'd be in range of those who could bear children. I ought not be 50 and seek someone in their 30s for kids. Admittedly, one does not connect with others so deliberatley, but naturally, but you can imagine that the people you'd meet and or connect with --for ends of the serious sort-- would be around your age. And so if youro 40, then you can likely bet that you'd be meeting people who are such, and perhaps younger. You ought not ignore your deliberate plea. And despite being rather novel for our time, but the ability for medicine to support ones fertility into such age --for 40 is different than early 30s--- is meaningfully there. One perhaps does not naturally wish to adopt, but the option is not a hinderance. The process of doing so is more civil than fraternal (as in pregnancy), but our veganism and simplicity is not clearly a flagrant hurdle for us to integrate into the acquistion process. And indeed, be it for pregnancy or adoption ---and other comparable measures-- that the limit is mostly in who you'd have the child wish. Our want is one thing, and this inquiry is there to afford us room and leisure to have such want, with some sense as to the threshold in which it can no longer be had. But, upon it being so, and through laudible means, ie. a partner, that so would to decide and or deliberate to the end of a child, and thus prepare for it.
 The nature of this inquiry is that, of the process and or deeds within having a child, if were wondering about how were going to spend our time doing that, so does this speak to our lack of interest. For it is not an encumbant force upon you to have a child, merely a paraly of demands in order to earn what you seek from it. If such demands are not things your trying to figure in order to get what you want, then so it seems we don't want it. My life is somewhere other, as is my love. A child, like a partner, is something I could love, and greatly. But my current loves are of a secure and felicitious sort; and I wish to tenure that, as I would relations or a child. "And it is not a pet, in which you can have there by your side, and attend to them as much as that accompanying love requires. They are their own person, and your love ought be fashioned in the tenure of that."
So, reflection comes in two forms: the present and ahead. For we may have some amends, but of the sort which require facilitation, while others may be of the day, in which we can orient towards. This, to be clear, is not to say that we are getting into motion, but directing the action at hand. For even if we do nothing, in activity per say, so does those procure an effect, for which our near term aligment amends.
 On that count, the short term is such that we've done a little free range, but little else, and are feeling the bareness from too little of that, and intensity too. We are working tomorrow, then off till Wedensday; so we ought be more deliberate in our action, at least discretionate; as it is always a good thing to stroll twice before doing anything, but you may need to exercise too. "Let the Ark wait till you can rightfully attend." No matter, were a bit fine, but not entirely together, and coarse work will not sit with me well. The stakes are small, but we can do better. For one, the merriest of hatha's is in seated, arm stretch and neck rotation, plus some side stretch and twist, followed by leg twist and feet comfort, and ended by some rotation of supine and prone. A mereFlow would do nice, but merely for elevationo during the day. I think it is imperative that we make a pure standing portion of the morning routine, and give more emphasis to an soul folding one. Food wise, we ought eat, but all likely not grains. Potatoes are key here, as would some veggies do ---there may be green beans in the freezer. At the very least, our natural sway into the afternoon may be one of tired rest; and so may we fill ourselves with veggies. I'm alright with that. In many ways, this is the crux of our life, in that, if it is to mellow and faze, then let us settle into this well, having done what is right and neccessary prior. There are such requsities for a more uplifited and brimming life, in which you move fully and with stride --through the day, and not sleepy-- but so may circumstance and condition detain --or direct rather, for which you ought merely attend.
 In that, I see benefit in getting our hygiene said and done with. There's little consquence for spending the day having already cleaned. Cooking is no pain upon me, but shopping may not be to your delight; and so being resourceufll with what you have, and sensible as to what you later need (when you go) is important, as your posture about this will dictate the food you eat. Having some veg is a fortifying thing, as is some cranberry mix prior to the afternoon. Exercise is key though, as it creates the want and need for food.
this is an extremely usefull insight. Simply, of the work you have to do outside your employment, do it as to open up your time prior to work.
 Work will always be amended to; and indeed, there are requirments, or boons at least, beyond mere itinerary. That, and our life has general demands, as to be functional in any environement.
 If we are to thrive though, then we ought have all our boxes checked. Work is done when its done; and indeed, missing shifts leaves you with a feeling of unease, as if you don't have the right to feel done. Besides that, of the things you need to do outside this, be it for home or ability, do these; for they are neccessary, less your free time is experienced temporarily, until the effects of ignoring those things comes to pass. And, to my point, if you cannot strive becuase time and or energy is drained, considering that you must still go to work, complete those adj. tasks, as to experience rest and comfort properly. In the morning, shower, wash and prepare, as should you eat and recover. It would serve us well to wake up early, or at least continue our conssitency of appropriate sleep.
A crucial fact has arisen. You cannot lift wild dinos, only tamed ones. This was our plan regarding big tames, and that of Ovis. Now, we must tame them prior to transport.
 We will transfer all our things, and we need to be lvl 88 to do so. It might serve us, though cautiously, to go into the mountains and make some XP brew --as there is an XP boost for the weekend. I don't wish to ware ourselves out, but through sensibility, it is really just the trap setting and or food getting; better yet, the stress of seeking these things. Overally, meat eaters are easy, as their prime food can be gathered after their hunger has dropped, and thus their tame is quicker. Managing torpor is the trouble; as the Casteroides took less than 10 minutes to drain --at lvl 150! Everything else is somewhere in between; and I hear the Megatherium is quite high; though we can check. At any rate, as we did for meat --where we brought a preserver-- so can we bring a mortar, or at least a full narcotic supply. For what it's worth, the Megatherium has half the torpor drain compared to Casteroides. So we surely can manage. As long as we have some sense, then we can just park our argie and selves in the enclosure, bring all our narcos and or berries ---just get a couple hundred from trike, and it slowly brings up the torpor. We can then go afk, checking in every so often. I knwo its risky, which is the trouble in all of this. But setting things up properly can hedge the concern. At the very least, we can read, do dishes, or work on things that favor us checking more often. As a matter of safety, you ought judge the situation; in the case of oru trike, this was no matter. As for torpor, understand the drain, have narcos ready --as much of our ware with Boris was in having to go back and forth several times.
 At any rate, consider us lvl 88 and with our main tames cryoed. We have some means, or position at least, to get resources, so we may use this advantage to make better gear/equipment. But more or less, we can just make those things on our official server. The reality is, that we need a place for the Ovis. I would like to have our main base along the upper range near the Green Obolisk [stretching through that field, where there's griffins and redwood nearby, to the desert strip, where sands are in the south, rocky hills by the redwoods, and central mountains and canyons to the north. This would be a prime settling area]. As our goal ultimatley is to make an Ovis farm. Real farms will come aboot, and neccesarily for the Ovis --including Honey. But we need to accomdoate for the fact that we cannot carry the Ovis until their tamed. We may implement the Quetzel strategy, but this feels risky, considering that there is no solid way to get the Wild Ovis into the Queztel's platform. My thinking is that we set up taming pen in the Highlands. A grown ovis can be easily reached by us being AFK in our base --which holds compartively less risk than taming in the wild. The value of each ovis is such, that a) their level does not matter, b) they give some couple hundred Mutton at maturity (which takes 3 real hours). Obviously, we kill the males and keep females for reproduction. Then, we can use the Quetzel, or some form of a boat, in order to bring large amounts of them back to the main base for slaughter. For our purposes, I want to remain central. Aesthetically, I'd like to see mountains, the sand, and some east west direction as to see the sun set and rise. I do like foliage, but this is no essential matter. Our aim will be to get a motorboat --which requires deep sea diving for black pearls-- but a normal raft may suffice. I'm thinking that we put the farm near the east side of the highlands, then ferry the Ovis down to the coastline, near the desert. Then fly them up to our base. The distance of this may be negligable, but also significant. I am sure that we can use the quetzel to transport, but a raft may also do. Otherwise, having our desert base, ie. chitin farm, in equal longitude, makes for a consistent scenario; as our base is likeyl latitudionally in line with the desert base. "Using the boat reduces the distance to one of the lower base, rather than Hypotenuse."
Most importantly though is yoga. For monring pose, I'd like to have a soul standing motion --where you brings arm up and float them down. Consider hands to heart, shoulder shrugs and or neck stretch. MereFlow is good, though I would like a proper salutation, where we do chaturanga. Lifts are more concentrated, and admitteldy, not so properly built. While sun salutation is more of a flow. I've no problems with fair lunge. Legs and Supine are a bit long, but their form is fine. Supine in particular, could use a bit of shortening, as we do not really do it as it is. I like the idea of brining knee to chest, and various forms, but it ought be a little less meandering. Easy legs remains strong, and I wish to make an all mighty compliment, namely in seated, staff, groin, some form of supine and prone. Easy back can be extended; there is that initial supine vid which we can use. And I do like our intitia proneSupine, but as prep for sleep; and some more things could be added. Neck and Seated Twist are solid but drawn out, and we'd benefit in having them be a bit briefer. Lastly, I think we'd benefit from more core work, namely bridge. Plank is one thing, but I see much benefit for our back in doing bridge. There are likely other additions, and we may have a fuller session that is all rounded. But at least for a particualr point, bridge; add only what is in harmony with that.
Ark is a problem, like Diablo. The issue though is not that they are bad, but that we play them irresponsibly. The motive is usually one of exhasterbation, where we feel overly compelled to do something. Theres this restless high we get from comepleting something; but we can get to head down with it, and exhert ourselves till we drop. Running peak dungeons was like this for Diablo. The funny thing is, is that were not even having fun at that point. The issue being, that we feel in our bones that if we stop, our care will go away. "it is hard to see, that if you let your desire fan out, that you may actually return with real curiosity and want."
 There are a few elemnts to this. One, is that the wear of the game is similar to writing, in that it takes time and concentration. If we have little to give, then we tend to just not do it. But games are compulsive sometimes, and so you work upon your barren state. This causes much loss. Moreover, upon us being worn down, if we play from that state, then its like playing through an injury. I know you want to play the game, and there may even be good reason. But you a) must value your time and health foremost, and b) appropriatley spend your time doing so. If you get home from work, stretch and are all done, hop on. Its double tame rates, lets grab a rex. Fun. But if you want to go for a griffin, and its standard rates, then be deliberate about the fact that your not right for 2-3 hours. We made a great choice for instance, not going for a rex tonight. It was not appropriate. I mentioend this earlier, but then I meant that sometimes, out of need or simple good, ought you appropriate yourself to the work to come; whereby your leisure and comfort is ordained to your course of such position.
My life seems to be garnered, or driven, by observation.
 I wonder though why this is so, considering that at one point, we let the world pass us by. Never once were we so astute. Yet, I can recall that there was a sort of defiance and or invalidation, during those neutral years. "We'd make remarks about things, as if to say they were dumb. In some sense, giving us reason and or cause to not do things." It was always such, for woman, extra curriculars, school and work, it was not worthwhile or of interest; and because of some observation.
 What I am suggesting though, is that we did not actually learn something from what we saw, and thus resolved to do not, but rather used what we saw as an excuse. Like, 'haven't you seen girls, their so dumb'. And thats why I'd avoid them; when in reality, I was just too nervous.
 In truth though, there sits a source for this invalidation, or aversiveness. Namely, in that girls did not seem to like video games, nor mucking around, staying up late and eating junk. They held no connection in my mind with anything fun, but merely in the good in themselves. This good was something I always sought and still do. I just never wanted to do what it took, seemingly at least.
 I really want to take games seriously, revel in movies, watch great videos and hear wonderful music. I want to see the sun set and rise from various locations. And I don't want to pretend like something else is sought, 'to which we need apply ourselves heavily'. I always hated this impression I got from others, as if they were under some influence or sway. As I would say things were stupid, if only because they felt in conflict with our wholesome self, so did friends of mine who'd embrace that, or shift their sights to it, harckon a contempt from me, 'as if they were vying for smething stupid'. This was so because I wanted them to be with me. Colin getting his car being a key instance of this. "As if he were gone." Worse though, was a sort of envy; as my aversion to this things (while putting them down), held in it a mix of want and or striving. On one hand, it held, as mentioned, a girl I liked, who I wanted without the seeming need to party and be cool --to loose myself. On the other though, we'd dream about being in such circumstance, where we were parting and cool, but as ourselves. I wont trivialize this view of mine, or the thing in question, but the basic reason and or huff I had against 'partying' was that it felt a little ditatched from our preferred situation of friendsip, fun and kindness. It seemed more crude and vulgar, promiscuous and cavalier. I know that these elements are not always present; as I found in the later years that most people did something of a similar sort, but were not as 'rowdy and or vain'. Still, it did not resognate with me, bantering and socializing. I felt no real umph to the moment. I prefered to run around outside, or hike through the woods, 'get wet a little'. I just hated hanging out. "A moneky wants to swing!".
 To return to our point, I had this self centered sense, 'as one outside the crowd, yet stout in their ways', that we were (in our dream) central to said crowd, but authentically. Finishing grade 5, I was such, and into grade 6 and at some point in grade 7 also. Naturally, through my years and ways, I've attracted people while doing very little but what I want. I seldom yearned or coined for people; nor did I embrace that chance which new scenes could offer. Always though, I've held and loved but one or two friends, mozied in with whoever else, and distantly spoke with those who felt (compared to me) far out. The big boys, the hotties, the girls with big butts and no brains, or the nice ones who were not much but dumb fun and goofing off. These impressed themselves onto me, while others, despite being more bookish and polite, were free to ignore. "Clearly, we positioned ourselves in relation to everyone else. And of those who were of, or seemed to be, of hottness and esteem, I ignored; but as if a condition, in which everyone else was either meekish and or a flatterer too."
 And so one day, those who were by my side, and those who were at best nearby, went away; to lands far off, or out of my routine. We were left without, though with little missing. Yet, Colin became less and less my friend, and all be it years later, so did Justin slip on by. I was with myself, a bit of duty, an abscence to what was besides our common expectations, and an otherwise bunion of ignorance. Our youth, albeit content personal riches, lacked real delight. This time in our life is hard to explain, in comparison to how it is now; considering that the structure is not too dissimilar. Perhaps, one does not live by their inner wealth when only one kind of coin is present. As it were, we still had a form to our life, and close friendships; that, and we were endowed with comforts and pleasure. But as our friends ceased, and our duty was stated, so did those pleasures run contrary, and our selection of goods felt not enough, when there was no one but me around. Here our interest in health came about; as had occured prior when our relations with Ashley and company broke. We grew a fair love of reading and study; invented sums, and experienced a light, albeit dry peace, 'in a lawn chair on davison, stairing at the warm evening sky'. From here, we exhibted ourselves in a truly firm and confident way.
