To this day, I'ven not truly had a relationship or friend as became of this part of me.
So the thought I had yesterday was that I should cut to the chase with all these things of importance, put them in one section of our notebook, and simply erect them with the mere need of me. Just earlier today I thought to myself about the coming winter, and how it would be nice to notice the change of light; and this related to our set notion of making a sun and moon calendar.
 Now, this is great, but I'm looking for a reason to do it; as if it were some good, rather than fancy.
So I'd say this statement is for, though it comes from no different place.
 And that is the fact that of our cares, we place them under the view of want; for which much of our fear and guidnace is barred to.
 But I see, for one, that when these turbulences cease, that we are not with much care for 'affirmation' (if you will); which is to say, the stating of something to be, based on how you currently are; in the sense of how your feeling and how you'd like to be, ie. the motive, and the determined response, as more often than not drawn from some present aptitude or experience, 'as to certify some reasonable anticipation'.
 Writing is not so black and white though. And much of the time our words are not hear as either bandaids or encouragment, but rather refinements, or at least remarks upon the good we see; for it marbles it more, deepens it, and in a word, furthers its innateness and positivity; as opposed to breaching the gaps of our lack, and holding ourselves in --against the storms of want.
 What came to mind recently, was the notion of sums, and how we felt that it was not of true interest to me. In some sense, our roots, as engendered and perhaps natured, was so of conditioning: be it of tradition and thus the rule and suggestion around, or of our own fortifying amongst the uncompramising force of life; for which, to use an anlogy,'a slave, upon liberation, would still have to abide'. I say this then, because of our work and care, it has come from such grounds, of remedy and freedom. Years ago our measure was against the norm, and thus our stability amongst the difference around me; to the more recent era, where we grew more foundations and calm within the world, but of our own wit and sort. And as such, the matter of sums, or clouds even, comes as a floating ember, of a warmth who I could barely see, whereby even in our current liberty, so are our limits bade by the perpituity in it's tenuring.
 In a weird way, what I saw from sums is something that relates to the polis, or broader world. And that is the fact, that of our want, or care at least, 'of these things beyond our scene', that so are their motives linked to the invovlement of other people. A medley of affairs comes to be, in both vain and proper ways. And I do see some sort of reservation from those finer wants, of such folk and the world away. "In part because we feel innured to a place more near; and, in deference and humility, 'of what we can reasonably permitt ourselves to forgo', that so do those older, and to some extent lingering engenderments, lay. For which we don't rightfully or merrily open ourselves to things farther away."
 This is neither here nor there though. For the point is made along the lines of clouds; which for years now, have brought me so much delight, but never passed more than a surprising experience. And in our regards for deepening this care, 'and making it more near and known', we could never find the effort to do so; as it is a fact, that as much as we are conditioned, and privy to such a way, that so are such things as the clouds forgotten. "Their nature is nothing more but than to bring a moments delight, but little memory; for which, by a conditions nature to persist, and thus bring you through time, so the former is forgotten. Though, here lies our interest, or motive rather, as to bear more solidity to those moments, as to not have them leave us as time comes to flight."
 And so, whereas clouds bring joy, but pass on by, the care for sums is never latched onto. Strictly so, for the value I found was always sprouted into a conlsusion of worldly worth; and how, surely, the nature of the discovery would bring us some. And for a long time, it lingered as something to hold the future's promise; and indeed gave me much angst, 'as if our chance would pass'. But oddly though, as I do with all things, from Gods to mannered men, the joy is in the discovery, or work at least, to form, uncover, ligthen and grow; the triumph of which as best remarked within sums. And in some sense, as our writing was valued based on the magnanimity it'd bring, yet we for an age held too little ease or time to consistently do so, that of sums, it held not such worth, but rather served me in a most innately joyous principle. Yet, that wish for writing sat upon a recognition, and to some extent burden of a condition; to which, of the remedy of it, so did I wish for the uncompramised means to it. The point being, that as far as our condition is concerned, so does the matter of pure joy hold little worht, but instead those things which are best commised and fitting for it (the condition).
So I'd like to give an account about sums. Not so mucb about how it realted to or effected me as a person; but rather, how it actually came to be, and our feelings surrounding it.
 It was November 2015, and I was at Colin's dad's house. We had been smoking, nothing out of the ordinary. I sat in his living room, went on (whoever's) macbook, opened notes, and without much prior knowledge, nor but an incling of sense, we wrote out each number of the keyboard --the top row. Only, after each one, we typed the = sign as well. The details are not so clear, but it is better to say that I used the = button as both the + and =, but only in effect used = for I did not hold shift. I believe the line of thought was, '1+2=3+4= something something'. And for whichever reason, the idea of adding the digits without showing the operation became clear, and lead us to the following.
 We wrote in a small notebook, from the first line of the first page, 1123581347112358, and I was in awe. So cool and interesting. I followed this with 22 through 88 --as 9 just repeated itself. I do belive I did the same thing, but with 111 also; and perhaps I attempted 1111. What struck me though, is that in most cases the sequence repeated itself; only, in certain classes, ie. 111, certain beginnings did not. There was always something random that occured as the sequence went on, and I couldn tell as I was going that something was off.
 What it turned out to be, at least where the story goes, is that we made the mistake of using a whole term as a result, then added those digits. So, we went 112358 to 13, which went 1347 to 11 and thus repeated. But the error here is that the operation, as we came to know, was merely a manner of index, rather than the amount of digits. And so we made a truer sequence, starting with 11 and back again; only this time it went 112358437188988764156281911. This is where our contemporary view lies.
 What followed though was not the current model of today. Indeed, the work was proper and the results so. But why things were occuring as they did, at least how the operations occured was not yet known. For we went 11 through 99, and 111 through 888. 1111 was equally clear. But some day, at least a year or two on, we realized, in part from dabbling, that this manner of sequence had nothing to do with the ordinance of 9. To our delight, you could do any pair, triple or quad of starting numbers, and have them repeat, within any ordinance, or degree rather. And this came to be so, that of the number line we were working on, that the ordinance merely became the height of that, and that the index, or at least line which our values drew from, repeated that base-height iteration. As such, this made it more clear why the terms would repeat upon being added to the heighest number; or more generally, why adding 5+8 would equal 4 within range 9. You simlpy counted, going 5 over from 8 on said line, and the result was naturally 4. In the times prior we would use a mental crutch, thinking for starters that range 9 was all there was, and as such, the common number of 13 --for which 5 and 8 would sum to-- was merely being so, but 'summed' as it were, by adding the digits of said term. Our beginning mistake, as mentioned, was not taking this term of 13 as one point, rather than 2; of which, when adding to 4, left the 3 as the prior term. Indeed, our insight was correct, in that the 13 ought be a soul term; but the discovery, of which our current model is based, is that you ought not merely fill in the blank, 'or do a behind the scence operations', whereby convention tells you to internally sum the digits of said term (to make 4), but rather, that of range 9, you are merely going to a point 5 over from 8, of which, given the iterating nature of the line, brings you to 4. As it became known, 'there is nothing outside the range which is considered number'.
 As it were, we recently formulated everything. I'll concede that the way to denote the terms within the line/index is not so clear when compared to those within the starting sequence, ie. 11, 222. At any rate, of such sequence, to completion, this on it's own is a run. The height of the ordiance, or range rather, is the class, as you may have runs within lines that go 123123, or 123456789123456....Also, I must mention our most recent discovery, that of these lines, they are merely positional, and so, for notation sake, we say that the 10th point, or a range of 10, is really so as to go ....89A, and so forth for 11,12, going ...89AB, 89ABC. These symbols go in the seuqnece, as if you were dealing with any point/index. For 2+9 in range 9 is 2, and 1 in range 10. As it goes, 9+1 =A, +1 =1.
 No matter, with runs, you have sets, which are more or less some small collection, or whole --as to make a whole set-- of comparable (or total) runs within a class. So, of range, or class 9, the standard set is 11,22,33 up to 98 --as 99, or range range, is redundant. Of all possible pairs, or combos rather, from 11,12,13 up to 97,98,99, so does this remark the whole, or total set of such class.
 The final matter is that of degree, or kind. For in each run you have starting terms, and these act as the kickoff to the sequence; and are generally what characterize the run as being done, for be it of neccessity or calculation, such point repeats. But the nature of each run, within a class, can vary in its kind; namely, that we may begin with 2 terms, ie. 11 or 44, or we may begin with 3 or 4. In that case, if we began with 111, we would add these three together, then continue to sum the nearest 3 terms. Just as 11 becomes 112, and you calculate 1 and 2, so in 111, in becoming 1113, you focus on 113, for which 5 is made. By my testament, all runs of a second, third or fourth kind, of each class, express the sequential behavior epitiomous with sums. The only thing to note, is that a) certain runs, in lower classes particularly, will exhibit overly simple characteristics. The 22 run within class 4 is the example of this, as it just goes 224224. Better put, 44 of class 8 just repeats itself. Another point, is that the limit of kind is so by the magnitude of class. I will state that there is perhapds a roll over effect, for which my claim is not entirely true. But generally, for common folk, you don't do runs of 4 in class 3 and lower. This does not illustrate the complete error that my claim could be, but for instance, doing 1111 within class 3 just repeats itself. This is common and remarks perhaps a mere standard of operation. For I have not echausted the effects of such runs as 11111 within said class. In fact, it would seem, that such run would proceed to 111112, to 3, then 2 and what not. I do not know if this creates faults, only that it is in some sense the opposite of reduction, or contraction rather, 'as your oveflowing'.
There was a point in my life when the oblige to progress was a good in and of itself. Of those we listened to, so would we feel satisfied with the fruits that we could share with them.
 This changed though, starting with food, in 2018, when our progress was no longer this foundatioanl prescence, merely a part of our being. And because we needed less care for it, we had less to concentrate on.
 This brought up the question as to our real motives. For in our prior years, we saw much, be it of genersoitiy or acclaim. But as of late, I had been asknig myself what actions we were really going to take. Would we actually write, share sums, lead?
 There is more than one side of this; but part of me has grown to feel that rooted in all of this is a sort of instability that prompts this concern. And that such instability is in fact a tell of where your going wrong, 'as in a splinter causing a throbb: said cause being the thing the effect points to, for you to find and change; or perhaps reinforce and tend, if the effect is good'.
 There is an odd form of acceptence in this way then. For indeed, we end up seeing the world for what it is: far and long. And this exhibits itself in all things good and bad, 'as we see and feel remorse for, or desire of'; the point being though, that of such things, there is more or less a great deal which goes unchanged, and as mucnh as you really knew all, 'for which knowledge and age raise the scope of what that included', that so are you not thrown of or elated by the fortunes of you or others.
 I say this though on two ends, one being our formative plea against the grit of being so 'accepting', as comes about through centering back via our evident seperation --from that which has us feeling so unerved. Secondly though, is when we are inline, our steps are more than present, firm and generally rich. And we think from here, with all forms of content. The world feels slow, away, 'with not much on my mind'. It is a process were part of, for which the deaths and or bounties over disitance strike us not. Only, I feel there is some truth that hasn't been said; and will be from these grounds.
As regards the basis for sums, so the frontiers lie in its demonstration. On one end, you have the inquiry of its cause, which is to say, the general rule that produces comparable results within all runs. Similar to this is the notion of addition, whereby any combination that satsifies the general conditions will yield a result privy to the character of the operation. My suscpicion has seldom left the notion of implicity, or at least an idea which I coined through that phrase. On one hand you have the demonstrations of repeatability, and why, within a given class, certain terms pair together. In class 9, two, five and eight, one, four and seven, three, six and nine, all run with one another (in their trio respectively). Why this is so, can be shown, or at least pointed to through mirrored addition, where you take a number, such as three, and repeatedly add it's value to itself, and track the enumeration. The result is reached when the final sum reaches the ordinance. In the case of three, it takes three steps, and no iteration. In the case of six, it takes three times also, but takes one iteration. In the case of one, it takes nine steps, but zero iteration, while four takes nine steps also, but with two iterations. The insight though, is that rather than fully enumerate the terms, you can simply enumerate till they reach a value that, if simply summed, would yield a simpler term, but one that would then enumerate to the ordinance. Case in point, seven takes three steps to 21, which becomes three, and thus takes three more steps to nine. In the case of eight though, it either goes to 16, which is then seven, and you multiply both, or you go three times to 24, which sums to 6. This is the key insight though, of the contrast between 1,4,7 and 2,5,8, as the latter's shortest route to the peak is to some degree of 6, which then takes 3 steps to the peak, but with a further iteration. 1,4 and 7 can reach three in the same amount steps 2,5,8 reach 6, only the former then enumerates to 9 without further iteration.
 The second part of implicity is one I can only remark upon in inquisitve, rather than demonstrative ways. For it is in truth our finest insight; though one which I have not put into detail; and its obscure nature has not helped me in attempting so. In general, my thinking is that, be it class two or some other,of kind two, three or four, that the reason the repeatable effect occurs has to do with a neccessary, or implicit nature as I say, within the invokation of an iterative operation; and that by doing so, so much as you foloow the iterative rule, 'whereby your kindred operation is bent to the repeating line', that so does this force the sequence, or run, to repeat. I cannot demosntrate or state why, but, as for the mechanical reaosning, so the uncertainity lies; but, of the conduct of whatever it is, so is that implicit aspect the avenue which I feel proper in thinking about such (process).
From the beginning, there are two elements to sums, one being the iterative line in which we operate on, and the condition which 'moves the domino so to speak'.
 For the latter, this really amounts to the starting scenario, by which in the case of runs, you have some set amount of terms, and based on that, proceed to sum said amount in peretuity, ie. if you begin with three, then you sum those, then the newest three. Another example, which gives reason to call this element a condition, is diamonds, or something akin to Pascal, where you take a run of repeating digits perpendicular to eachother, and simply find new values based on the two terms which hover above. In all cases, this model begins with a key term so to speak, all by itself; but then the two that run to its right and left, on the second row more or less, so is our first sum between them, in the main column, and one row down. This is where it naturally goes. Then this first sum becomes the other corner of that which is summed from the proceeding left and right edge. This is the condition.
 The second element is of the scale we operate on. In the case of natural arithematic, you simply add N+1 infinitely. In the case of iterative arithmatic, as in the counting of hours on a clock, you go n+1 up to the peak, then begin again. In addition, if you added nine to four, you'd reach one; as would be the case for one saying, 'hey, what time is Tim going to be here, if he's coming in five hours, and its eight o'clock?'.
 One oddity lies in our display of such enumeration; for we may be vertical or unbounded. In the former, which I'd say is most common, you act as though each term, or spot, has a block per value, and that per our running condition, there'd be two such stacks, whereupon the first sum, the third value would be a combinded stack of those. In the standard case, a single bit next to another, creates a double bit and so on. Only --and this is the heart of the method-- that we act as though there were an imaginary ceiling, 'which we call the peak, iteration point, ordinance', and that when adding terms together whereby they'd overcome such threshold, you merely add the difference to the next point: and indeed, this next point becomes its own value, and only of the difference. So, in that standard case, from three to five bits to eight, adding these latter two will leave a difference of four. In some sense you can imagine, that as you reach the peak, go over and build up the next stack with however much you have left from the prior sum. "Interestingly, you may do this in an equal, yet more illustrious way, by switching the direction of the stacks upon their overflow. The results are the same, but instead of a long city block, you have somethinig akin to an arrowhead, or fletching as I call them --though, the initial name has been lost to me."
 I bring this all up though, for this vertical method, albeit standard, is not the soul one. For, in our starting condition, we could just move the points over and over, 'as if we were bounding that many spots over'. So, if you imagine a natural number line, and have a sum of two and four, you'd begin at one and move over by the latter amount. You'd merely bound over that may strides to the sixth. In an iterative case, the number line is layed out in equal fashion, differing only in its values. Comparing the two, the first 18 terms of the natural line run through 1-18, while the iterative one, runs 1-9 then 1-9, yet still reaches the same point. When we have five and eight (for the latter), we bound as we normally would, going five points over from eight; and because the line goes 8,9,1,2,3,4, so is 4 reached. The difference is that we don't show value, visually that is, by magnitude, or stacks if you will; in their case, the value is merely shown at its point and then combined to show the effects at the next point. Whereas the act of bounding explicitly shows position. In some sense, the stacked case will repeat the arrangement of stacks, as will the procession of bounds repeat also.
One way of lookinmg at the stacked approach, is that instead of starting each sum from the same base as the prior terms, you simply add the value frome the top of the most recent term. So, in the case of three and five, you move one spot over, then, in line with the top of five, you add three. Then, when adding five and eight, you add five atop the floor of eight. And, where this differnece in approach lies, is in a repeating block of the ordinance, whereupon five being added to the top of eight, the amount 'sticking out' is four. This creates a bit of an ascending stair case.
So, the week is coming our way, and there is a current of good ability. I see it though in a bonding sense, that even if the day next comes and were not privy to dedicated work, so may we keep ourselves up, 'on the draft so to speak'; and produce, or at least re-accumulate, some of our lost rigor and tear.
 On that count, I merely need to eat enough through the day, maintain our great oblige to warrior and spots, keep our momentum forward as to do a comparable job in recovery, and bring it together with some time at the park. Perhaps, if I may, seeing as we work the middle of next week, that you let the first session come, as best fits it, then wait till the pre-eve to work, 'on monday for a wedensday shift', and have that be a more training emphasized day.
 But, for the real good in these days, is that of our liberty. I find that an exercise day is not terribly conducive to far abounds, 'and that those may come when the feeling is fair, and paried with our ableness; which, mind you, we can acquire through heading over golden ears, and going to Poco'. No less, much of our cares fit on the most fit of days. Diablo for one, has its new campfire the days next, and likely some annoucment for the current season's event. I'd like to get back on our Spiritborn, 'pound the drums' so to speak, and a) get in the rythm for the new event, by which we'd start our Sorc, but b) keep this part of our routine, or whim of wants, in doing whispers especialliy; a few dungeons even. Playing Battlefield is fun, and I am enjoying the campaign. The missions are nice, but pretty long. All is fair and well. Star Trek is also a delight, with some surprising epsidoes so far. Also, I would like to get some books to read. I've really enjoyed reading our print, and am entised to bring them with us. "Great for merrying our heart". But I would like something more presidential, and also something that remarks the perceived norm of an ether prior to relativity. "I believe this fairs insight to the imposed notion."
 For tasks though, I need clean my room: remove my bed, clean all sheets and blankets, scrub the floors, and organize my closet. In tandum with this, we need our wintere clothes, namely our extremitites; and a place to put them. I would also like to start some of our models, namely of the sun and moon, the mountains, and perhaps outline of a municipality, in its given committes and actors. "As not to spark alarmed, or any worth believing in on others account, that some committee did this; considering it plays only a small part."
 I'd also note some remarks about food, namely, that of our Brussels today, we found much life in having them just around lunch, in realtively small portions, followed then by our smoothie. I see our cucumber and peanuts being paired with Kale for this purpose. In tandum with this, a slice of tomato, sunflower seeds and Kale are an almost certain root of our day, and pairs nicely with fruit after. Similar, our kalecumber and nut salad pairs greatly with our smoothie for midday. There are indeed pairings of grain, fair light ones, and otherwise selections for fried and or comfort food. We may, foremost, remark upon these with the sense of bearing our forray into them, ie. our place and or quota for spending --to simplify our days. We did, and could still improve, a similar matter as packnig for work; and how this can be done bit by bit through the day.
 Another thing of worth are these clear skies; which, albeit not as saturated, hold a depth of virbance, and by that, hold little heat as to difuse the horizon: bearing more clarity for Baker to be seen. "To the northern path we go."
Can I even make the remark I wish to. Perhaps a blank statement will allow me to unbind this notion. That is, that of the world, as perhaps portrayed or known, that it can be as such known, or considered, along an outward lense, whereby you see all in relatio to you, and by virtue of things blending together at greater distance, 'as something a 1000 miles away seems as blurry as something 2000', that so there appears a world around you, all connected via said haze. Or, you may live effectively with blindness, but per your understandable ignorance, in a state that exepcts certain things, as in the position and or movement of peopel and states around, but you do not immpart yourself as some relative force to that, but rather see it as some far away dynamic which operates independant of you. This notion taken to its extreme can make your own body feel unclear, as if it is apart of nothing. You may also see times flow, whereby all that happens that you may not know, and may indeed happen, gets swalowed up by the general mass of other things; leading to the fact that most things get forgotten.
As a boy, we indulged in joy, finding little to get in our way. As a teen though, the matter of adulthood loomed ahead, and we found our relief in satiating our preperations; and much of our comfort came from that which sprouted from this ease.
 As we reached that new era though, our tree hadn't formed, and while lacking much, and turning to things of a remedying or rebelious sort, we in one way or the other found refuge in ourself. I'd say that this wasn't out of desperation; our seperation from lethargy and destructive affairs was that. But, as if a kite being carried by this new wind, or updraft, so self regard was caught, and carried through the age to come.
 We began with this quite pleased, but found our surroundings hard to meld with. We lost a friend, our naievety, and eventually our joy. For in this process we became a walking man; in speech and stride. But, in bringing this to fruitiion, and bearing ourselves on our own count, we were left with nothing but the stalest of stability; and those forces of destruction emerged.
 As the history goes, we found more clarity and direction for ourself. The words of men became a comforting ally, as did our surrender to a life whose quality and permit was bade to ourselves. "We were the measure." I found though a great deal of irritation and nerve in accepting this; as we felt ourselves, to some extent, burdened by the want of some place else, 'far away, full of adventure and fame'. But, even as this ironed out, we found this inability to accept our life as it was. "As if the simple measures we took to harnis our joy was enough; despite being full from the latter." As such, we choose to leave our position; perhaps in response to our life feeling stuck to a condition; for which our circumstance gave leave for us to find room and remedy to this pressing weight. Idle times followed though and we let our passion guide us; to some place different, 'whose far away appeal made all motions beyond seem neccessary'. But they did not come, and the idea of the future lost its venure.
 In most ways, all that has occured since is a cultivation of our sense of responsability; and how, beyond our mere acceptance of happiness from our little life, what there was around. For indeed, the disease we found earlier from being content was so unsettled by the apparent suggestion of reason to be discontent; as if, of norms and world affairs, that much was either ooccuring or to come, which was worth preparing for, or at least not warranting a moment to settle.
So a discpline is a kind of craft or trade, likely employed for money. A profession if you will, of skills that you leverage for such coin.
 There is purpose though, in say the case of Jordi, who as a engines officier, is employed on the enterprise; and, per the need of such ship, so has Jordi been compensated for his ability. But, as for the purpose of the enterprise, so may that be judged on its own, while Jordi is either a) compensated irregardless of that, and merely grasping the oppertunity that someone elses interests provide, or b) he shares, to an extend at least, such interest, for which his employment is not entirely pecuniary, but for the purpose that bestows that.
 In our own case, we work in a store department; and as such, provide, or contirbute at least, towards our branch of said store, which on the whole, provides a collection of such services to customers. Moreover, whereas Apple may provide phones to folk a world away, so are our customers local, and more than likely of our community. "Surrey is more or less local, while those within Ridge Meadows are part of our direct community."
 In this way, the prescence of people brings what the value to pass, and considering the baser needs, so doth the hotspots of resources, say water, 'then being handed out', refelct our own portion of the extraction, manufacturing and distribution stream. In that, we are of the most latter, 'handing out if you will'.
 I find that these remarks speak to a state of ours which we all to often struggle to embrace. That is so, that of this purpose, or clarity rather in the complex of value'd offerings, that so does this speak to me clearly, and connect us to our path, 'as to at least understand where I worth lies'. Absent of such thoughts, of our worth, we see only and care only for what we need. But, with a brighter mind, we may remember, and at least produce (as in this inquiry), the plain and evident thoughts that bring more reason to what we do; perhaps, best put, that there are such reasons, 'of which our state, as can be described as ample and tot, sees, or finds at least'.
So, all of society is really the enforced and or elected agreement, by which some standard of civility can occur. Perhaps this is natural as one gasping for air; only, one cannot be without the latter, while the former is something that developed, and is thus more of an invention than a ingrained characteristic. As a cub learns to hunt, so has man learned to speak and cooperate. That is his norm; but in how he conducts himself neccessarily, or at least via regulation against any of his acts which conflict with such regulation.
 Now, culture seems to be that which naturally spurrs from his humour and want. It differs at least from the resourcefullness that society is born upon, and instead remarks upon something warming and enlivening.
 In such ways our culture is one of consistency, harmony with our civil working, and otherwise spent on a good stream of living, rather than particular moments.
So the height of our youth fell under a developing despair. In an ideal world, we'd have more friends, woman, prospects in both work and career, and by that, have some experience travelling, having fun, going out, being happy.
 But our time was not such. The reason why is a point worth discussing on it's own, but I will simply say, that of our care to do said things, I had them not; at least, as much as I did, so were the means an end to my wishes.
 Much of the story, as it goes, runs along the fact of our feelings during such time, and how lost we felt. The quintisential moment of this was during the first break of our semester in college, and, having much ahead regarding studies, we felt so unappointed to the ends of it all, that the ability to not care was absent, yet our actual wish to care equally so; and so we felt an overwhelming sense of despair. "I wished not to wake up."
 Now, what follows is an element, which to this day, i still struggle to define. But, I may have a good way of describing it. Simply, of such days, they were bent on the freedom we had by virtue of being young, and the resources and time to cultivate the oppertunities around. "For, why wouldn't you." Much of the good experienced came from the harmony of being on track and all the comforts that we had leave to delve in. "Homework was done, go out if you please." But because we were not on such path, so did an alternative present itself. In and around May, we were becoming burdened by our prior year or so of smoking weed; and we made several attempts to quit. One day, sitting at Justins in the morn, we made a choice that would be lasting. In that moment though, we found ourselve, more or less following, in a enthused state upon reading an article about the richest sources of nutrients in food. For whatever reason, for me and Justin both, the idea spoke to us; akin to one who learns about maknig money and naturally finds excitment in the promise ahead. So we felt the same about eating so apparently well. We made the worst smoothie we'd ever make, but somehow felt good in a whole swat of ways; namely, in this unspekable pride and acclaim, as if we'd done something principally well. But, also, in our actual heart and bones, a feeling of rigor and vitality. The details of this and what followed are not important, rather, as to why we leaned into this line of activity, and how such a line can be described. For it contrasts with a path, where one has some destination in mind, and works for that foremostly, and pleases themselves in ways that they find leave for; and the better their actions are, the less contariety these pleasures bind. But, I compare our life which has gone otherwise, in that, of what may please us, in staying up late, eating what we please, watching porn or reading twitter indiscrimately, wanting cars and clothes and ventures of fun, so are these not what our course of live made room for. As I'd put it, these are open pleasures, in that they are merely sought for what they are, and done so as much as they don't intrude upon that deeper path.
So, two rules seem evident, and by that, eligble to base all our reasoning on. Our choices, or acts rather, will come through less than concieved ways, ie. from former habits, ideas, whims, suscipions; but so can our reasoning act as a routing force, in both a backdrop way, or one of actual disposition.
 The first rule is of truth, and that be it of the Gods or public statements, that all that really is of concern is whether something is true. This, to be clear, removes a great deal of confusion when we mistake our inquiry as to the nature of truth, and not whether something is. Per our rule, truth is a given sense in terms of whether some idea or claim is becoming of reality. Imposition and or immanence may be part of both, but whether one or the other truly is the case is what we seek, 'so much as we seek the truth'. I feel that our method of inquiry can infuse this notion of truth, and include in it, be it entirely, or at least in some specific way, certain properties or elements that give bearing to things subject, or inquired against, such measure. When we think of ostentation for instance, we may a) hold a degree of reserve, in how our wisdom, or measure rather, should be stated, and by that, what the properties for truth are; but still b) remark upon the metrics and or features that we believe truth to hold (in the topic at hand); at least, given these factors, as a backdrop we'll say, how do our subjects fit to it. Indeed, the grand inquiry is similar to a refelction, where all the material is there, but we only wish to bring our view of things together. And by that, our idea of truth may in fact be pre-mature, in both judgement and the established scale. But the idea at least of their being some truth, is one that, be it in gauging the manner of things, or in regards to our outlook of such efforts, 'as to have a certain kind of faith or hope in how truth can ultimately or not be found'.
 The second rule is one I'll build from a statement given by Plutarch. Following his sentiment, of which Seneca also shares, 'and Montainge too regarding death', that we ought have prepared both our sense and familiarity, 'or in other words our thoughts and consideration', about the likely things to happen to us; and, of the point, that given our human manner, that to avoid our irrational, emotional and or naieve response, 'which, being rule, has a bearing within us to occur', that so ought we imbide through writing and reflection such preperations. Too, there is the matter of returning to a normal status upon being tossed this or that way. Better put, things occur to us, and we may, akin to discipline, form ourselves as not to be so suscipitible, 'as is often prescribed regarding the potential of pain'. But, the matter of precets and reasoning, 'for which cultivating principles is a general look at this', that so are these meant to keep any heightned results of those experiences in check. As someone severely injured is not bade to prepare a means for invincibility; rather, given the (we'll say) moderate endurance and or incorporation of pain, that so may they not curse the Gods, or damn those who put them in harms way. In my fairest travels, 'where the fear of something I suspected besides our whimpering view to be true, could be', I never found such calm and gallantry in the experience of seemingly suffering men. And indeed, as I have more recently been struck by while reading the Gallic war, that nearlly all who fall upon such threats do so amidst an assortment of commradery, loyalty, purpose in fighting, experience in both the preperation and culmination to such affairs, and, in some sense, a familiarity with the actual pangs and or misfortunes of such acitivty. It is an odd thing indeed, and nothing but the pointed base for these remarks. But they serve as an example of what cultivating (through reason and experience) one's character can do, especially in these most trying of circumstanses. In more moderate, and more likely put, civil and or agreeable tidings, the risks one has are of eny's flame, rhetoric's rancour, labor's sigh, and pleasures plight. These qualms sputter in and out of your life, giving minor moments more negativity than they should, nor making abled feats so light and easy to bear; nor do the finer moments in life get suckled on as they freely could; and you may generally lack a center of peace and direction. "Our human nature lies." And the rule, is that we may respond, divulge, invest and or promulgate our time and method regarding the world's fruits and chains. But we must fundementally attain and heed the tides of our stomach and heart. "For the truth in that matter, is that those burned at the pyre hold even more delight, when compared to a kingly drunken fool, they know their course is from reason, and thus affords no deference to pain, 'as if it were something becoming of plighty, but merely a burn to their resolve'." Know and as such form yourself they say.
Our nerves are a litte inpinging. One symptom is that of role playing, and how I feel solemn about embracnig other worlds, when this one is detached from me.
 There are roots to this for sure, but surely our choie has been made; to fix our scooter, call in for our shift tomorrow, possibly walk the fay following. There is not much else. Folks at work also bring me a flatter type of cheer, as I see myself somewhat ungrounded, and thus unable to share enthusiasm, or feel as content and unawkward with those who I simply pass by. A good morning always helps, to Cassandra, Morgan, Ybraj, Bella too.
 I remark of the fact that of our preperations, they are small, and that of our shifts,they are shot; and of the one which is long, so this gives us more time to learn, and our breaks are double. Even our shifts the week following, which may remain as they are, so does this bring a fair decision to work less in the weeks following. As more often than not putting our shifts up with due time will see them taken.
 Said remarks remind me of our core thoughts; that of the truth, so do we seek it, but only as much as we are not living with respect to a condition. Only, as for work, which we delight in how poor it is not, nor the time coming, that so of our broader conditions, that so would we embellish the truth, relish our progress and succour the discovery: but only if it were something which we were pleased to make light of, or earn liberty. Work feels this way, as we our bound to it via our neccesity. But so as a man are we equally bounded. And my ripest comment is that of what we are conditioned to, it is in probale abscence to that which would endear us to the clouds, and better yet, dispose us to both the constitiion and inquiry to see them farther and in greater detail
I feel as though I have judgements to pass upon certain sources for information (and impressions alike). As I revere, or at least find deep worth in certain books, and forms at least, so are there thsoe which don't measure up. But I take the advice to listen and not worry too much about the fact that someone is speaking, but merely be open, and be only cautious about the worth of their words. I am not always able to sit like this, and need to affirm the detriment of their words. One symptom is that of envy, where I wish to minimzie them for I fear others are listening and praising them; and ignoring us. This comes about though from a poor constitution. Indeed, our choices seem free to avoid this; but still, in our own controlled ways, there are still words of the sort. "Hearing about Battlefield or Civ for instance." And the reality, is that I could just be pleased with the words being said, or not, but therein also extract any worth, or not; but from a well set and rested ground, as not to be perturbed, and one of discretion, as to take in what is in fact useful, and let other aspects pass by.
 I compared this to life, in that, of our manner and ways, that it pays to state your care and assent. Only, when we are flat or stiff footed, we tend not to benefit from a forming of hearty and lifting substance, but rather contain our unnerved and squimish impulse. Our remarks then are not of someone, so to speak, who is taking things in a letting them by, agreeing to that which is either pleasig or useful, and not being unfruitfully taken by that which deserves not to be so, nor feeling annoyed at the thoguht of such things comnig your way; that so, in our life, are we not able to remark of the good in it, the fairness but also right of the deeds around. Were bracing, similar to that sense of envy.
So having an attitude regarding money which is quite feasible, makes for much ease in this world.
Our department is quite united, similar to a group in highschool, who stays for an event, and some hand things out, others work in the back. It feels extra-curricular.
 But it is our jobs, and we more truly spend our time working than chatting. Some feelings of guilt or envy may arise; but it is true that folk go on their way. What we need to compare in is in saying hi as we pass on by. I look at Brianne, who is not somebody I really connect with. Yet, I have a fair relation, where I can be cordial, and not feel awkward. There are other people though who I don't know their names; some of them I'd like to. But a good morning will do.
 The backroom is often seperate from those picking, so I don't get to speak much to Paul, Audi, Sam, Vienna, Will, or Mo. But say hello, or 'morning Mo'. It gives you ease to make eye contact later on. And as for those nearer to me, Matin, Moji, Teresa, Terra, Kim and Carry, Carli, are all fine with me, and share their enthusiasm. I am friends with Joey and Alex, and to an extent Andy. Chenoya to; I can speak with them, and do not feel awkard around them. With Bella though, I am open with her more than someone like Morgan or Ali, yet we don't really know eachother, or have much to talk about; and so it settles to that former status. Which gives me a sense of known tension or conflict. "As if were avoiding eachother." Cassandra is similar but less so. In each case, say good morning, and I don't know much else. There shouldn't need be small talk. With Morgan, we go on by; same as Ybraj. While Chanyoa is pretty open with me and things naturally flow. Bella is in between. Sonal and I greet eachother often, and I greet Nargus --who does not avoid me doing so. Bella is just not that nervous or willing to offer anything, or be open to my offering. She is like Aron in that way. More or less though, I mean only to be friends, as I am with Joey or Kim. If she is not open to me, let time by, and you will become more acquainted. Be polite and address her, as it is weird not to and then make little contact following. But go on with your day.
The fall has come; and, to my pleasure, quite slowly. I noticed the cool air some weeks back; and to my surprise, the leaves have stayed up. They are falling, and soon, those that are left will quickly loose their coat. But I do enjoy the look of it all. Foremost is the evening sun, vibrantly shining on the leaves. The big trees that line 193 are quite nice, as is the big one in our back yard. There are these smaller ones though, orange usually, which are so nice, and catch the light so nicely; I realize I need not but my eyes, and perhaps the quality of grace, as to see and appreciate the very beauty right outside my door.
 It would seem that our scooter, as all vehicles seems for a young person wishing to leave their door, is but a vessel for their want and glee, to leave and advneture, go far; and, too, whether deep down or embellished by their fortune so far, that they feel langer and poor when this frontier is removed. As we did for the world, so are we in tune with what is privy to me. Indeed, we take for granted our legs; but we hold zero frustration over their reliability. If they leave so be it. But while they are ours, they are in control more than any vehicle or device could be.
 It is through economy then in which our position settles; and generally, less we are one of buisness or performance, our spot in such affair will be local and set: moving only with time. There is not but cheer and joy on my mind to remark upon such procession, 'ignoring the possibility for transfering pisitions'. The roads roam around, and offer various angles to see light and scenery. Little routes for walking, strolls too. All marbled by the evening sun, 'who showers our basic delights through the top to winter's morn.' The good will not fade
So, the coming of the tides, is one of the hardest things to deal with. I realize, that what I want in life are those relationships which occur from good health; and such health as would be measured by some sense of constituion; whereby I sit in leisure and rest gently, and thereby proceed to the outdoors in plenty.
 But the sun always sets, nor is it easy to see what it offers when it rises. I compared the solution of actual figures, whereby learning more about them allows for individual pieces to be seen amongst the harmony of parts. Prior to learning their is no such harmony, merely a soul piece. Of Mountains and clouds though, or the gradient of the sky, seeing these so adjointedly can be overhwleming, and not be so pleasing, nor satisfying; for you really see not. Knoweldge brings a sight to the various elements to such object, whereby it appears a compoisition. The peak of a mountain then is not merely the tip of a single shape, but a part of a complex of shapes, who in combination create the mountain; while you may gander at that one top part.
 Equally, time may be seen this way. Practically, we rise, and stretch firmly followed by a walk, or walk only, and stretch and or walk later on. We eat a slice of tomato with kale and sunflowers, then fruits, then kale, cucumber and nuts, then smoothie; or cooked veggies instead of these past two with grains following. It seems fair that you'd go for a walk sometime after dinner, and leave that hearty chunk of evening for yourself; and then pitter away prior to bed. This slice of the day then, upon dinner, is nicely placed as a time which sits between the evening and the downs of lunch. Prior to those, we generally go for a midday walk, likely before or right after a smoothie; usually when we've done our work and wish to merely eat and rest. Coming out of this we usually go for a walk, as if you get up after the slow part of the day. But a fairwell walk so to speak is had sometime around this; it may be pushed to do so, but it is seen as that final exchange prior to bed. The effect is that the niceities of the day can be enjoyed without any sense that your missing something. It is often that you may go for your late afternoon walk to see the sun, or even as it begins to set, 'and you bathe in its vibrance'; and then knowingly see that the deep blue is perfect for your final walk. And you need not fret then, for the stroll you go on prior to dinner need only be as would provide pleasure now: as something later on, 'in those precious final times', is ready and willing.
So I am feeling the effects of not training, particularly in work; and I do notice it also with respect to gaming. It seems our grip is anemic, and we come in and out of rigidty much. Also, our actual momentum, in terms of natural motions which sprout from firmer ones, is falling. We were doing well with our morning lunges and frequent chaturanga, but recently this has stopped. I feel plush and prone to heating up, as are we lacking vitality in our bones --where we get cold easy. I find myself tried, though sleeping well.
 My need is to do some mixture of high knees, pushups and squats and running. Something quick, but surely all three. A more homely blend can be had in between; and much of our cares center on the surrounding stretching that goes on --and all our working benefits.
So the analogy for the mind is that of a wood set for a blaze; and thus learning is a like a match which ignites such timber. The result is original thinking and warm views of things, 'as you create them, even of the trickiest or harshest subjects'; as opposed to them being instilled in you, or accepted out of reverence, awe, or incomprehensibility. These bolt onto you and act more like guide rails in which other thoughts can move. "Wherever you go, it must be in sway with the way things are." But these personal thoughts are created in a way that is in commission with the rest of your thoughts; and so remarks about nature and justice are not restrictions but rather other parts of a complex, in which other thoughts can work with, or at least move around.
 I say this not so much for the sake of speaking of the mind, but that, of such a topic, how considering it from an analogous view, aids in showing us her concived yet undefined features, or nature at least. As such, the analgoy for nature, or the All, is that of an abyss, the deep, whereby through demonstration and or belief we concieve of their being some defined floor far below; or we may perhaps not feel certain of where such depths go, yet still remain optimsitic of our ability to delve deeper, 'and of our current knoweldge, or level so far reached, grow'.
 Perhaps this analogy with not be needed if we coud perfectly describe things, and then there would merely be a geography which we could accurately detail. The issue though is in that of identity, and how without certain maxims, it is ever so hard define things to their core. Moreover, if we avoid such maxims, or first principles rather, and lean on descriptions to convey everything, we realize that everything appears far too distinct and granular as not to be ordered and ordained without some guiding factor. "For why, in a vastless world of bits, would they move at all and thus compose."
 The analogy for nature then has both the structure of knowledge and how we may see it, but also the method for knoweldge, and how we may find it. For the analgy that best fits to this notion of depths, is that of composition, or ostensibility. For we may believe that deep down their lies certain rules or indiscrimintable facts; yet, we remain neccesarily endowed to the formations of things of those currents at the surface. Indeed, one of the cruxes of inquiry is that, of such maxims below, that they are in some sense unneccassry so far as the things they wish to prove, or at least serve as the basis for, are unknown; for a cause they may be, but to an effect which is, in point, not defined, 'but for something outside itself'. And thus, if such things were known in and of themselves, then maxims would not be needed.
 Physically, there is a similar crux, 'or whale so to speak, moving in it's own way, but within the ocean', where the composition of things may in fact be but an orchestra of finer motions, and that, of our described and or innate views, that they be of such things. Indeed, the luster of our minds and novelty of lively being fair a somewhat exlusive membership in relation to such orchestra. "As if the sounds here don't apply." This does not sit well though. For in one, you have the concrete fact, or insight at least, of earths age and that of bacteria; and how, all grounds for lives creation are centered on the profusion of that age, and not our own. "It is un-anthropoligical nor geological to ask how man, or mammals even came to be, any more than it is right to ask how the current continets formed, when in fact the truth lie in the more or less orginial motions of the earth's formation, of which the current distribution is but a continuation of that." In this sense, we know that the debirs from the suns formation clumped together to form the earth, and that through this clumping, 'which in effect erodes the surface of earth; everything is pumbled to the bottom, leaving a flat top', that so did this continue into plates above. We can see then that this point of clumping debris and that of plates forming (and then moving thorugh tectonics) holds no clarity (by our ignornace mind you) of how such procress formed the actual plates. Equally, of the earth forming, holding certain chemcials and geological forces around them, that so there lies an equal mystery as to how this proceeded in that which, upon further procession, lead to bacteria. That era though, whereby the origins of that which developed into the last points leading into the bacterria we know, is not at all unclear as a time where the mystery of life procurred.
 Similarly, of the mind, you have the certain fact of our senses, and how more or less mechanically they function. "Descartes optics and adjoining theory of vision hold no mention here." The prevailing view of conscience then, or this experience of feelnig seperate and or aware of your senses, is what is adimittedly obscure. But I would say the problem is not so diffuse, but in fact quite clear under a qualified lense. Simply, if you imagined two lines that intersected as to be perpendicular at their joining bases, is it possible that the space between their farthest ends would not be that of the square of each line added to one another? If you imagine red, or hear a tune, or even consider motion, do these things not obey the very mechanics which occur in real life? Indeed, our vision is not so real as we perceive, and it is really a descriptvie ignornace of how light functions, as to see what red actually is. We see it as this appleish color, that is quite different than the sky's blue; yet these are in effect the bending of light as to produce a bluish oor not respond. Indeed, as the pre bacteria era, so there is the pre color one as regards our descriptivie development. But the point, is that however the function of us seeing red in our mind, or being aware of this or that or posturing images or thoughts deep inside, that these are not in fact real in any different way as would be the case outside of our mind.
 And so, if we remove the rather small curisoity of life, give way to the truth in our senses, and have faith in the removal of ignorance as regards our mind, 'and hold no unqualified, or rather mistaken, or gun jummping views as regards awareness', then all is in fact comparable, 'the orchestrat of things'; and that rocks, water, our minds, bone and the stars, are but weavings of the true fabric, 'whos origins and mystery need, and surely must, be assayed under the inquiry which is suiting for that'. The gravest mistake man can make, is in using the methods which give insight to his surface level experience on that which lies below. The key example being that of time and continuity, and how the things around us appear and behave in a certain way; and we mistake the depths below as needing to be so annured as to cause that. But we have it backwards in this case; that such depths need only abide their own rules. Indeed, our understanding of things is not complete, merely progressed; and we know only that all is made of the same bits, and that such bits mark our current line of digging. There may be more, but that is not the point. It is that of such bits, what we know of them is how our methods for inquiry should be concieved; and that, accordingly, our higher views of continuity ought merely be melted off, as to give way to the finer happenings below, and how this in truth relates to how our percieved compositions are behaving. "For we don't see the stone behave as would be the case for its finer parts. But in learning about such parts, we can better see how it does in fact so." And, considering that our mind is the medium, or appartus which concieves this, we can then parlay the fact that of our thoughts, 'of the rock so to speak', that the composition of its parts is what we percieve; and by that, the qualities of such parts are in fact within such conception; the point being that our mind, as part of the system, composes and responds in a way that is comparable to the things it sees, and thus shares quality with.
So, I have put this off long enough, but daylight savings is coming, and I think it an oppertune moment to a) formalize the motions of the sun --considering how unchanging they ar-- and b) the ups and downs of the moon, for these are in most ways a mystery, and with potential to offer great delights through the year.
 I also wish to speak of the clouds; for our lack of wording brings much collapse to our grandest awe. Similarly, color, such as that of twilight gradient, ought be known, in both a) the locations of the gradient, and b) their actual color and hues. "There's this greyish purple that comes on vibrant nights, and I want to recognize this further."
In regards to health, it seems there is a solid foundation of kale, sunflower seeds or peanuts, tomatoes or cucumber. These come quite easy and are easy to bring home from work. We have much fruit, trail mix or straight carnberries and our smoothie. The mid day is smooth and effective. I won't say our grain game is off, but I do see that were not fitting oru choices to the motions of our day; and end up worrying about what we should have. There is seldom not a balance of heavier and lighter grains. And based on what you have, it seems to best to look at your day, what is coming, the fact of the vibrant light and later set, and how between there and following into the leading stretch to bed, that there is room for eating, generally what we please. Our stores are low though, in Barley, lentils, beans and soon quinoa. Bread and popcorn should run adjacent to such things; and our balance of things, as motivated in size by work coming, ought merely do so in terms of timing, as your day goes.
It seems that our life is devoid of chocolate. If only we could have some; I'd feel great rejoice.
 The age has been a weird one though; as it began with the concession to eat such delights, all while partaking, and being somewhat content, with the authority which bade me not to eat it at all.
 In that lies the deeds of duty; the need for independance; at least the respect for it to come, and that which lies in our otherwise time in dependance.
 I find that quality is an overlooked sentiment; and that when I think of the sun, or a way we play a game, or these words coming out, that there is a quality which I delight in. But it is another thing to recognize the deeds around, and how these are hampered by choices of worse quality. Indeed, we struggled with this an age ago; when we consorted to only oblige such deeds and indulge ourselves otherwise. Perhaps we just needed some more friends. But such friends only come when relating to something. And I could not spend enough time as to let years pass (and thus accomodate) in being so indulgent.
 It may be so that we take things to an extreme. And it is also worth noting that our time in becoming so extreme was so that our fortune of friends was gone. And so we reverted to something else. The point of not being so extreme, is that if you had more of what came over time through a modest stried, that so may this be fair in both stability and pleasure. It is perhapds just one facet that our development lead us away from this modest possibility.
 I don't feel it unimportant to note that much of our friendship developed around some committment, or at least adherence to duty. We did not live lavishly or recklessly, 'or at least in disregard or dissdain', for being responsible, kind and polite. Yet, these were just manners of life; and ahead of this sat the objects of schooling and ambition. "At least the tradition and or path etched in with the the prospects of a career. Making some of yourself as it were."
 Let us say that our avoidance of the extreme lack of duty, and at least that which was devoid of decency and propiety, and our abscence of ambition or reverence for higher things, lead me to place which held oppertunity and friendship for one as such in the middle. And there was indeed not. And as we said, it is one facet considering our developments from this point; as opposed to one which came about more moderatley. It is another when considering what we actually found down such a path; and how the value, albeit perhapds beyond modesty and by that not essential to one so aligned; but so, in our extreme, we found and in some ways sought insights and ability according to that. Life became known in that way.
 During our peak of this, when we felt somewhat absent to things and thus indulged ourselves as our stability allowed, that so upon this feeling off, we turned to ourselves. Something good and evident came from being so aware and tending. But, while the years prior held their efforts in the mean, our maturing of this left us somewhat indolent at the innatness to such ways; and how our formative efforts drew no more huff or deliberation; and we only received what came from it. In two ways, we a) futhered our efforts in regards to the ideas of ourself, but also b) furthered our view as to what was good for us, and worth accepting in terms of the good we experienced.
 I think, as the story goes, we wished to remove all that was not in harmony with this path; but the ultiamte wish was in living outside the duties which felt a weight upon such experience. We could not reconcile this though. And eventually, we were lead to a position which was stable, or sufficient rather, and our console was in the acceptance of the good in such expeirence; and how it was needed but also worthy in what it provided. It sought not to remove itself from the deed; nor did it find itself lacking in what it wanted. We could be self-regarded, creative, leisruely, and foremost, amongst this, sufficient. But we felt that this was not laudible or of orthodox to accept as a mean, considering what it gave.
 Oddly enough, I find myself in this state where the method for stability is so fine and calmy accepted, that our care for the goods it once provided are faded. Indeed, they come when we are good; but I don't even feel compelled to uphold them, considering how fine our cirucmstances are. And indeed, such content is not so precious, as our eveness is quite befitting for one who was bearing provision on their own.
 Where my thoughts lie, is that we are not happy. And that is so, primarily, because the burden of responsibility is so, that depsite it not being so bad, 'and not worthy of yearning for somewhere else', that it may not faciliate, or run without friction agasint your freerer interests. Without such interests, I am not so happy. And despite our scenario being well, the good I seek is in philosophy, nature, limber and peacefull recreation, gaming and worlds afar. "These words to, or those from reading, flowing fluidly in and through my brain." I know that we can have chocoalte. If we did, we would not be thrown off, for our scenario would stay. It is the fact that doing so drains our ability to experience and develop those dearer goods; and that missing this, is like being blind to a prior friendship.
Let's admitt the fact that we are uninterested in stating why things are well, as to better situate ourselves amongst our discomfort.
 We can speak to that forwardly, rather than soften our surroundings as to accomodate its weight. "Let us ligthen it."
 My train of thought is not too clear, as to what the issue is. I can remark about it endlessly, in terms of what it's about, but not so much in any way as would reflect us having an idea about, as opposed to some affirmation of what we already know.
 What it come's down to, if I may be so bold, is the pressures of knowledge, and how differing views create a sense that differing actions will occur. Much of these pressures are brought into our unversed or tenured minds; and in a form that is overly concentrated, and results in us turning our brains off, rather than contemplating and or assaying what's recieved.
 Where my issue lies is in the struggle to accept, that akin to one's eating habits, 'and the common notion that many eat poorly and make themselves ill', that so is one's diet of information and assassment similarly their own to either benefit frmo or bear fault. The issue, and I will say that this describeds the heart of our feeling ill, is that in this latter case, it is not so much our own deference or sympathy to one being so ignorant and dumb, and the oppertunity they miss, or angst even that spreads upon them; but rather, the inert response by them, which I in our own standing presume, as being more or less idealistic and to a confrontational end. So many dinner party conversations as it were, come down to the hodge podge of dissagreements people have; be it about clear cut topics, or about what someone says. But when my view of this expands across the world, there is this experienced of being stretched, and plausible, if not likely [for which our own weakness and bias may fill in, as to impose what may be so] motions bear greater weight than our nearer views and or living would recognize. That is, 'the stretched view of the world leaves blank areas which let smaller inklings or doubts hold greatrer weight; as if a small weight were placed amidst the center of an enlongated (and suspended) string; or rather, if someone hits the root of your wrist comapred to the center of your forearm, the pressure is less. "And so, in being so stretched, as if fibres were expanded far and about, so do small bits of friction and infancy make themselves further known, than had they been about the tighter and well formed parts of us --which, naturally, are from what know, and thus what is most common and near to us." Our lives, so to speak, are right in front of us; and in some sense, amount to such frictions and oddities when were off. But it is overly gleaming views born for the sake of want or the constricted abbrasions as born upon adolescence and impurity, that leave those scars and tatterings; for which our less than composed sensibilities, or state rather, 'as may otherwise be thrown off, by something to see or hear', that so does our statue of being so stretched get caught on and disspraportionaly weighed down by, those hooks and granules of lust and doubt.
 This is an all to winded way of saying that of our sight and or sense of others being elated and bethrothed to the happenings of the world, that so this mechanism of, we'll say, our own character creating cracks for poor ideas about this to take root, and the adjoining factor of snagging, or circumstantial inflamming of these cracks getting hooked upon our own elated states, .....
 The question, is in our abscence of direction, and how this allows for our naievties to take solid form. But said direction rests upon it's own mystery. For the reality is that we are endowed to the ways of nature and as much as we literally feel compelled, ordained, organized and of want for the sake of our material needs. "For which all effort is deemed credible, or not if such ends are not the case." The point, is that such a course brings with it wishes and appeptite born of such condition; and that as one who is cold seeks warmth, or food if their hungry, that so does someone who is employed in labor, or a professsion at that, or the basic road of man, that so does this create a slew of wants and whims. And my querry into this sits upon the issue of validating such whims, and whether they hold any right when their cause is considered.
 It would not do me ill to remark upon our history, and how much our present is born from it. In a word, the resolve to whims, or acceptence at least of the way things were when we acquited ourselves to the seeming neccessities around us, that so of the fears and want that came about, so did we form our anticipations around this; seemingly on the cemented faith in the foundation of our life. "That if our wants were so, and our fears as such, then so would we attend to them, in as much as their sprouting from our way of living was valid; and such validity seemed so as much as our response was to something seemingly neccessary, and thus of unquestionable worth in what we orient ourselves around." Our life was mannered in this way, and many ideas and cares came about. Namely, our foresight of fame and contibution, and perhaps humour amidst the social arts. In tandum with this was the fear of these things not being so, and how the thought of them not made us feel like we were invalid to our parents, and worthy, or at least unlikely to recieve, affection from either women or friends. This mourale so to speak moved with us from the day we took account for ourselves; and in most ways did not cease till our later time at Kamloops.
 We are now in a moment of uncertainty though, and in all fairness, the dynamic at play is overly complex. There is the fact that we may leave, as I feel motivated to by our parents irreverence to discretion regarding the news. Our experience with this has become softer over the months, and despite seeing ease and worth of a transition, I hold no aversion to our current place, and am content to let the good on offer provide. But as of late, I've felt our world views become burdened with the allowance of any merrit to media; who, in the pass, I angstly pelted for being thin and bare. But, of late, we found worth and ease, as history has shown, by learning more myself. Now, I have learned so much, but find the divide between our family forever wide. Every bit of reason and belief I have beckons the futility and parchedness of various parts of the news. One resolve is to simply learn more, and see the surer and broader foundations of law and governance, and by that, earn our peace from clearer knowledge. This is where this idea of every person's knowledge account is there own, and I merely tend to mine as I see fit. "Indeed, the more I know, the less I care what other's do." But when I don't know something, which I own stale state may not be able to balance with, that so does our state of uncertainty flounder under the so sure pronouncements of others; or, their disregard for anything mattering at all.
 I wish not to described what we did earlier, as this cause of us being poor, and the effect of impressions leaving me ajar. I simply wish to state, upon which most great things are made by, to the truth at hand, as pertains to ourselves, our wants, reason and ways.
 Perhaps, it is a worthwhile point as to our movement in live if we are without care of how well people's knowledge is. I don't care if they eat well, why should I be bogged down by their ignorane. For one, it is this more or less unsolid but neverthless inflammatory view of them acting abhorently due to their quality of views. This indeed comes from us seeing what other people watch, be it CBC, question periods, random statements from trials, bits from speeches, monologues and or claims from pundants and speakers; that our own knowledge, or at least grip on what is not in question, 'for one's view that Trump is bad doesn't erase the function and from of the Union, for which I am not begrieved'; but as much as I can't speak to that, or know rather, than the associated, and in some sense, literal or at least meta-inference that it is erased (because of such view), leads me to the whithering reaction of horror if that is so, but also blatant rebuke, indifference and destitude to the quality and or strenght of such claim. Indeed, one kind of example comes to mind, that in a court case, there are detaileld and drawn out discernments over facts and deliberation; but there is also opening comments, or those similar but sifted amidst the rest of the lengthy process. Referencing such comments without the discretion, or at least sense, that they are so and of such pillarying, deafening, personality driven and or pointed way, is truly misinforming; as the remarks about such comments, without such understanding, makes for inferences and claims that fail to be stated upon sources and or subjects that are decently portrayed. I can't describe this well, or mention it's proper name, but in queestion periods, people just say things of an oppositional sort. They demonstrate nothing, but make a mixture of ad-hominen critiques and those of a causal sort --that so and so did this. There is no real deliberation though, as nothing of evidence is presented or displayed, nor any real investagtion of that. It's really an odd thing. And I am at the moment ignorant of the formal function of such thing, and it's name even; but, of the worth, or substance at hand, that of real inter-personal relations between MPs --or those in any upper and lower house-- that so do such animated and or pointed exchanges feel, by my judgement, unbecoming of such ways; but also, in terms of truth, unrelated, or at least explicitly unequal to such 'in house relations and correspondance; for which all action is cultivated'.
 And so I rest a little, knowing enough to find peace in the real happenings of governeance and the world. There is opposition, and odd and or wicked things that occure. But blemishes and or injuries these are, of a truly unified, if not functioning body. And refering only to the blathering, if not irrate speaking of those questioning periods, is like ascribing the heart and matter of a (court) hearing to it's opening/closing rather than (lenghty) deliberative remarks. "Indeed, my point is perhaps that, of one's knoweldge, if it lacks what comes from such deliberations, than I think you know not. And of commentators and reports, if they speak little of this, then their utility seems small."
So I need some aid, by our word and reason.
 As it were, Bella has become an affectioned part of my mind, and the thought of her being interested in someone else, or forgetting me at that, has me in a small bit of dissaray.
 First, I do like Bella, but not with all my heart. I compare our care for her to that of Mikala, or Amon even, where I feel more possibility of connection and thereby interest, rather than any formidable longing. She is no Simron, nor Holly.
 Speaking of her actually settles me, as we are clear on our intent. The tricky part, is that our modest care for her may be elated by parts of our impulsive mind. Watching porn has done this; as has, per our natural condition to like others,been a little parched and or uncomposed, due to our history with folk. Were also tired, and this makes for more or less urgres, and complimentary sparks of grief. I see her talking with the curly hair guy --he is Brandon, Angel is the other guy. Then I see her talking with Anthony or Alex, and a fair but of other people --like (the girl whose name I know but is on the tip of my tonge) and Skyler. There is something about this which bothers me. Seemingly, our talks feel less unique and special; and although I appreciate the candor we have, considering that we felt awkward for some time, I can't help but read into this new repor of ours, or at least discussion, and attribute some sort of affection to this. There have been moments like this in the recent year, and generally in this latter part of life; but I am not genuinely fooled. But there are feelings that sit a bit squirmily and with despondance. As if there were small lips of excitement, at some prospect, or by the mere affection that our conversation brought me. But then to see her talk to everyone else, it is as if it really didn't matter. Our lack of clarity then is in whether this is alright, and if we should chase her, as to overcome this sense that were on the outside of her interest. I don't mean to do this; nor protrude, as I did with Holly and Adara.
One of the things which comes to mind is in the assumption of elongated affection, and how being with people over an extended period of time, brings you together despite your lack of direct 'chasing' or connection. This occured with Jasmine; but I note that our feeling towards her was perhaps, if not in part, mixed with the motion of our life, and how it would be. For I remember her being quite social, and having lots of friends over and what not. She had not the reason or strenght of me, a man, to forgo this. "She was most able and leader like; but with no value or exhertion that wasn't like anyone else. She didn't use her strenght to make her own life. Perhaps as a adventerous boy, who held no reverence or accpetence of imposition, to see life as I thought it." The point being that I don't know if we ever would have worked together. Indeed, I have mentioned that our choice to be friends with Nick, Austin and Kaelen, over say Gresham and those folk, was a result of us being split from our prior school, and our more strong and determined view, or posture at least, had less that was (over time) around it, and so made it easier to go where I thought right.
