Indeed, we did not go to Pitt for this reason, as our ablity to go elsewhere had more room to take hold.
 I guess, in part, what I'm wondering is that our so called developed affection with Jasmine, or even that of Pam, were so that we simply did not want to embrace them; for we were not simply being with them, as I all too well could, but that we were embracing what they were apart of as well. My only interest was to spend time with them, and then be on our own, 'or do as we please so to speak'. But this felt strange, as we were seemingly outside what they either like (also), or who wondered why I wasn't with them. "You like her, why not us." The same thing happened with Colin and the boys.
 I think it's hard to forget that this average attempt to be in the world, be it with women or for careers, did not go over well. And that avoiding these things when we were privalged to, did not create an otherwise abundance of joy. And so I am in a similar spot, where I have this care for Bella, or wish to hold onto the possibility that she will one day have affection for me --by simply being strong and kind, funny and wonderful. Truly myself. But will I become part of her life
Theres just something about girls, cute little things. It's not all of them though. It's more oftehn than not someone who is social, or at least has some prominence. And this is appealling. But I fretter over the fact that a) we don't really know said person, b) feel we shouldn't, consdiering how likely we are to relate, and beyond that, would ever considering our particualr ends. There's just nothing I would rather do less than be out with, say Bella, and her friends. this is so for a reason I don;'t entirely know. It binds itself to that reason that, despite Jasmine's care, we'd only be with her, no one else. "Or down a road I can't fall back on." This care, or strut so to speak, in which youth culmintated into apathetic meandering, to the forward delights of 'our otherwise self'.
 One thing that has come to light over the recent years is this sense that if we are ourself, normal yet weird, that so doesn't this interfer with connecting with tohers. In some sense it rests on the seeming truth that connection is more about deeper intimacy and trust, and feelings according to that. And that such thing comes from being open about yourself. It is hard to do so, especially with enthusiasm, considering that you may not relate in what you have to share. I have always felt electric being this way, and presumed we were attractive. It takes time though, and more meaningfull forthcoming; which, to be fair, comes more when simpler personal matters come about. We don't tell Bella that we like Planet Earth in order to attract her, but because we feel safe enough and good enough to do so; and this comes from general knowledge, which more or less comes over time, as things come up, and the prior repor acts as kindling for this momentary match.
I thought today about knowledge, and how I had some in relation to our learnings about constitutions. I thought about other parts of life, and how they could be similarly known.
 This following could speak to so much, but also little, in that upon this realization, what I knew was that any motion in life, purpose or cause, is brought through a sense of ability, and that be it unaptitude or langor, that the according wish and or affirmation of an aim merely remark that contrast to one who is able, and naturally sees and goes to their able'd end. It is like money; if I can reach it, good, and I will considering the worth. But normally I cannot, and it is merely wisdom that points me in the right direction, or speaks of its worht, for which I must conjure and prpeare myself, 'as if to make some blind, yet best put attempt'.
I think that I'd describe our time, and life ahead, as privy to responsability.
 What this means is that we are to proceed and accept the relation to deeds, and thereby a scale for good and bad which is commiserate, and to an extent drawn from this.
 It is strange, that public service is that in which someone who has enough excess, or stability at least, in their personal life, to afford effort for others. A family, in some sense, is similar, in that one, in their personal tides, affords time and care for others --though close to them when compared to society.
 It has been an ethic of mine, or concern, that in order to experience the fertility of leisure, craft and care, that one must be enriched with peace, calm and content. Wisdom hath brought me down such path, and guided my actions as to form myself in relation to that which sentences us.
 The basic note is that one who must achieve triumphs of the greatest sort, or even the futhest of everyday chores, lies in a current that requires commission of an equal sort. "While those who are tasked with crossing the road, bear little weight on their back." Indeed, first principles of the bark and crown sort, magistrate our right, which is to say fair, and by that lauded reason, to form ourselves as we do. In a literal sense, "and please, petrify me if I digress," if an aim is just, then only some acts are permissiable, 'as we may not kill another to reach the esteemed end of prosperity'; in equal manner, only some acts are deemed worthy, in that they are effective --barring their permissive qualities.
 What I'd note, and again, 'pummel me if I digress', but in these affairs, which is to say the writing and or inquiry one partakes in order to learn first principles and worthwhile acts, beckons us to write and or inquire as such in a distinctive way. For if we are looking for our foundations, whereby our true ends and thereby gauge by which things are measured agasint is known, then we must bind ourselves to philosophy; for only she may aid us, and in some sense properly promulgate us, to search for a truth that is not yet known. Many paradoxes come to pass, 'less we take certain things for granted'; and indeed, only the act of inquiry, 'which is best described as an act of consideration, and of factors which philosophy holds domain to', can afford us any scent and or taste of the truth, nor any progress and solidity in the ends we find. In tandum with this, but nonetheless seperate, is inspection, or assessment no less, whereby the nature of an end, 'for which some activity is usually placed', is identified in and of itself, and then weighed against the ends we deem just, in order to size the fit. This kind of inquiry is not contemplative, nor much of what we truly mean when we say inquiry; for we need not evaluate the basis for meaning, but merely effectiveness of a subject. In any given case, our goal is to best describe an object and its relevant worth as it pertains to our ends.
 I will say that as sound as these methods are, 'of essential inquiry and that of objects', that so do these somewhat precede any real statement of truth. For in those, the progress is already done, and we need not mention the peculiar aspects that each method brings, 'and bears contraiety to otherwise fair results within each approach'. The example I'd use is that different religions, 'which are based on essential beliefs,' each have their own according amends. Only, as we investigate objects around us and how they relate to such end, we may find that such end is invalid, 'or shaky at best'. I'd point to the comparability of most phenomena --including the rather un-modern skepticism of the physical nature of experience-- and how all of it rests on rather mysterious foundations, 'or hazy ones at that, in regards to concrete demosntration'; and as such, how essential arguments of the demonstrative sort fair no acceptable conclusion so much as this is true; for a) such arguments rest on premisses that derive themselves from cosmological and or cognative elements, 'of which we are unclear about, and equally then of such premisses', or b) that of such demonstrations, that our progress is so as to lack any school of thought no the nature of demonstartion, and how such statements hold any bearing in reality.
 At any rate, there sits beside me a history of thinking that concludes that their sits some esscence or being outside of me. Not in the way that my backyard is outside my house, but rather the air amidst a weeving of web and fabric; whereby those form the linens of compesition: yet some ether flows in and sifts about. Ignoring the phenomanl domain, it seems clear that all there is to reckon is that which ebbs and goes from such webbing; and that there are but mere arguments as to the wind around. Indeed, the will to live in this fabric world is one that invokes one to the plane of existence. Indeed, when I loose my job or show up late to the next one, each provides an experience of loss and angst, 'for which the civil essentials of money, food and home bolster'; and as such, the remedy or at least avoidance of this creates the grounds for rest and comfort. Indeed, the truth or at least inquired basis of things rests little on the sentiments of 'existence is one thing, the winds around another'. But as folk, I believe, as perhaps dictated by our experience, that our doubts and or being persuaded otherwise is but a mistaken and or missued motion of our otherwise objective ways. In some sense, I happily ignore the claims of others when our objective conditions are dealt with. If I am asked, or consider in priavte, the farther nature of things, my response, or steadyness at least (when in private), leans on our less than strong feelings, 'for as far as we are concerned, our heart and home is fine'. Of postured fears or advice for hell to come, so my objective understanding is light yet resistant. As for governed threats, 'for which we are deemed to lack piety', so may I put my richest understanding's to the test, 'and enter deat with a lust that equals our peace upon a night's sleep: one seeming like an eternal mode of such temporary rest'. That I feel this way, history does not fully tell; for an age ago, perhaps our sense would be different. "Perhaps our orginal views would have brought us in line with Protestantism." But I all merily and truly thank my fortuen, for living in a day higher than the past; where I am privy to an understanding that has come mechancially abound; and even more, that however far or not it's come, 'so has the polis bade me liberty to believe or impeach it'.
 What this comes down to, is that if someone were to ask for your admission to a crime, they'd be most willing to accept it. "Apparently my ability to be honest about wrong doings is lauded higher than my reliability to admitt right ones." No matter, I feel that as honest as I may be about that, and how willing we are to accept it, that so by any God or being who would surround me and bear me oblige in this or the life ahead, that I consider my honesty to be in commission with him, and that he is satisifed of me doing so; but that, as such, as honest as I am about that, that so may I be honest about other things. And as we bank ourselves to the facts of our admission to error, that so of other things may we equally bend to. If someone says that I may not do this, then I resort to the frank truth of admitting a wrong, and how as acceptable this is, 'and in harmony with, or at least without contratiety to any view or philosophy', that so my honesty feels laudible elsewhere.
 For I am left to only admitt my bearings around, and what calls on me to oblige. That is in esscence what I'm trying to say; that of the truth, if some error be committed, that the right of this excedes me in measure, and thus my word is beckoned to it. Yet, equally is this so to my employeers, to my common man or family member, who I ought be cordial with and civil. Equally, my body aches after drinking, or clambers upon sugar and grundge. Watching television has this narrowing effect, as does commentary and or pedantry cling to my normal sense, 'like some woodland weed, that floats in the air and sticks to your clothing.' Specific things flutter about here, and I attend to them in no seperate manner than I would when lacking air. "I care not, nor find reason for one to deny me gasping for breathe. And as one so imbued with humanity, so am I merely grieved by others doubts or perdition that any such person, 'who wasn't insane or of vitrolic sort', would deny me such, or insist my actions be such."
Some good things come to mind when I think of what is oppertune and good. Far away, lies this irreverent interest to email professors about Sums. Closer is our dealings and treatment of Liberty, and the more recent 'by me'. Our room could also use a clean, or at least organization with winter clothes. Our finances too are in need of furnishing. We went through this a week or so ago, but we've derailed. We need to email mom, return our controller, and confirm our christmas presents and computer aprts.
So we have three choices: talk with our parents about expectations, heed their wish, or move out. I think, in such a moment as this, it pays to remark what each choice actually means; for we may be apprehnesive, or dissapointed, about certain ends; but this is not to do with what your choice entails. May we find strenght and ease in what we do.
 Moving out holds the most ambiguity. For one, the choice is to either stay at our job or transfer to another location. I feel as though the former is of interest. That being so, we only need to find a room, and thus afford the bill. Stock varies, but within Pitt Meadows and Maple Ridge there is a 700$ rent, or less even. Worth noting, is that your job is fixed, while your stay is variable, as you may find a better place. Whether that be for money's sake or the places quality. These numbers will balance out; as we have some 7000$ in our bank, and the continuation of our work. On that note, you may change your availability, keep the 15 hours worth of shifts you already get, and most likely, just grab more as you please or need. It is worthwhile to note, on the ends of our time and living, that having a shift scheduled only bears upon our ability to have such a schedule. Our life is not surrounded by nor privy to too much fun, and so we please ourselves through ideas and activity. This can feel a bit unfitting while living at home; and indeed, the thought of a new place may not afford such ability; but the circumstance is such that your no longer at home, and need only acquiesce with the roomates or landlord. Sound is the greatest factor then; but not their judgement. As we said, when moving to Vancouver, that is finally ours. And the great differene, is that we had no sustainability, and only found such right before we left for Kamloops. Were just switching houses, not johs, and the good and reason for the increase in work is more than amendable by me, and accessible by our current load -of which we more often then not give up-- and the lush ability to pick up.
 In terms of what this entails, indeed, it centers on your ability to get a room. These are available through marketplace. And, there is a general principle that your peace upon looking for so is such, that as long as you have a place now, that you may wait to find one, and thus not fetter. I am fairly convinced that our parents care not of our work load if we are looking for a place. As such, you need only look on marketplace or craiglist, and find the room thats fair and available. Once then, you sign, formalize, and set a date. Indeed, our biggest transition will be the switching of our shift prep; as moving in on say Monday and having to work Tuesday, means only that you need to orient your preperations through the new surrounding. That first sleep though brings it all in, and all is good. Just as you ought learn someones name, and afford proper contributions to neccessary things, so may you inquire as to their aims and values, while sharing your own; and mentioning the oddities of the floor and light sensitivity. At that point, what matters most, 'seeing as your good living is established', is that you meed your pecuniary ends. Every month you'll have to pay rent; and indeed we have a damage deposit --which acts as a sort of good willed asset, that you hold onto for subsquent depositios. We also have to pay for food, which may go over or less; but so will we get tax refunds, gifts, family allowance and gift; as do we have our own money, and ability to slightly adjust ahead, as well as pick up a shift or two. "God please bear us the devils merry slight, by giving us two days on, one off, two more, then two off, for some short roation of weeks."
 At that point, all I have is the ownest to responsability, the doubts which make converse challenging, 'and full of fear and fright as we heed 'I'll think about it', for which we fear that we don't know'. But so is it true, that for Sums and relationships, or some novel transfer to lands far, that the question comes in ways that are oppertune only for, and made relevant by our lack of current stead. "Time is needed for progress and insight to make; and so we need only presently oblige: for which motion will come."
OIddly, this moment of distress, comes in part because of a stimulus, but is recieved and emboldened by our natural condition.
 You see, I have no heading, of which this potential change of course may bring itself around. Rather, we are merely stable, and concentrating to keep it so. Better and or oppertune choices come from this; but so are we not on the flipside of things, where we think to be rooted in duty is a wrong thing, and that we should leave. Perhaps by heridity or ambition one may leave such chains. And despite wisdom saying we merely change one kind for another, I merely remark that in making our burdens more deed like, that our condition becomes less than that which prompts us to begrieve it; and sure, we bear a weight and the according finesse, but were stable.
 I don't lack the inquiry, or oppertunity at least to think. But my condition is such that of our point of progress, 'or place that it's made', that so does this lie in a tumble desirable and rocky properties. Foremost is the small amount of work we have to do. But immediately I would reply with the sense, that although you may work less than you would if on your own, the difference is not much; and that when we think of the fact of having to provide for ourselves otherwise, the only beams of me (so to speak) that give way, are those of the more broad and reflective sort. "Our place and way to children, romance, friendship and success." It is not a matter of ability though; only that we'd flourish in our stability, 'upward, content but also with perpacity, as to bear potential and or oppertune remarks'. "To lack fear of trying."
Our dad asking me to work more, or at least come to terms with our current position, has me somewhat ruffled as to our state. For I felt rather set and swell. Indeed our health bears less promise, and our resolve as not accredited. But we are good.
 For some time, the nature of those efforts had me thinking about being somewhere that we could be more aligned with ourself. I feel this to be fair, and there is indeed a balance of natural coming to this, and the shared good which I see. But I more than that think of such thing when our time at home is off. And being told that such time is going to alter, brings my thoughts to that transfer now. Only it is out of need almost and so I feel nervers. The availability of rooms is not as much as it was before, and the current stock (while being low in number) has more options on the pricier side. I would remind you that all transfers come from a place you already are. There is the notion of force, in that you are evicted from your home or tenancy; this has never occured though, is not truly happening now, nor holds with it less access to amends, only a different need. "We are more willing to move far away if the scenario dictates. If we are at home or already in our own stead, then a transfer can come when it does, and so any momentary lack of stock, or fair prices, 'or reply from those desired', is held not over a burning flame --for that, we'd go where the preference of circumstance dictates, rather than our more stable wishes."
I am feeling angst. It is so, because the boat were on, however well set we are on it, is not stable itself. There is no real aim to smooth its edges (no communication), and the arbitray power of our mom and dad to ask us to do things is not assuring.
 We are not in panick though, or in a similar situation as we were when we left Pitt Meadows those years before. Anything would do then; and indeed, if that were our position now, we'd afford an early increase in cost, or extend our range of search. Were also at the end of the month, in November mind you.
 The reality is, that you may be asked to work more; and perhaps we will just do that. But so much as we don't, the task is not very pertinent, but merely a tell of deeds ahead.
 I submitt that our greatest fret comes from the complexion of our home, and our adjoining need to live and act as I see fit and neccessary. These don't always match; and I feel the weight ever so much when this is so. I care not to overlook our cramped sense, and choose futile things. But our action is quite unclear. "Not so in truth, but in the haze that blurrs this, and makes it seem a more sizeable tas; which we are no match for." At least, the point can be made, that our ultimate place in our current home is not one that is easy to fully lean into, and by that, bear reason towards such goods, 'and by that, earn much fervor and strenght by virtue of understanding'. It feels like were avoiding an alternative.
 It is important to note that our physical health is quite taken care of. Work is no less better. The latter has calmed itself into a great form, as we no longer have to wake up so earlier, and go to bed as so. Now, were more able to sleep as is well, and earn less conseuqnece for falling out of line. It is the winter, and our practice is not overly fresh, but we are adapting our diet around the suns early set. Indeed, we struggle for an era almost, by this pestering need to workout, 'and fill in our shoulders'. This comes in a just form when you train at all; and the feeling of solid chest and lower body is enough for me to feel confident and great. The actual physique is unimportant, and a distraction for the relations you have; and what you really ought deal with where there not. That being said, it is clear that our foundations are not privy to much leisure or whim in our practice; and as such, there ought be a sense as to whether were returning to a place where we can train, or if we merely need to push ourselves a little. Each are essential, for in one you are a shadow, and the other, if you miss, then you become the former. In being so essential, regarding the latter, 'where you have the means to train, but not rythm or momentum', you must simply abide this inherent fact, that to be in a state where motion comes easily, that you must bring yourself through motions, 'as to set the pitch'. The critical thing, is that when there is a moment for you to train, wherbey you won't afterwards, that the essential element is in fostering the energy for such motion and then accomodating it --as it is not likely to be overly fresh. For instance, we missed an essential moment a few weeks back, and are only coming off it's effects. Right now, we worked out on our last break, and are sorting through the stiffening of this. Now though, on this shorter break, we were to do some free range; minor even, but neccessarily. We have enough momentum from work, but needed a little formatting, as to bring ourselves in line. "Were to jumbled otherwise, and without enoguh lift to move gently through these coming days." But we have 4 day break next week, and surely must do some sort of training.
 I'll mention briefly, that our biggest hurdle is the fundementals of eating. For exercise brings life to us, but comes when it does; while eating has more strucuture, as it is so repattive. In that, being unsure about this clouds our foresight of comnig exercise. "That haze so to speak, blurrs all." What I see is that there is a moment in each day where heartier foods can be had; and from that point you accept the fuller portions of grain. Prior to have ruffage and shrub. This threshold varies though; as the sun sets near 4, and we wake up earlier, we find the hearty point leading into this. But if the sun sets around 7, your not going to have barely at 6 and then eat rice and bread at 8. It is not clear though that you should eat barley at 3, as you might now. Indeed, it matters more about the rest of your grain, and perhaps, in this later setting point, that you'd eat normal grain leading into that, and thus plan your hearty foods prior to that. What matters then is what hearty food are you having: broccoli, soy or barely. Moreover, that waking up early allows this.
I am thinking of the past and how it relates to now. Not to get sidetracked, but I am thinking of our time in Vancouver and how fair everything felt; in terms of the work-life balance.
 Now, I am thinking about our time last year and how it was spent on getting out there more, being busy. And I relate it to now, that what I seek is less friction and haze when it comes to our physical and dietary practice. Work is enough, as is our goods that surround our time through work. But we get burdened by poor acitivty, and struggle in amending this.
 Another thing is that of us ever wanting to live here at all. Indeed, we felt like leaving for the sake of finding that place where our efforts could be better prioritized. And indeed, this feeling I'm having right now was one I had during the fall; and on a lasting basis, 'as we were without work, yet with little means to move; and we had to do one or the other'. In part, Pitt Meadows breathed a sense of openeness in me, and I just thought of it so well, that the thought of staying here was an acceptable one. It is odd though, that upon getting hired and being steady, that I hated the news. In some sense, it intruded upon our ability to see our happiness, within this demanding yet stable condition. Were there now, in that were not able to see so much happiness in our position; and so the thought of working more feels like a pressure, rather than productive good.
 I don't have the time or care to add more good; as we are only feeling off because the nearest goods are off. These are naturally off in both our havnig to balance with them, 'which creates a fair sort of firmness', but also to fortify oursevles against them if we fail to succeed, 'as their lack creates shortcomings which we must fend off'. In some sense, being balance creates positive views, 'for we can rightfully see ahead'; while being worn creates defensive views, 'as to say why things will get better'. Oddly, we write with no soul or color when it comes to the latter, quite solemnly. When were stable though, we write about how good things are; yet, we source the subjects of good from our solemn times. This in a way cements them as being important, but we only have clearer views. But then, when we faulter, they have been given breadth, but lack any color. "And superficial impressions cave them in, like some hungry molecule that ravishly ingests a needed charge." It also reveals where we lack conclusions and inquiry, for which our parents feel like an eroding force. If we lack our own sense, then their own feels more prevalent; yet hold a structure atr least, on baser things, and we feel no construction to those, 'rather what our parents differently propose'.
One of the odd things about me is our view on violence.
 For whatever reason, the reality of it freightens me, per it's destructive nature.
 Why I can't forget about this is not so clear. But, that I deal with our grievence is moreso.
 Simply, I find that an accurate, or at least investigative view of such dealings is more than steadying. You see, much of what freightnes me is drawn from the screams and putridiy of people in visual media. "If that were true," I find myself saying, with a shutter. For whatever reason, these portrayal, however true they are, latch onto me, and I feel disposed to their portrayal. Yet, because were so latched, we find ourselves bringing up the sincerity of our belief in the matter; and for some time we felt that the depictions were off. "They lacked a certain reality, as most dramas do. You can't quite put your finger on it; but something was off."
 One way of looking at it, was that the display of violence blead into my mind, where I knew what I was watching was simply a movie or show, but that it represented someting real. Such reality could not escape me, 'as only dumbness would convince me there wasn't'. By that count, my choice was to either accept that what I was seeing was the truth, or deny that it was not as I was seeing, but by that, admitt to the truth other: the complexion of which I could not find.
 This expeirence burnt at me for years, and I found myself feeling rancorous about certain shows and or portrayals. "The worst of myth and legend, or reinactment at that."
 The fairest way to describe this was our denial of the unimaginable. For whatever reason I could imagine it, but different; largely centering on the social element; which is to say the way that people were represented, and how the best of shows were really higher fidelity depictions of thematic characters. "Which creates for a rather visceral and ghastly show, but akin to a grounded narrative or tale."
 I've always reflected on the world in general, and thought mightily well. But there is this batch method of thinking that you often hear from those more sophisticated; or it is more of a narrow experience, a story if you will. Neither of these reflect reality in a whole or sincere way, as far as I know or am concerned. "Both are far worse than anything that is."
It is possible that our reaction to mom and dads request was overstated, and that upon some thinking, we are actually able to incorporate their wishes.
 Simply, if we do as they say, 'which, I admitt, reflects the forward trajectory of our lives', that we are in esscence without trouble. We've likely taken for granted that working as we do is sufficient, and questionable on its softer nature. My overall life may not addmit of such concern; but so is such life progressed by continual motion; and this plea for further work is merely a pointer towards true sustainability of that. It is not, to be clear, for us to have more room to think. Yes we should; but our life is foremost guilded with activity, 'be it earned or in oblige'.
 And so, our parent's request does have some sustainability. Our reaction though comes from the somewhat needed suspenion of our ideas, and to amend to our activity, 'for ideas are more like whiffs of flowers, blowing up in the wind; but as dollops of excess, on the forests solid ground'.
 The true difficulty is in lacking the comforts, and wish for them at that, that we'd otherwise be privy to if our ideas were both absent and not to return. The truth is that we are not happy enough to live like that; and indeed, we can not afford that; as it being more expesnvie to serve your comforts and relief, only beckons you to work more to sustain that. "And then your parents have every right to ask you to work the needed days for that --were you to move out."
 This brings up an issue of value, and indeed these moments when we right, strickenly I'd add, don't make for much light yet firm views on principle. Our experience serves us; and in better moments we could reflect and act. I say this, for it would be best if inexpensive things brightened your day and made your committment to work, as opposed to thinking, more pallitable. "As we have less on our mind, and thus more room to do; but generally we don't have anything, in either adventure or pleasantry." And so I just feel this bareness, 'like we ate too little and now are less able to eat more'.
 What could we do then, to increase our 'liveliness' so to speak, and find more ease to accept more of a burden --and turn it into a deed. It's an odd thing, for if I were a child, I had all the great things you could imagine; and I hated school and sports; but if I had the friends I do now, say in highschool, and I was tasked with increasing my workload to 3 days a week, 'in order to rightfully stay home and enjoy my life as I did', I would be more than fine. But I have no friends, or those at least; and funnily enough, the idea of working 3 days a week, and having all this fun around, is a paradise. Were not in that scenario though; and I have a troubling amount of energy to support ourself in viewing our time otherwise.
I think one can afford to be rather resourcefull and or pragmatic if their in a moving state. Whereas, being in condition that is reachng for such regularity, needs no 'practical guidem but rather patiecne and discipline'. Right now, I feel as though our footing is off, but our substance is good. "Were building up to something great, and indeed things have fallen off; but just put them back".
 Two things come to mind: shopping and storage.
 On one hand, we don't like going to Superstore in the monring. We just don't. But, say your off one day and the next, you dont want to have to shop at Superstore the latter day's evening. And so plan on going the prior evening --like tonight. Going to Langley Markey and FreshCo surround the essentials that can only be gotten at Superstore. We should also even out our shopping list, and add a direct icon on our phone.
 As for storage, it is too often that we see something old, believe it to be poor yet not rancid, and we won't eat it nor cook more. We should deal with this. There is nothing wrong at any rate with getting a tomato, eating part of it, waiting 2 weeks, then throwing it away. The price was already spent; and whether you eat it or not, you can stil buy again --and just get a slice, bite it, and put kale and seeds in your mouth. I also think it would do us well to clean out our pantry, as there is much there which is either garbage, or no frequently used. Stuff that in the real pantry
 The way eating normally works is that you progress throughout the day; and if you miss a beat, go on to the next. The best state of health comes when all spots are hit; as they make that moment of the day filled, and allow for further points to be acted on. In a broad sense, eating too little on one day, makes it hard to eat the next. The core of this begins with kale, tomato and sunflower. You may just have kale with almonds/seeds, or you may skip entirely. Following your activity, 'of which, in any state of motion would be free range, warrior or running', you have your fruit, normally oranges. From here, you can add trailmix and or a kale and peanut salad; all while you reach the peak of your early day: the smoothie. From there, you want to have some sort of bulk. I prefer broccoli on off days, edamame on work/training days, and barley prior to work. This marks the point where grain can be had, and your generally more rested. In the winter, when the sun sets early, this can come prior, and have that walk/ride at set cool off such bulk; followed by grain later on --and some sort of walk to finish the evening. If the sun sets later, there is some inquiry to do as to where the bulk can be had. It is clear that if the timing is such that you would not want to have full grains after, then you may use such grains as a similar lead in to the sun walk/ride --as we do with the hearty stuff in winter. I am not clear when bulk would come prior to that. Indeed though, the balance of barley, kale or soy is a good one, and is quite fitting. "It keeps one full, and leads into rest and carbs."
 We have several questions then. Do we have kale for our salads, nuts and seeds for these and trailmix(for work), fruit for the morning --which we often do-- and smoothie supplies --which comes im bulk, but is limited by frozen kale and bananas. It doesn't hurt to underestimate the amount of Kale needed; as you can always freeze the moderate amount currently had, then chow down on something new. "It is 2$ for something that lasts several days and is great for you." Then you have your bulk: am I having barley or broccli, or soy? Soy can be gotten on sale, and is fairly worthwhile to buy many of the 1.88 packages. You don't need much, as it is merely for that days moment. Then you have your evening, which amounts to full grains, ie. quinoa, rice and lentils, potatoes and bread namely, and your more delicate foods, ie. samosas and potstickers. Carrots are a good midday and pre-bed snack. Corn and tomato sauce add a fair bit to those fuller grains, and permitt more emphasis on them, rather than lean on starches.
We work tomorrow and are then off till Friday. There are so many shifts; some 4 or 5 hours. I accept our parent's request; if only out of faith and reason; and we'll let our course run, as we adjust and or find a place to move. Indeed, much of our qualms come from the sense that our ideas are too ill-formed, and are thus up against firm views around, 'for which were worn from having to resist'. I realize for instance that the media is not with the purpose nor evidence in making claims, merely reporting. And if there is a disucssion or mock debate, it is for the sake of converse; comapred to the judgement we pass on people actually making claims; which itself rests on the function of our constitution. If someone says, 'democracy is failing, or Trump is bad', these are mere words; but more reasonably, their views, opinions, marks of discussion and converse. I prefer reflection, as to see all and remain tenuable --to things we understimate or do not know. But those who act otherwise are not some threat or imposig force. Only our ignorance feels unsteadied, rather than any truth about the swayings of the world. In a different light, you could say that 'democracy failing' is a statement which we cannot genuinely refute; for we take it for granted that it is actually so. There are ebbds of our days that feel more impressionable to the words of others, 'and our expectation of them creates a sort of blocaked to a feeling of regularity'. But this merely amounts to a response on our part, where such sensitivity causes us to notice whose around us more --as if their some potential threat. When this reply is over-tuned, then things of the 'democracy is failing' sort, speak to me, in that we either invalidate the claim, or know otherwise. I would take refuge in the insight, that perhapds in scenario and posture we are off, to be struck by such a view; but so may we bear insight, as to the fact of such givens we don't know.

In a way, the act of it is masked in the whole of our lives; and it is comparable to one eating tasty dinners; for which I am not concerend about. Watching porn though is so exhilirating, that I obsseviely refute it, 'for I feel off in believing it as something so good, or so poor in being in denial of it; for the normal idea of sex, which in the simples form relates to the porn your seeing, becomes this thing that is no longer commonly fine to ignore, but this great thing were missing'. Again, I would like to masterbate; and indeed, i accept our postion when it comes to Bella and company; and I'm fine with this. Having more shape to those latter allows for you to partake in the former, 'as your not thrown off by the unease of not wanting to go further than the banter, nor the constrictive force that grieves you with others for being so different'. I have a fair bit of thoughts, and although the feelings are initially clear, and our address of surrounding facts is fine, we forget the starting feeling. Because of this, I would suggest we mention our clearest feelings, as to let those other sparse one's trickle through.
 Namely, that we are not on route for Amon anymore. I realize foremost that I don't wish to be her partner or friend. All we seek is that which comes from our excitement towards her. She doesn't really show me much interest; and that could be due to her age, background, or character.
 It seems that at the core of us is this fabric composition; which may become excited and or pleased, or stiff and dry. Indeed, there is a propensity for things, and duration of lack, for which we become more elated and excitable. Relaxing feels so hard then. I only see though, that we are too often riding a novel and or momentary high, for which we feel just in our passionate view. "For in feeling so well, we stand by our interest with more confidence, even though looking beyond from our high perch." The truth is that there is bareness below; and both our aptness to relate to someone, and the general neccessity to connect, or at least reality of the situation in which you are around someone, regresses you from that novel high, and instead your with a view from that lacking ground below. Better put, the reality of things routinely comes to pass, and you see how far apart you are, and how your confidence made you see what you did, 'as if a dream'. Such a state can inspire effective action; but the facts often portrude. We work together, don't really speak much; nor do we have much of a true interest beyond that emotion and or gluttony as sparked from our perch. The point being, that when the incompatibility or natural seperation comes into view, your true rather than impassioned nature is put to task. But this we can take for granted as principles coming into play; 'as we don't have a genuine interest or selflesness to flirtingly expose ourselves to every random girl we see, nor those in our department'. Interest may be the main source for contradiction, as we more often see others drawn to us, but not us to them, 'for which we are fooled into thinking the former is true'. The point being, that albeit a question for truth below, that so much as your integral cares are bowed to, that so are there few actions which we are privy to in our passionate state; and as such, the realistic standings of two folk fairs the scenario, for which you must accordingly attend. Only, we are coming at it from our non-passioned view, but rather from the steady or lack there off foundations below. And from here, my wish for someone dissipates, as we realize how off we are, and how treating that becomes a care on it's own, but also, upon doing so, reveals and or guides us to different wishes.
So it seems that we may do things as to clear the way (so to speak) for something we enjoy; we coudl also apply this attitude to things of a challenging sort, 'as to make our efforts easier to bear or accomplish'. On the other hand, we may do things that give us the means to do something at all, or expeirence something different. Our studies come to fruition here, as for whatever reason, when we lack comfort, peace, or are inundated with doubt, langour over the thought of labor, or insecure about our own future wants, or that placed on us by others, having the formation as to a) bear less of an inmpulse and or effect from such things, ie. being prinicipally strong, as to incorporate and or 'let things by' that come into you, and thus leave less of an impression, and b) respond to these in ways that are both lightening, steady and proper --ie. not being evoked to further passion or concern, or in other words, sitting with the effect, letting it sink in and loft away, and so such as there are sparks and embers, and your own potential kindling, that your not taken about. For it is important to note that although foundations lessen the intake and potential for poor development, we still benefit from more lively and commiserate thoughts: as to put out and or quoft the inklings of a starting flame. "If a spark hits, and the emebrs are surging, our reason and experience, 'all of which we call on and dissolve', can be used to sufficate the flame, 'in that we prevent it from taking in oxygen and thus growing to a unmanagable, or at any rate unfavorable form'."
With more space in my mind and a bit more breathe in my soul, i can make more suited remarks about our time and way.
 Simply, I see that when we are in tandum with our condition and amend, that we may sit more easily, but also make for potential things. The point is that we don't want to be longing, nor fear our content. And in general, I cannot say what prosperity will look like; as much of it does not fit any active desire. But, that we would write more about sums, start a youtube channel, discipline ourselves for monetary sake, or simply dance, so can this procure through our stability.
 I also meant to mention earlier that my aim with Amon is not to go off on some fling. I don't think she would be into it; not to mention the reality of what then follows. And because I don't have a genuine interest to be with her, the path seems dim. No matter, I am open to someone who actually excites me. Again, most people don't initiate flings with you; and for all those boys out there who engage with such, how much did you text with said girl. We don't do this, and as much as we don't, we ought not be confused into thinking that potential attraction will magically bring things out. But, if someone I like asked me for dinner, I'd sit; no different than if Justin wanted to smoke I would, for I'd be off, but with someone who got me, who I could out of neccessity and care share my concern and or stake on food. If a bunch of people at worked asked me, I'd say no. "For they know me not as to warrant me doing something wrong." In a similar light, my stability may offer potential for those who I really connect with; while we more or less handle our succour for sex; notably by removing ourselves from porn. Nonetheless, my reply to this recent feelings is not one of diversion or acceptance, merely understanding; as to our actual place in things, what I need, and how I am really going to go in relation to such demands. For I dream of people seeing me, knowing me, so I may get more from work. Yet this is a fantasy. I partake in as much as strikes me. As much as I don't, I either deal with the pressure on my own, or dream of being known, 'as to recieve time from others'. The former is what seems more present, and that our dreams are neither good nor likely. But I am merely filling in the holes which our dreams yearn for otherwise, with substantial things.
 I will state that our mom and dad are a good prescence in my life, and for better or worse, their wish for me to work more is something I can get behind. Simply due to it measuring up to our independant time, and thus what we can all agree is fair. Whatever the case may be, at home or not, whatever is fair makes you feel permittable, to rest and see, 'as your not afraid nor yearning from a premature line'. So doing this makes me feel alright, and able to angle other actions and efforts appropriately. Our home then seems a solid point of our life, and something which balances our return from work. There we have much buzz with other people; not all, and indeed this made me feel awkward. But as regards Bella and Cass, the point is that there is nor should there be awkwardness, but there may be no potential for friends. When it comes to Joey and Andy, we are friends, and I enjoy talking to them. But there is no friendship. Even Tristan, is someone who I consider a real friend, is not like Justin, in that we share values. That is something we must make up for in myself.
 All I'll say is that during work, when under pressure, our lackings are revealed. And foremost, when were in such a place, and our prior dreams and passion fall away, 'or are seen to be futile and hopeless, or worth abating', so do we feel struck, and perhaps overly motivated to find worthier things to put our mind to. In this very moment, I see how rather sullen and tired we are, and in hearing the news, I see clearly that the emotion that were beckon to is one of drenching, whereby our response to what we hear is so as to overide our prior concepts and intention. Which were in themselves but views and ambitions that steadyied us by their promising nature. Oddly, as we recede from this, 'as not to rebuke or affirm', we pass to the point of irreverence, where our life is in fact just it's own, and there is no connectedness. In that, I see how the idea of violence and cruelty can be alarming, as there is no unified or calming sense that removes this from happening. But you only need sit into your actual place, and the potential that the various other beings around could be, moves out of sight. "Your tugged by this as you move form a unified sense, to one of self-experience and environemnt." Indeed, and I will end hear, that for the sake of remedying our lack of pleasure and comfort, 'for which work and stress foster me to seek', and finding an otherwise sure and ready path, that we ought reconcile first principles, and deeds of our life. For much of the peace which we cannot find is so from us lacking what people otherwise expect.
I have always preferred those dear goods. These are not like food and fun, but rather joy and satisfaction. Their things that you do, get invested in, feel important or impactfull.
 As a child, you may do both, as we did indeed enjoy treats and leisure. But as you age, and the reality of the world comes to fruition, you must take part in deeds to abide. And because of this, enjoying the comforts of the world, as to abate and or soothe your condition, removes your access to those other joys. As a kid you can have both; but because you are spending effort elsewhere, the reduction caused by indulgence only holds you back.
 As such, we have not gained our satiety from food, but rather literature and creative passion; of our own fruits and that of others. This has always enthused me most. For as a child I enjoyed nothing more than outdoor hockey, running through the woods, making Lego or playing games. As an adult though, I found a stark resolve to these, as our care for a career dissolved; and so we found ourselves rather despondant, and at the mercy of works demands. If we worked, we would feel less guilt or pressure in 'forming ourself'; but so did that work cause toil, which our lack of comfort could not make up for. After a decade, we are realizing that our simplistic life, as drawn from our time of dependance, and the frugal short-termness wherein such dependance was not a resort, but merely the scenario at hand, that so our current life, more or less endowed with independance, affords the effort required of this via our developments in our truer joys. Again, we retreated from this our whole life, as we neither had nor admitted the comforts that made such work feasible. But, as it were, we resorted to what really filled us, in our leisure and joy. The differnece being, that in our time of dependance, we had all the means to committ ourselves to. Work came periodically, and showed us where we lacked. But so our emtional uneveness lay; of which were still in.
 This is the tricky part you see. For desire and passion are like gusts of lifting wind, bringing us forward by the hope and expectation of something. Yet reason describes our true scenario, and fits us to an end which is more appropriately right, as our wisdom and care truly prescribe. For we know that the wonders of reality, as we see them at least, are not conducive when in the company of drowning and or selfless delight. Sitting on the floor is the key contrary to this; and as a foundation for better things, so do those former intrigues wash us out. But, in accepting this, the rationale at least, you must see that your hopes for certain friends or fortune is folly. It is not that there is no hope or potential for folk and effort that fits your current stride. But honesty excludes us from the whimsical want and elation that those false desires treat. Were doing ourselves no good. And, in some sense, reflects the fraility of our attempts; for it is in our incompatibility with something, 'yet the Siren effect from their outer appeal --and matched by our equally inward want', that has us so elated; for if not, we see that our truth is not so fitting, and thus with rational means towards them. "Which is why we hope that were given something; or we conjure some image of ourselves, as would be worthy of their attention." Give me money sure; but I won't work for any by myself. Yet thinking this will happen only strings us along, 'making up for what we lack inside'.
 This lack though is the hardest of things. For reason does tell of solitude and uncommonality with general folk, in as much as your hopes are higher and of kindred might. I think that cultivating what this means will give our reason more footing, 'as to give us more composure amidst a determined struggle'; equally, we may bring out the goods of this path, or at least bring more light and body to the strands already in fruition.
 I struggle in saying the true reason and meaning behind war memoirs, or that of surgeons. Why it brings light to me, I do not know. But, when I think of our truest time, it is in treating literature, 'whether in conforming it, as a painter does to model ahead', or in reflection, 'as to extract our thoughts on the matter'. Nothing brings me more peace and cheer. And indeed, the moral of our story so far, is that for the infinite good and meager demands, that as history and or nature has given me in our hunger and distaste, that so is our retreat from fancy, 'which I'd reckon is folly', or disposition, 'as would reflect committments away from our real character', that so in the regression from these, do we need to embrace something elsewhere. "For that peace and cheer will be absent." The good of those prior things is in their hope; and their purest of goods are not conducive to more prevailing and lasting joy: which makes their committment, 'however needed for such success', to be contrary to said joys. But in hailing ourselves to such, or being swooped away at least, we are more or less whole: just of a composition which is either directed to unsought ends, or merely vain, by the eventual sense that your anticipation held no fruit. Noticing this is whats brought me away, to see how we really are, and where we have to go. But this new state, or reoritentation, needs equal luster and resolve. "For it is one thing to recede from folly and garulity, and another to be nothing."
So I both loathe and hate the way girls look in porn, when there in camera view, sucking dick. They seem so drawn, like 'oh my, what a big dick you have, I can't wait'. It is that 'I can't wait attitude', that causes so much hate, for it imbues in me that other girls, normal ones I mean, have equal want; and thus, because were so sprung up, we feel pathetic and envious, for we lack that. And, considering that they want it so bad, how poor must we be to not be having it.
 The thought which comes to mind is that each person in a relationship is into one another. Hookups and flings are naturally one time things, for no regard for the other is paid; you don't think, just act on your baser impulse and interest, then move on. I think we struggle to meet people because we are not so into them, nor open for them to be into us. But I feel it wrong to think that people come together for any other reasno than they like one another. I think back to me and Emily, and how oppertune it seemed following our chat. But we still had to talk for at least an hour; and the idea of us just going at it right away was not well received. Just being my self procured our admittence for sex; but it took alot of effort. And we spoke for some time prior; and she was young.
A life of philosophy, so wonderful and fine, yet with potential for grading harm.
 You see, the trouble with this kind of living is that it relies on you alone; and thus if you fail, the results are according to the portion of yourself which has messed up. In other pursuits, you rest on something established; and thus your mistakes don't impact you fully. But it is as if you were holding onto a rope, and began to let go; you'd feel the difference entirely. The point being, that since this life procures sustenance through personal action, you end up being on your own; for much of what you care for in the best of states, and what is needed to reach there, is not but for you to hold. When you fail though, you are left with nothing but your half empty self. And this only becames ever more clear as your progress; as in some way or the other, you can never cease your oblige, and thus even in flourishing you are on your own. It is worth noting that the unending demand on you, and how experience only comes so far, and thus viewing your goal as important, or at least special, is required. It it comes down to the trouble of applying your efforts towards yourself, and how in the ups and downs of this, you need some form of committment; and this bends itself especially to some broader purpose or end. It is hard to merely see our efforts for the sake of some delight; as being even slightly out of this shine lets a whole medley of impressions in. This is why religion is prominent, or some mode of generosity or craft. It's just too hard to sit in the moments of abscence.
I thought' I'd clarify this, for peace of mind. There is no pressure on the GPU to be at 60 degrees. They are designed to be around 80 for long periods of time, every day, for many years. To quote gpt, 'it could be at 50-60 24/7 for an extended period of time and be fine'. And the reality is that because these temps are so fine, that their gradual decline after the videos are off, or were put the pc to sleep, is fine. It doesn't matter, and so we can let the zero rpm kick in. I'm bothered mostly by the unresponsiveness of amd, but this seemingly is no matter. Let the video raise the temps to unproblematic levels. We can customize more effectively our gaming temps.
I must admitt my heart dropped when my scooter turned off. If only because it was while I was riding, and it was all of a sudden. I accept this, and place my action and thought on the two discrete ends: one being that we fix our scooter, or get a new one; as we have both money and reason to do both; or two, that we don't have a scooter at all. We have done this before, and found it not too bad. Walking to work is fine; you leave at the half hour mark. Walking home becmoes the hard part, as it is hard to rest. Maybe we'll find a spot, near the overpass, before or after. Or we may stretch prior to leaving; though this means our breaks need something to do. At any rate, our free time is not too ill-effected by this, as we simply walk, and manage our day through this. There is actually benefit, seenig as we walk more; and the simplicity of it all is comparable to using our PS over PC. Sometimes it feels like our motive to go out for a ride is because we can. Indeed, we don't live in the most ideal of spots, as there is no great place to sit and read. But our knowledge of any street makes for suitable walking; as does the sky offer herself vitally. All there is then, is the scenario of suburbia, and its layout of propertys; which in effect, block your walks and views that would be there if the land was un-gridded, but rather like the country, or woody at least. Indeed, suburbia is a strange element of modern life; for urban settlements are not a recent developemnt; only, they were much smaller, and at any rate, were more akin to city states with farmland around. Suburbs are like extensions of this; our Ridge Meadow area being a tertiary portion of the greater Vancouver area. A key point to this, is that suburbs offer cheaper housing; and as such, allow the purchase of a car, as to travel into the city --where the work is. "The commute pays for itself due to the cheaper pricing afforded from being so far out." Ironically our expenses are cheaper in the city.
It feels safe to say that our life is oriented around philosophy. This could change, in similar manner to us potentially leaving the floor. But it is where we are now; and my choices are being made from its orientation.
 As such, I feel it is fair to souggest that a) we will leave our current home, and either move somewhere in Pitt Meadows or Maple Ridge, or leave for somewhere other, or b) stay at home so long as doing so works for our orientation.
 One of the things which strikes me, is that our biggest want is equal to that when no one is home; or less even, who you have to anwser to, 'or who take up space in your mind in holding some exepectation'. What I seek is that openeness, to feel no expectation but those I set myself. In many ways, those that are on me when home, or even as a tenant, are comparable to what I would have on my own. But there are broader resistances and or regards which prevail, and minor ones too. In a way, or own minor concerns rub up against those lattter, while our broader ones rub up against the former.
 Indeed, this is more of a lay of the land, rather than reflection as to who we are and what things mean. For I am only trying to think of the discrete choices at hand. What qualifies either is part of this discretion; but we are not truly deliberating here. Merely outlining
I think some broad perspective can shine a light here. And indeed, I like this notion of outlining, or saying what things there are; and not deliberating what this means, or what we've determined.
 For one, I see our finances as untroubling. The government gives a modest amount to those old and with low income. So I feel personally relieved. And, for myself or dependants, we are given pretty reasonable dental and eye coverage. I appreciate this. Our paystub has increased, as we get 4% for vacation pay [which is not some bonus which your not taking elsewhere; as unpaid vacations are merely days you are allowed to book off. But that is not hard to do normally]. We also get 11 stat holidays, which amounts to nearly 100$ a month, which is a huge boost to our budget. We can almost afford an average of 800$ rent. Working 20 hours a week gives you almost 1500$ per month. Rooms are avaialble in Maple Ridge and some in Pitt Meadows, but they are all over Vancouver and Burnaby. I digress though.
 One reoccuring element is that of prospects. I've worked on, or at least come to terms with our reasoning for such want, and how most of our thoughts regarding the matter are from either longing or affirmation: which are in both cases the result of our time loosing it's flow and harmony, but rather being boxed in --as a result from some poor feeling or intrusive thought [likely bade by an input upon tangled grounds --such as that which porn burries within us].
 Many of the things which seem great, and are, namely of girls and working abroad, are not so easy as they seem; nor of worth in the way that your dreaming of or envying others for. The reality is that they are hard to achieve and make use of. And I don't shrink away from that thought; as I do admitt that our life's stability and ease has not been so right, and thus procurative of such efforts. But I can sit here and say that whatever oppertunity there is, that it is not value'd souly on it being so promising, but rather defined by it's relation to our determinations; which center on making our life right, and the maintained balance of that; for which, in true balance, keeps a demand, 'for which farther pursuits are not seen as capable, or so much'; or we just don't care, or see at any rate. Indeed, I have only lived as such prior to Kamloops; and I recall after fitting ourselves to work that we were on the up and up. I do feel that much our life would favor the goods that came from actually being alright; and the notion of having the stability to see promising pursuits would be exchanged for efforts and goods that were both near and meaningfull to our lives. Being in the city gives more access to meeting people online, and thus having casual intercouse, dating, connection even. There are more meetup oppertunities, generally more places to see. Troubling me still is the want from this, 'in the offer of this bountful place', and the more calm and lush meadows were familiar with; and reconciling how we are as to live steady and oppetunely amidst the former, while seeing ourselves as potentially able to be more nomadic, and thus go to different areas: yet, this does not provide for the cultivation of a single place. Indeed, are we trying to live, or make a life. It seems that our handle on philosophy is such, that of it's continuously requiring nature, and the norm of making but progress, that seeing some place new, and expecting that there will be places past this next, is something that differs than forming a life. Indeed, I have faith in being both right and true to ourselves, and letting our harmony run with that wind. For we seemingly will be well enough to care not whether time or fortune prevail; and we may apply ourselves, or humour at least that which strikes us as potential. And indeed, all being good enough to let the seasons come and go, or cirucmstance change. Right now I feel were on the early ups following a prior plateau; and I hope to bring our steps into more reasnoable and effective lines. One mark of maturity is to lay the tracks and take the steps you imagine yourself doing when in better times ahead: do them now. It makes your current space stronger, though requires that you admitt your faults, and deal with them amidst this less than optimal place.
I feel right to determine things. We already know what there is.
 Simply, working 3 days a week is hardly a load. There is much time before and after to enjoy.
Motivation for joy is a varied thing. As others being around, or in view of your efforts alters the pronouncment. But so may the form of your joy hold it's own by virtue of such form; whereby, in the case of writing, 'our eyes are enough; and the elements treating us are of worth considering that'.
 As such, I was listening to Ben Moore exclaim how much fun he had making up characters and stories for tabletime; and it seemd obvious that his point was that he could just do that all day long, as it felt so good to create. The foundation of this, nor any prospective rise, is not tied to theres, but enhanced by it. It is indeed a privalge and honor to recieve attention. But a great meal does not become so when you have friends. It merely adds to it, heightens it in certain ways.
 The point being, that there's something about these kind of efforts, interests and delights, that direct one differently, then say those who earn their pleasure from excitment and thrills, or partying and high expense. It's just a different form. It's about worlds, people and characters, actions, progress and fun. From books and games, your own writing and study, to the decour of the world, all is here. Its a life where your on your own though, rather than one of gathering's and public scuttling. That is why there's a privalage, for an artist or maker, as their normally private time is breathed upon by others.
 One idea that ticks me is this notion of purpose and contribution, and thus arguement and judgement. When we study physics for instance, I often find myself up against the norms, vying for a claim, 'having to point my thoughts in relation to other's'. The reality though, is that my commradery with people is more along the lines of civility; whereby I don't have this unified nationalism with all the folk around me, but merely a good intent. One is from me, and spreads around frmo us, 'with fair content, or even flat: for those who stand inwards, but with no remarks of others'. In a way, as one may question or at least situate demonstrative reason, that so may a person situate themselves as mere people, who are living their lives under a broader load; a load which may be characterized as otherwise ideal, of a form, a group, a symbol so to speak. As such, my moral reasoning and fabric is more about actions, and how we would describe them morally; rather than some moral framework, of rules and teachings, that our actions are weighed against. "It is our common experience and observation of pleasure and pain that holds our context for making choices; or in certain moments, reacting and or reflecting."
 There is an odd truth in life, and it is one that supercedes the dialectic. That is so, that of the case of whole's and particulars, it is hard to define one withtou the other: yet a paradox ensues. The truth is that we are likely mistaken in our beliefs; and surely so for we wish to avoid paradoxes. But we've only admitted probale truths, and neccessary maxims. What is true is that we have a life worth living, and thus orienting ourselves around, as to guarentee it. "And in the case of philosophy, heighten it." But this looks for an approach that is commiserate with the rules and activity around this; but not so for the sake of them. That is what frameworks are: old truths, rightfully or not determined, that our actions pivot against. While the contray is so, that our in-born deeds are delivered, and we use what knowledge we can to magnify the quality of our experience. To earn more, bleed less, endure, and by wisdom avoid.
 On the floor of this is the avoidance of other peoples convictions. "Those who are outside themselves, yet still formative. A garulous example are boys who act out in youth, to cover up their inferiority. There is the truth, but their practical lashing out." When it comes to democracy, physics and success, we are at least aware of other's admittence. And indeed, as someone by their own, we feel the heat and or coming of such admittences when we are low in ourself; usually in rest, or sickness, 'when we hear the sirens caling'. And indeed there fumbles about thoughts which occure when we are in any sort of state that is not a flourishing. "When we are so, there are no concerns, and we more or less be; but as we are not, and were propsoed to such a state, we think and or grieve; about how to return, 'for whih we reflect on the elements around us and determine the route to proceed', or what ails us, for which we feel melancholic about the nature of our lack, 'how it is either unfar, without hope, or pressed upon us'.
 And so, the way's of philosophy, which I may coin in this context as the sprouting of your life, as to really be, and not hunkered down or in pursuit of: the states and or wishes of those covering their lack. Again, experiencing the growth and moments of such treatment are fair in and of themselves, and neither warrant nor elicit any concerning or critical respone. But time has her ways, and circusmtance in tandum; for which new things ask of us; or, as these new things naturally require new amneds, that so in old tasks do we find ways that we could improve. No matter, we are always dipping out of that lavishing state, and the return to steadyness --ie. progress- and flourishing itself, isn't immediate. Like an athlete, our effort cannot simply be that of want and rising effort, but also understanding, of normal lows, their endurance, and the forbearence to cushion the edges that (in those moments) we sit upon. For sirens and imagery either draw us or haunt us, as if to carry us away to someplace easier; or nag at us as we resist, but in isolation from our stable highs. "It's as if we got lost in the forest; and instead of heeding the witches plea, we hunkered down in the brush. But we are not home, and the witch taunts us while we remain. The forebearence needed is that which prevents us from hearing the witch, 'so that we feel less prickly in our foresteed time'. But also, we should not be so disposed, as to have the incling, or rather susceptibility, to be preyed upon."
 Something that people may experience, is that in their fun and success, that they still have troubles in their relationship. It's just something that gets put off, or finds incidentla, yet surprising conflict. In the case of philosophy, such troubles never come up. Manners are not an issue, nor misdeeds of kindness. Itinerary and enthusiasm never lacks, nor does following laws and rules. There appears none for someone living liking this. Most rules are like a condition, where attempting something has some sort of stipulation, or restriction. There is nothing for me to pursue that has such rules, or relevant one's at least. Indeed, all I recognize is what I have to do; and I fail not to recognize the worth in that; in the way that it is needed, and what we'd be withotu it. I also notice when we are short of this; and use the a measure of whats just as something to bring us back in line. Currently, we are working too little for us to count on. And so our cirucmstance feels precarious. We will deal with more shifts, and whether by circusmtance or constancy, that we must travel there. Our attraction is not absent though, and the utlity and or reward from cars, mobile work, relationships and pleasure, don't exist that far outside me; and I do feel when we are in quieter and or lower moments, 'that these call out to me'. There is some confusion, namely in our amends to these things, or their means. Of their efforts and deeds, if there is clarity in what they bring, so are we able to be so prospective. We linger not though on such goods, for they are very much of circumstance, and irrelvant during our truer time. "Itinerary is apart of life, and needed, while being prospective is a part of life, but merely oppertune. It is less neccessary, and so not to worry if when we leave states that would otherwise sprout committment. I only see, as a reminder perhaps, that bringing ourselves to such sproutfull states correspond with the care we have for those states to begin with. Let this not simmer though. Our awareness is enough to see and find peace, in our general proceedings, for which this broader goods can be apart of."
 Our conclusion, is that what we seek, in tandum with our lower ways and what they reveal, that what is sought for from this, 'and truly of that which we see from above', is not so as is deemed good for that which eases our time in these lower states; rather, what helps us sit in them, and remove any stalking force. These are the matters of the constitition, and it's credible backbone for which society maintains, physics and its elaborative nature, and science, as to ground our thoughts and serve inquiries and truths that mitigate drowning and or unwanted concepts, via comparison. These amount to learning about things which can be known wholly, and using them as scales for which relevant yet obscure and or accepted beliefs/truths are balanced by. The complexity of a computer, in how there are all these tiny processing units, that in bulk create digital compostion, can be used as a analog to our mind, and how it's own tiny elements create our sensory compositions. The point being, that having that clear knowledge of the former lets you sit with the obscurity of the latter. And indeed, what we seek is the runway to inquire; but when were stuck, unable or uninterested, it is nice to rely on things seperate and concrete. "For erdict and or frustrating comments about the subject were progressing, or on the runway with, can't always be thwarted by virute of what we lack on such subject. Sometimes our developments overcome such intrusions. Other times though, they are broader, and we have not progressed to such height in any concrete way." I seek then the formation of the earth and plate tectonics, the development of micro-macro life, the way that computers and waves works, the phenomena of color, 'and how there are but varied saturations of things which are not in fact color', and other random things which serve similar purposes, such as spectorscopy and carbon dating, 'as to certify bones and astronomy', and the economies and populations of early to current modern countries.
As for work itself, it seems overly apparent that your mentality ought wrap around the oblige of your efforts, as to harmonize your steadyness and strenght. This is hard to do when you know your leaving, or have intentions to at least. And I recognize that being at home is not privy to your perosnal feelings, and so despite working more being a means to liberty when home, it does feel like were bearing more in the same temporary state. I feel that, for relationships, generosity, production and discipline, that these things must not only come from a position which has been felt in, but also seems perpetuous. It is hard to take our limited offerings now and spend them on things who are not so elated upon the small window we gleam from. No matter, I would say that getting sleep is a priority. We should have the sense that 11 is too late, nor is 10 to be langored over, or looked pass as some point where you should really turn in. This is a primary instance, whereby knowing that 10 is a reasnoable threshold for eating, that we make more efforts --from such clarity- to eat more, and thus do so, to our sustenance, but also ease, for we don't have this wofting sense about when we'd eat. Without that condition, we end up eating much later. And this brings your leisure in to, as you have this sense of where your day is going, and that there is need to eat before a certain time. I feel our work-prep is solid, and nothing has really been out of the ordinary. Indeed, the biggest struggle of the recent months is our exercise. Which I can only say is so because of our dragging need to work out, and our infrequency of doing so. We aren't active enough, which makes training harder, and if we do so, it causes grief and thus less activity. Were at the bottom of that hill. What I'd say, is the amount of stregnth training you need is in that small amount that fits ever so often into your actual activity. They are the standard; and indeed, they may hold themselves under the lens of form and intent; but the ethic is one of surrender, as to really just do what you want, and bolstered by some understanding and intent as much as you don't, but all so you may live better and happier; rather than succeed in appearing well, or satiating your concern of not. The fact that such concerns whittle away as your more active and to an extent train, is not encumbant upon such activity. "Our peace with this relieves me of having to use this as an affirmation, or token of confidence."
So I had a pretty solid day today. We walked to work, exchanged words with people, worked pretty well, had our mix while we shopped, then went to Vancouver and back: now were here.
 I feel the notion worth centering on is the consistency of work, returning to it, pleasing yourself by the ease in which you experience, considering it's obligate nature; also, taking in your repor with other people, be it Amit, 'who claimed to be excellent, and we had a nice time with that'; or Kassandra, who I spoke about Harry Potter with. Andy is our bud, as is Tristan, and Alex. I notice that we may come on too strong, or it's just not the right time, and it is more of our own composure that perceives this as a slight, or some hint that their avoiding you. People glance and I notice, or they respond in some weird way; though, it is never how people treat me, even Morgan, but their look. I just can't get a read on them. Amon even, who giggles when I talk to her, I can't tell if she's blushing or merely amused. At any rate, I see how other's going out on their own, or doing something away from me, feels like a cementing that their not of interest to me. I feel this need of ours is but a small sliver of a thing normal to all people; only, depsite being small, the entirety of the space is absent.
