But we need not fill it with wrnog expectations, that others are so curious about you, or in awe, or focused on you. Rather, we should appreciate the energy we get from speaknig to Amon; and rather than let this carry you away to some promised time ahead, let it perferuate your current moment, 'as to breathe something nice into the work at hand'. The moral, is that you need not miss this, considering your letting it in. I wish to live discrepionately, for words, worlds and wonder. I do. But this blessing of other's qualities and or experience upon you, see your work as this thing readily apart of your life: and make your life so flowing, as to enjoy the in between moments as part of this flow: rather than how it often is, that we reach a peak of effort at work, long to leave, and see our free time as this culminating peace. "We forget that we return." In that case, when we do realize, it is somber, and were cautious about the return. "For in our relase, we became seperate." Let this be your life. And you don't have to leave, either home or Pitt, or your own place at that.
I notice that the idea that Amon or Bella look for a time, person or place after work, is almost like were being left. In an abnormal way, we might have had some normal converstaion, and in my head, I'd say 'oh my, she likes me'. Then the thought of her getting called over by her friends shows that she really doesn't care.
Our emtions are in a better state than they were a few weeks ago. We've stopped watching porn, but still think of Shawn, or someone from work, and we use our grippy. It's nice, and has left me less impulsive and reactive to things. Notably, were not so heated up by any girl, 'with googling eyes'.
 Our scooter may be fixed, it may not. I am not paying for anything. They have a 48v 15ah scooter for 700$. It goes very fast, has full suspension, is hardily built. When it comes to water damage, the bottom seems the least out of your control. We can make attempts to add protection through sealants. Otherwise, the top is the least of our worries; and the rain only adds the condition of the ground being wet. At any rate, we can get the segway from costoc for 600, return it within the year if we like.
 Our computer is fine. The input seems a little off, as it doesn't always detect it normally. The effects are little though. Otherwise, it is near silent outside of games, and not terribly loud for most games. I've tried Civ 6, 7 and red dead. There was some wine, and this just may be from the particular game; so be it. No crashes yet, and it runs very smooth.
 Grandma and Grandpa are coming christmas eve, then them, Holly and Jaime, Carson and Morgan, Corry, Lucas and Ryland are coming after boxing day, for the afternoon, and then the holidays are over.
 I am posed to our work-life state, rather than a moderate version of working very little, begrudging the work, and finding trouble in preparing for it, while still being satisifed. Now, we work enough to be a) just, so that our indifference is laudible, but also b) sizeable as to create rest and satiety.
 Being busy creates much ease, and what is leftover is want. We mustn't forget, or be dissmayed rather, by the effort of this, and let this normal workload be the measure of our success. I feel this want to go out there with everyone, and be romantic and fun, chivalrous, and let go from our duties, yet born from them. This is not our way. I maintain it not. We only need to live in harmony with work, rather than despite it. Bring it in, make it part of your day; train and prepare to walk and rise, and your off time will have a natural bend as to procure this, and nourish yourself from what you've done; but then all the amount of free time, and increasingly as your virtues blend with normal and sought action, that your freedom for personl want is open. We mustn't overlook the nature of our path, in that it is a pendulum of work and living, each being best when it is a suitable and pleasing moment within such rythm. "Too much do we feel frail upon work, to conjure some exlcusive moment."
There is a peace that occurs when you are not rapurted by love, nor desire. Chocolate and sex are quite different, but compare in that you get these remarkable highs; but then seek them, which overts you from peace. And indeed, there is something about a relationship, in that it is not simply about love, but bonds, trust, union. These may be needed for love, to last at least. But the point, is that this manner of purity, joy, awe and delight, these come from ease, calmness, posture and might. Such things are thrown off by want. And indeed, neither friendship nor romance differ form a vegetable, in that it offers things contrary to good. But they are hard to make. And one ought not confuse the pleasures oppertune for that which leaves one surrendered, 'as they trust that such person will forever come'. They need not want, nor grieve over abscence. The good can be there as those which come from the sun, or reading, things of the sort. Indeed, we may say that their are things in your control; to go out and see, or take up the pen. Their calmer and less intwining goods. The point though, is it is not the good which comes from them, rather the peace that comes from not having your wishes swell, your foresigth rot with expectation, or fear at the potential of missing out. This is the state, by which all things good are experienced. Indeed, when were in this state, we pay no genuine regard; things come, go, we act and parlay, as do we muster and oblige. Their reactions, attractions, interests. Indeed, the history of flaw and habition are what compell me to form myself against the lust and whims, that take me away from our steady position, 'and the experience which flourishes from this'. For I truly like the flowers. And when we see someone having sex, or think their time doing so was easy, see yourself as no different, in that you are pursuing something in equal sort. The truth is that in one way or the other they fail. "As those who succeed forget their situation at home." Only peace brings all things out. For you do nothing that fairs consequence; and all things dealt to you are unjudged by someone else. Our experience is then rightly dealt with, as we are free to accept or adjust.
 That is what I mean at the end of the day. To perhaps be flawed, as the past has disposed us, and our fortune gives.
I think that the basis for our thought is in the denial of moral reasoning, and the contrast of pure self regard. For in one, you naturally and sensibly avoid that which bears consuequence. Indeed, I laugh when other people fall, but help them up. It's our nature. I may steal, cheat and what not, but only at my own expense. "There is an ignorance, or arrogance, amidst all of us, in how such actions, 'which normally are but for our own consquences', affect others." No matter, there may be an ideal world, where we all work together with eachother in mind. Leadership is required though, and I know none. I'm aware of more or less many, and I see them; but their prescence is merely one of display --as the bulk of people's action reflect. Again, of ideals, we are all ignorant. Not as reflects us, but one another. This is not so because we lack moral reasoning, but are personally parched, 'as occurs when you drink too much, or eat too much whithering food'. It's as if your spirit has nothing to wrap around and animate, 'for your body is so rigged and runny'. We tend to avoid trying in this case, or fidget in our attempt.
 Indeed, my intake of potential regards come's from the world around me. And much of that ties into me when we are in more open and active states. I perhaps see the world; but in all reality am not struck by it. "It is not my buisness whether someone received the right service in Uganda, or was harrassed in Algeria, nor Kejhistan." Odd indeed that rules and custom are for more or less local matters; as we are inscribed not to assault one another; which is only relevant where I go. There may be more to this, but it is for police and or authorities to deal wit those who cross this boundry. My role is merely to heed it, and let the broader managment of resources get distributed. Only those who judge me for minding my buisness, however well, but not in any ideal way, have nothing but their own conviction of their ideal right; which may be true, yet only rests in them and their enthusiasm. I would be also. But perhaps the truth is that life is inherently limited, and we all struggle with providing for ourselves. Indeed, I do have free time, but it is spent in between reoccuring roles. Take for granted that a family is not itself a moral deed, 'as to source the next generation'; but is rather what people with the time and effort available seek. In that way, are we being selfish? If I have the time and money for my own children, what about those who are starving in Africa? Of the good in bringing a child to this world, what about those already present? Refering to what is realistic is a thoughtless comment. Understandable, but overlooks the question at hand.
 All I ever say, is that there is a great deal more of me which is undealt with, and that by doing so, the results which our younger and immature self came short of become ever more present. But, whereas our responses within those prior times left me with a life short of what I had now, it was also one, 'in its true lacking', that was either full of exhaustion, envy, longing, and otherwise inconstant satiety. And as such, we saw nothing beyond ourselves; for we were hooked on the goods we sought, or somber about their lacking. I simply have nothing now which I can say is worthy of an ideal, despite what I percieve as possible around me. In fact, my deepest concerns hold the notion of worth, and whether the ideals of our day are so as to feel guilt in lacking commission. Who is speaking of death, or physics in a right yet curious way. Who knows the world in a broad yet light way; and who views the world's people as anything more than something which is good, or bad by our shaky view. "Who is aware of the persistent mistake of attirbuting another's flaw or error to others, many even, or all?"
 I don't live in a world full of ideal flaws nor solutions. It is a natural place, with propogation from that which progresses everything. The details of texture and time are but what precedes and (presently) honors us; and to come is that which reflects our acts and habition.
 If there were no rebels or despots, we might easily accept the limits of earth, and how distribution ended up being unequal due to the amount of resources, rather than an allocators judgement.
So I reckon that our time is disposed to something of a similar sort as that of other people. Different objects sure, but within the same domain, that being the realm of pleasure.
 Where do we differ though? For I don't find much comparibility in our principles, nor how we think of such desire. It does not seem so that we merely like chocolate while the rest like vanilla.
 And it seems to me that it comes down to what pleasure is, namely, that it is a moment for which your body rewards you in feeling pleased, upon some sort of use or consumption. We may take it for granted that there could be any moments which weren't this. Or indeed, we find ourselves overly thin and frail without such things; and perhaps it is just our time and fortune that treats us to some form of pleasure rather frequently; and so the reality which is exposed upon a moment of lack, is in fact not given due credit as some prevailing problem, due to us having means to reach for what relieves us.
 Then there is peace, and all of what wisdom and history implore. Such a state is not stationed anywhere different than that in which you experience pleasure. Indeed, we wonder how pain or sensation is experienced; and we imagine it as we do vision, in that there is some sort of response; for which, ala Hume, our movement and or oblige is but part of this granularity; and in like manner, pleasure excites us in some assembly of granular ways, and pain likewise. This being so, peace, or the other kinds of calm and wholesome comfort, are but their own states of the body, of which pleasure and her vivid counterparts are merely such, 'more excitable'. Indeed, joy finds herself fit to acocmpany such moments; but the matter of our experience comes to frutiion. As there is more than moments of pleasure or calm, but foresignt and anticipation. Better put, our body is not really anything but a formation of assembled responses; some which we take for granted, such as gravity and the earthiness of our bones; to more minor things that give us surface dispositions, such as want of sugar --compared to our need to breath. As such, there is more around us than we take notice of; and so when we have sugar or get excited for something, are body does not become ignorant of the other things around.
 Our body is similar to a volume of water, and how small bits of toxins or cleansers get distributed throughout the whole. Peace is merely that state in which all the burdens and delights --as manifest in certain degress of lifting and or impinging ripples-- balance throughout the body.
 One hard to explain idea is that of self-regard, or principle rather; and how, one may have some part of them that is worked inside them, wherupon some other part of them experiencing overt fear or delight, that so does this other engraved part feel the move. This is why our pleasure from chocolate is repelled against, as there is part of us that loathes the coming grundge, as does the part of us which conjures foreisght become weary about our efficay in duties to come. Indeed, the worth of a principle ought be determined, as to merrit it being such a moulding factor. For indeed, it would be nice to indulge in chocolate, and then be surprised by what we experience later on; and it is another to know the latter, yet still act on the former, but thereby sour your taste for it based on our moral feeling of it. Indeed, the task of man, or at least a mark of one, 'in one way at least', is to reach for some sort of form, as to acquiese with the continuity of things, 'to which some objects hold only moments of good'. The struggle is in being to toutfully moral, 'as one would in wearing far too much protection on a bike, despite needing some no less'. Or, we have the experience of free delight, where futile choices are no longer made, and we are disposed to the principles which guide us through so; but then in error or novel lapse in standard, we realize even moreso the bad to come, and the good that's lost. "The more of a man we are, the harder it is to come back upon the slightest slip. Were so formed and perfect, that a single scratch makes us dramatically less so. Yet, to respond to this in a vapid manner, shows perhaps that your maturity has otherwise settled, but only quality rather than time; for if the latter were so, you'd be more than yourself in such maturity, whereupon such lack, you'd have more to lean on. But such is the process of bettering one's self."
So Amon looked at me today, as if she remembered me. It was not a look of surprise but familiarity. "As if I am the one she more or less sees, and responds to when we seem to be seeing her."
 I don't know what this means. And indeed, we may expect that if it is not fraternal or affection, that it never has been. But on that note, how it is now, which is generally with banter and playfullness, that even if this is not fliritng, so is it enough for me. We must remove this grotesque sense, where we objectify her, as if to make her pussy a goal. This is not what I rightfully seek.
 I admitt then, that we are in love, but not in a way that I can dispose to. That is it you see, love in all her attractive beauty. This is why we get married, as to committ this wonderful feeling. There is little peace though, less there is committment. "For which I will not change what constitutes our basis for relation, or aptness to do so, and by that warrant true connection. We ought not force our bond, as to bear us the feelings that motivate us."
 On that count, when she said hi to me, 'in which I thought that she was coming from a place that new I either liked her, or thought the way I treated her as being more or less so', that she was coming from a similar place. Yet, despite this, I do not want to be overly invovled. If it happens so be it, and if it doesn't, I don't want to be dissapointed, nor parched from our garulous want.
 Besides that, I get the impresson some time that were not being good to the people around us when we don't try to be with them more. There is no obvious reason why I shouldn't at any rate. But for one, they are not all on the same page, and thus you don't stand out so clearly. Also, I feel that this concern is rooted in what you get for other people, rather than them actually being upset with you. It's a manifestattion of you feelnig like your not on par with people; of which the dis-ease caused has you concieving others as being upset. In some sense, it's a way for your body, in it's want, to motivate you to change, 'for if you dont, they'll be upset'. If it is our satisfaction, I am inclined to be where it is good for me. This is perhaps a foundational question, and relates to Amon. In the same way that you have intent for free range, yet have a mould which says only as much as 'do what is right for the morn', that so may you have anticipation or resolve in the matter of her or friends, but with a mould that says that were the world, our being, our means for interest. These things are like other circumstances, coming and go, for which you may or may not ammend. But I hold myself to that true account, whereby it is we that were concerend with. We may over indugle in our standards, and be too tight towards what we deem contrary. But it is one thing to avoid Amon for you fear what your union would bring, and another to not pursue, via passion or some esteemed attempt. I care little for the suggestion that were letting ourself down. If were presented with oppertunity, we'll take it. But there is not much which is in line with our principle, for us to worry about pursuing or not.
 And so the point is, that if she comes or goes, say this or that, or you this and other, let it be, of that thing which is enough; that a moment where together you are, brings a cheer based on who we are. But lets not let our heart bind itself to her, and feel over the moon or under the weather for the quality of our efforts in that success. That is what I wish to avoid. But am willing to accept, reasonably and for sufficiney sake, what our ambilovence provides, or if the oppertunity is never so near for us to rightfully tend to. "She may still be far away, considering how we are; and I don't fear, nor humour our elation, of what could occur if we got close. For I inevtiably fall back to our heart, in being already whole and centered"
I came home today and thought, 'Amon may like me, I really do feel that. In her own way --as I don't much expect her to come up and profess that she likes me, if she did-- that she still had this very nice, flattered, yet somehow bonded hello with me. There were just words in it, asking me how I'm doing, like we weren't strangers'. But I've taken this to mean she does like me; even though when I came home I merely humoured the possibility, and would let our interest come to fruition, or find our conversations enough. Moreover, we gave up on our urges for her, 'which are in effect cementing your probability in getting with her; as the more I like her, the more chances there seems of succes. Wherease, if you don't like her as much, then you have nothing to count on but her liking you, which is ever so discrete, and rightfully so'. But thereupon us masterbating, we have less interest and are thud dissapointed. Again, she may not nor ever has liked you. But I believe it in my soul that there is some fond attraction; the simmerings from somewhere deep. But I cannot humour this, as easily that is, for this more obsucre sight of her, in which our wish to have her mirror my interests is lighter and easier to sit with, is gone.
There is nothing like having the energy to remark upon something in a whole manner of ways, from all sides, and not get lost, nor sidetracked.
 I am thinking of Amon, how she is, that I'd like to see her. Only though if she's keen to see me. Perhaps were making it up, as we have before; and realized even earlier in the year. But she seems different, in how she addresses me at least. My heart tells me it's true.
  She does address you differently, but only as a mark of familiarity, and more or less friendliness. It is not some preface to a bond. You were considering whether she liked you, and tied this to how she interacted with you. But upon her reaching this normal point, you think that this is now deep state she's in, even though she is just on familiar grounds. You were deeper already, and so her going to this appears this growth, but not to something which the excited change warrants. You feel this means something, it presumably does not
 There isn't a choice to make. We only need let things play out. Were not trying to fuck, but merely assay, prompt and or invite interest. But it may not be there. Indeed, I admitt we may be wrong; and beyond precadence, I simply cannot say now that we are able to see things clearly. But part of our change of attention in the recent weeks has been in how she addresses me. We don't really have time for conversation, but banter. It's usually about work, or cordial. But my response is to how she looks me in the eye, and says hello, firmly and deliberately it seems. I am brought back in, to how I felt before. I then thought she was married, for indeed the picture looked like her. I see her differently now, and the apparence may be but similar. It says she lives in Vancouver also; and indeed, there are many of the same name, which doesn't make her having someone look similar with the same name guarentee that. I moved on though, having accepted this. And my instinct now is that she wants to know, as do I. I see us doing a video chat, very simple, no pressure, but apt to kindle connection; where we learn about ourselves, our family's, future's, wants. From there, we may currate dates or meetups. Most of all, we can live with the other in mind, knowing that we care, and have someone they can melt into --a little at least.
So I had a fairly decent thought. For an age it seems, we've been more or less apolegtic about our choices and or place in life, due to how we became ordained upon our youth's end. Beyond explaining how this came to be, I must admitt that our current situation isn't overly propserous. We have no formal elducation and thus credit. By our age we have less time and reason to acquire this. Our lack of friendship opens us to elated views, 'which end up diverting us from more realistic ends --as we see them otherwise as flawed, but then give the reaslitic oppertunity no chance'. And in short, we are put in a place, where acting right amounts to labor, whereby our needs are met.
 This is the reality. And for many periods we held room and ease upon our parent's grace. And all seemed well here, as we were fine to let life be in those moments. But I realized that when we looked over the mountains, that even with our given home, that we still had limits. And this brought more attention to the position that was sure to come, and fairly so. But, in being at home, to be broadly confined yet locally free, meant that we had to upheave ourselves to be as such as to thrive in such localle. And this was never respected nor given address. I understand how, for we were meant to be on our own, take care of our way. "We did not need to adapt to being at home, for that is not where we were set. Our choices only brought us too and fro."
 I cannot say much on the matter, but my greatest dissent comes from the wearyness of philosophy, and how we rely on her to warrant our labors, and otherwise see them as promising and fine; yet, such an attitude, of indiference and appreciation, has no one but me to see; and when were tired, and hard bent to wear such cloth, the words of others, who see or speak to things differently, becomes oppressing. For we either admitt to it, or let it slide by, 'wherein our rooted feelings of philosphy get covered, and to a degree invalidated'; that, or we rebuke it, as to avoid this contrition, which is itself exhausting and hard to do. Indeed, when we think of the constitution, we may rest on the quality of given words, and how they don't speak to the fall of this. But this knoweldge isn't used in refutation, but in how we react, as not to care. Most things which throw me off are events and their mentioning; that, and the portrayal of something bigger than these events can speak to, or measure up to, yet each are compared as similar.
I may be reading too much into Amon. And indeed, let's say were not. Is it likely that she's fully endowed to, nothing else on her mnid? Likely not.
 The mistake we made in the past was letting our own excitement be mirroed onto others, who were not so. At least, as much as they were, it was not romantic.
 This may be so for Amon, and we might just be treating her in a different way. Let it come about if this is not so, and she does in fact have her mind on you. I really don't know. I would hate to overestimate the situation. And indeed, what I want is someone who sees me, our candor and hue that is. I know we don't have any other connection. Were not hooking up, nor are we the best or even moderate friends. It's all looks, from me mostly, and over time I feel like I am gettnig them back; but I may be noticing something that is not as I believe it.
 As with Simron, fret not if she forgets you, for she didn't care if that were so. She makes me smile, and I talk to her with a thump in my step. Recieving this is what brings her to the same, or she may feel weird; that, or she is merely amused, but not fraternal.
 It seems so hard for the less we try the less it seems that anything will happen. But there isn't some thing to happen, besides some imagined embrace. I accept this. But there is the matter of another, and how they feel, and that something just should be said. Because maybe we can go on a phone date, maybe we can fair some feeling to one another. This is not something we pursue though, rather it just happens, as we be ourselves.
I am physcially poor. In part, and most likely foremost, due to our time on the floor. Which I can speak to, in that my aim in life, or pursuit, is in that of leisure and craft. I find no recompence from the Gods, nor the state, nor folk around me. And so I nneed not glory and commends from this, 'in order to justify it or satisfy me'. It is in truth what I seek and best explains our joy, and the contrasting situation to those trhings and deeds which I find myself overt from.
 But such interests live and breathe off of your energy. And indeed, the benefits and or flavor of money and friends was not with me at the time when I leaned into our pursuit. And as such, we simply added more fuel to that fire, by doing the liberating thing of sitting on the floor. The issue though is that, considering the posture given and it's spring of breathe, that your affability with such pose can make you less able and willing to be accordingly. It comes down to the state of your muscles, and if there suttle yet firm enough to sit well but also with strenght. Now, the issue is not so much that you were stiff and thus sat uncomfortably, but that, per the aim of such pose, that you'd do something from it. But being so ill-bent and thus unnervedly sat, makes your motions evidently less pleasing and productive, but also impinging, janky, and to an end which leaves you feeling subject to having done such motions.
 This is why we feel good working out, as our muscles can rest easier, having worked. It is not the structure though that they bring, only their firmness. Indeed, it is yoga and her opening ways that bring us really strucutre; but it is running, pushups and squats that bring life to our muscles, and respiration at that. Yoga has more to mould from as a result.
 My thought is one of progress, as the reality is that in a state and your lasting condition that such heightners of vitality are not always able. As doing them from prior stiffness does not elevate you much; at least it brings you back donw. The ideal scneario is where you do a moderate amount of activity, then stretch often. It is seldom a good thing to do only the former.
 At heart, we must be active. And idneed, we always walk, but this doesn't faciliate much. We must do free range. This keeps us moving and light, for which our basis can seek and bear higher training. Such training may be in higher intensity free range, such as spots, or it may be in running; and each may incorporate drops of puhsups or squats; and as your solidity builds, you may concentrate on these. Squats usually come first, as your ability to drop and stand is naturally easier, then to plank, and drop and pushup from there.
I fair nothing better than from knowing myself. Our writing and ways serves me more than that of others. In their case I am relating, as to steer and or connect with who I already am.
 In my heart of hearts, I am looking at Amon, as was I with Vienna, Mikalea, and to an extent Bella. They just excite something in me which all can relate to, that feelnig of love and or luster. Acquiring this is troubling though, for you must walk the line of gentleness and patience, or go right in; which does not fit very well at work, and for people who are not so far-situated, as to give reason to try.
 I think it's important to remember that our feelings on the matter have become more cemented as we begin to think that Amon liked us. I felt this way back in the spring, before determining that she was married, and thus unavailable. I still looked at her from time to time; but indeed, as the summer approached, I had no recourse to look past her. In the recent months I have begun to notice her more; but with a genuine distance from that fraternal thought. It's worth noting that Amon strikes me in a charming way, and from this, where we just naturally was pleased to talk with her, I found out she was fantastic at picking; and some of our talking aorund this has brought more of a spark between us. Nevertheless, she has come back on my mind, and I see her more, but happily, and without concern for what it means, 'or that were missing some chance to greet her or tell her something'. I was just glad to share bits of time together, but with no prevailing expectation. Pardon my gratuity, but she became a object of our fantasty; for she was on my mind more, but again, happily, or at least as something which we could fraternize with for our own pleasure, but with no stake.
 And it is from this that we have perhaps procurred, but at any rate noticed, that she is more charming to me, or at least more clear and adorned in her greetings. That is all they are after all, and our chats about her productivity. Again, as she laughs and we lean in a little in your banter and charm, I merely enjoy the forray, but take no meaning of it, nor any concern. But it was the other day, by the LPs, when she had come, and I didn't recognize her, but then when I went to the LPs, and she passed by, there was just this connection, and I said morning, and she 'how are you', in a very meaningfull way. It seemed so at least, like we were looking right at eachother. I thought I saw her also looking over at me while she was staging and I was up near the 50s. And then I said good by.
 Now, I am having those thoughts, where I am right or wrong to say hello or goodby, and that we need her to see me, as not to forget. My sincere want, and what I deem right and appropriate, is not to ask her out, or figure out if she likes me. Were letting her go in some sense, and perhaps rightfully; for if she agreed to a phone call, or even some outing, she would not be mine, and we would not change. There is not problem with us saying our peace, being who we are. We merely like her, and wish to fraternize, or at least spend time together. We are not endowed to this. In truth, that phone call, is viewd by me as a date, and in turn stems from her confessing an interest in me. I feel too awkward otherwise to suggest anything; for why would it be anything else. I wouldn't ask Bella to hang out or go for dinner. In part because I don't want to. And indeed, our graphic thoughts, or those of a fraternal kind, are not what constitute an interest in someone. Yet, in being with any of them, I can't say I have any interest besides such fraternity. By that I mean connection, being known, which is itself not so fraternal. But in some sense, there's a deepening of our acquaitenceship. This does not seem so easy to provoke. Nor inherently of interest to other's. "Would you like to know more about me>?" I'd ask. And all this means is, 'who is your family, where are you from, what do you like to do, odd and common; your facorit things,values, hopes and aspirations'. There seems to be no one who I connect with in any real way, beyond peerdom that is, where any of this is known, or understand at least, and by that, drawing of us while we otherwise live apart.
 I can't state clearly the basis for our motives with Amon. She is a girl...so? I have sexual urges...is this wrong, misplaced for adorned wants? Were co-workers....would you see her differently if she wasn't? Being together as students is one thing, as you naturally have time to sit down and talk, and have some present comparability in which you can convene together. At work, were generally on our own, and only able to speak in the backroom. And it is not so easy when were coming and going, and our only real starting place is one of family, roots and value. Amon works grocery and I fridge; so there isn't always a common ground. Funnily enough, as I sit here writing, my heartfelt interest of her is off, and I can't really picture her; as much as I can, it is faint, and I am but a little impressed, and by that unengaged. As if I am not interested, and unconcerned. The harshest move then is in accpeting this, as is natural, and dealing with the conflict of, 'if i leave our thought of her and by that interest, we'll remain apart'.
There is a clear factor which remarks upon us, and be it one side or the other, so we are.
 And that is so, that of our want, our wish, the root of our will, 'for which our direction strives', that so may this be brought upon and anwsered to indiscriminately; to ends which fail upon their means rather than quality. What we want, as we breathe, is just sought for and ridden towards.
 There is another side though, namely that of determination, reflection, inquiry to. Which is all to say, that as you take note of your authorities, or the world around, or your self even whereupon whatever reason warrants success, that certain actions run contrary to said means; that in this, you more or less surround, admitting faults and instilling highs, adjusting and cementing, enjoyig and enduring. It's the sort of experience which one is aware of, and brought under scrutiny as the array of your experience proports. In the prior case, you have a want; and perhaps there is reason to permitt this, so long as your able. "Ride the wave if it is high; for it being not is all that keeps you from doing your thing." But on the other side, we do indeed experience something, and let it's qualities run through us, but we a) take note of such effects, and how the impetus for them is to be assessed in producing such ends, and b) we have some sort of sense prior and through the expeirence; for instance, during inebriation, we may naturally be unimpeded in our want, yet we stil hold some sort of sense, 'as if a marshal aside our task'.
 As in all things, the truth is much different than knowledge, which is in effect some part or factor of which we particularly judge. Yet, a harmony of insights creates the truth, for all is contended. "Truth is what remarks 'I', and thus the aim of our inquiry. We are made of much though, and thus our truth is such composition." I say this foremost, for in such truth, our actual life lays: in all it's complexity. Yet, individual strands or portions may live more indepentantly as the base is whole and sturdy. This is why the impetus for friendship needs no monitory or worry, 'and your free to head right in'. Yet, in our doubts --about other things-- we may find ourselves less open or ready to so willingly proceed. Indeed, some situations call on our reason to forray the risk at hand; or we may simply be too conlficted and or held by our deeper uneveness.
Similar to real physics, which are relational and not based on some principal, so our thought compares, in that it is a mere relation of notions and ideas, 'the act of which is thinking'; no different than two particles moving in relation to one another, that motion is merely this relation.
 My thought then, or one at least, is of our notice of effort, and how it is brought to ease upon certain actions, and drowned by others. We see our efforts as nomrally and in some sense absolutely neccessary. It has been a whole thing-a-mobab living with our parents, and existing as though we were with such freedoms, but not so reliably; as our parents rightfully saw me going on my own, as did I. Their reason may be paramount, because I don't mind living at home, but only wished to leave as our parent's did not let us live as though we weren't meant to go; for which I often argued against, but now accept. Living at home, as we currently do, is at the very least viable if we live in a way that reflects there basic want; which is that I work, and a fair amount --rather than what is purely practical for us while were living at home.
 At work we are amidst people who don't really know who I am, and as a result, we feel seperate from the warmth they'd bring, and compressed about our own concerns that they are unaware of. Indeed, much cynicism has come from this, as we feel others are an impingment upon me. "It is them being there yet unbonded, like a free floating particle; whereas, if I was alone, I would more or less be fine. Indeed, the positive effect is good, but the abscence of negative is similarly nice." The main issue is that in regards to food and fitness, music and life values, we are an enigma; and we imagine ourselves as being so, which infurriates our seperation. No one really cares, which itself rubs up against our want of folk. For indeed, as we mentioned above, that we may have this unbridled impulse; yet it so for we are so detached nor enamured with anyone, or anything sometimes; and as such, were overly prone to find attachment. But were still afar from one another, and all because a) I permitt the reality of work and it's required labor; but thereby our prosperous address, and b) in relation to this, but on it's own also, we strive and oblige our care for leisure --which is games and the outdoors-- and craft --which is more along the lines of study but industry also, 'in a word, art, or some structured digestion'; that for each of these, in like manner to our prosperous oblige to work, wherein we lower the load but also hold whatever weight there is, that so do we also approach our peace and joy, as to treat them like memories, 'engravied in, warming us through the coldest of days', but also in raising their flame, as to heat ourselves up through the actual effort and activity.
So we did it, we saw the error in our way. We desire Amon, she makes me eroused and ripe with excitement. But I do not want her. The difference being one of feelings, the other of like. And I thought the other day that there was an avenue to see eachother, and act on our feelings. But I believe we have overestimated her feelings, and underestimated how much we care about one another. It is really just the feelings were moving from, yet there is not much else.
 And so be it, for what we want is our workplace to run smoothly, and our time aruond to be fufilling and commiserate. Everyone is either good or indifferent. There is not much which is negative. And I realize that when we remove our wants from other peoples care or view of us, we don't mind looking the other way; which removes this trepidation and unwanted feelings. With both Andy and Bella today, we just came and went, and were not afraid so; and our exchanges were all the btter. With Bella in particular, who I for a time felt off about. Now were pretty much friends, co-workers no doubt. And I just let it by. For we are at work, and seeing as our life surrounds it, so is she and many more part of that.
 When it comes to Amon, you may become friends with her, but our yearning for her should neither guide this nor set expecation. Were not trying to pick her up; nor are we indifferent to the fact that were primarily co-workers.
So I can't sit here and say that Im not a little greived.
 On one hand, we have the insight that were not doing what is needed to let our feelings flow. Were not becoming friends with people, or anything more than infatuated. At least, the way that we are around people doesn't warrant the infatuation. I'd even admitt that if someone felt that way about me, that I'd feel the same, uncomparable. I imagine though someone who was really into you, that if they really were full of energy, that I'd be put off based on how well I liked them. If I knew them more or we got along in some other way, then I wouldn't mind. There is something repelling about overly enthusiastic people. I appreciate it, but there needs to be something more.
 I can't help but feel a pang then, for I don't have this with Amon, and I can't exactly say I have genuine care to. My concerns are of rising above work and commiserating my time and deeds around this to be joyful but proper as well. I mean for work to be a place where I can be ourselves, open, but also in a mere friendly manner. Worrying about others is what drives me far away or twoards them. Let's just be.
 But I am cravinga little, and our enthusiasm prior, and as often experienced, leaves me feeling without. This is the impetus for our dissapointment, because there really was something I wanted. I just feel like we've imagined the procurment of this all; similar to how you are brought to view things via a lens of porn when you've watched it. It sparks you to view certain things. And because of our want of some friend, and or being intimate at that, we get over elated. And indeed, no one is thwarting you for this, but your only pleasing yourself. I just don't know how to make friends. And it is clear that something is off, because we don't actually feel great about asking her out, or anything along those lines. That is the ember that creates a relationship; you go out, do things together, learn about one another: a date. Yet I feel situationally and a little emotionally unable to do this with Amon, or Bella for that matter. Yet I don't see anyone else. Indeed, we wish for her to see us, as it rises above the weirdness I feel in thinking about or acting upon our feeling. But it is not likely too come. At least, I don't see it coming out on pure grounds, but by some more material matter. And it may just be the workplace; or we are too distant in many rights, that we don't normally ask people such things --for which it would be easier to ask her and have her say know. Doing so means too much of us, yet I don't have any real or genuine thing to invite them too. So I believe they like me as to make this a non-issue.
 Amon looked at me the other day and I took it to mean she had some affection for me. An unspoken interest. Yet, I realize that we are not much of friends, and that in forgoing that, whatever interest or affection we have for one another has no-where to go. I am paralyzed by the notion of asking her out; as I feel so weird and odd to indicate we have any real interest. The hardest thing is when there is banter, or her prescence, 'which I so very like', and knowing that she doesn't feel as strong as I do, or anything of that matter --beyond our workplace friendliness, as I do with Bella. It is one thing to point out that our care in life just poitns us away from such things, and so of course it is not naturally easy. But, of this overstep, in outlook at least, the insight is one of how we form something with somebody, even somebody you don't like. Indeed, it may merely be about having things in common, rather than chemistry. "The fire needs woods to burn on." Indeed, currated means for dating may afford us that; at least there'd be a place where the context was one of dating. But I am not so confident.
I wsa thinking about this this morning; namely of Ahsley and Sam.
 In the case of Ash, we had some familiarity with one another through our junior years. She even hugged me in grade 9 --though we did not know eachother. In grade 11 though, after the winter when we were hanging out with Shauna, she started seeing us at lunch, when we'd all sit together. We became more or less acquiantences. Then, early in April, she texted me --out of the blue I believe. We message here and there for a little bit. And then on Shauna's birthday, around the end of April, we sat together during the party. There was in my view a sliver of feeling towards one another, but nothing clear or deliberate. We were just on the outskirts of becoming more than friends. Oddly enough, it was two weeks later than we started dating. First though, I entered a crisis, where I felt similar to how I do now, that I was so worried that she did not like me. I recall me, her, Colin and Nadia, were at Colin's house, and as we were upstairds, I became this mope. For whatever reason I do this; it's like the silent treatment, but a 'hey, tell me you like me'.
 In the case of Sam, I only really noticed her when I moved to PMS, and saw that she was dating Scott --we were all in the same english class. We did coach together though; I can't ever recall talking. After we really met, I had two parties, one in the second week of may, the other at the end of junne. That meeting of ours was in the later part of April, and I went with Seb to Sara Lauchances house, by Taylor Wynns' --we smoked a pipe on her front poarch. Anyway, I had an alright time, but found myself in the lower basement, on a couch, talking to Sam. For whatever reason this started the spark between us. Her and Scott had broken up a little while earlier. I believe Seb had some feeling for Sam prior, but no matter. Me and him walked her home that night, and she messaged me on twitter the monring after. My gosh, I am such a kiddy fool; as I rememebr noting this to Foster, as if 'hey, look, Sam messaged me'. It wasn't as though we were doing anything, rather she merely spoke to me. Such a small thing. Relates perhaps to what we'd expect, considering how little we put in. No matter, we became on more speaking terms during soccer. And on our first party, she came, slept over; for a moment we just sat there, then cuddled. It was so nice, and I was fine with that. In an odd sense, if I knew the situation would never change, I was fine with what is. We started going to soccer together, went to Grad together --though I recall being somewhat seperate from her, and again, a little mopy, which she even noticed. We spent alot of time at the after-grad though, which Scott noticed; in his view I was following her around. Weirdly, I never tell people what i want, or what they should do. It is not so much that I lack confidence, and thus feel like I don't deserve it, but dont pretend that I am doing enough to make such requests. In a way, your interested in me, and I am happy your here; but your not my play thing; your not that interested. My immaturity is in perhaps not taking the situation serioulsy enoguh, and failing to realize what I have, what it requires, and seize that. I will note, that after our sleepover we did go on an actual date. And oddly, following the date, she invited me over (after dropping her off) to watcha move, and we cuddled. But prior, after eating dinner, we went down to Bonson, and sat on the bench. And there, I would not hold her hand. I don't mean that I was literally waiting for her to do that. But in an odd way, despite wanting to, I felt it was wrong; like I was putting myself into a place I wanted, but wasn't warranted considering out situation. She was a perosn who's place with me was meaningfull. We weren't merely escalating to some sexy end. We were with one another. In an odd sense, let's say it was wrong to imagine or at any rate ask for head. I felt no difference in taking her hand. We weren't together in my mind, enough at least.
 One frail thought is that of our want and offering. With Ashley, we almost felt like it was natural that we'd have sex and she'd suck my dick; but it was mere formaility that it seeded would provide this, rather than her wanting to, or me asking in real way, compared to feeling like she would just offer. When we fooled around a year or so later, she asked me to go down on her, and I steered away.
When we broke it off with Ashley, we went to college, and initially thrived. The new routine and focus felt great, and I was settling in. But near middterms I fell into a deep depression, centering on my true lack of ambiution; and the conflict of having to work hard --considernig the college challenge, and stakes at hand-- yet I had no real motive or care. Realizing this had me feeling helpless, as though I were apart of plan which I cared not about, and thus felt no drive; and in some ways, experiecned the amount of success that this composure brings.
 I have a hard time reflecting on the fall of that year, as we were very much like we are today, and the man to come; but the details were different. We studied not the Gods or ourselves, but nutrition; and we wrote not about our views, but Sums. We had no plan for ambition, rewards or even content; but rather accepted our place, at home, with no sense of a future to come. It was as though we were a child, free to do as they please; yet with little friends nor dessert, merely what we determined to be good. I don't know why we decided to go back to college, as I wasn't feeling ill; but I guess I remember that during the end of the year I felt a confidence, a desire if you will, as though I were able to do something, and this excited me. We decided to go, and with more sensibility towards our aim. I recall speaking to several career advisors, asking them what I should do. I determined a path which was more or less senseless; and truly so, for I left college. My aim was to get two degrees in nutrition and kinesiology. I did not consider the load of this, as I had nothing but a clear run to achieve this. The gauge seemed to be on our education, and little else was considered. This did not hold though, but to an opposite avail as the year prior. In that case I felt helpess, and aware of what we lacked as we came to the first tests of our semester. Whereas in this, I had all this youthfull ambition, but it simmered away, and I made a clear decisions to recede.
 One note worth mentioning was that there was a guy in my Bio class, who I sat beside and had some repor with; he wished to text me and talk about health, but I never did. And I felt as though he were put off by this. In some sense, a poor foundation removes one from an otherwise good condition; as we just didn't share too much sense. At least, the sense I had was all my own. A guy from english asked me to run, which I did; but never again. And I made a friend in criticial thinking; and I went to his cmoplex and worked out; but never again. And similar to the first guy, I felt he was put off, for when I saw him some year or two later, he was not pleased to see me. In that english class there was a scenario that our time in Vancouver compared with, as we were not on a first name basis with everyone, and so certain people would come over and talk, and we would not very much partake, as we didn't know them. I just sat there solid, yet alone; or at least a littler weridly seperate from my otherwsie friend, who was talking with the people who had come over. This is how me and Colin were, and Kyle at Save On --when we went bowlnig, and I just stood there. Byron was more or less similar, or Ben rather, and Joey to an extent at Superstore, Mike at Personal Training, and Tristan now. I just latch onto people, perhaps because a soul person is all I really want; at least, what I want, can only really be found with one person; as I seek that commradery with another person, who I can share with, laugh with, reflect. When we hung out with Shawna and Nadia, my first instinct was no, as I simply did not want to be with them. In Spanish I recall, that we were great friends with Silvester and Shauna, but were more or less ignorant of Nadia. We just didn't have that relationship. At Save On, we were friends with Kyle and later on Seb, and Adara, but not Kelly.
 My thought is that this selective process, or at least occurence of friends, remarked what I thought of people otherwise; that being with them somehow felt harder, and in commision with a life which was more difficult, and for aims that I was not concerned with. There is this buisness of altering yourself as to be more pleasing, amiable and or effective around other people, and work pursuit. Neither of these things felt deisrable too me, considering the work required, and the conflict in being otherwise jovial and mannered. That is our story indeed. And I ask myself whether the result is properly so, or if we should have changed our wants; obliged them at least as the prompt to see them and expeirence them came out. We just found a better and riper way to be ourselves. And my point, is that upon the nestling of this and solidifying into our essence, that we bore this confidence and thus belief about how others saw me; that we were great, and our destiny was on high, 'if those at work, or in the world, saw who I really am'. I feel we've been taken from this, and only progressed our path, 'in being a man whose care is deearly themselves', when such wishes and whims faultered. We've had no success, in women, money, progress, recognition. It is so hard, for we carry so much with us, that there is seldom a natural fit. Yet the basic skills help us, in finding jobs, houses, in building things, emailing people even. These things all our fundemental activity to move further, and not lie confused and idle, 'and thereby liable to become inflammed and over-expressed'. Such skills though are there for you to 'meet the demands' of any given scenario: to be so tall to ride. It merely sits you at a table, eligble for potential. But it does not mean we pursue, only that if we say the right words, or attempt something in due course, that a result may come our way. Be it a friend, a charmer, a want, ask, or compliment even from some other, great. But we are merely growing, learning, at last as our time cares for, to earn something from us acting well. It affords much utility; and allows for things that our harsher road does not easily afford. "Imagine if you could not speak at all, and all that would be there with such basics, and otherwise not."
We are not so simple, as to be well formed or satisifed, and by that count, free to pursue whatever tickles my pickle, on a whim, by no measure but what draws my will. We have a mature angle, a habition to the root of our way, and the discretion and principle to recongie when the way is off and need of re-station, and by that propensity --as to situate and continue.
 It actually is quite simple, for you see, if we met someoneo and had all our eggs in the basket, then we'd act in a way that, given our interest, we'd confer, ordain, arrange and promulgate. But as it were, our eggs are always falling out, and so we make no such procurement.
 As it were though, this 'falling out' has been a part of our entire adult life; and because of that, we've never landed on a scenario, or skilled communication, as to procure much; and as such, lacking a prime directive, means that we have this impetuous want, 'as to bear ourselves to some direction, to give us and clarity'. And the positions that do this are those which bring excitment. In our better strides, we look for refined adjustments, and words to accompany it. "Bear my a finer truth along our path to being serene: may me further the route that's seen us go, merely better." When were off though, 'which has a escalting likelihood upno our core dissolve, since we lack the support around', that things which elate us pull us ever more. "Were so susceptible upon our lack, that a little prick of change brings us farther forward then we really need; were just too tempted based on our brazenly parched state: the years of maturing have left me with nothing else: and as I say, 'having only such, you feel it tirelessy so the moment your not'."
 I have a taste for woman as I do chocolate: an acquired taste. And as my growing stride still seeks clarity and aboundment, so do we wish for and move towards accompanying pleasure. "Guards so to speak, or softeners, whereupon our firm release, were not squigled into relative flatness." Making friends is the cornerstone of this, and being fraternal at that. Each seems weird in it's excitment, for I don't often find myself interested in one, nor with much but enamure through the other. I seldom want such things, for the folk at hand strike me not. "They appear like chocolate when they do seem pleasing at all; and by that, display their brightest side. I seldom find comparibility and by that gentle want, in terms of their common core."
As the prevailing thought in our mind, Amon has changed for me. Prior, she was like Holly; someone I was excited to be with, and confident wanted to be with me. In a similar vain, as I doubted her want, I rushed forward. I felt yesterday that my very whim to do so was not well recieved.
 As of today though, I realize that a) there is some sort of friendship or bond, and that b) my priority is only to further this.
 For most of these things, sex always motivates me. "At long last," I find myself saying. But we miss the mark. I understand the excitement, but it is born from a un-set principle, or directive rathber. One that we indeed have, in leanings at least; and as such, are not souly bent in forming myself for the sake of love, venturing and fame. We may still currate something though, as with all things. And for me, skill comes into question when interactisn are had. You ought not force them though. Let her by, and make no large remark as you pass.
The challenge in all of this is in our determination to live via our own directive, and not that of baser want. Of course we want Amon, as we do chocolate, money and touring. But these things not only take their own efforts, but in that, afford less effort for nearer and dearer pursuits; and in many ways they run contrary.
 One element to our approach is in possibility. For she may leave, or die, or turn out to have a boyfirend or husbamd, or find one at that. Or she may just not be interested, or put off even. But time does fly; and if you recall, when we took our mind off her in the spring, that was 8 or 9 months ago. One tact is to allow yourself to go, and her at that. Missing a day is no matter, more will come. Nor is any moment an oppertunity, but merely a moment that draws on us naturally, to say hellow, inquire as to how she is, share how we are and so forth. My feeling is she is just there to work, do her job and that's it. No gossip, no fuss; I don't sense she has much complaints. Or if she does, it's like I, in that we care about the kind of work that were doing, not that were working at all. The trick is in letting yourself be as though your fine without, or that nothing may come. We truly find in her something which brings me light. Like Simron, she is hard working, well meaning, but with a good sense of humour. I see right into it, and it's why I have her in view. She compells me like no other. And indeed, as I remove our sheen of sexual desire, 'for which I realize how far apart we are', I can actually take delight in her little giggle, or her this and that. These don't exilerate me, but they shouldn't. Were not here for thrills, nor do we pretend that we have much to procure that. As far as I'm concerned, my heart is there, and I care. But this needs no stirring or compulsion to indemn. Let it go where you may be as such, and worry not if such a moment doesn't come, at one point or forever
I don't wish to dwell on this, as I feel there is no genuine consolation, but to lean away from the pleasing idea of having her, or hearing her say she's interested. It's what we all want; but now that it seems plausible, 'and more or less likely by our own enthusiasm', the reality of the scenario feels even worse, and thus embers of envy, shame, dissapointment and haze come in.
 I think an important note is that of the future, and how a fair outlook is not so for there is much on offer ahead, but rather we are progresing ably, and solid enough to far proceed. That's the good you see, 'that I move on'. And it is nice when there is good acompanying it. The right gratitude here is who we are in contact with at work: Bella, Andy, Carry, Carly, Teresa, Andre, Lenae, Dorthi, Amon, Joey, Tristan, Alex, Sam, Hazzle, Rohan, Paul and so on. These folk are like heaters amidst the cold of our indebted way. Such a way is grooved upon us; and so it's nice for them to be there.
 I think part of the confusion comes from titilations that bring your sight beyond this path. Again, the strength lies in our steadyness on the path; while titilations are those which bring your site pass this. I don't wish for Amon to be that. Rather, let us tread our route, caring for all that shares the way. There is a youth in us, a wanting glow, which in our oblige and sense, stays more or less close to us. The imagery and dreams which come to mind, 'to promise us or please us' are not worth our time, nor fretting over. Instead, let that glow brighten as you make your place known, 'along the actual path'.
It is one thing to hear from her directly that she does not like me, and another to consider it myself.
 I ask myself this one novel rule: has she shown interest... and the reality is no.
 I am a bit of an anomoly when it comes to things. I am terrifically kind but confident also. I say nothing I don't wish to, mould to no one for some unclear sake; and I spend my time and word in no ill-found way.
 Because of this, we partake in other people at full tilt; even if the substance is irregularly soft; sometimes awkward and or faint, but not full on in a heated and or rude way.
 My experience has been that we act as such, and thus earn kindness from others. We may overestimate this as something which is otherwise normal.
I am not entirely on point with this thought; and usually I surround it with occurences and or impetus' for such thought, to bring it out. And I don't wish to be so reductive; as this overshadows the feelings which were are mostly commenting on.
 I think for our recent life we've been under the assumption that the desires and dreams that sputtered around would come true. This is so, that in our state which rose from further in, and had this inmitigated output, 'as little resisted us, considering that our effort came where there was no wind', that so did far off delights feel ever so faint, and by that akin to a whim. That doesn't explain it well; but I mean to say that in our process of being so sound and true, that there were moments upon our lapse in this, or uneveness rather, where wishes and want of garulous things came to be. And for years we felt these would come true. In an inverse sense there were similar doubts and or contentions which we felt would be overcome, 'through some justifying matter'. The clear point I'd make now, is that we have no worries, but are also not of the mind that our wants will come pass; so much as we are ourself. "As long as we are a person that treats people with mere indifference, and at most offers them humour and kindness, but not active interest, so will our connections be few. And in general, as long as we are a person who reaches into Montainge, and feels ripe promise in finding out more, that what this is indicative of, in how we are, is in term a consquence towards you neither becoming rich nor with easy love."
 I will note that in our frequent content, that we are more or less so but on a path that sees these wants coming to fruition. "As if we are fine and content, but similar to work and how it is but some route were on, and thus the good is that which makes our movment feel fine, that so does our general future, viewed from our content, still have this wants dangled ahead, as though our place in being so content is not one in which the good is measured by us continuing that, but rather something which absolves us from concern, and sees our efforts moving ahead and towards said wants." In having this view, when we were not so contnet, we held this aversion from media, and news in particular. "It was as if we had this air filled foresight, of which incoming broadcasts rubbed against. And this infuriated our otherwise excitment at what was to come." Now, I see how hard it is to accpet that our life does in fact bring us a certain way. But it is not one in which we must concede those wants for some other resolve; and foremost because were not weighed upon by broadcasts, and there pressure for us not be both content but also imaginative. Instead, I am letting go of our imagination, 'and not letting our natural confidence extend but to anything which we are able and likely to do'. What's developed though, is the process view of the world, and how all around there are tiers of activity, and as much as humanity and state are invovled, there are complexes of processes, each weighed and angled a little differently for each level. So when someone says China is a big threat, or bears rhetoric about Trump and America, there is no real concern of mine, for I abide the prescence of processes. For bad things to happen, effects have to run through the tier gambit. Moreover, it is right to view each tier as both fine and zoned. For effects can occur that merely require more effort at a certain angle, while larger shift requires a change of form. The point being, for instance, that for the indictmet to war to even occur, there are several processions in place; if this progresses further down, there is still a level of due process, where the potential for issues has been surpassed, and were at the point in which a real conflict is being decided; for which their own processes are in play. And much of the trauma at hand comes about beyond this, but still in ways that reflect the operations that follow; and the early points of this are more local. "It is a long and decalate road for the world to end. And I fear not but those thoughts are o,bied with fallacy."
 I guess our point of inquiry is one that beckons to the reality of how were living, and whether philosophy, leisure and craft, the outdoors and light deeds are fair enough to afford you a) a natural content and thereby potential progression, and b) dearly satisfying as not to feel poor and lacking without make-up dreams, and the according wear that comes from fending off the belidgerent excess; namely, of dreams and their wanting nature, that the twisted accompanyments of this being less so, yet birthed upon that genuine part of you, which in however missing, holds dreams according to that excess, but thereby an according amount of twists. It is in many ways easier to be fool who epxeriences there folly in one foul burst, 'whereupon there misguidence results in objective infraction'. Commpared to someone who was nearly perfect, but in being less so, and in some sense naieve to let their passion seem credible, that the anxious and or insecure sides of such passion bear there source and matter from your core, like your dreams. In a way, our dreams bear credibility but our confidence, 'where we overstimate ourselves as to give warrant to the dreams likely happening; but in equal ways, our grieving passions take hold of me in equally rooted ways'. The parts they cling onto though are of the parts of me which are unknown, 'the uncapped ends so to speak'. And to study the world, in it's past and yambering present, create's opemings for your otherwise learning and experience to be taken hold of. "Watching movies with killers and Vampies has this effect; whereby the prescence of these, which in a better state sit as flat and unsubstantive things, get turned into real figures upon your rithing passion."
All my faults come to light when our shortcomings in life are noticed. "for what else would signal our wrong."
 Yet, our life has not come about in begrudgement of our perhaps ardent principle. Were not a devout man, suffering over his noble good. Rather, we held the basic tenants of joy and high-regard through our childhood and youth; and merely embellished these in a more bountiful way.
 It is worthwhile to note that our youth peetered off, as perhaps those remnants of joy gave way for the pleasure in others. But we had no care or course for success; and indeed, happened upon other's due to the consistent prescence of school; but we did not much earn it, or maintain it as if it were apt to be scarce. And as such, we more or less slipped, and lost what we began to feel of worth. I simply could not re-accelerate from here, and any notion of time ahead, or pleasures now that we had grown to known, were too far off in what we could manage to acquire; and in relation to what our natural bend was willing to provide, or at least direct ourselves toward. And so we naturally turned back, feeling the cold of being so without. "Circusmtance had provided a warmth; and indeed, so had it emptied out our inner heat, to which these new goods were desired. But as such, when these new goods went away, we had not much the skill or warmth around, to re-adjust, or appreciate what we had left. Everyone literally left. Colin in Spirit, Silvester almost entirely --though his spirit stayed-- Ahsley through great tumult, and Shauna in iconic ceremony."
 It may be wortwhile to note that when we had nothing left, we found worth in tending to our school: both in study and the future. Something about this gave life to elements that, per there floating place amidst our future view, so they came to be. We saw nothing otherwise, and this brought a new flame, a draw if you will. Oddly though, prior to all this happening, and indeed before that, we had no such sight. In fact, we deliberately ignored it. Indeed, our time with new friends fell away for we still lived as we did prior, only with new things going on. In some sense, seeing as I did made me not want friends, as I felt uncompelled or averse to their means for connection. As we aged, the ability for us to ignore such things became less so, and we were more open to other things. "We lost some of our pretense, and thus motive to remove oruselves from others. With this gone, we gave it a try." We merely enjoyed new pleasure though, and perhaps took for granted how we were suppose to be. The pleasure left, but we were not in any better way able to be ignorantly stout. Life just felt dark and stale. And so thinking of our future in a career sense brought life.
 Our tale begins though when the commitment to this faded away. I'd best describe it as a true and faithful process that admitted the seperation of yours and your only self. As if we were giving in to some self-conciet, for the sake of, and with neccessity for, some outside thing, of which we were oppertune but also in need to oblige. Upon our breakup, looking for something, and of the sort which was highly acclaimed, was calming and assuring. But as our grief lifted, we realized a) that to such mentioned ends we were not committed, and b) foresmot, we were aware that we were giving up ourselves, or better yet, had given up ourselves; or at any rate, we saw that we had no self. In some way this seemed so out of right, 'as if tradition had opted me into due course, yet I witheld'. It was also so out of temper and finesse; for in truth our 'witholing' from the steps that one would otherwise take, 'through puberty we'll say, and our otherwise sustaintment of the free and wild joy that would then wilted down', kept me for a time so personally endowed, but with little else upon the pressing need to do so; and so upon the culmination in college, there was not only a frame of sentiment which had me feeling off, but one of feeling, 'and care at least, which stemmed from the recesses of my heart'. Recesses mind you which were not absent or tucked away, just flatter than I once had them.
The divets around me, filled with hope and need from folk and county, where is life without this? I see plainly that were filling in for something. But an animal is more or less eating grub or reacting, and man is not so different; only, he has filled divets, or habition or tradition, which softly currate his way: rules so to speak.
 But were wild and willing, sensible enough to see our right and ability within labor, and with the affability to rise above it's burden as to make it a mere deed: and soliders have been hit by shaprnel and stayed calmer than I often have at the sight of a short shift: my bones are with equal potential to bear whatever load there is.
 Indeed, igrnoance is no small feet here. As when I was younger, and through highschool especially, I did well by our natural means to; and as much as I did not, I only fretted over the embarrasment and scrutiny of the act of not doing well. In the bacground say those prestigous and or parent-drive rules of a career ahead; but this was never some constuctive reason given, merely a customary and or fictutous assumption. Without this though, we merely pittered along; avoiding what we felt like would be poor results, in how other's saw me, rather than being ill-fit for what they prompted me to do well for. Life was just never that hard nor our foresight so beckoned to any concern.
 We never made a choice though, and from those pressures me doing so became less permitted. Moreover, the reality was that our dependance seemed forever, as what were we to do otherwise. There was indeed some actionable merrit to the proposal of earnign a career, if only because it was a more lucrative form of the basic principle of living on our own. Prior, the future always felt like this thing which was comnig. And indeed, I never expected we would not enter it per the norm; but I considered we'd do so when we got there. And so most of my time was spent just balancing the moment. No real drive or intent. And due to our situation at the time, the thought of further ploding along felt more than I could handle, or desired at that.
 It is weird though that I did enter a certain state. For I pittered, fell, then amended for school; which I mustered only little, and fell even harder in seeing our true self. We then hobbled together, and brought everything toward me which felt important and meaningfull, namely the cultivation and habit of nutrition, and our novel and or embraced leisure and study. As it were, the motive for 'the future' as it was given in our youth, and what I see now, was on that basic premise of independance. But during this 'weird period', that form drifted away, and my thought and care for leisure and craft became embossomed upno some faint and unboned frame. There was no sense but that for my life to come, I'd stay home, and live right and well as it was for the activity I deemed fair. This is the birht of our discretion around activity, and how doing some of an esteemed or fair-worthy kind, brought me a clear sense of value.
 In truth my life has not changed. One major development is in the haze of this early process, and it was not by our own view proper, nor dearly desired, too live at home indeterminately. At least in that future-absent sense. And so we got a job, grew able to speak to people, and well. And then moved out, lived on our own and handled ourself. But we fell quite discontent from this. Indeed, our reality was one of work and independance, and the former was rough. But despite it being more less soft than our oppeprtunites prior could have created, it was only that which rubbed against me, rather than the pecuniary side. Moreover, I just dreaded going to work, and felt it got in the way of our health and enjoyment. We went through several periods where we bent our time towards work, and allowed for pleasures that were commiserate with that. This only made work harder though; and perhaps we weren't willing to depart from our dearer cares; or it just might have been so much time doing so, that we had nothing to really heighten our pleausre, namely in friends. At least an abundnace of them, or anything which warranted us living in garulity, and away from our true leisure, and in avoidence of work's rougher sides. It seems a pattern that we just cannot pick ourselves up, to forray into the lands of recreation and friends, and procure something meaningfull that becomes satisfying and warranting on it's own.
 I did not see a route for this, nor held deep desire. I still don't. For the frame in which even the most elaborate of fun provides, 'with the most lucrative jobs and social excursions', speaks only to certain items of fruit within that forest, rather than the puff of all the trees. "For the wind tickles all of them, as does the sun cast upon all their heights." Even with our trunks I care for this more than carnival life that people lived. Even the general fun and pleasantry that people's normal work-day and social life gave, was not worth it, for the joys of leisure and craft were something that had more breadth, even in their mature state. "Getting muddy and eating junk more or less." At any rate, we truly amended to our life being so, at least the abundance which our self-regard would provide.
 And oddly, since that point, 'where I wanted to move outside, for I sought nothing else', has treated me to words and ideas, days and mornings, evenings and sunsets, that have never seldom been anything but tot, of might, reasonable and firm, determined, tender as one would be while flourishing but also strong as one with passion and care; and beyond routinely, 'as if a season or two this was so', it has been consistent, in that upon a days lapse, or a week or twos slow, the movement pipcked up, and with higher yet easier paces. From the summer of 18 to now this has been so: 7 years!
 Rather than mention our lack of friends and our naieve ways we expect them; and the smattered conceptions that lead me to see this changing, 'as if one day we'll be so as to seep this', that I would prefer to say what I currently, and in many ways always have. For one, I live in Canada, which means that moderate employment is accessible, as are dental, optic and medical needs given. The outdoors are set for our sight and time; as do the roads allow for vehicular travel. "The age were in has sunk in, to where electronic and thereby better pitched variants are viable: for a monk like me." Our work houses some of my closest people: Andy, Bella, Amno, the memory of Simron, Tej, Matt and Ben. Were filled with commradery as we trel there.
