So our yoga platform is in and working. A nice thing is that if we make a mistake, or we wish to add a part, or adjust, we just change the time settings. There is no need for exactness. I haven't used the skip feature much; but it does allow me to add end parts of videos. Brainpower is our most used one. Not sure why we don't do rinse more; we did in the beginning, but it seems long now. It starts and ends with your toes, and I appreciate the arm work from brainpower. Root to rise is also nice and simple. Standing in general is great. Squat can be changed to just go from the first one to back. Fresh monr has not been done yet but it is a great seated piece; and upright, which is suitably attached, is itself really good as you stretch your hips alot. Arm pull is fine, but has too little epmhasis on our neck. That is what we need. Some more arrangments of recovery would be nice also, namely those popular ones where shes seated, folds, then does childs pose. I find that a soul video flows better, and has a vibe. Also, removing edits allows it to sink in more and for you to breathe.
So I wonder if our troubles can be traced back to anything, or if this is just some natural predicament. I ask, because I fear that our attempt to do more exercises will prove futile, and perhaps leave us worse off.
 The truth is that we have sat on the floor and worked out before. And indeed, amidst our troubles, we've done it and felt great. Indeed, I would summarize it as a 'stepping out of line', whereby upon exericsing, we do what we should not, or upon not exercising, we do what we should not. In the latter case, not doing anything makes everything you do a little worse. And worst is when you train you are limited by your ability; which our insceurities can guide us out of line. In the case of exercising well, there is a drifting line, whereby stretching and resting, gives more room, and thus more means to do things else; or when work comes. But if you don't, then it becomes easier to step out of line; and the routine of work gets hard to meld with.
 Lets say this simply, that our interests paired with our mode of nature, makes consistent amounts of high exercise volume a challenge. And being so inconsistent can lead us to 'roughly return'; as if were urking our way back to form. But this more or less leaves us broke, which causes more or less an injury. Indeed, we are dealing with the basic return, or recuperation, so that our motions are not so jerky. I've also noticed that the standard for exercise need not be so much.
 In life, I realize that most peoples expeirence is more or less inconsistent. Those who we'd deem well because any kind of moment, are not likely to sustain such experience; and under the weight of realtions and work, and the meddling if not directly impairing effects of plesures, that they are not left with a life worhty of envy. I make this point, because even if we sat in a chair and had less devotion to our ways, we'd still benefit, and in some sense require to be well. For we woould either a) have to block out the ware that came from being unfit with pleausre, which would b) cause even more disruption; and indeed, a cascading cycle of debt so to speak, and more degrading pleasure.
 And so, let me just say, that we sit on the floor, and we work little, for we enjoy the fruits and gentility of writing and study, gaming and all things media. The outdoors also shines. The matter of application vs content is it's own scenario. But I sit here now only wishing for consistency, but also some growth. For I bear the admittence of some exericse; only my view of it has drastically changed. What is paramount is that you bring you limbs through some range of motion. Indeed, doing so in a recuperative or stabilizingn way, as yoga does, is seen and done for such. But pushups for instance, or squats, are about repition, and ranges of motion that put you under load. Running is similar. I feel this could be better seen, and that perhaps in our life we will switch away from this. But my experience in the years of late have been that a) doing none of them leaves my ability to sit, work, and breathe (ie, our metabolic rate) in thin and raspy states, and that b) doing them poorly, which is a relative term, per our consistency at the time of any effort, leaves me in our notorious pains. Indeed, my life doesn't lack the boons of exercise, but rather is encumbered by the pains of not doing it well. There is some complication to this, but generally, I push carts better when I've done pushups, as can I stand and fold better when I've done squats. Doing pullups gives me a great gait and rear strenght. And such motions just give my body the ability to breathe.
 The insight I have, is that what is proper is, foremost, that these exercises are done, and to the standard that we keep. Were not looking for muscle growth, but constitution. As such, we have to a) consider what kind of exercises we can do, in the variations that is, and b) what kind of volume is proper for each one. Indeed, we are limited by our operative means, as we only have so many forms of bars, platforms to push on and objects to squat to. Running is equal, as we only have so may places and or times to do so. Free range enjoys the benefit though of being set for anywhere. And indeed squats compare, as do pushups. Pushups, despite requiring much deliberate stretching --which in a literal sense, rather than rinsing or opening, we neglect-- can be altered by the height of the platform. Be it a box or a bench, this reduces the strain. And this should bear note, that volume is not so much an issue as your ability to do any kind of exercise well. Indeed, it is best to do lower squats, stable yet continous pushups, and flat pushups. But if you can't, then do the best form that you can. There is not enough variety, nor suitability, in lower, or in some sense worse kinds of movements, despite higher volume. I think the lesson foremost, is that similar to a soul deep squat, or standing up, or doing a runners lunge or downward dog, that a few pushups, pullps and squats create all the formation you need. One of the great effects, beyond the rehabilitive work, is that doing resting and or stabilizing poses bring the substance brough from said movements into a better line. Think of it like were creating material, energy, meat and breathe, and then moulding it for functionality.
 As such, our pushups are fine at a raised platform, as I simply cant plank and lower down without my shoulders rounding. For squats, my knees and hips are not great, but you can at least do a few, finding something to squat to that is higher. Pullups are out of date on my, but we can start from a higher point as to give ourselves some initial mobility and or momentum; we can focus on lower, and also hanging; which in it's own right can aid our shoulders. "We require as much momentum as our shoulders lack starting stability; which then procures as much need for recovery, as were not doing the exercise well. Beginning in a higher-leverage position makes this awkward start less so, though warrants no neglect --which pushups especially relate." Indeed, repition becomes easier once consistency takes place, but just do a few pullups, two rounds of squats, and a few pushups twice. Form is key, and indeed, the heart of your postural fertility comes from the in comparison-enormously rehabilitive and or stabilizing work. Free range and running to. Range of motion is just that critical thing, that little cog that if left out bears all sorts of consquence. After doing two rounds of squats, you'l naturally go to three, with a bit more reps. The stanard is nearly met, soldiifying only as we do this sequence more often. Pushups will naturally become more attemptable in their full form, which pairs wonderfully with running. Pullups are similar, though we have the most room to go. But, per our life, treat pullups like a deadlift: their essential, or foundationally bearing, and a few will do. I'd work my way up to two rounds of repition, where the reps are 3-4. From there we can start incorporating it into running, whereby everything functions. Just as your yoga fashions the substance brought from range of motion, so does running bring out the effort used in said range of mottion in a more unified yet working way. Pushups are the clearest example hear. And similarly, dropping for a few squats is great; as you run back and forth a few times, each time dropping, theres your few rounds. I have known this all along, but it seems obvious that the volume is the same, yet the sustenance of 'tension' through the running engages the muscles used in the drop, yet without having to do it and cause ware.
So, when it comes to pushups, using the broom for stretching feels paramount. Our various flexing tendons for our shoulder and chest feel most comrpessed, and these need be treated. I think our back and lats get opened up from standing, childs pose and downward dog. Squating is rough, as is running. Generally we need to breathe while seated, as this gives your hips a wanted break. I think that the crucial stretches are for your hip flexors. You can do this prone, in upright position, or in lunge. Standing even --which may be underated. Our neck could not be overated as crucial to tend. The pure neck sequence is great for that, as is the Ease sequence. The shoulder sequence (prior to pure neck) has great upper body treatment, which your neck lives from.
 It would do us well to grasp our surroundings. For one, free range can always be done. It benefits from some form, be it in rounds or reps. Generally, a bout there and back two to three times is sufficient. I'll note also that running does not need to house our exercises. It can, as a compliment by said exercises --where you do them as you will- or directly, where you run in conjunction with your exercises. You may simply do two rounds of them, and thus run in between. Free range works similarly when at home. And indeed, free range, if it be on it's own, can still happen at the park, with running, or anything aerobic for that matter. Obviously, if running is the highlight, free range is a easy and nautral thing you may will: but will no less.
 The lay of the land is such, that on weekends we have a direct place to do pullups and running. On non-rainy days, we have a direct place to run at Pitt Athletic. If we bring a box, we can do pullups also. Worse case scenario, you do your two rounds at home, then go to the field. Some balance of this with exercise prior then going to run is fair, be it of neccessity or wish. If it is raining, I feel suspect going to the school to run. Pitt Elementary is a bit easier, assuming no one's out there running --recess is 10-1030, lunch is 12-1230. There is the baseball field by the overpass; which in all admittence can be reached by going through the dog park. Again, if its not raining there isn't an excuse not to go afar. If it is raining, than that park is surely yours. And you more than likely could just go and run for a moment or two; though bringing the ladder would be odd. Indeed, this is one of those things, walking around with such thing for a brief moment. The more obscure the destination is the better. If were to self-conscious, or feel weird and suspect, then such aid can certainly be had at home, while you just manage to jump at the field. Indeed, there is Eagle Park, which is to far too walk, and with chance for people being there if it is not raining. Though, on a wet day likely not. We could in fact check out the new Lions Park. No one is likely to be there if its pouring rain. You run in, and boom. It occurs to me that not running reduces the need to go somewhere and exercise. Why not do it at home. Davy Jones works great for this; and indeed, if were off Saturday, or Sunday, or it's early in the morning, why not do it. And again, if your grip is the only factor, if its raining, you can probally go there. It is not some peverse and or occluded option. Just not a pure given.
So I like Amon, point blank. But it is crush like, and this makes our work-place scenario truoble. It just seems far-fetch that we would ask her out; and indeed, whether it be worded as a date, or simply for a walk, the intent is clear. We have no real connection after all, whereby we text or do anything together. Indeed, I see we are familiar; she addresses me as such. But I look at our other interactions with people, and there is similar familiarity. I am drawn to her in an emotional way thouhg; as though she were someone I wished to have. It is hard to say whether our view of her response to me, or offering at that, compares. A few weeks ago we determined that we were looking at it wrong, and that she did not in fact like me. I heard a coach say, that if she did like you she would show interest: Amon has not. Part of me thinks that our enthrallment is an emotional one. But indeed, were reading far in about her being equally obsessesd. My heart tells me there is a novel interest by her. I just see her looking at me sometimes. Which brings me to my real point: how are we supposed to move with her in mind. She comes to work and I feel nervous, like I have to avoid her for my sake, or in fear of revealing our withdrawal; or I wish to see her, like it's a chance to be seen, impress her, sprout her interest. But as of late, I've more or less accepted that she is not so intent on me. But this is perhaps our own acceptence of her doing so and according excitment that has caused me to retreat; as if we were too far in, 'ready as it were, to take her hand'. What is our state then? We exhanged hellos earlier, and my prevailing thought was, 'nice, were one on one, not awkward, I can be'. But I feel ocnsuded then, or somewhat down, at the wish for her that I am not pursuing. And indeed, this is just the ebb of things balancing out.
 The key agreement is that she does not want to have sex with you. She isn't waiting for it, nor vieweing you under such lens. Such an action, 'as perhaps encapsulates our passion', is amidst more real and developing bonds. Indeed, my heart wants what I yearn for, and imagnie her to yearn for. But this is hard to pursue, and expect, on it's own; especially if we work together, and our age. I am not against those real things coming to be; but indeed, our wish for what our passion evokes is fairly false, or sought with indffernece --that we may show but not expect. In a way, our motivation warrants us getting to know her, or attracting her as some may do. But this is hard; unclear at least. If we try to get to know her, we are in a way showing our cards, that we our attracted to her and want something, and thus are inteested in knowing her to get it. That is weird to me. I would begreive if we asked her out and she said no. Like how much would we have misread things for that to occur
I have a passion for Amon, likely because she is a girl. Her face barely comes to mind; though, when I see her for real, I think about her body. "Like were so close."
 Yet when were close I'm kind of repulsed; similar to when finding a new place, as your giving up what you know.
 That is the point seemingly, that we know her very little, and if it weren't for our loins and heart, we'd care little.
 For one, I see little signs from her that she wishes for me to make a move, or that she has her own like and care. She makes no effort to talk to me; and indeed, if there ever were a chance, she leaves me alone. I don't find myself really looking to talk to her, less we shower her with energy; but like we do with Tristan or Carli. "Somone to complain with, or express yourself to, for the sake of the day."
 When we told her about her picking, she seemed to laugh, and I liked doing that. There is no other reason for us to talk, and indeed, she seems to engage me not. Which again, may not reflect true like, as perhaps she's nervous; or us being that way is not really reminiscent but of what we want, and thus has nothing to do with her. In a way, we talk like this to people we don't like, as were taking their attnetion for our gain. But of her like, be it there or not, I see that we don't have much of our own; and indeed, part of the passion is in exagerating whether or not she likes me; without the passion, there isn't much there. I make up reasons for sure, but they don't move me in a meaningdfull way. I found myself wanting to speak with Simron, go to dinner, walk, share time together. I liked her as a person. My like for Amon is not comparable.
Let's follow this energy and state the nature of our circumstance. My aim here is not to say what we can do, but what there is. We may adapt, and use reason and candor to adjust. But I wish to best state the weight at hand.
 We have agreed to a non lease, 900$ per month tenancy. The home is between Riverside and Hammond arena. The walk is slightly longer than our current, though there's no overpass. Were at the western edge of the flat, and it dips down towards 203rd and the bus loop. Coming home will be slightly harder; the slope isn't too tough, and nice going down. The main road there is quite nice, with a large walking path --and that cool woody path that connects through 203rd. Its realistically a +- 5 min ride. It is worth noting that Langley Market is down the road --207th I believe-- and that from Hammond and many of the streets the Pitt Valley mountains are visible, and some of Seymour. There is a general loftiness to the area, and flatness. Similar to Killarney. The sky feels ever clear. And because were no the lip, looking west lets you see farther, as everything drops. The south is not clear to me and the east is generally blocked by the river road trees; though, from there, near the parking lot at the far park, there is a gap looking south. I recall being there and seeing the far south east sky. It is worth noting that we have great access to the industrial road and thus Pitt Atheltic. We may not be in the home were currently in, but it is very accesible. And there's something nice about going into Pitt Meadows, rather than leaving it. The northern area is nice also, with that through road near Value Village; and the east stretch by River road is nice.
 Now, as for the pecuniary requirments, 'as all else is of familiarity and pleasure', the utilities are included, and all in all, were on our own. This means, basic soap, basic toilet paper, a bar of soap and toothpaste, deodorant, and our food. We pay just over 30 for PSN and Phone. I will remove Fit 4 Less, as I seldom use it; and I prefer shopping during our break. "I find it restorative; and short of supine, we can stretch in the fridge or bathroom."
 As for food, let us state the givens; as I explicitly need nourishment, and the rest can follow as allowance or credit-like affairs. For one, we need milk, which I've determined to be a stack --meaning around 20-- per month, for one solid glass per day. This gives us fair amounts of calcium. Granola is going to be peanuts, sunflower seeds and cranberries. Cranberries are seldom anything but 2.5, and a lb of nuts and sees is about 3$. These can hopefully be got in bulk. Lets say we want at least one serving per day. This amounts to 1 bag of berries, and perhaps 1 pale of nuts and seeds. This is 10$ for a heap. That's half a stack, for 1 jar per day. Consider bread an essential. You may freeze things, despite this not being preferable; and indeed, the woes of economy have shat on us, making English Muffins 3$, which is about 6$ for a week of double servings. No Name bread is similarly priced. Indeed, the value of bread is so that it get's consumed in large portions, so a third of a stack per week is a fair price.
 As it stands, 1 stack for milk, half stack for jar of trail-mix, 1/3 stack for bread, two stacks for PSN, Phone and cleaning. I am not entirely sure of the toilet paper cost; this may average out to less by patronage, and soap too. Soap is negligble, while Toilet Paper is unclear. Looking in, the mighty 100 pack, or 30 triples, is 20$, which is quite inexpensive. That would be 1 stack per month if you used an entire role per day. I can't imagine using more than every few days, which means this could last a few months. Even the standard 24 purex is 13$ wholesale, which is literally 1 or so every other day, for 6$ a month. I'm just going to throw soap in with this and say half a stack for washing. I'd say more, but the prices are clear, and I don't feel that were using too much on our own. You blow your nose a couple time's per day, and wipe your bum once.
 The next thing is condiments. We surely need salt and pepper, the latter being the most expensive. You can buy 575g black peppercorns for 12$, which amounts to 2$ per 100g. This may range up to 3$ elsewhere. You can buy a kg for 20$, which sounds like alot, but think about how much you use per day, a couple grams --which may be too much. That means 100g is likely to last a whole month. At the very least you may not spend more than 5$ a month on pepper. Salt is even cheaper, literally a third of the price. In bulk, you may spend a third of a stack on salt and pepper.
 Now oil is the big one, for margarine is kingly, but we can cook more. So popcorn can use oil, as can naan. I don't know how oil is when making croutons. Also, I don't think I've established the taste for oil and salt, rather than margarine. Indeed, we only use two tablespoons per bowl, and another two per toast serving. So margarine is about 50c per 100g, which is hard to see, as a full tablesppon is deemed as about a 7th of this. I am not sure how much we use. Generally, I buy a 900g tub, and it lasts for more than a week, and this is about 5$. And I am surely not the only one using it. By those counts, if you round off how much I would use on my own and solo, I'd say were at a solid stack per month, which is not too bad. Oil is one of those bulk things that you don't use too much. In fact it is quite cheaper than Olive Oil. Think about how much you use for potatoes, and a little dollap of margarine. This is like toilet paper, where 20$ could last for months. I'm just going to say a stack and a half for salt, pepper, margarine --there are no other main condiments.
 So, bread is a half stack, condiments are a stack and a half, milk is 1 stack, washing is half a stack, trail-mix is half stack, psn+phone+odor is 2 stacks.
 Total is .5, 1.5, 1, .5, .5, 2 = 6 = 120$. This does not include potatoes, which can be got for half stack, almost too much supply, popcorn, which is a quarter stack for large amounts, say half stack per month. So a full stack for potatoes and popcorn: light grain staples. Then you have full grain. Quinoa is quarter stack for 1 bag, which lasts a reasonable week, which is 1 stack per month. "One is lucky to afford such expenditure." Barley is quite inexpensive. 5$ for 2kg, with each lb being almost worth a week. Thats barely a quarter stack per month. Lentils are 1.5$ per pound, and are not eaten in gross amounts. 6$ probally get's you a month's supply, though maybe a bit more. Probally 3 quarters a stack for these and barley. That is my general stack: barley and lentils, which are mixers to Quinoa; which itself is usually 2lbs per week, or 150g per day. Barley is sometimes had in solo amounts, though not huge, and only once or twice per week. I can't think of any other grain. Indeed, besides potatoes and popcorn and bread, our main food source is grains, so I'll round it to 2 stacks per month. That is 3 stacks for popcon, potatoes, barely, quinoa, and lentils. Bread is another stack but we included that. This leaves us at 9 stacks, or 180$. This includes everything but sauces, fruit and veg --which is not small order-- and fried food. We live on our own, and cooking things in unique and pleasing ways is what I got. Porn is not a wish of mine, but jerking off is surley. We can use the fleshlight all we want, in full privacy. We have our own washroom. Barring sound, we can listen to music indiscriminately.
 How much are oranges and kale and apples anyway? Or bananas for that matter. Well, bananas are about 5 per dollar, which is like half a stack per month. Oranges very, but indeed, if you wanted two, the small one's can be as cheap as 5$ for 10. Apples are a bit more expensive; it is our luxury that Langley Marek is close for such things. Things are seasonal, but Kale is 2$, with a handfull of seeds, usually an extra pale per month, and a slice of tomatoe --which is itself 2$-- adds to not very much. Let's say a tomato is a few bucks and lasts a week, while Kale, which I can only picture having for breakfast, can probally last the week, for its 2$. The extra sunflower seeds are so, that you only have a small spoonfull. They are cheap as it is, and so 5$ a month is fair --2 pales for the little pinch. Kale plus tomato is probally 5$ a week, 1 stack per month. Round up to 1.5 stacks for our monring salad. Thats 225 total, and more or less, 1125.
 Looking at it, 15 per weekk, with stats and vacay pay, amount to 1173 next, 20 per week next close to 1500, and doing 20 half the time goes to above 1300.
Like the Ents rising, we have not been in such a moment for an age.
 Long ago it seems like, that we have any real mixture of stability and peace. Indeed, we had some of each while in Kamloops, and this year prior. But not till we had left Vancouver, the eave at least, did we feel both ourselves and sturdy.
 Now, we are more or less set on the path of renting. THe home is near Hammond, which has some limitations, but is, in the broad sense, quite nice; as you see much of the north; and novelly, the angle is cool. Also, the height is much different than were used to, and this gives us that farther view of the sky. The main diamond is closed off; which indeed removes this openeness to grandeur. Besides being generally nice, the far field is open, and is not about 400m from our home. This is from our current place to the overpass; and it is a public park, which gives it value the worth to walk so far. There is also a slight lip between it and the parking lot, which is a delight for reading. Were also right next to Riverside; which is not an elementary school, but drop out school; and so there's no real recess, and you can more than likely go over there and run; and there's pretty good pull up bars too. This could be a gem. There is loop A, which goes from the hammond path and thorugh the culdesac behind our house; this compares to our current 193 loop. Then there is a superb loop B, which runs down Thorn and wraps back around. Super nice, either way. Going down Waisterly is nice too, as there's this thicket of trees; and indeed, there is an alley right beside waiverly, which makes doing a loop easier. Indeed, if you wish to go a bit further, there's a grid near that little park. And things loop back through the Hammond lot, or by Tristans. Even the south road, that lines Hammond school, has a great view; and indeed, our walking sensibility is not so ardently 'of loops'. We may just go up and down.
 No matter, the price is 900. He did say high speed; though I can't imagine this means ethernet. Maybe we can connect somehow. He said he and his son play playstation; his son plays Rivals, so I'm guessing the internet is sufficeint. I calculated our income, and at 15 hours we make more or less 1100 per month. I did a hard done budget, and besides fruit and veg, all things amount to 200. This is great. Per the circumstance, we have our parent's general support; namely in buying things in bulk. Our aim here is not likely for an extended period of time. Maybe a year at most, then we'll look at transfering. The anomoly of this place, barring the smell, which as a concner --that we may adapt to regardless- is still in waiting, brings the situation to a generally prosperous degree. We have our own place: kitchen, bathroom, plenty of space. Great walking, a slight slope and no overpass to work, and direct for scootering at that. Langley Market is right there; and indeed, the farmland won't be our backyard, but we can travel to it easy enough. Probally better this way. The whole woodland near Fairvew, Westview and the Hospital is ours for the reckoning. We can go to that lovely park near Laity. And the point, is that say we stay here for a year. We more or less plan to work 15 hours, as we do now. We'll adjust; and there's surely time to remark upon the want and ease in working more. We may even value the place enough to button it down. But, we are on the cusp of breaking even; which itself bodes well for (say) the 700 room which we may have rented, and likely will in the future. But, so much as we don't, we may a) simply work more, by which we calcualte our credit left, and just work the shifts needed to pay it off, or b) use some of our savings. Let's be more realistic and say, that we either move home in 6 months, or switch to Vancouver, whereupon the benefit in our life may change, but were no longer accumulating credit, or chipping away at our savings. That 6 months, through the summer we'll say, is 500-1000$, to live responsibly, but within our want; and how we extend our duty is without requisite stake. Right now, I have two 2000$ funds, plus some near 3500$ in our savings. We could spend one of those 2k, and have the rest left over. My view on finances is such that the merry and working way of 15 hours per week is abundant for a room. This is a suite were renting, and we may just live life this way. I find my feeling for Amon becoming more real, but by that, remarkably flat and unenthused. Our interest lingers, but only because we have nothing else. I hope that is something that reason can make. "I only have the hint of a momentary taste, of such peace and stability." So we'll let things go. I wish we could forget the smell. It wasn't horrible, just unfortunate. I am sure there is some effort we can do, compensation for what remains, and our own indifference, adherence and or appreciation (by which bad is viewed as being in spite of good around), may carry us through. Tristan lives nearby which is nice. We can go sit and talk. I generally find work well. And indeed, our health is poor; but running should help, as should being more consistent. Our duty in life is what wares on us, and in some sense the call of our acitivty is to do nothing, which can be troublesome when our reason and scenery is clouded by persnicitinous and loud TV. And consisteancy will help. Moreso, it is worthwhile to exhude the confidence that come's from merely leaving what is good now, not what bad your taking on. Were not going to work any different. There is but a small but of excess that may procure; and indeed, much time can go on for this to be even noticble. And we may, thorugh reason or merry tact, adjust to that, or compensate; of which time is not so derailing, nor fortunate at that.
So, back to budgeting. Bread is a half stack, condiments are a stack and a half, milk is 1 stack, washing is half a stack, trail-mix is half stack, psn+phone+odor is 2 stacks.
 Milk is the clearest thing, being 1 stack. Bread, on it's own, is usually 3$ per bag, and you may hadnsomely enjoy 2 per week, and thus a full stack per month. We determined that dish soap and toilet paper are so bulky that they only amount to 10$ a month. And there a bit of a write off when it comes to other people getting them for you. Salt and pepper are quite cheap, considering only you have it. And margarine, similarly, in it's 2kg for 10$, is likely to last a while. 1kg, or one of the 900g 5$ one's, seems to last a while. Jeez, the one we have now has lasted that long, and were all using it. This means about 1 per week, plus the 10$ in salt and peper.
 Maybe we'll reorient this, for micro sake. 10$ is a stack. By those counts, Milk is a sure half stack per week, and thus 2 per month. Dishsoap is half a stack per month, while toilet paper [20$ for 30 huge one's, which each last say 3 days, means 20/3 per month < 7$.] We say this is 1 stack. We round this off with the soap, and these are naturally gifted, or bought in bulk. So 1 stack for washing. Bread is just over a half stack per week. Indeed, we buy it alot, but it is generally stock things, which can be frozen, and we may not literally have a serving per day. So it balances to 2 stacks per month. We erroed in our trail mix calculation, as we said what 1 per day was per week. Reasonably, a pale of sunflowers and peanuts and bag of cranberries is 1 stack. This create's a mighty heap. I reckon though, with dried fruit considered in general, that this will indeed last a week, as 1 per day, more even. It is indeed a primary srouce for nourishment, and should not be underestimated. It is quite expensive, if only because were considering a high daily usage. 4 stacks it is for dried fruit and seeds. As for grain, potatoes are 20lbs for 1 stack, and this is enormous. They likely go bad, but are a real staple, and are only deemed more than 1 stack because the value can't be fully accesed. 2 stacks per month. Popcorn is quite cheap, nothing more than 1 stack per month. We said that bread was 2 stacks also. That's 5 stacks for our lighter grains. As for heavy grains, you primarily have quinoa, the deepest source, and the compliments of barely and lentils. Barely can be got in bulk, 2kg for 1 stack, with lentils being a bit more. Barely is sometimes had on it's own, but were nickle and dimming based on how much we really have. Lentils are likely had more consistently anyway. At any rate, a lb each per week is nearly there, and indeed, bulk averages out if it's more. For simplicity sake, 2 stacks for barley and quinoa. Quinoa is similar to trail mix, im that it is had without shortcoming. It is worthwhile to note that we have a variety of foods, in our lighter starches, and similar to milk, full grains are but a moment in the day, and so we surely have quinoa each one. It is 2lbs for half a stack, and indeed, this seems ample for the week. 1lb for compliments, 2 for whats pure. Thats 2 stacks though of quinoa per month. "It's 150g per day at that rate, which is ample." So full grains are 4 stacks, light grains are 5, trail mix is 4, milk is 2, condiments are 3, washing is 1. Right washing off for a second. Our basic food total, not including produce or snacks, is 9 for grains, and equally so for milk, condiments and trail mix. Thats 18 stacks in total for all food. Literally 1 more stack for toilet paper and dishsoap. We then have 4 stacks for fixed costs: psn, phone, and rounding it off with toothpaste, soap and deodorent. That's 22 stacks, or 23 with washing --cleaing is toothpaste and deodorant. This adds up, as were giving a fair amount to trail mix.
 Let us leave for the moment the notion of affordability, let our circumstances go: for we have savings, our parents generosity, and our ability to make the difference through extra shifts. "1 is equal to 80$, which is 8 whole stacks, from 1 shift." We determined that kale, being 1 or so per week, at about 1 stack per month, and tomatoes, being something similar- thought a bit more-- and the rounding up of 2 or so sunflower seeds, 'for that almighty breakfast', was worthwhile, but at any rate 3 stacks. For veg. Then, half a stack seems fair for a market bunch of oranges. A bag even is generally that price, and you get a weeks worth of single servings. Thats 2 stacks, maybe 3 if you wish for more. 5 stacks for an orange and salad per day. The orange is for a moment, while another is for taste. At any rate, potstickers are about 7$ for 2 servings. Indeed, 3 of these creates 5 mighty servings. If you had two per week, thats 8 per month, and something like 3 stacks or so. Now, for a real delight: ice cream. I should in fact include bananas in our budget. Were not having smoothies, though, orange, milk, granola, suffice, we may still want bananas. Literally it's almost 1 stack per month to have 1 per day. It's near neglible. But, for ice-cream, say you may have 1 per serving, and a fair amount of milk, say a full cup. Berries are 15$ for a large bag, and you don't use much. Maybe 20 servings for the full thing. 1 bag per month, plus 2 things of milk, plus 16 banans, which is call it a stack, amounts to about 3 stacks, call it 4 if we include our other banana purchasing, for this upper end of fruit. I can't think of much more.
 220[ 90 for soft+full grains, 90 for adj. 40 for living] is for certain. 5 is produce, 4 is fried, 4 is mighty ice cream, and 1 for washing.
 What can be bought in bulk is as follows: all washing, frozen berries, condiments, full grains, milk to an extent --as it lasts for more than a month-- popcorn. The max we could ever spend is 220 + 10 + 50 + 40 + 40 = 360. This is exorbident, and suggests that we are having all of our grains and starches, but also fried food and ice cream. By all accounts you ought budge for 220 + 50, which is 270. We make nearly that, though perhaps a bit less. With parental aid the discussion is mute, and indeed, we have savings also. But, if we worked just one more shift, we could push this value up 80$, before we even notice.
I want to figure out my income. But balancing out taxes + stats, ontop of normal wage, is hard to do. I'll give you my last 3 paystubs. Note, that I do get vacation pay, as an extra 4%. On my paystub this gets added as hours, but the total just seems to be an extra 4%. I'll give you pre-tax deductions that statuatry charges. You tell me the norm, and we can predict what things are like moving forward.
 Slip 1 --- gross 467.32 19.38 pre tax 27.48 stat. 420.56 total 25 standard hours ---I believe the 4% is calculated on top of this, into the gross I gave. My base rate is 17.95 per hour --the exact vacation amount is 18.67, which is not exactly 4%, so a little weird. But take it as more or less. I'll just give you the gross through net for the next 2
 Slip 2 -- 411.34 gross 19.39 pre tax 23.3 stat. 368.76 net 22 base hours, 16.44 as vacation pay --vacation pay is added into the gross
 slip 3 -- 383.29 gross 19.39 pre tax 21.08 stat. 342.83 net 20.5 base hours 15.31 vacation pay ---it adds up that the base hour times standard 17.95 rate + the vacation pay give equals the gross. It ends up being 4.1%, so take it as it were in our coming predictions. Those are my slips, lets get on the same page, so we understand. based on the hours I work in a week, over the coarse of a year, what I can expect.
So, I have 3300 in coast, 110 on credit, 370 in cash, and this includes the 500 deposit. We also have 4000 elsewhere.
 Consider this thought. Your getting 510 every paycheck, which leaves 120 after rent. There is a stat in februray and April, and we get a third paycheck in April. This leaves us with 80$ of credit, assuming we spent 200 the three months. We also have an extra 510, which you can take and spread out over the next few months. May has a stat, though june does not. In some sense, a stat month is one that allows for 200 in spending without credit. June and august have none, but we have 510 to pay those off. September has one, and October has 2, which makes up for august, and gives a third pay day also. So the 500 from April pretty much covers the excess of May through Septemebr, and October the excess till April. Working one extra shift per month basically acts as a stat. Our life may accomodate this well; considering the harmnoyt of work and pleasure that living on our own provides. Also, this is all on a standard 3 day week --15 hours. Our situation is also an anomoly, as we'd normally be paying hundreds of dollars more for a suite, and thus working more. Normally, 'and I have not moral nor pleasing problem with this', we'd be in a room, some 200 dollars cheaper. Indeed, in that case, if we struck a balance of say 300 in spending, we'd still earn the extra 1173 --which is what you earn over the year-- in saving, which is 12 x 173=some 1800$. How about your computer or scooter then.
 Lets admitt though that we made 510 from our first paycheck, but are likely only making 400 or so for the next. I am sure we'll get a bunch of food. And indeed, we have an enormous amount of money; and that support can't be underestimated. A couple hundred bucks, 'most moves spend that on moving'.
So my gaming leisure is quite fantastic, 'and set for months to come', but my viewing habits are off. We finsihed the first chapter of Easy A, and are left with nothing but the Sony Conference, some early impressions, and noodles and broth. I figure that we finish modern family, then go onto the second half of 2016.
 As for finances, it is important to rememeber that the notion of finances requires a view that speaks to the lenght of life which were partaking in such finances. I am not going to do that hear. I will say, that of our income, which is concerned with our life now, that there is a stat in feburary and april, and an extra pay day, which gives us 500$. I was payed 510$ a few days ago, and this coming week plus the prior will be our second paymet; this is only 24 hours worth, which is short of 400, leaving us only with 900 for the month. I am taknig the fourth four hour, and we'll go from here with our expenditure in mind. I anticipate some shopping from our parents, and this will surely even out whatever natural shortcoming we have going into the month. We'll be fine starting next week.
So lets brainstorm our planner site.
 First, the pay day/periods ought be shown across the board. Akin to a calendar, we can see other things two: shifts, appointments, not sure what else. Even notices though games and what not. Things you like. I reckon the site should be set up with two things in mind: the timeliness of things and noting things down. I figure that the top half is a bar, that scrolls a certain range, and it shows upcoming events. Rather than elaborate on this, you might have specific parts beneath. Indeed, these kinds of effort are constructive in their making, and similar to our note taking app, are not of worth because of their continuous use. Some parts are just for our sake right now, but earn the formative value --which remains, and needs no further attention-- by being so integrative (now).
 So one idea is actually a focused one. Rather than have everything shown, as if that's relevant, you basically have a module for showing dates at the top, then titles at the bottom that trigger relevant dates within the module. You may have a moon chart, that pulls up the ups and downs, or relevant dates in the module; then you pick shifts, and you see when your shifts are. Indeed, rather than it be a calendar, let it really be focused, and just show the dates of your shifts. You could integrate each group to the general module based on what it needed. So when you clicked shifts, there was an option to show past shifts. And similarly for pay dates, you could also show pay periods [start, end, pay, ontop of one another, as a column, and a row of theses -or you just toggle the period or the pay].
So, for food, a outline of each item is first, prior at least to organizing them.
 Fixed - psn, phone, toothpaste, deoderant, bar soap
 Light Grains - Bread, potatoes, popcorn
 Whole Grains - Lentils, Barley, Quinoa
 Condiments - salt, pepper, margarine, oil
 Cleaning - toilet paper, dish soap, freezer bags, garbage bags
 Energy - dried fruit, peanuts, sunflower, bananas, milk, edamame, corn
 Fruit - oranges
 vegg - kale, tomatoe, cucumber, peanuts, seeds
 Snack - potstickers, springrolls, banana, soy, frozen berries
So, it pays well to take note of your current efforts, or way at least, and recognize that a lack of motion, or at least awaareness of peace or pause, is merely an insight as to your current state, rather than completion. We've been working out more, doing more yoga, trying to be more active, and consistently so. I am feelnig the benefits and that is good. But were merely over that hummp, where exercise can even be done, and were all gruntled and rot. Now, were rather soft, yet whole, and able. I even feel our hips being more sturdy. But this is only an invitation for us to refocus, and bring our alignments to more sustainable efforts. We need not push ourselves, nor wait for tomorrow as though it may not come.
 What has come to me are merely those things which feel apparent upon our lasting success. Namely, some for of rest, or non mobile stretching: yin as it were. We need no restoration now, but long breathes. And any form will do, and our guidnace show equate. Our yoga seems nicely split into standing, lunge, rest, seated and rise. Very easy. Food is not so hard, given that we may just have two seperate bowls of grain. No need to stuff yourself wtih one big one. You benefit though in having some sauce; the curry is obvious here. Be it tomato, soup, store bought sauce, or corn even, this helps us eat more grain. Pairing craisins with fruit is nice also, in taste and punch.
 Our finances are such that the budgeting for a 900 room is nearly stabile, which in effect makes our 700 room overly nice. It is so, that our needs going into the room will likely be met by a bulk shop, where most things can be got. Then, seeing as no one is really promising me this or that, 'and they are all considering buying a house together', that I am totally fine to pay a couple 100$ in savings to make it through the year. Indeed, we get feb, april and may stats, and an extra payday in April. Picking up a single shift also gives us much breathing room. And indeed, the given of being employed, the transferable nature of this, makes for a rather pleasant foresight. As we may stay, switch around, or transfer off; all while working as we relatively do. Indeed, were spending a bit more on this house; but it's ours, now, yet still close to home: its a great deal and scenario, as we keep things familiar, yet get out of the house. And my point, is that of the good this offers, so is it obvious; as we can shower, cook, jerk off, listen to muisc, and otherwise see our time as free and worth looking to --which makes work easier to embrace and return from. But as so, whatever cost this bars to us, so is it temporary, not to exorbadant as it is, and in line still with a normal, accessible, and similarly desibrale route which this more or less stable path only engrosses, as being equally good and demanding, yet that much easier. For whic, by fortune and grace, we will make it through these weeks ahead, the month or so following, and through the year to come. Our concerns are met.
I cant emphasize enough, the continuous, or at any rate procedural nature to our health. It is perhaps our ignornace, or loose grip, on the matter of our past, and why the day is as it is. For we have done much better over the years; but perhaps too much, as we are so active; but now engaged in work, and settling on our self-conscious image; which prior to work even, brought us to move awkwardly.
 No matter, all we can do is address our current scenario, keep the boat moving, or pitch it back if it's gone off track. Restorative, which is to say non-flowing lunges are great, lifting, and bring us out of our potentially inward slump. Resting moments are primed for this, as we care not for mobility nor movement, but may lack the stability, and at any rate peace (to do nothing), wherbey we sink or burrow in. Lunges really help with this. And indeed, the breath is our friend here as it carries us through the recovery period, and back to more mobile states. Indeed, I recognize that with the weather and our current ability, running is tough, and recovering at any rate from it. These kind of days make it hard to shower, cook, and indeed, it is because I see nothing but the days end, from this moment. And so it feels like everything is done at once. There's no intermediate areas; nothing to really do. Tis the nature of our household, that we feel closed off; as though everything were harder. Or better yet, unset for how we'd do things as to make them accessible. This makes our active moments too ill-formed and ill-handled in post; and our resting times too still, and thus unproductive in training, but also chores; and we move inappropriately in our activity, 'which brings with it the slugde of views and satisfaction'.
 Dear to me is this view, of life's winding yet ever present river, of which we are bound to, by what it offers, and in some sense, all we can respond to. "For fish, not the richest spring on land can be useful: even the mightiest tuna couldn't acquire it." Indeed, life seems spent in one of two ways: committing to, enacting and or acquiesing to such path, 'as to even be, as seperating from it melts you away'; and then the volume between, 'the area that makes up the curve'. I've spoken of this as though it were tree sap, in that it exudes from the poors of the trunk, based on how strong it is. And indeed it is like this; but a better way is merely on of nature, and our requisite, or neccessary rather being is so when it is with said river, or ground if you will, 'for which our lack of footing sees us float away'. It is by this nature then, that our posture is upright, active and alive, and that all the tuffs of fauna wafting around, or the glimmer of sun shining high, catches your able and receptive eye. Indeed this is what you get from being truly. But so is there a constant to this all, whereby the bones of being, whereupon even the abscnece of light you still are, that so does this demand attention, and regular assessment, admission and adjustment. For there is a peace in all of thus, where our being is made. Indeed, it would suite us as animals to settle on the fact of our nature being of breathing or death; and that indeed, all matters of starvation or injury lead to one and the abscence of the other. For no one breathes easily, and by that feels the imbuded, or at least corresponding peace, when they are insatiated or in perril. And as such, what is good, 'by which we see that there are goods', are so, for they bring you, what upon gestation, produce deep and pleasing breathes. For you must breathe; it is in most ways your effective esscence. And if you are well, than your breathe will be such. It is not to say that the breathe is whats good, but rather, that whatever scenario your in, or condition rather, 'in that you have this or see that', that so will your breathe be good. "If someone's breathe is hefty and compressed during a wedding, the love is off." What seperates man from child is not in want, rather the rules the former follows, 'to permitt their experience of breathe, and by that the view around'. A child is given everything, and thus in their play and awe, they feel no restriction or worth in things, 'for they are formless, able to do as they will'. But we are animals, who need food and shelter, and thus admittence to what affords this. And indeed, it is a batch of sense and oblige to see such neccessities and committ to their means. But, of that will, in which all things good are seen, so a man works for this, and finds peace, when his needs are met, routinely, and he experiences what he will steadily. A childs expeirence is but temporary, as all prior to him are those who grew out of this dependance, in order to sustain themselves, and by most counts, then provide.
Some goods thoughts are just to precious, or important, to miss, and thus warrant a brief mention.
 That is so, that when we move, at least when I consider our prior time's on our own, that eating in the evening was much eaier, as we could a) mastubrate early on, as to please ourselves and fend off food want, and b) there was less friction or apprehnesion about noise and smell, and by that timing; and so we would make bread, popcorn, and indeed, we also had better storage, and so we'd make ice-cream. Our general free-going had us go shopping; as we had more place within the day, in terms of pleasantry and ease of demand, that we'd go shopping, and also cook our bulkier foods.
 The point, is that were leaving in two weeks. We have the carpets scheduled; and I am hoping that in conjunction with cleaning the wall, some possible OZone like thing, and ultimatley air freshening what ever smell remains, that the place can be what it is without such impingment. And at any rate, Justin said he is speaking to the Landlord next week and getting papers; and we can surely disucss it then. Financially, I find our debts to be of no concern; as it is our time to do as we please; and indeed, none of our time there, during or in these weeks prior, or in what may come, or could at any rate considering oru transferable position, is done by anything but my control. And so the 7000 we have is fine, the two upcoming stats and third payday will more than make up for an excesses, and indeed, if we spend a couple hundred dollars to make this thing work, so be it. It really is no pain upon me. It leaves us with less of a sum; but such sum is not relevant to our current or further employment; and at any rate, nearly all times ahead will be more affordable, and likely in surplus. "This is a good balance and pitch for the few years ahead: whereby time's offering will meet us already content."
 I wished to say though, that as a principle, and one which is relevant for our time now, is if we live at home, and are less able or open to cook, then we need to prioritize the sustenance of raw food. This means Kale, seeds and tomato for salads, or at any rate, ample amounts of fruit; berries and dried fruit compliment eachother nicely. And indeed, smoothies are fine. Peanuts, Cucumber and kale are beyond welcomed. And the point is to have less grain later on, but thereby not fail to have enough raw food prior. Smoothie, cucumber salad, fruit, milk and dried fruit all combine to acheive this. Indeed, having craisins with berries or an orange, and then trail mix later is quite fine. And indeed, recognizing all of this tends to bring you to have a smoothie where you'd normalyl have barely, and then hold off till the evening for you hearty portion of grain, and likely some bread.
So there's potential to stay an hour lnoger on our four hour shifts, and only work three of them. Reza will let us; its just not a given that he's there. We can balance this out.
 But it is worthwhile to note, that in your budgeting, you have this fixed cost of rent, the volume of living expenses, and then excesses for saving. In a normal place, we'd bob in and out of that upper end of living expenses, working as we do now, and indeed, stats and third pay days act as reserves --to be saved, or pay off when you bob over; or if your income dips comparably. In our current case though, we have our fixed rent, half the volume for expenses, and stats and third pay as a mitigating factor.
 I think that it is ever so important to look at our life, its worth, our need, by the measure of independance. Nothing else truly matters.
So I'm moving in on the 31st, more or less. The carpets are getting cleaned, and this is going to be 150 bucks. I reckon Justin will split it with us, and Dad may pay for our half --as he's storing things there. We also have to pay 50$ and 16$ for fit 4 less. Not thrilled about this, but you learn. Were pretty much just above 400 for this coming paycheck, and we had 510 prior. Not sure what food were getting, but much of it can be got bulk. Indeed, 5$ for 750g of viriginia peanuts from walmart, a delight. Sunflower seeds are dirt cheap. Craisins are 2.50, and last quite a long time. But you have produce, which is reocuring. I dont' actually know the price at langley marekt, but for instance, we got 5lbs of oranges for 5$. Indeed sales will be our friend. I feel like oranges are generlly the cheapest, and typically they are quite cheap at langley market. Indeed, what we want is that one bit of fruit in the morning. Then, Kale is likely 2$, but it may be 3. Again, sunflower seeds are a write off, but a beefsteak tomato is a couple bucks. It lasts a while though. Our budget can't really account for those times that we want a roma, or apples even. Bananas are like sunflowrs, a write off. We got 3 medium and 2 small for a 1$. Indeed, we need not rely on our produce, at least so stretched out, as we have more control of our cooking, shopping, eating and rest when it comes to grains. Popcorn is so cheap as are potatoes. And indeed, potatoes take on a glug of oil, which is itslef quite cheap, and a bit of margarine. Bread is really not too expensive, when you consider how 4$ gets you a weeks worth of double muffins. Indeed, 2kg of margarine is 10$, but this may last longer than you think. And toilet paper is literally 30 massive rolls for 20$. I would say that a) don't fret or berrate your mom and dad. All plans are made with worse case scenarios in mind, and they are there for you. We tend to fixate on the micro scenario, and losses there. They don't, as their macro, and there for me in such ways. So let your micro waiver, or let it happen at any rate. It is tricky, as we have our food bill for this month, which is likely 250, plus the 70 or so from other costs. And we have not much more than the rent. But, we are working 16 and 17 the following weeks; and we may choose to stay an hour longer on those 4 hour days. Our shifts are no issue, and indeed, the dream, 'as Tristan put it,' of living as we do working such short shifts, and at worse 4 of them (of the shortest kind mind you), is likely to become obvious once we move in. So the balance is there. And indeed, we will get the februray stat, and then another plus a payday in April. And honestly, even if you have 300$ on your card, that's like 3$ in interest. It will balance out by April. As we did not come into this month with a full schedule, and we had the fit 4 less slip up. Indeed, we have 4000$ stashed away, plus 3700$ now, plus whatever we get payed on the 25th. We have to give 900, and will have our credit to pay, but still, that leaves us with 3200$, and some 550 coming the pay after, and another 500 after that, plus an 80 stat. Indeed, lets say we spend 200 --im anticiapting some form of a pre move shop-- for Februray. That leave us with 500 in total. Minus 80 right away, plus a little from our January excess, then the 150 or so that you have in excess within februray. Thats more than 250, which halves it. That is in effect your debt. You could easily pay this off, have 3000 cash, then bank the 600 or so from your extra pay day and april stat.
 I think that produce is the main concern, and where we'll bob above our pay. Bulk is cheap, and probale for offers from others shopping. But oranges are 5$ a week, kale is a few$, as is tomatoes. Bananas are cheap, but if you want ice-cream, and one per day, likely a few$. I have said, that we need no savings, as we already have some. Our computer is fixed, our scooter is a free return, and likely with that money for the time seeing. And indeed, we have 3000$ cash, plus 4000, and were not quiting anytime soon. We may even move home for a few months, and save another few grand then. The point, is that when we level out our living, as 700 in the other place seemed more than fine, we would not only accrue little in terms of expenses, but be able to cash our extra pay. And you put taxes on top of this, which I have not till this point done, and your in the green. Indeed, the 500-1000 we get from this tax break in April is another boost. And Ryan, my friend, spend 200$ on produce for these few months, till you get that April surplus, pay of your credit, adjust your work and spending, and use whatever extra as dripping cash. "For October is even richer."
Our observations are sound, in that we came from that starting admittence, to see Shauna and her friends; but through whichever root below we failed, and were left with less. For we wanted what they had; but in that root, brought too little towards it. Prior, we dappled in indifference and or mediocrity; but in our scenario; we were generally merry otherwise. But this sapped away as our embrace to friends withered off; and the part of us which could sit where it was plain yet all the while be merry, softened, and it was as if the gentle spirit that produced this drifted away; moreover, we had tearing wants from that which we lacked, 'which only prodded our inner weakness'.
 That is all to say, that we went through those times. And indeed, we came out during our second college year, 'to a point of self-determination and ability'. From here though, there followed not but a receding view, of that which our new and strong-founded steps could provide. There was this sense, which we carry still to a degree, that by simply being who we are the world would greet me. Not to skip over things, but this transitioned into seeing 'that being, of our mere selves', as not being so prospective but rather satisfying, and that when we looked at our measures for stability, so were they so if such satiety was provided. Before, in this peak of adolescence, we felt not only enthralled, or in tandum with, but also of exepectation, as if by pride, vanity or real confidence, we felt it was natural that we'd receive such oppertunity and fame. This never came to pass. And indeed, the realization that our way was enough, 'and not some kindling to spark another fire', brought us to our current point, where being so content is not so easy nor becoming of what being proper means; which is, by all accounts, the heart of our adult reckoning: where we look close rather than afar, in both satiety and provision. But, in becoming engrained to the manners of responsability, we only see how unfit we are. And the irony is, that what one needs is not a lever and crank, 'as if the gold was so clearly beneath, yet weighted down', but rather we need a sense of where the prize is; or at any rate, what is the preferable result, given the admission to responsability, whereupon it being met we'd grant the fairest of a just experience. Indeed, we tend to view this as doing what is right, but without begrudgement or turbulence; for indeed, much of justice is in overcoming the forces that make you slacken or drift, and by that, either wear upon you, 'as though your principle carries you despite the resistance, or intertia', or you bear the embers of alternative imagery; or indeed, in envy, shame and privation, you exude what can only be described as the ego, and all the forms it creates as it seeks to stiffen you against things you feel susceptible for. "It is more of a reaction, as broadly speaking we admitt, or see at any rate, that it is our 'offness' that has us 'stacking up', per the weakness which said offness brings, and thus has us preparing for what may trigger such."
So I've been consistent, but our momentum has stiffened. And I find myself realizing the trouble of our way, how particular a path its on, and how our environment effects us. For it is one thing to feel the tigthness from trying core work, or squats at that; and another to propely address this, and or proceed, confidently as it were, through the day with such address, but also admittence of whatever lingering pain there is; for we deem it good, and allow for further effort to run it out. But this approach, gets caugth on things while were at home; at least when our home has the effect that it does. Moreover, it does not allow for our own resolve to take form, which leaves us in a state of intertia; and, as the days come and go, too little energy to make oppertune moments count; for which our momentum drags.
 Our short term is not so poor as we deem. As we are the one's determining it. And indeed, we anticipate a mighty shop before we go; but our funds are their own elsewhere; and so I need no apprhension at using our own financial sensibilities and outlook. Credit affors a small amount of interest per month. Considering the temporary stay at this price, and the novel stats and extra payday through April, I don't mind the starting 300 in credit.
 So, a new development: mom just gave me a 1000$. I did not ask, but merely told her I was feeling stressed. Coming into the month I did not expect to move; and by the middle of January, we were near the end of our second pay period, and not likley to have much more than rent for the 1st. We also were to pay for food this month; and a little hickup with fit4less added to this. And so we meant to work more, which is not much more than we normally would; but we keep getting these 4 hours shifts, and thus have to work more days; and this only felt neccessary, rather than sufficient. I reckon we can stay to 11 on most days, which is what we end up doing most of the time. But there felt like little room to consider, and indeed, not even knowning if we were going to get 'stocked up', was also but a low hanging potential. We have received the funds, are getting stocked up, are getting the carpets cleaned, walls cleaned (with Tristan), and mom is looking into O-Zone like things. And we'll add air fresheners all about. If the floors are clean, it should be fine.
So our work is a given. The manner of it may change, but not it's need. And indeed, like the floor, I take it on it's good days, and have no doubts, or at least belief, in achieving that.
 But we must be honest then. No regards are payed to our aesthetique. The value of good and robust health is all I need; and considering how hard it is to acquire, we cannot afford anything but all our effort for that.
 Indeed, it seems there is a moving and resting state, and we need sensibilitiy for both. In the latter, we need to be able to stretch, or at least do something comparable to yin. And indeed, lunging helps tremendously. But we also need some means of maintaing, or at least returning to that moving state. And here I struggle. As all things of the free range or aerobic sort require some sort of motion. And I find that as poor as we are, so are these things lacking vitality. Indeed, if we were better, these things would not be so hard; but it is a challenge to reach that state, when the minor efforts are not accessible. But, I notice that our endurance is what suffers, and that having this is what brings us life and ability. We move more, stretch more, sleep better when we've done things prior. And the full range and sturdyness that training brings is not much but for the strucutural capacity. Indeed, it helps us sit upright, and in one way or the other works our system in a way similar to aerobics. But I believe that withotu activity we whither; and indeed, rest is important, but to incorporate what's been done, and faciliate movement ahead. But again, doing high knees, or skipping, or running even, leaves my legs feeling narrow, our hips a little tight. Part of the issue, is that say we move to a two side model; whereby we do training alongside running, and otherwise focus on exercises following work. And we'd leave mornings that we don't run for lighter activity. The point being, that bring the rigidity of isolate training in conjunction with prior rigidity in our work; so we can deal with it all then; or, bear the weight of training via our dynmaic efforts, 'as these tend to produce and entwine our sturdier motions'. Indeed, shoulder rotations help tremendously. But again, we reach this point where we have no aerobic capacity; as our threshold is quite poor, and we more easily meet it. We can't grow it without letting it recover, but with the neccessity that we move again.
 Ultimatley, I need to find better times to do things which are needed, but consequential when done at the wrong time; and our overall status determines the breath of when such time is appropriate. We need rehabiliation, as to render the jank of our efforts unimpinging. And we need a mixture of restorative and reformative efforts; as to incorporate and settle the efffects of training, 'rehabilitation being a proper settlement, of that which was dealt unaligned', and then simmer and spark the starting and or follow-through efforts. Indeed, some areas are not so essential, or at least precious in their options. We can spend all the time in the world searching for recovery, but this does not fix the whole of light activity. Moving will help, as we have more room and mentality to appreciate this throughout the day.
Let us take a formative approach, whereby we move, rise or rest in whichever suitable manner, by whicherver trick or tat that aids us. Were only competing with ourselves: cheat as you will.
 Yoga with Bird is great for recovery and restoration. She has great seated, neck and groundwork, but alot of her videos are obscure. Many of them are also similar, with some more preferable than others. We had some but there gone, and we need go through and find what is good again. Adrian remains good for standing and seated ---Ease into It has a really nice seated, where she has hands behind, then turn her head, then she folds forward. She is prime for better lunging, be it with emphasis of flow or breathe (e.g rinse). I made the distinction of rise, meaning cross legs, groin, eagle; seated, be it for neck, shoulders or things like that, and then groundwork, which is on our back, knee to chest, hip opener and the works. Most pieces fit these, and generally we are doing 1 of the 5 of these. Indeed, there may be different emphasis, as to hunour your whim or direction. But we need only search around.
 As for activity, here our life derails. As the golden rule, is if work is important and thus that which makes it better, both in pleasantry and smoothness -as not to bear prolonging effects- then the activity which bears us so ought be done not only well, but with propiety; for it is all too common for us to take for granted the effort done, and do something else; which can have it's own twerking effects; and alongside this, we may forget to do other currative/important things --if were doing something else. "Finish your homeowrk, they say, before you play games; eat your vegetables before dessert."
 My history has shown the simplicity and treat through the free range model. It does have its applications as a pre-workout, but shines during non-working days, where we can still bring our body through an active yet unimpeding state. This keeps our system flowing, if ever so nicely, while also furnishing us with the elements for breathe, which makes our rest and recovery good. This model though requires real respect; and indeed, one thing we can do, is find satisfaction in non-dextrous things. These are quite bearing on our sitting, and fail to let our free range sink in. Such a soft and gentle motion can bring you to a narrow and firmer degree. Indeed, be it through a fortuned anomoly, or the simple tidings of expected work, it is ever so pleasing to be somewhere, where such regards are applicable. We must deal with it in merry but also trying ways, and address the our heart in the matter. "Having less to do is a breathe of fresh air, along with room to sidestep our standards, for these matters of neccessity."
 As for training, I feel that it comes in two forms, one being in conjunction with running, the other at home. In both cases a warmup is favorable, but it is most needed at home. The reason being, that when running, you tend to walk there, and by all accounts, move prior to any actual training. Moreover, you run through the session, which brings out the mechanics of your poses, 'imbuing life and breathe into them'. While at home, your just there, still, and without much going into the session, or during, or after --as you don't walk home. In this case, upper body is prepped and lifted by shoulder rotations. In a similar way, dynamic running --maridana, backwars, turning-- also brings you through. Lower body is less easy, and tends to rely on high knees and other free range sort. I think it would benefit you, by increasing activity and lowering strain, to do butt kicks, marching knees, and leg swings. Don't take it for granted. Much of the struggle were having with exericse comes from not doing things well, and not having the wherewithal, or satiety, to respond to this well. Less wear allows for your normal efforts to pull through, while moments of particular form bring it home. Were never quite there, and tasked with doing nothing, 'as if to fend off the negative effects as much as possible: but to an only limiting degree, rather than one which brings confidence and satisfaction'. I won't stop there though. A variety of forms and sequences really brings your consistency to life; as you aren't beckoned to one move or way, and the according drain of posture and positiivty. Take squats for instance. You can do wide, similar standard, or narrow. You can do side to side, where to turn each one; this creates movement, and allows for each one to re-calibrate. Indeed, you can do pulses, where you do 3 or 4, and just do this twice, to make a round or set; or you do it multiple times, perhaps increasing from set to set. I feel lower body is always the hardest and quickest to be left. Pushups need only a proper angle, and chinups need only a stool, and perhaps emphasis on hanging, raising legs, and lowering slow. Pushups even, can go knee to chest, side raise, shoulder touches; and indeed, upon the ability to do repititions, you can vary the reps and sets, in terms of pulsing and round progression. Equally so for chinups. Be it hanging, leg raise, or chinup, you can jump and slow drop, or if reps can be done, vary the pulse per round, and advancment of this thorugh the rounds. The one thing which I am not sure about is core. For we seldom, if ever, do any range of motion for our core. All our strenght comes through isometric. Indeed, we do alot of opening and closing through yoga, but no crunches, leg raises, bridges, back extensions, nor side twists or turning. These are hard to do though and we may benefit from learning how to do them, and what simple/home ready tools allow. It would not hurt us to have a core training, that has perhaps a shorter amount of bodyweight exercises, but then a focus on core motion.
Work remains a tough and demanding affair. But we can remark upon things as to ligthen the load. For one, learn, for each kind of run, what the proper route is, in terms of resting namely. For large, you can easily drop and pause within isle 1 or 2, and especially when getting milk. It goes without saying, that for all kinds of picks, lower bins afford you the ability to drop, catch your breathe, then rise. Indeed, much of picking is about sustainability, rather than fixed moments. For you may rest during milk within fridge, but prior to this your just moving; and so even if you dropped during yogurt, this brings harmony to your whole day; as you've been moving prior to this, and likely picked before. There are few moments to catch your breathe, and so its not about some fixed point during a run. Indeed, fridge is rough as it is quite open, but admitteldy not too heavy or long. And you do often gaze and stay still. Theres also not as much pushing, and you get to stretch pretty decently in the fridge. Discreteness counts when resting, and grocery provides, pretty much throughout. It's one of those things, where the call to drop --to bin 7 or 8-- is an excuse to stay there for a moment, and keep going. You'd figure that the rate at which your asked to do so provides enough moments to recalibrate, and sustain yourself, that even if its not a complete stop --as with milk during large- that your still taking advantage, holding if you will, the drop your called to do.
 Now, there are alot of people I like talking to, or seeing at any rate. Alex, Amit, Bella, Andy, Joey, Sonal, Tristan, Carrie and Carli, Teresa. And indeed, Kass and Hazel, Reza and chenoya, are a fine prescence. The boys though, in paul, morgan, Mo, a few new lads I have not introduced to, lorenzo, danielle, alonza, the one guy with the glasses; these guys I don't jive. But take Mo, who I don't have much of a relationship with; ask him how his days going, say something funny, about things being busy, or tell him something; just a moment: he and all can be one to talk to. Now, being so upfront, or at least in jest, is something that needs some grounds for. I have none of this with Morgan, nor Ybraj even. In his case though, he seems kind to me, and you could tell him something, or ask him. Morning seems enough, if weirdness is your concern. But as for what you feel hungry for, and are apt to recieve, so are there people there, and more for you to at least give a witty hello to. Brandon is the other guy.
A basis for life which I'm fond of is one which, in quality, remarks happiness, but in object, fashions itself around activity. For indded, much of our younger life was a balancing act of doing the things required of me, while still enjoying, and prioritizing at that, what was wonderful and dear to me. Food, gaming, getting wet, friends, light recreation, humour. I cared not for cars, girls, school of the advanced sort, nor what tradition shown ahead.
 Taking this to be clear and true, what I reflect on is how we were pressed into this mindset through our transition into adolsecnce; where we had not, felt longing for what we lost, and felt burdened by all the things we had little choice in doing. And so the idea of ourselves, and the manner of interest that surrounded it, 'and via a source of quality as came from ourselves, ie. prudence', rooted within me as this thing of prevalence.
 Through the years, we never distanced ourself from this care. But our sight of things did change. Like clockwork, we would hide inside, given our parents admission, and we'd simply live well and be happy on our own. But we'd find this untenuable; as it was not satisfying doing something that felt rather un-realistic; that is, we never felt like we had unadorned freedom to be as we were. And indeed, be it the notion of oppertunity, 'as our younger years affored; which is to say, that pursuing things was deemed, and supported, as something of worth in itself'. That, or we would simply work. Indeed, the cause of this, out of emphasis by our parents, or our own want, came through that unevening process, whereby our satiety could never really settle, and so we sought things of the dreamish sort, or merely conjured plans to change our circumstance; to some position that felt new and novel, 'and relieved us of our containment, or feeling of', or at least we made more modest changes, as to gain some control. What followed though, was a chapter in life where we found acceptence of our roles, and found them to be less a burden if we partook in them well, and that they also affored us the time and mean to embellish in our interests and wants. Yet, as we felt that our prior age was unsuitable, per the obviously prowling of us 'living at home, not working, no farther plan', that so in our steady state, we felt a similar sense of something prowling, but in relation to the permitt to be so steady. Leading into us feeling so (steady), we felt a strong sense of wishing to leave; as though our own dissatisfaction was lurking around us, 'giving a scent as though we just had to go, away'. But this scent changed as we no longer yearned to leave, and felt calm and happy with our work and ways; such a scent, as said, was one of contempt, or needed explanation, 'as if we were wrong to be content and satisifed; as though other things had to be addressed, and thus further effort should be done'. Indeed, the harmony in our life produced a generous amount of ability and softness, and we took advantage of this, as born from the part of us which, despite our care and calm for reason and delight, still sought pleasure and abscence from work. But doing so brought us financial bareness, and similarly for our life condition.
