To this day were still re-growing the what we lost, in terms of some place in the world, some integration with other people, friends even. And truly, choices matter, and the one we made to leave Vancouver and move to Kamloops was one that, albeit grand and shining as respected that side of me which enjoyed comfort and freedom, so it sapped, as said, all the formation of ourselves, and any really source for strenght and perpascity.
 As it were, we felt this, and intended to leave and return to something comparable to what we left. Looking back at such a time, we see how well we were; how steady and ironed out; and given our sense of what such equation equals, so is it more obvious that such a time was ready and valid to proceed, and perhaps accumulate more than we had. Right now, we are on similar grounds, and soon to broaden that. But we've come from a chapter filled with no real plot; other than our character. And it's tough, because all that character building was in some sense a solidification and sharpening of the part of me that at heart sought wonder and good, and via mature and candid wording (or resolve). But twas a farce you see, for the good we sought was always there, and needed none of the character fomrating we partook in. All that was aimed at filling in the holes left to us when we removed ourselves from any semblance of work and living. To justify the approach and mentality, and as it were, 'keep the ghost and ghouls away', we so formatted ourselves. But now, in this new year, on a decade mark of a year, we are not so burrowed, or open rather, to attacks and or threatening. We are more strong and sturdy internally, and need less fortifying or rationing of whats incoming. But our momentum is not here. Our health is in a pretty harsh state, and our view on all things adventure and friends, is relatively bare; for this things were always blown up in sentiment and form, but not substance. We have not the fuel that our wish for relief provided, and so all there is is the reality, and promise of our efforts. "The pleasure before was in the imagery of what could be; which was pleasing enough to forgo any sense of how we'd achieve, or to hold back our all to quick reckoning that we would." Were too pleased to be a fool, but are more aware that we have not.
 But the foundation comes to play, of what we see and value. What moves us, forms us, what shows promise ahead. Truly, we are a man, who is subject to the natural care and want of this, and thus encumbant, or at leeast all to common and exquisite approach to satisfy this: work in an economy, use your time to earn purchasing power and exchange such effort for what you desire and need. Foundation is about covering alarming fractures and holes, as to have some sort of steadyness and inquiry in life; to have your head up, as to at the very least be sprung be tenuable remarks or ability (of yours) even; or to throw yourself into something, 'and forget that there's even something to improve'. I feel that we spend too much time worrying about slight detriments, and hold too little time for broad yet useful thinking, while we experience moderate effort and rest, comfort and joy, through basic and fundemental things. Running, for instance, feels enough to fuel our engine, as does a meager planner, for holding our sight of itinerary and funds. In this balanced environment, we may reach a point that is stable, 'our own as it were', and by that, 'upon adjusting to such stability', you may be more open and honest about what is important, desirable, on offer, or even possible. We have tried online dating, and the services are relatively crap. But there may be more. And indeed, relations are themselves a subject no different than work prospects; and as far and scoping we are for that, so may we be for relations, 'or other such foundations'. No matter, we have felt for a while that there was at the very least oppertunity, and by some counts prospect, in contacting professores about our Sum inquiry. We wrote the letter, we just never sent it. Perhaps it could be better, perhaps we need to more ardently liason ourselves. But the access is there. "Were too afraid to fail, or bear rejection, as the promise is gone. Only of what they offer though; not what you see, care for, or can produce."
 I lack not the heart nor method, nor that even of moulding matter, to bear inquiry into Gods and Polis, leisure, love perhaps, hopefully our manner, and surely our own utility: I need only stand
So we have this impulse deep down that comes primarily from pressure, and the sensible wish for relief, and by that account, desire for that which you feel (and have more or less experienced) pleasing. Amon is this mark for me: the one who feels this part of our heart, which longs for something to make it whole. But she may go her way, another taking her place.
 I feel the right approach is one like Bella, where we think of her, view her, see her sometimes, but hope for not, wish for little, expect nothing. And because of that, the year has gone by and we've become relatively close. Not true friends, but more than acquaintences. Matin for instance, is similar, in that compared to mine and Amons little banter about her picking, that so do I have this banter with Matin about how well we are. It actually brings a light conversation. And this is fine, as there's no pressure on me, no stake. In fact, my imagination is telling me to see her outside of work platonically.
 Amon is different though. She is like Simron and Holley; this person I see, the one who will fill me. But I realized some things to day; worth a share indeed. For one, she is not that funny, nor that kind, is kind of a grump, not so much a spazz but something like. I recall Teresa mentioning that she gets into a hissy if she's not in grocery, and today I saw that first hand. I'm not put off by this, but it was something I noticed. And indeed, we heighten our view of her, so that every interchange we have, the kind farewell she gave me as I passed her in isle 3, these spark my imagination to say, 'she's longing for me, sad to see me go; or that she thinksk I'm no longer into her, and that something is forcing her to forgo changing that'. These are all a remark of our boiling enthusiams, that would be elated if she was likened to me. Yet, and I reaalized this not too far back, that we have not really gotten to know here, and truly her treatment of me reflects that. There's this anger then, as though were so apart, and in seeing how there's nothing but our dream of her, that she seems to in reality ignore us, and care nothing about us. This overthrows your acquiantence, and for me at least, tender friendlyness. It is as if wwere on the precipess of being friends. But without our dreams, our view is rather sparese; and I can't actually think there's real interest or intent from me to do anything beyond this. Matin for instance, actually compells me to talk with her, and learn something. Amon draws me little in these regards. She has this sort of attitude, which I find more or less charming; but our appreciation of this is not recipricated by her. And fairly, most people are like this. You generally bring things up. But Bella even, makes remarks to me in passing, mentions things, about her or work, and is quite open to our own remarks. Kass is not like this, but upon a hello, there is some sort of banter. I realize in the case of Amon, there may be a literal language barrier, and besides our fondness, 'which she is either aware of, or not thinking about', that she does doesn't see me or bear any effective entwinement, or beyond-acquaintence, similar to me and Alli. Indeed its hard to view our repor as being fueled primarily by me, and that she is in fact like Ali, genial and relevant, but indifferent. Indeed, something else I saw, was that this sense that she cared for other people, was engaged in the pornographic fantasy that I have about her, but with another, 'recieving from someone else', is not really becomnig, and that she is in fact herself and extrodrinaday, but also ordinadry, like you. Everyone is like this, and indeed, our view of things, as born through genuine want and fancy, but either inflammed or at least overly expressed via our parchedness, is simply out of line with the reality at hand, a reality of which you have many friends, a fair amount of acquintences, and peers at that. We are together in those respects, and this is only more. When we left those years ago, I felt like I did all those eras prior, where time melded our autonomy into the group behaviour, 'where we rested at the top, unexalting, original and true, yet ourselves'. We left though, and in part it was out of youthfull embrace of freer oppertunity. But we left our family, our friends, and even romantic potential. For such fruits ultimately come from labor; and be it at work or somewhere else, so is this 'familiarity' a good soucre for you to afford such effort. "Something we felt too drained to partake in these prior years, on our own." Indeed, the times are different, for me, as were not at school, nor of an age where we invite girls over to swim or do homework. Were adults, working, with mixtures of vice and expectation, but also a lack of the care (per say) to swim. It is hard for work to be a place where you massage someone into having sex. This kind of play was better placed in the peak of our youth, 'where childhood had left, yet we were not disposed, nor employed in the manners of adults'.
 Perhaps we may just settle, and let the pain be soothed by prudent retreat and leisure's softness. "Feel not to brokem or worn love, but rather see, 'and fear not when leaving'."
I'd like to make mention of our finances, as it's stressing me. Foremost is our regularity of shifts. For some time we've been getting 4 hour shifts; which are not so much better physically, but mentally nice. Reza, who has asked me every time he's there to stay, is an option for me, as to extend to 5 hours. The issue, is that he may not be there; and we can try and speak with Dev, who get's in around 9, but its just hard to count on. The idea was that, if we got 3 4 hours, that we'd just stay an hour. But generally, this is counting on such happening, for 12 is not enough. Indeed, I have been getting enough shifts, but we sought for less work days, longer shifts. Our approach can accomodate more days however, as we are transfering our physical effort to running, and lowering our training. It causes too many problems, and I believe that running can more than suffice, and in many ways achieve us the taughtness we care for: it may be the reason were not experiencing this. Working out is no more essential, and should be done as feels right, rather than if it were neccessary. Because of that, I prefer to have our income be measured against our schedule, rather than probable staying longer. Indeed, we got to stay longer today; but oddly enough, it felt like something we were taking advantage of; and I'm fine to do so, especially if our hour-load is enough, but the idea of extending to 11 felt fair and natural. The point being, that if Reza is there, you can expect to extend, and otherwise appreciate leaving at 10. Generally, I find that first bathroom break to boslter us when we close our eyes, either squat or fold, and eat our trailmix. It is a sign of might to not have to, but pretty standard if you do. Generally, I prefer our break being later on, and indeed, do time it so that you have a sense of stretching and shopping. No matter, this extension should be an addition, something that lets you say, 'this week were only doing 2 5 hours, as we had these many extensions.' But we shouldn't plan on this. Indeed, our rent is 900, and our starting shop will provide a large amount. We have stat in feburay, then a big sum coming april, plus taxes. Worse case scenario, is that we have 500$ on credit, and use that extra pay to cut it off. We still have the 700 from mom --which is the rest from which i used to settle our current debts. And indeed, we may just use our deposit towards rent, and by that count, let time provide what was otherwise an equity --which me we provice later on.
 Right now we have 510 from pay 1, and just over 400 for 2. Thats rent. Right now we have a 4 hour today, and a 4 on Thursday. Next week we have 2 5s, then 3 fours through the end of the week. I find these blocks agreeable, for what they offer now, their tandum with moving out, and the breaks in between. Indeed, we can balance out the 2 pay with the expected stat, but generally, this pay 1 for feburary will be 30 hours. Again, for this thursday shift, or any of next week's end, extending is not required, but optional, and a bonus. Also, just tell Reza you have plans. Respond if you will, with the knowledge that you can; and even then, know that if you have the general sense that your leaving for the 4, and he asks you, view the benefit of that hour, and how, at least when combining this sense through the month, you may afford yourself a cut of a four hour shift. For you may pick up or get 5 hour shifts, and thus find that you balance out your accout, that from the extensiosn prior, that you may cut a certain amount of shifts; at least, if this balance makes you dip, the amount is likely less and easier to justify.
Hey just checkin in. Had a few questions too.
 One, we talked about cleaning the carpets and you mentioned you'd split it with me. They seemed pretty grimmy and are likley part of the smell, so I thought about having them properly/steamed cleaned. It's 150 (75/75) to do so, which is probally more than you had meant to chip in. I dont want to be stingy, so whatever you can pitch into that would be great. But that is what I want to do.
 On that note, for my deposit, is that going towards the rent on Feb 1st, or do you want to hold on to that? I would prefer if I just gave you the difference, because the the 500 I gave was part of the money I've earned this month so far. It's no big deal either way. Also, whatever you put into the carpets, seems best to just take that off the first rent.
 Also ---and this is my dad talking, but I listened-- is there a lock on the laundry room door, that comes into my room? If not can I put one on? My dad said its pretty straightforward.
 Again, I hate asking for things because I feel stingy, but wanted to ask anyway
So I don't want this to be some foundation for principples, but rather a reflection, as to serve better action ahead.
 We felt quite poor after training, and so we focused on running. Doing so on both days felt great, and non-limiting for work to come. They also happened rather nicely following work, and this morning, and were immediately enjoyable and rejuviating. But, in the evening, my arm and shoulder hurt, or I just felt a little compressed. What I noticed is that this was not a sign for me to do hatha, but rather a symptom of us not doing any prior. Indeed, your mention to recover and rest, meaning prana and long poses. Hatha should be done in morning, when your the most malleable, and also free to be upright. It tends not to take anything away from you, despite requiring something of you. It makes more sense that you'd do your forming during the monring, prior to running. Indeed, this is where your warrior and lunging come to play, along with chaturanga.
There's something disposable about our view of women, as its like we just want to huck and chuck them; or we just want the attention, or some immediate embrace. We don't actually think of them, trust them, nor deserve their trust.
 Compare this to our younger views, when you were all in on the idea of being obsessesed with someone; that they were all you cared for; and in some sense, this prompted proper address. For sex is quite simply, as it is disposable and forgotten;. "You don't need to kiss on the lips prior." Whereas love, which we'll call this heartfelt want and enthusiasm, 'this elation', requires more delicate steps. As you can't just consume someone, or throw your whole being at them. You have to learn one another's name, what they like, where their from; then maybe spend time together, share a kiss, a hug, then further romances. Sex at that point is in some sense a consumation, but otherwise progress in your connection. Marriage seems to be the end of things, 'as though we are one, and thus celebrating our unity'.
 Amon and I had this moment of dear excitement today. I staired right into her, and a little bit from her to me. It's funny, we only talk about work and nothing else. Though she seems to care about nothing else. We discussed it for like 10 minutes; and funnily, I note that she makes no effort to see me; which makes me feel cross, considering our prior times of 'elation'. Yet, when were talking about work, she makes an effort to show me, and seems more or less excited to. I just felt so free and wanting to stand beside her, speak with her, show her attention. It is what I want; not sex or interest, but connection, to share, laugh, engage. I don't know how to take it farther though. Perhaps there isn't much to do, and we let that which feels parched upon release, go. We are teamates more or less, and so long as were on the same team, we can speak as we do. It should not be about getting her to like you, or admitt you closer. It is about expanding your means to engage. She excites me, her round face seems ever more charming. And I only wish to throw myself in: taking further steps is what I am learning to do.
It excites me like nothing else when I speak with her. When she walks away, I die inside. As she approaches, I'm nervous. Funnily, the truth is in this detail. For I feel nervous when I am trying to avoid her; and I am trying to avoid her as not to reveal our intent, or feel dissapointed as she leaves. Were coping, trying to forget, and I fear being seen doing so. For whatever reason, we don't just stop her to talk, as we do Bella. In her case, I find no trouble in doing so, for I find no stake in, whether she stays or goes. Its easy, when your just doing what you want. Near the end of our shift, I was just doing what I wanted. Asking her about her pick was all I could really do. There is more I want to ask her: what she likes, where she's from. This is all because her qualities attract me. Like they do in Bella, or Mikaela, and less so with Kim. Even Tara is quite kind, and that likens me to her. I appreciate Carry, and she seems nice to see me. Carli is very nice to, and Teresa is a bit of grough, but careing for me. Amon simply draws me the most; but there is not avenue in there. I think this creates our rot images; as if you change the module (so to speak), whereby we may participate. "Oh, she likes me, and she wants it," this is easy to respond to in our mind. I just want to be more personal. And it is not like Vienna, who in her nature, is someone I'd like to share what I actually like. Amon is pure beauty, a great laugh, a nervous hunmour and strenght. I want more from her, in what I can bring. I feel that, if it were easier for us to speak with her, as you do with Tara or Tristan, then I wouldn't feel bad or nervous, as we'd not feel like me missed our chance, 'or we hadn't built up repor in the day to be more personal." It sort of just happened today; and we had spoken a little, that talking to her near the end felt easier.
toothpaste, deoderant, bar soap
Light Grains - Bread, potatoes, popcorn
 Whole Grains - Lentils, Barley, Quinoa
 Condiments - salt, pepper, margarine, oil
 Cleaning - toilet paper, dish soap, freezer bags, garbage bags
 Energy - dried fruit, peanuts, sunflower, bananas, milk, edamame, corn
 Fruit - oranges
 vegg - kale, tomatoe, cucumber, peanuts, seeds
 Snack - potstickers, springrolls, banana, soy, frozen berries
Popcorn kernels
 Lentils, Barley, Quinoa
 Salt, pepper, margarine, canola oil, ketchup
 toilet paper, dish soap + scrubber + rags, detergent, freezer bags, garbage bags, floss
 Cranberries, peanuts, sunflower, edamame, corn
Plates, a few cups, bowls (for grain), spatula, stirrer, knives, forks, spoons
We have 4 mid containers and one big one.
 Big potato bowls, large fryingm pan, pots,
So I was out shopping, couldn't really think or reply. All seems good, but just want to confirm. On Friday I'm giving you 900. You've already spent the deposit, and so for the time being I'll pay 800 going forward. Let's just say 6 months, and include the carpets in that. About then we can bring it back up. But this works for me.
I have to say though, that this all feels a bit informal, and I don't mind that, and I am appreciative of the place/price. But say 3 months from now you need the money and raise it back to 900. That's fine, but there'd still be a bit left on my deposit. Normally you'd just hold it --and assuming the place is in good condition-- I'd get it back. I actually prefer the way were doing it, but we need something in writing to make it somewhat concrete. I feel this whole situation is little bit off the books (so to speak), which is fine, but I do want something that gives some certainty to our agreement. No rush, but I think its a fair thing to ask. Hopefully the landlord get's in touch with you sooner than later.
Did you find the key for the laundry room door? Also, what time can I come to grab the key for the house? And we'll do a bit of a walk through to --so if there is a hole in the wall when I leave, we know it was me. I work Friday morning till 11, so anytime after that works. I guess ___(I forget her name) has to move also.
Sorry if I'm saying too much, I know it's relatively simple. I'm coming on the 30th, 900 then, 800 moving forward. Get the landlord/agrreement, email it, we'll sign it, all good.
<<<<<<< HEAD Amon is someone who I care for, as carring for peopel is natural. But as we age, we have more to do, and can't be so unbridled in our care. That is why we date; to make a point for spending time together. I don't particularly want to do this. I don't want to stop our shine at all. But its hard for that light to show through. In many ways, from Adara, Holly, Brandy, Simron and now Amon, I've only wanted to shine. And I figure that as our light goes away, and she seems there no longer, 'as if in the fade', we think about sex. Were brought down by our natural rest, but feel distraught over forgetting them, but were not there, and so we have sex. I thought about just holding Amon, and this sufficed for our envious and lusting care. I think you have all reason to be yourself; accept what you get; understand that it's not about you. In terms of it being so, if she showed you know attnetion or care, then this is not some huge loss. And if she leaves or dies, thats her misfortune, not ours. In truth, she sparkles to me, but there isn't much that we really convene on, nor do I feel clear or interested in doing something further. At least in seperation from us just being our selves. ======= >>>>>>> 2185b23f70b174f14fc813aede1613155bb7916b
So, on Friday, we work till 10. I found that saying to Reza that we have plans, but will finish our final pick, wipes off any concern. I forget her name, but she is leaving the first part of the day. Then we are going over, grabbing the keys, getting reacquainted with the smell; and signing our agreement also. We'll bring the bricks and lavender, and pine sol to, see what needs cleaning, and leave the odorizers there. Carpets come next monring at 11, so we might just stay a bit longer. Kind of an odd time. Maybe we can call to reschedule. It is our place after all. We've nothing to bring, because were not staying there while the carpets are wet. Ideally, we look at this coming schedule, work 3 days then, and take sunday off. In that case, I'd be more than fine to bring everything in Saturday night.
These feelings I have for Bella or Amon, come from some part of me which is unsure, unsatietaed, in need of someething to prioritize. I realize that part of our motive is sex, yet this is quite seperate from how we see and feel about a person. Bringing it away, I see how detached we are; and why sex comes in --as if some confident and consuming motion, to bear you closer. Without that, we are not united nor tied to someone. And in some sense this should be fine. "Pretend like sex isn't your objective, and be pleased otherwise if nothing else is tampered with." But I see that were not so close, or at least I realize there is space where we are not. Our lack of a bond, trust as it were, feels more apparent. It may just be the measure of pleasure and pain that we address this; for without connecting to someone else, you do so with yourself, your ideas, views, station.
We are going to pay 500$ a month rent to our parents, and pay for our own food. If the rent was 700, so be it; we adjust our schedule, or rely on our financiering.
 We determined that 200 was the min for food, and that produce and goods were a 100 above that. To get 800, we need to work 800/17 = 400/17 each paycheck, which is about 200/17 per week, which is about 12 or so hours. 10-15 would surely work. I just want this etched in stone. I promise not to spend my savings. I'll just eat less, or work more. But make do in any case. I just want some reason and purspose to guide our study and vital efforts around. There is little joy nor rest without this. And I don't wish to change direction for a life elsewhere. Nor do I feel there is room for additions which are not ontop of us already doing well.
There seems to be two factors: condition and want.
 The former speaks to the nature of thingas, and how primarily it is pointed downward. We may take for granted how well things are, but it is through some fine tuning; or our own uprightness. "There is a force though."
 Then there is want, which speaks to what is pleasing and lovely, exciting and exhilirating. These are hard to refute as most desirable.
 Yet reason has a sort of way, in which we are like children who have no responsibility, or act like them at least when we underestimate the force of our deeds upon us.
 Nothing ever seems to be written but that which reflects our inconstancy of experience. And I can't quite tell whether this is so because we lack those ecstatic things, or if were not steady enough amidst our duties; and thereby at peace enough to reap the beauty of the world.
 As history has gone, we've either shunned or ignored those sweetest pursuits: of love and loin, or succulence and festivity. Perhaps it is just me, to feel so enthused at the touch of a women, for we seldom have this, nor have tried the whole platter. "Part of me thinks that once you had, the engrossment would cease and you'd be but with a friend." It is hard to say whether our festering want is part of something fair, or if it is a result of something poor. I need not say.
 Foremost, it is hard to be friends with people, and by extnesion form relations, if your not comparable in some way. And it can't be that you both hate working, or you both like some small thing. You have to listen to the same music, like a sport, go out for dinner or drinnking, or partying, or in study. Nearly all of my friends in the past 10 years have been those I talk to about gaming. We share that enthusiasm. I seldom find much enthusiasm to share with girls. They don't lack comparability, but degree of interest. There's this idea that girls don't like or play games; some of them indeed do. But merely mentioning it doesn't interest me. You have to tell me about, your bring me to tell you. That is the binding factor.
 Truly, it would seem that most people are not great friends with one another. I do not know, but I don't suspect that Chanoya and Bella have a real friendship. But they talk about work, or something they find similar -like hair products, if such a thing came up. We are the same, in that we chat about this or that with Bella or Andy, Tara or Carrie. We don't do it with Max though, nor Morgan, nor Ybraj. I don't know why. My only feeling about them --there's max, paul, lorenzo, danielle, the glasses guy, mo, the two new brown guys, alonza-- is that there's this very young rowdiness, and almost broness, which beyond me wanting to be part of the conversation and feeling left out --which ignores my relationship with Tara and Carli, who surely don't care. Weirdly, when I see Bella partaking, I feel seperate from her, like there's this side of her which I don't relate to: i feel somehow smaller.
 So I don't know if there is something to do. Indeed, life has never ceased to offer me cautions and or success from our way of balance. Indeed, coming to and from work is the heart of it all. But still, we need some sort of responsibility. It helps at least; because were in no real position without that. "But a mere temporary privalege." And indeed, I don't know the base reason for reading Obama, or writing as I am, or listening to Tree Tops, or munching through Dragons Dogma or Baldurs gate. I feel though.
 The contradictory nature of us lies in the ability to be pleased with that which we find so pleasing. Our eating has never been so strong in quality, but lacks quanity. Our health to. And this is to a sort of perilous degree. The consequence is that I feel more paused when I return home, nor truly enthused to leave work, for at least there I knew what was good and right (for me to be there). In our current state, evertyhing feels like it needs more effort; which makes it hard less everything is ready for us to do. "Our rest feels either idle, or unproductive, as we sink into the more passive yet consuming sides of our leisiure."
 And so in truth we are hiding. In angst, as we imagine replying, or needing to disclose our efforts and ways; but also in potential, whereby we stear away from things that would bring something towards us. "Martyring is a deep form of us: the silent treatment if you will; to be so seperate yet connected. Folly though."
 Bring it all together though. Your relationship with Bella or Amon, or whoever it has or will be, is not in some pinhole between a deep time with one another, and that of a more casual sort they have with other people in their life. This deep thing is not really there. It could be perhaps. But it happens through progress, and has but a affective link to you; as is born through teamwork and union, and that of friendliness and familiarity. Indeed, if you want sex and or fraternity, then some other approach is needed. You'll have to be that close. "And it is clear that you wish them to be so fond and affectionate, but then distant enough for you to act more lavishly: as if to avoid a sort of friend zone."
 That is what it all comes back to. The sort of experience which embraces the qualities who's goodness is so with respect to a prevailing condition; and that albeit lacking sugar and cream, it still has berry good and almond freshness. "It's as if we admitted we were part of a war, and what we sought was peace and liberty amongst this; the prospect of pleasure was not our directive, but rather the good that came from the calm and tender footing within our servitude. Truly, such good comes from such a relation; and our perview to this is from the right in the base for such relation; and how we ought to bear ourselves to such; but alongside that, avoid the shortfalls that come from avoiding it, no matter the pleasure pursued." There are men of burden, children with butterflies, youth of adventure, and I, 'a man who lives for leisure, bravery and kindness: and who bears knowdlege for fondness, in his adjusting pursuit: for the dealing blow is in all that contrasts him with being alot, never to shine, with company not from whom would see his glow'
I crave that which, upon our tambur'd lapse, that we see no more. Yet, when I have it, I wish for it not. For I truly want that tamber.
 Indeed, this tamber is a kind of excitment, which has me thinking of those craved things; yet they come and go, and stick with me not. It's our brimming nature which sees them, and so we have glimpses. Our view though is centered on duty and the good which comes from our commision. Indeed, there is something to be said when there is nothing but peace, 'calm amidst our conditioned sea'; and here, it is not the whiff and or memory of fine sights which perks me up, but old choices; and likely those that came when peace was not our resting state, 'but one of worry and slack'.
 What we are to strive for, imagine, be privy to in foresight or potential, such is hard to see. For our commission is to this rich form of life; the kind of life which bears itself through all manner of self address --and a structure to experience it. Perhaps there is truth in the notion of choice, and how past choices have merely brought us away from what would otherwise be a fruitfull attraction. Our ways are not immediate, or made for brief and or working chatter. Nor do our interests and care mirror others --outside of games. We simply are. And indeed I find older people to appreciate me more, while younger people jyust accept me, 'but go back to their spot of enthusiasm'. I want some form of energy though, from those who have it. As a male were drawn to females, as they have this glow. But their inner embers shine through a shell which is all to becomnig of their feminniness; in their manners, figure and hue. Were lit up by this. Oddly though, outside of strict and 'more or less dream-like' views --where the outskirts of reality are humoured-- we don't actually seek much, or as much as we do, so there lies but a common seed of doubt; whereupon an 'electric' interaction, we hold no confidence that such will return. This brings us to want, and from there satiety through dreams.
 Somehting that has always alluded me, these past 10 years, is wholly being ourself, yet attracting or at least taking part in outward matters. We just imagine that some person or prospect will come. But they never do. And indeed, it takes time for you to merely be and fashion some sort of connection. "From the deepest levels as it were." It takes time, but admittedly has shown itself. At work for instance, a year ago we were beneath everynoe, hard strung to merely be. Now, were above, care free with most, unconcerned from our mere being, and pretty calm in our place amongst other people. We've no real friendship though, and certaintly no reomance. In fact, of Bella, 'who I merely fancy', and Amon, 'who I have real want', there seems little room to realize this; and our sense of 'merely being, and over time creating attraction', seems so far off from where I am currently comfortable being. There is this resistant reaction, whereby we dismiss our feeling, 'as not to feel like were hardstrung by patience, and of with murky guarentees at that'. And its all brought back to that thing which favors us: something new, fun, enlightening, relieving, strenghtennig, fortifying, clearing, pleasing even; all so we may better settle, 'and receive what we otherwise make no pure effort for'. It is true that such effort can be made. The room to do so has never been clear; and our connectivity with others does not help this. It is as if we think bringing our self, 'rather than letting our shine draw', is the road to more acquirment. This may be true, or bear merrit at least. But I think there lies a different truth. One that perhaps, in being base and essential, bears the greatest load, 'or shortcoming', in respect to what those aformntioned goods 'and ointmenrs' provide.
 We just want to be ourself. And indeed, there is a questionable foundation to this, and whether we are missing out on being more than ourself, 'and that, as such, you could be both', or if the self we want to be, to the extent at least, 'in scope and truth', is properly assessed as our actions reflect. Because I think at the end of the day, whether it is guidance from Socrates, reflections by me, inquiry to smoothen things out, or effective stability and grace to bear pleasusre commiserately, it all comes down to you gaspingly and or reasonably prioritizing yourself, but thereby hoping for things to come to you, 'as if to balance the great feeling from such priority, with those of the pleasing sort, from without'; and in tandum, we bet on that greatness bringing us so; be it in our calm yet mighty words, 'when we bear ourselves forth'; or attraction. One has never brought me much; nothing more that is, than mistaken glances and glares. The other though plays a more real and limited role; the ceiling finds its threshold of allocation in how much our efforts for greatness can bear other commissions. In effect, the field for our experience, which is to say the world which sets conditions and rules, but pleasure and success also, that so does our navigation of this, when in commsiion with our greatness, in terms of what is neccessary but also pleasing for that, afford us less room or deep care for things else.
 Our house is not a place of reason. Confusion and trying things has little air (so to speak) to breathe and deliver. When we know though, the issues are small and generally soft.
 I think that the broken pillars are of soul concenr, and all else is but a parched memory, 'reminding me that it is really that which blocks us from such, that matters, or is indicative of something'. Foremost is eating enough; for nothing functions, especially work or sitting on the floor; and the running and stretchng that accompany that. Just as we were fine to see our handling of rent as this near term thing, and somehting which we would work for within the months that we could surely imagine, that so ought we have a similar 'rent' for our food, and by that count, by yours or others resources, afford this. Because we are not eating enough. And corn-edamame as a base for barely, rice, quinoa and beans, is quite fantastic. And not terirbly expensive. But neccessary. Were going to eat a lot of trail mix, from walmart, more than a cup per day. It should be like water. And the 20lb bag of potatoes should encorauge us to eat it like its popcorn. Oranges are fanstastic, and bananas-dried fruit and milk can easily, and likely better replace smoothies ---maybe we can go back to having ice cream. Fashioning long form posese, be it for forward fold, squat, lunge, or the nice twist, these are crucial, and reflect the beenfit that comes from doing core poses in a deep and lasting fashion. Range of motion is good, but you need to breathe in order to set your body. Restoration just opens it up.
OUr faith, is that by pursuing what we seek, 'the grace of god so to speak, for thee good life', will also bring what I wish for upon lacking this.
 For our spirit elates us, and elevates our formation; taknig any pressure off of weak portions. When were flat, these cracks become noticble. And so we seek, or fret over the lack, of such elation.
 The irony though, is for those things which elate you so much, that you often seek nothing but what they give support to: those otherwise sunken, or hidden goods.
 This is the issue with peole, who in reason and warmth draw you; yet as people, wills, directives, bundles of fear, want and experience. It is not like art, or a book, where what has been given is yours; and all you lack is the bright eyes to see it. People can be engaged like this, but only so much as there is harmony by you. And indeed, there isn't something wrong which we often have to do, or incongruent, but rather uncommisatory; in that our natural and preferred bend to life tips away from theres. "And it is hard to spread your time thus."
 I think the biggest pitfall is the feeling of envy and depravity; as though the pure comforts of something are whats being missed, and by our meakness, or the other's fallibility. "So unfair it is," saeth the cynic, 'who frets over his loss by another's error'. This is not our condition, not our way, not the balance that need be struck. Rather, it is in that light, that elevation, the breeze so to speak, that comes through all manners of things, 'yet most nimbly and elusively from people'. Our actions and content should reflect our achieuvement of that, 'not what we may garulously earn, or be pleased with beyond our own life sprouting'. In truth, there seems not but empty dreams, if your expectations are inferior to the reality, 'whereby money, sex and fame are gathered'. Our belief otherwise feels like a symptom of our seperation from any clear or expeirence good, 'of that truer sort'. In that, we may embrace, and at the very least rightfully fortify, 'if the company and cheer of men is not by your side --rather than humping or infamy'.
Our great age has come in many ways, from many paths, to ends not so unclear.
 But choice has really left us in a pitter patter; with time being all that's required for ease to take way, and new things happen.
 I think its right and fair that you recognize the freedom you have to achieve, 'and as we nearly accomplished'', a lighter means for stability. At home there is comfort; but no real duty, no plan, and so too much air comes in a chills my vulnerable parts. "Why aren't we eating food, or out there with Bella or Amon, or Tristan even."
 And it's because I've grown fond and fair of great living. The sort of living where the world feels near and calm; the sun shining, graded through the day. And the feeling of security and attnetion through the morning, 'coming to not but a solice during midday'.
 I recall Vienna making that Origami, and this feeling so unique that I wanted to share our past in making books. A cord was struck, 'that this is would bear connection'. And if you want my insight as to the nature of your place around other people, consider what motives and means you take interest in, but for the sake of 'connection', 'as if some route to bring us closer'. Verus something, with someone, that you actually want to do. I like joking with Tristant; it's why he's number too. I have no problem with Chenoya, and I find her quite genial; but I would much rather be emphatic about BG with Andy. And I surely want to talk with Alex and RE. Tara takes no one for granted. She's like a nieve little kid. Yet she's old, with some problems; yet this makes her full of kindness. Bella is just a sweetheart, pretty, in a rowdy sense. She just has that stature. Which can feel forboding. And I like that. It brings me closer to feel at ease with her, 'like two tigers, who are powerful on their own, but with a equally solid rest together'. Words allude us though --pure spirit is all I find. And that is the weird anwser, that 'spirit' is not what forms attachment, merely motivates it. Perhaps that is what love is. I recall Brielle Jacobson and how strong I felt for her. She was my darling. Yet we never spoke. Jasmine had a different affection from, as we were solid, friends, impartial in pithy ways; yet as a girl, we had that carnal connection, in hugging. Love with power. She is the source of that view, 'that character takes time to fashhion, between people that is'. Mattine is really heartfelt, not realizing that she is very kind. And Amon, just has this attractive charm to her, as though she were a woman, who's nervous, yet hard working and effective. I compare not to her though, 'outside those minor ways that have me forcing connection'.
 I can't help but feel bad in being so apart. We are not woven like Bella, in our want or aversion. We share that natural dislike of work, and I've enjoyed are back and forth about that. But were not enthusiastic about the same thing. I feel there is a small portion of dumbness on her part, which makes her liable to laugh and chatter with people. She welcomes you in. Her strenght is that of an athlete, in that she has some formation. But this is taken further in younger guys, who come across as though their spirit is measurable, and by that, warranting of confidence and worth. "Those adventurous youth, at the peak of a prior game." There's a good point here, in that of Holy and Adara, and Bella too, that I feel them strong, 'as though the soul of an adult is there; which brings their beauty out'. Yet they still have the fashions and hunger of children. Amon feels different, as did Simron, as they both seemed more ingratiated to the work at hand. It seemed not a burden. Funnily, Amon doesn't even realize how modest she is in saying 'I've no problem, working so much'. She doesn't even act as though she is working hard. A girl like Bella get's the job done, but seemingly with more burden; which fits with the seeming amount of fun that surrounds.
 Our extra time is preferred in Oragami, as to accept our work, feel no burden, but respect it still: doing things that fill in our time between. "While those who feel burden, have more taste and admittence of things which merely come after."
So our situation could be deemed as the following.
 Were at home, which means less work, less expense, some family warmth and comfort. No stakes. It also means we have no real task, which sheds light on our life, in terms of things that compell us, yet were so bolted. Indeed, the heart of our discontent comes from this, that we simply can't afford to go out for dinner when we like, or do fancy things. Life was never easy enough, to do what was needed and also feel great, and afford to do those further things. "The good life felt hard to reach, and precious at that."
 It's important to note that your stability can be met in a simpler fashion. We have the means and time to reach that point, where we have both control and focus. At home, we have less of these.
 I would say, firmly, that our mom and dad are in our sentimental debt. Not in what they owe me, but in what they allow. It may make snese for us to adjust, appreciate our friends at work, recognize the distance between the reality and our love of Amon or Bella; and from there, just let time go. Look for places sure, but they tend to come and go. Surely there will be more in the early part of February --now that you mention it.
 Were not moving till March then; and Jess is seemingly moving, which will require our aid. And indeed, I wonder what I want to do with my life. Play Diablo maybe, check out more TV. I thinkk a regards baser matters, and the Kimmel effect, that at its core we are simply afraid, that the world will end, or fall into dissaray; and no amount of the contray will cease the concern of it happening this time. Understanding does so. And indeed, it is not that there isn't cause for concern, nor potential for chaos. We must only produce such worries from solid grounds. "And ignornce is a sore that creates unwarranted ones." Real books, as it were, can help, in perhaps astronomy, particualr things regarding the earth and life, or the history of relativity and its prior and post developments/associates. 'The world' so to speak is full of such things, from law, slavery, combat, innjury, not so much crime --though Anna's archive proves much. And that almighty Barrack memoir, or that of our presidents, 'who give us sight to how things are really dealt'. From the year prior, I can say, now, despite having a foresight that perks my mind, that we are still quite amiable to, and at the very least reseting to the Kimmel effect. "It does urk us still, and sometimes brings us off our feet; the whole greenland thing for instance." Yet we learned, and also re-settled, 'from our depths made prior'.
 This is symptom though of being idle. And indeed, less our hearts connect, it is hard to imagine your want for Amon of Bella will come to pass. Which forever has been deemed alright, or neccessarily unproblematic, as we had so much to focus on and revel in. "Our liberty brought us elsewhere, nor found any cause or commision for such love." Yet we feel like we should, when we have more free time. Indeed, this is a bit of a sterile environment, and not one like your own personal one, 'where your control and focus have reign'. So indeed, our want and formating feel discontent and also unwarranted, 'as though we should not be so measured or austere'. The reaction to this is usually one of comfort. Where we work enough to mesh into this sterile environment.
So I fancy Bella. Compared to Amon, my fervor is slight. I haven't really thought about her in some time, only really doing so when we had passed over Amon in the spring. And we just let things by. At one time we felt really off with her; and over the recent months we've developed a good repor. Were not friends by any means, and our footing is a bit different. But we share good personality when it comes to work and hunour.
 I realize though that this slight interest of mine is of the scoping sort, where we've humoured as something more, and by that, subject ourselves to lanes of impulse, whereby we confirm and or exagerae certain elements; such as our want, the worth of fraternity, 'while we ignore everything around it'; and that she shares any of our feeling.
 In waking up to this, it seems that she has her own life, and by that, is not so basically endowed to you; as you may feel, or be prone to at least. It is not that she couldn't like you; but that she wouldn't do so immediatlely, or souly.
 My point, is that our novel fancny and her at best mirror to this, leaves me with little to actually latch onto. If we were strangers, then I may ask her out; for our fancy was oour only real motive, and suited for that. But work makes this complicated. And indeed, mashed up with the possibility, our familiarity and friendliness with her lays the seed for our fancy; only the sturdines of it finds parts of us that overstate such seed, 'giving it more growth'. We dream in a way, thinking this view is true. But in seeing it is not, I feel somewhat odd, as thouhg I am suppose to favor and or heed the truth of our seed, in that it is relatively small. There is no genuine interest or want outside our fancy. And the second it doesn't appear easy, my true feeling is to go away. At least, this fancy and our awoken sense that it is not satiated, makes me feel poor for not pursuing it more forwardly. As though our lack of any substance beyond the fancy is a remark of us being so poor fundmentally; as to not have made, comfortably that is, any further progression.
 I see ourselves and wish for our groudned view to have some more coating; namely from the purpose of work. To let this be though is hard when we are not so folded into the basis for that purpose, namely of its provision; as we have nothing that is being provided for. And I admitt that our time at home could be less about formation and purpose and more about comfort; as if we were truly home. I take some things for granted here, in terms of why we are not fully motivated for this. But I see myself somewhat unskept, so much as our view is not so fully so. OUr eye is on something different, while the ripples of our discontent at home waddle in. We called in for three shifts, and some may think this reprimandable. I doubt not, though I have no grounds to invalidate suscipion. I'll stand by it at any rate; and I feel fair in thinking that were no prone to termination. That would truly be unfortunate. No matter, less our slip brings us away from the grounds for formation (in work), that so in that are we quite set. Those two shifts were taken, gratefully, and our new schedule will take place following this new schedule. We took sundays off, which will allow for adjustments (putting shifts up) easier, as Sunday is one of the hardest days to remove. It also means there is a surefire break. I also set our 6-11:30 as so as not to get 6 hours. And 5-11 seldom is 5-11 in whole, but rather 5-10,9 or 6-10. This may bring us back, learn about, and at any rate provide variety in our types of shifts.
Novel, fancy, wonder, delight, these things surorund much, be it women or mountains.
 But in feeling ungrounded, these things can feel like there wafting away; the scent of them having their own directive brings this. But even if we imagine them still and wanting, only the purest form of this will bring us to believe that they may take to us, 'as though we were succulent to their otherwise abscence'. This never comes to pass though, in reality or whats seeming. But, in being ungroudned, we cant be present, and feel right in enjoying all that is around me; for we in place, and by that count, 'without grief at the thought of not going where we would if no weight were upon us'.
 I look at our time with people, and women in particular, and I just enjoy their company. They have a sort of quality, similar to that of when we were kids; in that we were kinder, enthused by things, and open. Only for sex do we fret over our look and ways. But women are actually nice and unassuming. Their view of men, who they think are hot and worthy, feels like their own mishapen view. While they tend to just bond with those they do in real and friend-like fashion. Where you share something.
 When we were with Jasmine, I never thought of kissing her or anything. Her prescence was enough, and i appreciated that the most. Same thing with Ashley. I never had sexual thoughts about her prior to us going out; rather, in that stretch prior (to may 10th), I only wanted to be with her, and the thought that she didn't hurt me. In similar ways, when we split, her attention elsewhere was what killed me. Not that we weren't having sex, but that she was not part of our life. It seems that fame shares the form of this, in that you just want people thinking of you, looking for you, having you in mind. Bella kinda spoke out yesterday, jokkingly asking if Tristan was bullying me, and that was appreciated. Oddly though, I see the knots in our way, for I don't actually want to be sought, but in some sense be seen as impressive. I don't want to have our own attention be on such folk, but have theirs. As if were drawing awe.
 I can't help but think were not on a sure path. Our solutions for this generally have me off tilter, yet relieved some how, and indeed, bringing in the sight of others does this. A more suitbale compsoition would be one where we had sure fitting, shined, but were not wanting, but rather acted graciously, and was grateful for what I received, or was open to (by one listening for instance). This is the sort of shape where I dance with the tune of Drake, or explore the wilds with Geralt. Where all which is fine and charming draws me, as were not so bolted down to this one dear source, fiddling around. Indeed, as you have moments where you play Battlefield and those when you don't, that so are those nodes regarding girls and friends their own subjects. I find, for instance, that learning people's names, which has always been important, ought extend outside your near circle, and into other departments and high managment. There isn't always time for this, but learning about people really helps, in removing any sense of dis-ease, but also allowing for moments of pleasure and humour, but also relatibility as regards the work at hand. Whether or not people like work is one thing. Asking about school if that's where they be. In part I step back from this, for such a boring and plain understanding seems to excite no confidence in me that we are seeming attractive. Our depths are rooted to an extent, 'perhaps in the safety which we acept within our dreams, where other's have us on our mind'.
 In truth, things flutter, and be it lack of skill or non-genuineiss by our part, 'where such a flutter, as all things provke, gets turned into something bigger than it ought be', that
 So, let's bring it in. I've been thinking, 'why do we work?' It is a vital factor, but only because it's provision is not accessible outside our employment. We've rested too much on the simplicity of our needs, and akin to a cynic, recieved donation and or housing from others. In part this is poor, for what it would take to give you more, whereby the common wanderings with careers and friends simply can't be provided for by our family; who's keeness and ability mark the line in which we can't get anymore. And so, by their wish for me to have that, they don't merely wish to give me enough for myself; as it encourages me to forgo those other things. I can see the point in this.
 But, if we are working, then it seems we ought live in a way best for that. For work is trying, and despite birthing liberty, ought do so in abundance. "It is not oil well if but drips come through. You need buckets." And so, by the downward nature of work, ought we have contrary forces. And to evoke and maintain these, control is needed. At least, the effort to create such force becomes itself trying if your means to do so is squandered. Control remarks room to do this. "As if a better handle." Second, is we need, and benefit, from focus, whereby the fruit of our labor is at the very least in tandum with the foundation it brings. We need to appreciate it this, and feel ourselves within the bond of neccessity and liberty. Whether there is ease in work, gratitude from its corresponsdance, thriving from this, and oppertunity ahead, is one thing; but that we would feel fair and fine in our work, 'as perhaps reflects the basic understanding in this inquiry', then through all storms we have a direction for just. For we may fail, lose work; but we are clear then in what we need, rather than idle. Too much of our days have been spent wanting, longing, looking for a road ahead. I saw in our time prior at Superstore that all we needed came from this, and that what was more fufilling occured from it. But we have to be on that road. There is too much wandering, enviable and gratuiouts lust without it. And indeed, no one is ever still. And like one who has committed a crime and is subject for the effect, that so are we, outside our effective bound, liable to feel the want which come otherwise, or the uncertainty as we try for something just. Our wandering fancy and delight, the gleam of the stars even, need not be something fleeing or pinching of our unsteady ways, but can be what they are. Not something more, but fine in being small, quaint, lovely in form, but unsizable. In truth, what we'd like if things were well, guides us to where our footing should be. It was nearly so, but for family we sat not. The road is ahead though, and we have but an appointment with tendering, 'to bear control and focus as we can, and give remedy and life to that which flees from us, while remembering it is of things which in a better state are nothing which seem afar, but rather mild'.
Work centers me, as our provision reflects our need. It is unlike anything else, as you cannot avoid it. You have this sense that your with or without the means for food and bed.
 It is all you need though, and much of our sensibility centers on this being accepted.
 An odd thing is the possessive qualities of our time, where we see woman and think they like us, or we like them, 'and dream of being together; or in a similar light, we feel seen by others, or endowed for great words and wisdom.'.
 The point is not so much that these pursuits are wrong, but that they don't sprout from a spot of stability. It seems more like old tastes and whims that came about in times of instability. In truth, the greatest advent of choosing well, where stability is claimed, is that in all your efforts and time, that things may come upon you in any manner of prescence, and be acceptable; whether they be expressive or passing. For we are endowed to something more, and by that count, impressed by what surrounds, 'as we have a lifted eye'; or we have liberty to express ourselves. Differentm mediums, or crafts as it will, require different skills, and indeed, our old tastes get carried away, as we think upon our freedom how we can invest in those pursuits. But our core is what grounds us, and where we stand; indeed creating it's only layer of epxerience. Socializing, from a unique and man-like perspective, is not easy; though, apt to come quick once you let go of your boyish want. "For all you need is to know of eachother; which in it's simplicity, and thus distance from anything which would reflect want, comes so from submission to something other, but a therein peace to act so simply; which leads to not only common niceities, but grounds for further ones."
I think about the affection one has for a best friend; someone who you share your pure want and interests with.
 There is a difference though between children and adults, as one have everything provided for them, even if they work hard, while the other has some footing in their own provision.
 Because of this, I see how it is easy for the young to be friends with everyone, as they have no weight baring them down. It is important to note, that liberty, which is to say freedom ontop of deeds, does create concentrated forms of wish and curiosity, which may be shared, and by that sprung by another. But because your grounded, 'and not merely getting work done, to then return to your life', so do you need the words and or compatibility to be with someone. For we have our own lives; rather than those which go wherever the user chooses. "Even those young and employed, yet still at home, and otherwsie inexperienced with any regard for themselves," are merely limited in where their unadulterated freedom can go. But the wish is the same.
 So between me and Bella, I feel a fair connection, at heart. But she has her own life. And indeed, my sociability is not to apt as to inquire about this, or find room to share our own. As it were, I felt coming back to work that being so open, or willing rather to share (and by that count, un-nerved to do so), would bring me closer to people. But few ever ask, and our lack of prompting offers little. That, and we don't often interject ourselves into others conversations, asking about their problems and activity.
 I feel though that this is fine, and a remark of our life being so endowed and full of eminance through me. It shoudln't be forgotten that Bella, Andy and Tristan have known eachother for years. I was gone for 5, and have only known them for 1 so far. And our familiarity is great, and I hope to keep things going, rather than leave. But I do recognize that you we don't have this forward embrace of people, at least in terms of ways we compare. And you ought not confuse subjects with what is close to someones lives. For someone may be talking about something, but from the grounds of it being part of their life. That doesn't mean your compatible. The exchange their is given by the nature of your relationship; in no way different than how we shared with Carli and Tara that we were moving; but this was from something in our life, and our relationship was such that we brought that towards them. It could have been anything. My relationship with Bella for instance is not such where we bring things forth to one antoher.
 And indeed, I seldom feel this spark with her. She is truly someone with a mixture of confidence and charm; similar to me. But the matter of friendhsip, 'as though, within freedom, yuo went for something purely, and someone with that confident charm woudl be the most, especially if we were comparable in that'. This core is not sitting there on its own, but wrapped by other elements as reflect our time and experience. Truth be told people are in school, and by that we don't compare.
 In think that our own core is a little too wanting based on our own ungrounding. We've nothing truly fundemental and true which we stand on. For by that, our wants, however at the peak, are finer to sit and rest, and by that, wander, and feel not ashamed. For it is so that I would like this or that, but that we have our own life, as someone else with their own beating heart, so does this will clear and agreeable, and thus not aggregious or despairing, 'as though we should have that heart'. Indeed, the matter, and elation, comes from the outpouring of your heart, over that steady ground. It latches not though, so much as your ground is secure. And by that, Bella (for instance) is not this person who you can't get hold of, 'while all others sit in a blur, unnoticed'. Rather you appreciate all, taking in your time as it comes, dealing with toruble and deliught where it passes. Indeed, there is the most difficult manner of socializing, as an adult, in ways commiserate and non-congurent with your wonder and deeds. That will bring us closer. Perhaps it is just the time, and we could have learned more about people, and by that, built trust that we'd always see them there.
 There is just something which triggers our heart, that someone has this appeal and charm. Indeed, I think back to highschool where certain people did this, but they were above me in some way. I birng little now, but am not burdned by some short contrast in our heart. That is what excites me: someone on my plain. I guess my philosphy is to do things and orient myself so that this does not feel so naughing or grabbing, but merely something which pleases me, calmy. "As though there were someone good on our team. And only our own shortcoming had us missing them, or feeling poor that they were going an other way." Perhaps this remarks our own manner of heart, and how we wish to implore and heighten our liberty. We don't always have this though, and were somewhat like children. And so sex, and an otherwise unbridled heart, see somenoe as truly appealling. In part, we've not come this way, where we can become closer with respect to our heart. And sex doesn't happen one and done at work; only bars. And we could chat, be friends, but these require equal skills, and interests that we find less so, as much as we have heart.
Lets do a bit of a review, of work. Stating peopels names brings me a sense of clarity, and ability to be forward, or communicable.
 Sam and I used to be better friends. She reminds me though of how impersonal we can be, yet work well together, and with familiarity. She asked me for something the other day, and also told me to do fridge, and this is an instance of recognition. Were not friends by any means, nor terribly friendly. But were co-workers.
 Carri is a bit of an old goose, no bullshit, works hard, a good sense of humour. She doesn't care if I do wrong, but she has a good sense.
 Teresa is grough yet kind, and we used to be pretty close. I don't see her much anymore. I miss her though, and she almost knows me too well. To the point where I don't really know what to say to her, as were too familiar for small talk. Oddly, it always comes back to work, as though we just talk about that. Or, we are calm with her, and so don't even pay attnetion, fro were aware she's there.
 Someone like Kresha I barley talk too or acknowledge, and this makes me feel odd, as though were seperated from the room. She talks too some people, like Bella, but not much else. She's an example of simply saying morning, and even by name. We dont interact though, and so even passing by doesn't elicit much. These remarks of mine are so as to foster that fair reaction, whereby we do pass eachother close by, and can afford, or are prompted semeingly, for recognition, and we can give it.
 Alli is quiet, has some humour, but seldom says much. I put myself somewhere within the group of old people, and that of the older gents, like Andy and Alex, but less so with Paul and Mo. Bella goes wherever, which has me a little jealous and afraid. No matter, I say this because although ali is quiet, she fits in with Sierra and kassandra more than I do, and so it's not like were assessing her as someone on the outs, while im on the ins. Such an arrangment is more about how people feel about one another, and shared feelings collaseque. But its also not so cliquy, as people ultimatley go about their day.
 Anyway, Bella is a sweetheart. She get's along with everybody, isn't really a flirt, is pretty confident, a little dumb but not stupid. She's pretty, but not hot. She complaims but not pathetically, more with humour. We knew eachother somenwhat when I worked here prior, and when i came back, I recall asking her that her name was Bella. And from there it was generally awkward, as we would from time to time say something to one another. But picture this. There's a group of us, and were talking, and someone walks in. If Bella came in, I'd more or less step out, or let the conversation change to her and whoever else was there. Or, if I came in, and someone else left, we would continue, but with little electricity. Amiable, but nothing more. In the months that followed I found myself not knownig how to address her. For indeed, you pass people by, and your not always on one sure side of, 'they pass unknowingly, as I would with Krisha', or your not so friendly as to quickly say something as you go. I feel that were in that spot a good amount of the time, and this is nice; and in part where our overestimate of her interest in us lies. But no matter, sometimes we sinnk into the middle, where we say hello, and maybe she even says something to me. But were just not so friendly, or with much to say. This may be our destiny with everyone, less they be your friend. Again, this is good, and shouldn't be something which says, 'were stuck in this zone, unable to proceed'. Let this familarity be part of your work, and soften the concurency of that. Look forward to it, with everyone. Take nothing from them, or fear that your not going higher. This is our life. Let it be so.
I just can't sink into our position, one which has all the elements of wonder and grace, but also responsibility and satiety. I feel like were letting things go. Amon has nearly left, and now were clinging onto Bella, 'as if there ought be one person latched to my heart'. For whatever reason I feel like it's all going away; like their leaving, no one left when I'm there. Are they fleeing though? Will Kim, Carli, Carry, Shenoa, probally Teresa, Sam maybe, but Joe, Brielle maybe, Andre, Joey, Tara not be there? Young people move, and indeed excite our hear the most, yet we compare to them the least. They have no honor and courage, yet are open and perpsacious. I think there's an excess of fun though, which covers their boredom at work. They don't find it a big deal, but there is an odd way to this. One of the worst feelings in all of this is when, Bella for instance, or even hearing Sierra and Brenadon, talk about school; which suggests their fun will remain, but their base will increase. This is a tiddly imaginagiont I find, which does no compare itself to the kind of truth and living I care for. I don't feel like were accepting something, and by that dealing with that, admitting less fun. We've a different kind of expeirence. One though which has no issue being mine, 'as though we were a God, a stallion galloping across his plain. Somewhere were home, and keen only to share with those who partake in their own arts and crafts'.
I can feel myself beckoning to dissapointment. Our excitement didn't pan out as regards Hammond, we missed a few days of work; despite feeling good with everyone, our time with Bella and Amon feels either impartial, or unconsequnetial for our idle want.
 And indeed, the worst feeling is the shame in momving not towards that which we want or see, when were not grounded or active from prudence and deeds. Stillness, and selfishness to an extent, bring me away from such things, and leave our calm views of people in a turbulent state. I let myself see Bella, and notice her, as do I let other matters in. But were fine with this. In an idle, or at least distant state, I loose perspective, and primary feeling, and so feel like this woft of her scent is a) more attractive and b) unaccessible to us, which leaves me quesitoning our ways.
 Work is not going anywhere. The company has been around forever, and even thorugh Covid it remained. Managers go, but Amber, Debby, Andre, they were there when I left years ago. Even Bella, Andi, Joey, Alex, Chenoya, Kreesha were all there. Indeed, Pitt Meadows and the store will stay. The team may change, but so will the team itself remain. There are so many people there who work there for a living; this is where they go. It is like school in that way. I don't wish to reassure you, or give credence to coincedence. What may come is not important, rather, of things near and in control, 'as the store and team at heart being there', that so I wish to proceed in such manner, where ou life and work are steady, like we were at school. Let us experience this and all the color it brings. Where such strides take us, we need not know. We need merely make better choices. Which indeed, would have been to never leave Vacnouver, and to have moved into Hammond. We may still move still, but forget this. Your choices and actions are about the action, rather than that you would act. We are working now. Our means for food is high, as if our means for running solid. Yoga is expanding; we need only learn more. Star Trek is wonderful, as are allies and Baldurs Gate 3. Ark sits there, ready for the mountains and water. As do the fields of Novigrad whisper their charm. We've friends, a good one even, and a land of blue and green. And were safe, through time and its relinquish of sameness, 'and missed oppertunity, or moments'. Let us return to work. Thursday and Friday, 6-10, easy, and back to back. Then monday 6:30 to 11:30, great. 3 days off, oh my. But back to back 6-10. Were there, consistent, and able to see this. Even if the hard nature of work is hard to conciliate to. Let it be easier, and your doubts will pillow out.
The beauty of principle. It takes you away from your moments of uncertainty, as regards and specific and time-spanning knowledge.
 One such principle is that, of your fears and wants, ought they be close to you, not far off or wandering. In truth, one learns how to speak to people, how to learn their names, the importance of such, and even who's name it is especially important to know. But this is not for you pelter yourself for, if you haven't learned it yet. Realizing that you have some far want which we could become more able to achieve isn't something which should make you feel poor; it is the aims problem, not our skill. Indeed, it can motivate you, but for things that come closer. I'd say, at heart, that the aim's farness is based no your un-amiability with the subject/pursuit, with anything you have leanred, and so it just wanders and sits there, raedy to prompt you in moments where you oareparticalry senstivit to it.
 For it is perhaps divine and true, that beginning with ourself, in such a fortunate time learnign the reason and fertility of your own responsibility, that you have not learned to so casually invite a co-worker to dinner, or to even see such want with lauded lenses. Such wishes usually spark within me as something I see as novel or unworhty of hunouring. Indeed, passion arises, as we really seek such things. But our simple skill lacks; not in boldness mind you, as we have been so before; but rather in cultiavting something between such interest, and by that, ushering it forward. "To furnish a room then sit in it." This is the insight, of now, in no other way than learning the deli clerks name. In truth, our ignorance of ourself came to be as our younger years gave no way to any continuity of what we'd experienced as a child. When duty came, we shunned away, with little right or wrong to deal with it. The neccessity of such things felt off, and what behooved me became apparent. All this time we've never settled, wishing to go elsewhere, to be rich and seen. Our want of woman orbits similarly.
 Foremost, about me, is our position within propeity. Working, consistently, as I need, without need to go. All that hovers above is in my view essential. The effect, is that we are merely putting our noses into the forray of other peoples matters, but by that, enjoying commradery amidst our duty, and in that, familiarity and friends. Friendship is one thing, as are relationships, and fancy at that. I surround work though and the delights of the world. If this begs us to see our peers a certain way, so be it. I don't particualry seek friends. And enjoy their company when were in our buisness. I do like some people, and in different ways. But there is not much I like to do. There are those who excite me, to share my love of games and craft. But few for the latter; and indeed, their uncommon spot makes the whole of our acitvity feel want to share it. Perhaps this may be so, in a larger way. But single moments create this, and are good on their own.
 Indeed, the pleasure of a woman, the galavanting and fun with friends, this remark old memories of when I did such things. Though time and perhaps truth levelled me out; and we are coming, full and true, like we were a child, but strong, spoken, full of honesty, humour and delight. The privalge is mine.
 But the day goes on, perhaps to well to be merely pleased that work is done. For it is not so hard, and thus begging of oour attnetion for it. As we feel when not working, so can the triviality of work make us feel so. For one, it is never so trivial, as you must return. This is the point forgoteen, or hard to focus on, when your not actually depending on work. What you may think of, take up, or be merry too, comes amidst such statement, where you marble and rise above this roomy yet concurrent direction. It not feeling so concurrent can narrow what you'd feel like doing, 'as to do something elective, oppertune or ideal, is harder when the percieved room of ease is small, or without foundation'. In part, we'd be more open, and by that, more apt to make friends. This is a community after all, like an army, of a nation, that whoever goes and dies, so the whole lay. I'd like to be apart of this, and at the very least, bind my life to such an affection, for this country or that.
So I've begun to develop some feelings for Bella, truly. This brings with it some very complex emotions, as I can't exactly tell if were just responding to something we feel is given from her, or if there is some genuine want on my part, but therein some confusion, frustration, and to an extent despiar, over our ability to do so. ON this point, it is clear that we've come a certain way in life, and indeed, remarking upon this makes reflecting on our feelings hard. For I don't wish to say, 'well, I like eating well, and focusing on work, and gaming; these things are important, and so I don't like in a way congeial to others'. I also don't want to understimate, or at least fail to add this newer thought, that totality is a reasonable sentiment in all of these dicusssions, and indeed, how we viewed things an age ago felt appropriate then, and what develoepd did so by that context. And now, having made it where we are, and recognizing that we perhapos have a slant to our views, be it in ability or expecattion, that this is where we are, and we need merely be as best as we can be, for I truly do enjoy the sun and birds, the swiftness of work and feeling about, I do. And from here we can better reflect and realize, and at the very least learn something that can be used upon a future situation.
 One of the key elements is that of making friends, and how familiarity breeds this; though not neccessarily to a romantic degree. It is qutie hard, for I know that in all of our days we've not been forward with woman, but rather ambivolent, nervous and averse, and despite crushig on them, somewhat cynical, 'to thwart our own cares, to make them feel unwarranted; and otherwise deal with envy, for some achieve otherwise'.
