Pam, Taylor, Ashley, and to an extent Sam, all treated me forwardly in comparison to how I treated them, and indeed, I never took much, but merely acted; properly admittedly as things went on, but I never took the handle, never fostered much, and indeed, found much doubt and grief at the thought or belief of them not being into me. "For we sat there, moving not towards them but being in relation to them having come to us; but thinking thi gone away, our motion did not avail us with smoe sense that we'd keep with them."
 I'd put as so that we have more want and maens to move to someone. Though the same unclarity remains. For we could do things we hadn't in the pass, yet were equally unsure whether we can. We still don't take the reigns. Indeed, we've tried to share our interest in Adara and Holly --nicole too- and they did not mirror me. This speaks to how were throughout our fostering care; and indeed, we often humoured the idea of them being interested on that first moment of repor. The potential was there I thought.
 Where I lie is in a moment of confusion. For I don't think that Bella likes me, and looking at all things, I don't believe Amon does either, nor perhas that Simron ever did. And it is not so much as to why, 'for I never annocned myself as expecting so', but rather, how is it so that we would make it thus, and are we ill bread, or in some odd manner set, for this way to be neither found nor acted on.
 For pure heart and chemistry never seems to do much. And indeed, when we were younger, closer proximity couldn't have been a negigble factor. Maybe, in part, it is about the warm and yearning feeling you get from one another; which I realize becomes a feelnig of sex as much as you cannot sit with it. "It gives you control to ne so enstated, in your intent; while wanting a warm hug is kind of horrible; as it feels as alot as it is soft and inviting, and thus hard to identify as probale or purusable."
A strange day it was indeed.
 The two central facts, or themese, seem to be around Bella and our view of work.
 On her, we came in, saw she was in frozen, and commented on it immediatley. We said a bit too much, and went our way. I don't remember saying anything else to her; she made some remark about me, about something Reza said about her when I first started working. At this point I was feeling like we were being too friendly and she knew it. I kept picking up on these signals; or rather, it seems my body was tuned as to show me that everyone, particularly her friend at self-checkout, was aware that i had a thing for her; which gave me the sense that Bella had told her. In true fashion, I passed her in grocery staging, she said, 'sorry, I'm just moving my cart', I said in sharp fashion, 'I forgive you', she said, 'thank you'. A little bit of fun. Then, however, she was in the middle of the room, and she said something to Reza, like 'hey Reza, I have a new job for me,' as though she were making a joke about something she could do, Reza came over; and I was passing to go for my break, and I said, 'it's all a cover, she just wants to do clean', and she said, 'dont say that! Ryan, fuck...' This took our relationship to a new level, and not one which I feel either of us are keen on. I must reflect on all of this.
 On the flipside, we were doing produce, which by the way is excellent, as your around people, its really easy to go from isle to isle, and you can talk with your pickers and produce people. We were there with Tristan, I had a little bit with Skyler --which goes to show, that people you haven't spoken to aren't mad at you-- and then, as I was weighing, I realized, that we have so many people here, and are so happy about this. Foremost was this sense that we could be friendly with people. Perhaps we've been too friendly with Bella, as if she were someone special. But all in all, there is enough room for us to breathe, but also inner-mingle with everyone. A good example was today, as Sam, Bella, Carli and Chenoya were talking near the garabge, my name came up, but before that, I was just doing something genuinely, but felt room to chime in. They were mentioning something about a virus/illness on the swiss team for the olympics, and had I been there a second sooner I felt totally free and fine to ask about it. It is true that mo, paul, lorenzo, danielle, and a few other guys like that --morgan to, though he is a picker--are not who I generally talk to. I reckon Carli is like this. I feel, for Mo in particular, that I could be more friendly, ask him how he's donig. I do that with Paul. Mo feels like someone who I am familiar enough to be more friendly. No matter, I just felt like all was good.
 Indeed, the heart of man is whether he should be good in order to live well, or in order to gleam what otherwise runs agaisnt the good. The gettings if you will. For, I've always wanted work to flow smoothly, during, after and on approach. But it sometimes doesn't. And indeed, I notice our lack of friends, and awkward feeling as we pick and are in the back room. Bella was like this for a while, around summer, where we felt quite awkward. But this has more or less gone away. I can't think of a single awkward moment, from anyone. This is great, and holds much room for any imperfections, 'say tiredness' to find no room to be in. But it all comes down to a) wishing for a friend, or some enthusiams, say with Bella or Amon, or Michalea even, or Nicole, but feeling like your version of friendliness is not cared for by them, at least outside of passing, 'as they go for other people and fun'.
So Tristan and I are going to meet up tomorrow. I'd like him to learn a bit about me.
 I had a falling out near the end of highschool, and I switched from Westview to Pitt. At that point I was not going to school, and had no real aim. I started working out and taking myself seriously, like he's doing now. I watched all these producitivty videos online, and extended this to studying. My life just stayed this way, where I took my practice serioulsy, and enjoyed the fruits of this. I always got along with people but had very few friends.
 I would like to mention our feeling for Amon and Bella. As it were, were a bit hollow in such regards, and thus get perked up, but what we perceive from others, or just something that we desire. This creates illustory worries, craving, fear and regret; and that worse feeling in the world: that you are not able to be what they want, or at least, if ever you could, so is there eye set elsehwere. Like were entering a new arean, that we have no stake in, and thus no chance. Or, if we stay on our own turf, we once thought that others would come to us there. As of late I've felt we could just have some communion.
 I had a surere feeling that Amon liked me. But I don't find it hard to believe that she does not. I have little suscpion that Bella likes me, rather, that I like her, somewhat, and that she may be some mixture of overhwlemed, uncomfortable; or were just not so close, and what I perceive as avoidance, is really just her being natural, in terms of me. Most people don't go out of there way to talk to people, less they come close to them. So this is not unnatural. If Andy were by yogurt, Bella would surely go by (the meat). I feel forgotten then, or at least unketp in terms of our stake in the matter. I felt similarly with Amon, who I would regret not saying by to when I left. I am sure that all of this just means were with a good standing with them, and that we have someone to be there with during work. The nature of our work is such that this amounts to everyone going off and about, and so you come across alot of people.
I'll write, as I mean these thoughts to be meangingfull.
 It is so that the part of us which gets bubbly and enthused, has trigged from Bella. She is a real sweetheart; and my thoughts dwell mostly on that, how I feel, rather than any carnal imagery. Perhaps were so well as to sit with this interest. And indeed, we've walked a fine line, letting ourselves go, finding commradery when she was gone, 'or I noticed at least'; and being sharp and commensurate (with the root of our repor) in our responses. "I forgive you," so sharp.
 But this interest does not sit so easily, as it gets meagerly tipped over, into want, or at least fear of her looking elsehwere. One notion that remedies this, if we can sit back and let her be, is that she is part of her own life; and not so much as to reduce the proportions, 'as to say theres more than just what you fear', but instead, on such subjects or things around (of friends and fraternity), that so is she her own judge of character, member of action, particpant in consumation, and follow through. I look at our life and realize that much of it rests in the heart of community, 'making little dips to enjoy the comforts of some one'. We have been part of this before, and indeed, the view of others, and literal graphic imagery to currate it, bers a part in me which feels unquelled, and by that, prone to such want. I realize that this runs in dissharmony to our adherent view, where we sink into the mild yet immovable demands, bear liberty, and commradery in the process. Indeed, how such liberty is defined, for what, with what, so is this a question. But I believe there is an upbeat and forward sense about this; where those little parts of me, that per our nature, 'and thus not for liberty, but a moment's grace,' and or that which knots it up, that so are these in a fine state but ripples, dragging us down little, and calming prior to any ignition. "For which we barely notice." And indeed, if our state is off, and we feel pangs, as though enforcing, or at least bringing awarness to our engendered reason, bears to much feeling of removal and meakness, than so can you remind yourself that those within your departing view are their own people, characters and judges. And I look at the fact, that of the overal basis for which you'd ever mourn the success of another, 'and not with you', sits on something which over time is not worth grieving after; for what is good and lastnig, and generally sought for upon a run of 'ruleless fun', that so would you appreciate that, bear little scrutiny nor fickleness. And indeed, I mean not to be a cynic and put them down, 'as though their current actions were poor'; only that, in our finer state, where our own rythm within the currents of life which reflect this broader and deeper goods, 'the trunks and roots of the tree, rather than some spicy or exotic fruit', that so do we not notice whoemever we may see in one light, being part of this other. "For we are already brightened, and seeking not. Or fine at least, as commradery and the variosu manners of love come through familiarity, or at least learned lessons'.
It behooves us, as it would anyone, to reflect on the actual happenings of your life. Thinking about things and their nature is one thing, and pretty hard; and I surely don't have the means for that right now. But I can speak to things as they were.
 Nicole really set me for a loop, as I was taken back by our dissapointment but by our futlity; or better put, uncertainty as to why things occured the way they did. I just felt they were going a different direction, and they weren't. I had more or less thought of the fact --though perhaps this was later- that Holly did not like me for I did not show interest in her, to provide, or even do something. She struck that cord in me and I sung. I'd like to think she enojyed that. But she had no attraction or want from me. We were not her muse.
 When I worked at Marine, and met Simron and became similarly fanciful of her, I recal having these similar feelings, that she'd like another, that we were hardstrung to contain all our energy, 'squirming as it were, as though we were holding in a pee'. And a few similar thoughts, as I've had with Bronte, Amon to an extent [though truly, I imagined her being with someone else] and now Bella.
 What I learned in that scenario, or what I resolved myself to do, was let things happen. It was not our loss if she left, nor never liked me. For me, I believed, or was willing to at least, and by that, was open to letting things unfold. Perhaps, indeed, our affection for her would not be recipricoted, and a more substantial and or eventful collation would have to occur. I did not give much weight to that. I still sought a deep yet casual bond. I did leave for Kamloops, but I recall this was when worked settled in, and we were free and fine to move adherently. Pretty swell all things considered: eager to see the sun, and embrace the cold. "We were becoming warmer."
 I held a similar view with Amon, though we determined she was married. That belief has lessened, though perhaps inwarrentedly; and because we've lost the record/photo.
 I think an important factor is the default, or rather status and or environemnt in which your resorting to, or admitting to by virtue of ceasing your individual thirst. When we broke with Holly we did this with purpose, recognizing the offerings of work, and how much we could get from other people, and the result was relatively fine. There was less turbulence around Simron, as per the ethnicity, or familiairty rather, or presumptuon, we did not feel our conflictnig feelings swell into a enivormental angst. At meadowtown before, and from time to time now, I feel like the heart of commradery comes from Bella, Sam, Paul and the younger guys, with Andy, Alex, Joey and Tristant taking part, or at least pecking in. Sonal, Mattin, Amon, Jas, Tara and Andre to an extent, feel less prevailing, if only different; as I trust that these guys view work as a privalage, and not something which their own fortune is to avail them from. I would love to be happy for Bella, or Nick, or Sienna, but I can't help but feel they think this is some sort of out, an escape if you will. It makes our department, the people, feel disposable, not only willing but keen to leave.
 These are the sort of thoughts that require durable thinking, and such needs the fabric that can only be woven on solid grounds. I feel our view of the gods, as finely or needingly concieved, equals to such.
 I look back to the original experience, or relation to our current feelings, and those of Adara, Brandy, Sinem, Barbi, Holly, Nicole, Simron, Amon and now Bella, and it was Ashely, telling me were broken up, and hearnig go on that party bus, make out with Brody, then wishing for her to return, to no avail; then Sam, who was a breath of fresh air, but thrown out upon our chioces at the time. How poor we felt that summer as we were set to be on our own, and how palpible our concerns were about our drabbness during weed. And that new years party, where the pulse to engage with everyone was gone. That is not me and I don't wish it to, and I can't apolagize, or feel bad rather, if some girl doesn't respond outside of commradery and minor friends. So be it. We broke during the first semester, feeling as it were the emptiness upon this wanting, or at least objective approach; for which, as a trip, we had nothing packed. I find it hard to say whether there was room for us to properly be, through food, some writing, and the sense of our being. "Not to where we go, but how we bed, through and through, every week, daily."
 I reckon there is something important within that realization we had, in the warm evening, stairing up at the sky, so fine. "Without a problem in the world." Our choices from this were about activity: our life would be so, for no choice was infront of us --we weren't dropping out. But I did wish to do more. Not because reading wasn't fun, or I sought more grippnig books so to speak, but that of those problems, in which the role of our life reflected, so we wanted more. MOreover, we felt we could have more, and that we didn't need to hold back, and as such we could do so. Our choice to go to school seemed the most obvious and accessible. A new semester if you will. And I will note, that surrounding this, in creation and follow through, was not the want of people. It was more of an awkward hello upon arrival, 'as all arrivals feel'.
It would be so nice if I knew she had a boyfriend. Because it's easier to ask knowing that there isn't the possibility of a fat know.
 Also, and these are just my thoughts, you don't have to respond to this literally, but when I was in highschool and asked my girfriend out, I was in a similar boat as I'm in now. I'd liked her, was doubting whether she liked me, and I just asked. Oddly, we just became boyfriend and girlfriend. At least, we were together, a couple, a thing. It's not like grown up dating, where you meet some stranger and ask them out on a date and your nothing more than that. The thing which causes so much worry from me, or doubt, is that I'm too much in that former camp. She may not like, so be it. But maybe I'm just out of touch and your only suppose to ask someone on a date. The difference, is that if she yes to me, we would go on a date, but it would be with the knowledge that we like one another. That grown up dating is basically, 'grab a few drinks together, chat'. You don't like eachother in the same way? Does that make sense? I fear I'm being to young and not just going out for that casual, impartial date. In a way, dating is from the outside in, where you just meet, talk, and learn to like eacother. I work with Bella though, and already have a feeling inside me for her.
 I have more or less decided that I will ask to talk to her (somewhere in private), say I like her and ask if she'll go out with me. I would feel way better knowing if she had a boyfriend prior to this. If she says no, so be it. Also, I mean to do this (whenever I do) near the end of the shift. It means I can get out of there soon, rather than awkardkly see eachother the whole day.
Hello ms. Hickman. My name is Ryan Green, and I am subleting a suite from one of your tenants, Justin Inglis. I was meant to move in Februrary 1st, but a issue arose.
 In short, the suite was a mess. The carpets were hideous, the walls were more or less caked in yellow (from nicotine). I was aware of the smoke smell when I initially saw the place, but my dad, who is Vancouver Firefighter, enlightneed me to the extent of smoke damage. Also, when I saw the place there was furniture, and so I could not tell the condition of the carpets. I went there on January 30th, epecting to move in, and the suite had not been fully emptied. Not to big a deal, but it became clear how ghastly it was.
 I am not living there at the moment, by the plea of my father not to live in a place so filthy and unhygenic. I gave Justin a 500$ damage deposit, and he was meant to give me 75$ towards cleaning the carpets --absurd when you consider how horrid they were, and that he wasn't going to pay for them outright. I also gave him the 1st months rent just prior to showing up to the house on the 30th --a premature move on my part.
 Given what I've said, I don't know if I'm entitled to my rent pay back. I signed the contract prior to deciding that the place was in such a poor condition, and so be it if that is so. But my deposit is owed. And the circumstance is that Justin spent the deposit prior to me moving in. He has told me he get's welfare pay near the end of February, and get this, 'I'll try to give you most of it back'. We can debate whether I'm owed the rent back, but the deposit is unconditionally mine. How absurd for him to say that.
 I am contacting you because he is not showing any substantially initative. He has made promises to pay me back, but I feel the words are empty. At least, I showed not have to wait for the deposit. I don't wish for him to get in trouble, and all of this is just an unfortunate scenario, but I am owed some moneny at least, and nothing outside of speaking with you seems a sure and defined means to achieve that.
 Thank you for hearnig me out, and whatever you can do.
So I had this wonderful talk with Tristan, who is my running best man.
 Amon is married. Closure indeed.
 We spoke about Bella at lenght. He doesn't know if she is with someone or not, which has me thinking she isn't, for he, or I even would hear. I do not know though.
 My feeling is this. If we ask her and she says no, we are given much closure, and a pretty fair run to work as we believe in. Worrying about what we say as if to draw notice is buggering me, and makes our time less graceful or fair. Let's just rip the band aid off. The key take away from talking with Tristan is that we are not in our own little bubble, with everyone else together, 'within the norm of things'. Everyone is different, sees differently, has their own friends. If Bella says no, it will nice, or rather bittersweet. For I wish she'd say so, but the closure, or knowledge rather, for which we can move on and let go, would be nice.
 This does not need to be now. Do it if you feel right. And no hurry once your at work. Near the end of the shift, ask to speak with her, in private, go wherever that is, tell her your a fool for saying this but so be it, you like her and wish to go out. She may say no, or that she doesn't want to date someone she works with, and perhaps you can repose, or then accept it, claim it being bittersweet, and move on. Your own feeling of being with everyone ought be gailed by the winds of your own cheer and grace. For you to see, breahte wish and dwell, of what is near and yonder. "Those around me, or in family and afar, are those I am keen on, content with, and calm by; we need not hover, center on, fixate or begrived, 'as though we were poking our head into one of the many places we say'. Let there be wind, and mere harmony with all that is trying but pleasing and of commune too."
So I had a lovely day. A tough one in many respects, but I've come out with more composure to the possible ends of things, and also more know-how as to rightlfully be. Which in it's own respect, poises your want in a more true and welcomed way; such a way as to not bring dissapointment, envy or disgrace. The kind of notion that brings me close, without being part of a fantasy. I can't help it being there. In part, our want of her stems from such hazy view. The sentiment of, 'liking the idea of her' comes up, and much of our fear today was the emrbace of liking her in a way that was commiserate with a feeling opposite of this. That we really do like eachother, whether it's something about the other, or some shared interest. At least, something that brings us to do something. The esscence of a date. There indeed feels less matter in our 'idea of her'; which is a real want, but of the fansatical of ways. "her attiude and canodr, as brought upon by our speech together, ignites such a view within me". I felt so good yesterday because I felt permitted to embrace that ignition. But talking with Tara, I see otherwise, and as she put, 'she may be thrown off guard, and unaware that you even like her'. Much of this rest on the presumption, or at least guiding coals resting under those embers, that she likes me.
My prevailing thought is that the outcome is not important. I don't wish to act so rashly, wanting to be confident, dreading our reasonable doubt.
 My thought was to just talk to her, ask her to tell you about herself, or that you were curious, or that I wanted to know more about her. Two topics seem to be her baseball, which we've never really discussed, and her school, which we spoke about the other day.
 Part of the issue is the fantasy, which in its basic form is just about me imagining things, and finding reasons to make it feel likely or sure. Being pulled out of that makes me feel dividred, forogtten, that rotten rancour, 'as though she likes someone else, I am not worthy, and there's something wrong with me for not liking things she does'. But I still feel there's a possibility. And I need not be trepidatious. Just surpass the fantasy, and rest on the appropirateness of showing interest. If you truly like her, learn about her. This calms our own interest, but makes you more or less known to her. You may jump afterwards, but it will at least not be such a surprise. We also have friends who are there with us; with different views perhaps, and indeed worth hearing. But they listen to me, and talk to me in generous ways. Tara today did so much for me. It was hard to hear her, as it felt like I was torn from my fantasy. But I appreciate this. I feel less luster and thus less of a crush, but rather a simple consideration, as though she has excited something in me, or prompted her to be on my mind; which in form feels the right fashion, even if there are a few more bubbles hear and there.
The greatest insight here is the liberty to let her go. Indeed, it may just be development, but in part, those who you feel nervous around, or imposing, elicit you to ignore them, put them in a box, perhaps comapre yourself to them, or have a fantasy. The truth, is that there are those, like Tristan and Terra, and Andy somewhat, who I really jive with, and share about my life. Tristan foremsost; he knows of my love of the stars, and I feel fair and fine in speaking about what I like to do. Tara is just a great friend. Then there's those other folk. And indeed, these may remark a real divide, which you don't care about; like, I don't care about Morgan and whether or not he likes me. And this isn't just indifference, but a kind of irrevent aversion, where he strikes me not. Aryn, on the other hand, struck me initially, but then showed nothing. And in part, I could not bridge this gap. He's one of those folk I wished to speak with, but didn't for fair reswervations, but not one's that I counteracted. And so I do think you have to be a bit bold. I am not sweltering over the oppertunity, but possibility, where I can just be myself. Indeed, it feels a right middle ground to be forward, yet indefinite, nor proposing anything. If I had the best mixture of sweetness and heart, I'd say, 'can I ask you a werid question..tell me about yourself, I feel like I don't know anything about you. You mentioned you were doing a bachelor of science, why'd you choose that.' In part this is hard, if only out of fear that the moment that seems there is not great, or were not on such terms to speak about such things. When we spoke about her school, I was just staging, and some how it came up. And so I feel the pressure, as though I am forcig this. But I think the thoughts are right, and actions well. And indeed, let yourself be unsubject to the end, but right in the process. If you never see her again, or someone else asks her out for valentines day, so be it. that is our strenght, not to wait, but let be, and fear not that your passing. Settle into what you may do, which is admittedly forward, but un-intense, serious or direct, in what your asking at least. And truth be told, I do want to get to know her. She attracts me, her personality is great, and I would like to see if there's anything more to her. I submitt to the possibility that if there is nothing there, that she cares about to, then the imagined or at least potential humour and care shared between us is just that: fine yet empty air. Indeed, many of us have this. Matin for instance, is very nice and I really like her. It has to be a definition of a gret friend, or at least person your wokring with, to walk right up to them and ask them how they are. Their view of you is not with aim or purpose, merely pleasantrly, as though were good company. Amon is married; she is surely good company; and indeed, I felt great heeding not thought about here. And I did go up to her, was nice, and walked away. No assessment of what it meant. I wish for that to go away with Bella, who may or may not just be good company. It is hard not to compare what she is saying to me and to others, and whether or not she shows more attnetion to them. This doesn't realyl speak to all their action, just where ceratin moments bring you to.
Nothing effects my life more than how much I want to spend time with people. I never have changed myself for them, however much I would like to be with them. In practice, I end up wishing for them to want to be with us.
 And it is true that I've changed my self in this or that way, say in my hair, or how I view myself physically. But these were all very trivial; and indeed, through youth, our position in life was mirroed by school, and so our regularity there made our measure and view of things be seen from so. It wasnt't so much that I thought ill of school, or was against it, but how I was in it. It was just my life that I would go. And so I'd ware nice shoes, or everything small in matter, but seemingly with purpose to brighten your stride in such environemtn.
 I never went out to meet with people, or strived to excel in our studies. I'd make the comparison from now and then, that now, I'd still be as ambivbolent, but more effective, taking note of the base yet sufficient matter in which we could do well. In a similar way, I see how being somewhat cordial makes you friends, and lets you learn about people. Just step in, learn what you want, ask someone you want to hear more from what you like. Anyway, it dissapoints me that were so obvious in our current success, while back then we just did not have an aim. I won't pretend that socially, where back then we dreamt but never really sought, or acted with any discretion to sufficeincy, that we are only now realizing such thing, that we are insufficeint, or otherwise a fool.
 No matter, I wish to speak of our time leaving youth, where our stillness to people and progress were small. But I will speak moreso to the present, and this sense that comes, of the noxious sort, that other people are so forward and endowed, and by that, not only linked and bonded, but attracted to others similarly bound --if not in direction at least in form. You feel these stench upon you, as though you are just not the right kind of way generally. Our insight though, and the thing taken to be true along core lines --for dynamics occur, but are like speckles mingled about-- is that people are not so grimmacing or coveted, nor so deligthful and or enstating. They learn to like people; motivated by what they see, hear and feel, they become acquainted, friendly, relatable, and further drawn. Where you want to spend time with that person. And indeed, if you have some thing you like to do, that person being in contrast to that isn't a congruent, or at least unnoticble factor (in say the potential promise); whereas sharing such enthuisiams, eeen in a basic sense, be it in your custom to drink, eat, watch tv, snapchat, or hike even. Whatever you feel to lack is no real discredit of me, and by those counts, no real irration warranted by you; for who they see and commensurate with are those they really like. Flings, one night stands, casual and voluputous dating, are neither long lived, worthy of the term trust, nor so consuming in their experience. A lot of work, trepidation, balance, and otherwise innervation outside the fields of peace, make for a rather untremendous experience. Nice perhaps, but rather fixated. I find for the most part that applying myself in any way brings me out of those immediate lines, and into the more sincere fields; where true relations are bade. Me and Ashley were like that, and me and Emily sat and talked for an hour before having sex; she was not giving before that. And indeed, I did not even want her to want me. When I spoke with Ashley, we already knew eachother, which gave us means to talk. You and Bella, or anyone, don't know eachother enough, and so you can't compare to your later time with you and Ahsley. It shows, that such relation takes time; and indeed, creates moments where you have fun together; be it sex, partying or driving around. But you reach that. Nor is it easy to do so. Frmo sincere grounds especially.
As I enter these grounds of sincerity, my dreams leave; which are weird, and in smoe way compare to the non consuming expereince of leisure, but also commradey and friendship. There's no stake in these. Speaking to Danealle today, felt unabtrusive, harmless, and effective. His response was sincere, and indeed, in passing. This is our life after all. Don't forget that. So and so may have this or be seen doing that, or talking to him, but our life is still so. In many true ways, were either beyond ourself, faluting, and realizing our insincere ways, 'like those who party without reservation'; and more or less, everything you see is in their passing.
 Oour vices are small, and not present enough to divert ourselves from the false belief that someone else likes you. Such would excite me; as if some truly outside force, giving to you; which is in need when my life has no force to move forward. From within I lack so I seek from the out.
 I think its critical to remark upon the fact that much of your struggle comes from aversion. Its easier the less complex you are, as you drift with things, and have common comparabilities. We don't even sit in chairs; which is no barring factor, but food surely is. There is seldom a point where I relate to someone about it. This makes it hard to make friends. In being so complex, we are of our own, brought from within. And indeed, there's not been a moment where peace wasn't near and around the corner. But much of what i seek, and through natural difficulty and according aversion, martyr or affirm myself for --our ego as it were-- comes from the fact that your so deep and personal, that to relate with someone requires deliberate yet delicate action. Our affirmation defends our doubts that some like us; for we need to believe that. For when the updraft stops and were no longer on the move, 'or friction creates an equal effect', so we feel the weight of emptiness. "And affirmaiton bears against this, but in folly."
 Our dearest worry is other people, who flee from me seemingly; for we are truly ourselves. I want to believe this matters. Maybe in some sense it does. But it rests there, fairly, on the level where you are not reserved, or avoidant of your proper impulse, which is to ask people how they are, or to tell them about themselves, or something there talking about. Familiarity brings this, as you can join others and say, 'what are we talking about, or what did you just say'. And indeed, the prying force which makes my face flush and are heart slightly squeezed, is the want we've had, the feeling and upright delibery we missed. For it is really about what you want. "My past makes it hard to not let go, and proceed moving forward as I wish. Ask now, inquire then, be present further. Let the hopes and worry furl off." I know its hard though, for so much ease and justice creates acceptence of this. But were near entirely with not of what we sought; and willingly, 'but it is perhaps rapid for us to accept reasons might, however smooth it seems, from it being clear and obvious. For natural imperfection creates the most impure sensation, and by that begrudgery'. It is as if you slightly noticed you were hit by a cannon ball; for upon doing so, you entered a state which made it seem not so. Yet, the prescence is there, the thickets brought upon by past oversight and forbidity (ego). And so, away from the rot, there still drags us a little bit of so. And as I said with maturity, the more you are, the worse it feels to be not so
So the truth is that we work, survive, appreciate and sway into the good that present. Work, our home, our study and interest, the land. We already have this, are with it, on par, having been, moving, and thus we form ourselves, to bring up loose ends, rest of firm ones, and calm excited ones, as it is with respect to these goods.
 Now, a new good is welcome, and perhaps of a novel and giddy kind. The warm feelings of going out with Bella, oh my.
 But do consider the reality of this, that your not so endowed to one another outside of personality; which is indeed something a lot of people have. Do you really wish to talk with her, or is it more of a persona/social thing, where she is outspokem, and thus from a time when you weren't able to jump in, you feel you missed stuff, and now your stuck in a place is familiar but unknown. I reckon this is why I feel thrown. But you may ask, about baseball in particular; as I know this about her, but we've never really spoken about it. And she does go to school, which is nothing I care for; yet my throwness wants me to get closer and so I want to tell this joke about the hamstring --or ask if she knows the names.
 I found it insightfull to see that we are our own entities. Not interwined with others, or so fleeingly disposed, but merely apart, afar. I'm Ryan from work, and she's Bella from work. Were aware of one another, but our lives helm themselves individually. Someone might say, 'hey, Bella, did you hear, Ryan likes you'. And I'm just that guy over there. Now, were friends, or at least friendly, but were not so close to be referred to but in our relation in work. And still, by that nature, where people are just working about their day, going seperate ways, I am indeed someone going the other way. Tristan for instance, and Tara even, are with me, even when I'm gone. Not so much with me and her.
 And so I had this dream, or thoughts at least, that were quite reconciling to the point. That we are not together but apart, within our own lives, and through closeness or passing, around other people. "Like me and danealle yesterday; we were around eacother in passing; while Tara and I are close."
 And so, in truth, we may rest and proceed as we are, unperturbed by any noxious view that she is out their partying with other dudes, hitting everyone up; and that were pathetic and misplaced in not being one of those dudes. Rememeber, work is apart of all our lives, and for me, remarks the progression of it. That was when I felt so good; that I'd loose these thoughts. Indeed, their different when I actually hunmour them; as in the case of Bella or Amon, I am thinking little cues or remarks by me show interest, and thus I'm nerved to not say good bye. But life goes on, and we should emrbace all that is there, rather than the warm sensation around one item. We loose everything else. When we broke off with Ashley with peatered around that, and lost sight of who else we were with. And indeed, we've done that to an extent regarding work, where we left for our own sake; or at least overlooked the medley of folk and the overal community around.
 My advice is not to be so thrown with her. Were not best friends, I don't need to step into conversations. Breathe on the fact that she is chatty, and thus your not missing out on something, nor are you any less special because she's stopped to chat with someone else. She isn't part of ny day, but our day, in the store, the department. And she may leave. But we may stay.
I think all of this rests on a relatively sincere interest by me, but perhaps sits in a way as not to be so well expressed, as indicated by us being thrown by her. And admittedly, it is because I am unique, with nothing but my own to bring; and familiarty makes it hard for this to be brought forth.
 What if we just didn't like her, and weren't thinking of asking her out. What if we just went on the path of our life, and she was but a part of the whole. I understand that interest is there, bundled in, a little compressed, and by that, prone to nerves, doubt, yearning and thus misery and shame. "Misery is an open wound, when air hits the part of you that you had covered; and by these wants which you push down and put pleasure and reason on top, so upon these being removed, the want sits there, hard to bring to practice; the misery comes when you try and stear away from the effort it motivates. For upon reason being removed especially, and any integration with the broader good and ease, so you feel like it is it: and misery is simply a shame, fear and dissapointment, that your not into such attempt.
I just want to write a bit about this, but it seems that we may be present with everyone, and what we have. But if there someone who your not so present with, yet have an incling to be, then it seems natural that you'd feel like they were going away. Its as though, from my view, there is a divide, and so I have a hard time letting go, and just bringing myself. Remember that people are there, who are not just for you, but with you also. But others are not, but I am near them.
 I trust this, can rest, but some folk leave me less settled. For I've not been with them, and it feel hard now. And indeed, it was easier to ignore them; yet this conflicted with moments where we would talk to one another, and how it felt like this was coming from a place of nothing.
 With Bella, I feel fully there, yet I still have questions; perhaps rightfully so, as I just haven't been with her much. Unlike Tara or Tristan, or Andy even, who I just walk up to. I would like to do that with Bella; because I like talking with her. This seperation I feel, but wish to speak, has been formed into a 'well, lets turn this into a crush, that we like her, that this means we want to be with her'. Yet, there is no remarkable attraction, or wanting to be friends. I just appreciate her company. Like Holly, I just want more. Indeed, you have to be yourself, hold it up, be true to what you have and lack: philosphy is the marbling of this, as to bring form and structure, and by that, an advancing and procurring ease. And then flourishing in liberty --or the up parts of this.
I sit here in relative disease. In part, it seems fair that our foundations have been shook, as our place and time failed. I am moving our expenses up to 1000$. We can afford that for the mean time --and our parents will help.
 But, in doing so, there was part of me that enjoyed, or at least sat with, the pleasure and potential of havnig something with Amon or Bella. Learning that Amon is married is great, as there's no pressure, and I think of her not. She's just part of who I see. But now Bella has entered my mind, and turned me into the fray. For indeed, I've been a bit to suscpetible to langor over this possibility, as our foundations were not secure enough to 'cover up' the reality of this. Lucky our parents had gone away. But as they've returned, I feel not myself, and that I should just let things go. But were not present, nor seeking other comfort, but joy.
 In this process, we've humoured the fact that liking Bella may lead to something; which is overly exciting. And I want to believe that she could be excited about this alos. "Really, he likes me, oh my. I've never thought about him like that, but maybe. He's nice, funny, mature," I want to believe that she'd say that.
 But were on a precipice now, of honoring our committment to try, yet this feels like a rowdy outrcry from our trouble at the core. I feel that there is not mucnh on our end which would make Bella respond to us; only so, that I may know, and by that, go forwith a try. But were blind you see, to the reality, which itself causes despair. For it is as if I am being told to sit back down, but in ways that are just basically off, and in no need but remedying.
 The truth, is that I feel Bella and I are nice friends, and i really enjoy her company. But I've avoided her, or at least struggled to be present in any meaningfull way. And there's part of me that just holds on to these missed moments, where familiarty and composure could bring mere interaction. We ignored her, thought of her, and this was fine; until the foundations which makde room to humour this, or make such an excuse, fell away. Now I either want to approach her, or back away, in our real intent. Yet, I feel completley gone, like she deons't exist; and this is neither true, nor affirming: instead I feel pathetic and ashamed, like were cowering.
 Ideally, we would not make a rash move. I think it's fine to be open. We've spoken to Tristan and Tara, who have given me different degrees of advice. And I may or may not act on it. But indeed, I feel it would be better to settle, and hang onto that little nugget of truth, which is that I want to hear more about her and baseball; just as I did with her at school. Its hard for me to just be there if I don't know her. For she is talkity and chatty, and I have a hard time jumping in, or parlaying with her, if we are more acquianted. That's all I want, to be acquainted, so our expeirence is better. I think that humouring the optimistic and or novel fact of 'hey, if you want to acquint yourself with her, why not take it further, and ask her out'. The possibility is there, but not any known oppertunity. Maybe she responds to the suggestion of this. Maybe were in our head and she has no mind for us. It feels better to let things unfold; perhaps you'll ask her out then. But I'd be fine, if I could just show up and feel as comfortable as I do with Tristan and Tera, Carry to. That is the thing afterall, is that I don't feel comfortable being myself around her, fully at least. By that, you must be able to speak openly, as you do with Andy. And I can't do that unless were on the same page about baseball. And I'd like her to know I coach, and that I hate reffing. Knowing the hamstrings is a martyring affect, for you to feel confident that you'll attract her.
I feel myself thinking that work is easier to be pleased in. Our choices grant us such. And living just comes right on infront of it. We need not prepare as though some trying thing is occuring.
 Yet, were so uncomfortable though, as if were being asked to go out, have fun, let loose as though there is no tomorrow --for the abscence of fear is like that. And I feel awful, like I want to through up. For I have no energy or familiarity for this, nor not much to do.
 And I never thought this would be a day, where things got so right and easy that I was nausus about the lack I had for such free time.
 And it is indeed hard to bring it. Pure quality comes to play, and the word Joy and maturity feel more relevant; as though, in our peace, we'd work for those.
 For I wish people to be happy in whatever they have. And I don't want to think ill of them, or that their unhappy, only to make myself less jealous.
 But it is what I like then. And it would be the same for some other. As I may not like you, truly, but wish to know you more. I appreciate your company, and feel myself for the worse in being unable to have it.
 It's strange really, more than I know. Why are we trying to change anything, wishing for much. Because we can? The idea put into my head is a unpleasant one, making me nauseas, for it suggests that we are free to do so, that there are no rules, no measure, no custom.
 We can be hear mind you. We just need to accept things a little, take time to settle in. Trauma does this, or some disruptive circumstance. You realize things, how well they are, that evil doesn't lurk about.
 I guess the comparison is one where your in a working state, however light, and that you don't live fully, but in tandum with the marks that create a good day, and shift tomorrow.
 But I feel drawn, with friends and commradery. Even Bella, who I'm a little thrown by, is just somebody who I haven't been true enough, 'as if I weren't unafraid to be myself, and say hello to her; but instead, being so thrown, by her talkative yet bold manner, we held back, for we were taken by such and afraid'. That just being so, removes all my nerves. For whatever happens, were all on our own, yet together, free. This is perhaps what seperates me, that indeed, I've experienced whats known and peace, tranquility, felicity and joy. But via rules, and perhaps too many, for we were unable to be with comfort and stability in those weighing sides. Work feels weightless now, and I'm enured to go out and see. But prudence still lingers, on those rainy days, where my joy is rather in the soft, close compairons, 'of those enjoying eachother's time under the sheets'.
So my feeling of Bella is similar to Bronte, in that in that case I felt sure she cared, and was distraught at the thought that she didn't. For when then do I go.
 Reflecting on that whole time since is fine, as indeed, it seems to have started and ended with reservation, but more centrally been about choice, and the pain and adjustment required upon bad one's, and the inexperience, and perhaps refusal from how you adjusted, to actual be, like somebody, or just try to be your own.
 It feels as though there's a promise, a hope, a dream, of someone, as the world once seemed, to spot onto me, and latch. Were so bright arne't we? Why shouldn't I feel this.
 It is because your not present, nor active, and by that, fair or sinciere in your interest. It would be easy otherwise. And indeed, the idea of a date, or process into fraternity, is one that may not be so serious and or provocative, for those you ask. It is merely so, that you are with someone, by some matter of ease, and ask them for a date. We expect though that their in love with me already, and that the date just brings our bond to exist.
 Light in the matter, yet core to my heart, is this statement of remedy, and how poor choices prior left us in a state where new developments could be made. Instead, we overstepped, brought them within, or let them hover without, 'as though they were not expected, yet fluttering dreams to please us'.
 And there may be something to this which is merely developmental. For pretty girls, or grades of boys have this effect, where I can't just be myself.
 In the case of Bella, I've heard that she coaches baseball. I heard she went to school, and we spoke about that. Very nice. But I want to tell her that I coached soccer and whether she reffed, as I did and hated it. This is a small thing, but fairs a solid restraint on my ability to be present with her. I was not able to make such connection for it felt rather simple and we were thrown by her; for which our response was to ignore, or be loud, or at least more impressive; which is a kind of procastination-affirmative process, as if to make yourself known, when in fact all you wish is, in those little respects, be known.
 This is what I'm having a hard time to do. And as I resist the urge to merely do so, lean little on the fair oppertunity from it, but then feel the forces which seperate me from such willingness, to be vulnerable, unpresumnig and close, 'for not may be your claim', and those that bring our assumptions and fantasy to life, that so in this tug, I am left with no means besides that of breathing. Our words seem to have little effect, nor my views. My experience feels flawed, and I feel like were trying not to embrace but a new flaw. Nor am I very hungry.
 One insight is this. That if you were near, as you are now, despite the rampage, and held the confidence and familiarity of folk by you now, that in that there holds a hope born of similar fashion to the one we've had, in that both project something, some sort of confidence, expecation; in other words, a matter of false belief. And I say this, from this current position, in that the seemingly full dissmisal of dreams being a reality, still leaves with it the oddities and peculiarities of dating. Certainly for people you've already known. All I want to do is work with Bella uncerimoniously, but unerved also, as we once felt. We were otherwise fine passing by. But I feel like I'm avoiding her, or better put, not embracing the simple questions that reflect my curiosity and interest. That to this end I'd like to go.
I can't help but think of loss, and how we've been up to now. Bella just seems like another person who I've not done right, and will move on.
 Our confidence seems the proper measure, as if to accept our state, that were not so endowed from others, and as such, have only ourselves; and to le to general want, or ease in being youreself, shine thourhg. "As though you weren't afraid they didn't like you."
 I am not sure who I am though. Are we still living as though there's some problem? We left maple ridge, and from there, had no character to do as we please, ignore duty and deed, and act indiscrimanately. But we held not the wisdom to be right where we were, as we pleased, but just. Too many times have we fallen for the promise of being something, or someone even; all because we were afraid of being not.
 Indeed, the root of this begins in maple ridge, when we just were, little aversion or jealousy. We had no ppertunity to see where this would take us. Would we have the strenght to bear being ideal, and moderatley due to familiarity. Could we have been so confident and fair. For upon leaving, we saw nothing good or worthy of just being; at least we felt we had to change who we were, because we were not so familiar. And we weren't happy just hanging around, but could not get right in. I didn't want to though.
 Throughout highschool we had no ambition nor chase, for things outside of us; be it girls, friends, or some ideal. We just did what we had to. For there was such weight. It was not as though it were some free roaming expeirence, just absent of pursuit and want. I didn't overt myself from the deed, but dedicated little. I lived problem free.
 But I've realized that our life had left me, and we could not sit with the ideal and hand, nor without the place and chase of others. We had a problem, were not ideal, nor with fun otherwise. The weight was consuming. I feel a relative amount of it now. So we moved. And in doing so, we started this action, where feeling, or my sake, was humoured as the thing to treat. The unfortunate truth, is however much I gained, in friends and familiarity, we did not bear any final success. We had no ideal or claim of such, and so our standards dipped and we had no aim after school. We also weren't so privy to cars and fun like others, and so we worked for not. It was a similar situation, that there was something to do, but we had no diversion for somethiing better, 'as if, however much disregard, we were still pleased'; but we had no proper oblige, or ideal. We could not just be, for were not so freely dependant. Perhaps this is the obvious flaw in our youthfull performance --taking for granted our ease upon moderate success, but because we were naturally given.
 I reckon that if we had stayed at Westview, we would have just sunk into an ideal, or at least held more ground to oblige it; as though we had something to surely do. Whereas, having moved, we leaned on friends and fun, but could not go far enough to warrant any sufficient resolve to that. But we had no reason nor aim for something ambitious, 'for the future'.
Without a doubt, the source of our pain, or grieving, is the feeling we've lost someone. But it is all coming back, as though there are things we've lost before.
 And indeed, I admire wisdom and will speak to its effects. But reasonably or not, we've sacrificed a great deal. Indeed, it has been our calling to feel not while trying to be wise, 'for it has it's own pressures', and so called to somewhere we could be, we went, but left all behind.
 It is my luck that we are fine. Reason has never abandoned me in these regards.
 But I've just never been swell enough to let time by, feel its flow, inure to humility and fear, and create an experience worthy of the gods.
 Ineed, leaving Vacnvouer was not our decision; at least, life was not deemed to be slow and settled from there. Even then, I had a fine time, writing our back, meeting Kyle, playing Ragnorak; and we held prudent, never smoked, found ourself, in truth. In fact I chalk it up to woman and fame, or expectation that is, for me appreciating what we had when we left.
 Now I've returned. Its been a year in Pitt Meadows and I have to say I feel great. Parts of me feel unsettled; for ideal and or neccessary reasons I'm still attneding to our health and ways; while work seems have benefited, and is otherwise in safe and steady flow.
 But I have been overly ideal, perhaps resting on the dreams I had, that they'd come true. First Amon, now Bella. Indeed, I've dealt with these things well. It's just hard to realize what you never had.
 And indeed, it feels like we've left and are only now coming back, able to be ourself, show someone were interested: move for our want. Indeed, it is hard sitting, ignoring the mishap with Hammond, feeling the ignorance and pressure from my parents; while still accepting it, and seeing it not for a problem. I don't wish to mvoe to get a way, but for the reasons of focus and control. In this, despite feeling a little torn in the matters of comfort and pace, I feel that matters such as Bella, or things of an obscure sort, for someone like me that is: who has more to do, and has less experience doing so, that it pays wonders to have some reason, as if to marble your intrigue with a sense that, given your fear of not running, it will be alright; that being so, that whether she is here or there, soon to leave or forver in sight, that we ought be on her side, to her, not presumptive or consuming for ourself. If your curious then be so. The time that allows this will naturally open us to this. Were not a boy, so worrying about having something else, as though the reality of your dreams, is no longer right. We will settle, and perhaps find closure and strenght, where we feel the trust of others, who bring me enough to peace to dream elsewhere.
Having said all of this, and feeling a little more sure, I feel somewhat stricken by this thing with Bella.
 I don't know what to say, foremost because I feel like I am making up reasons to be invovled. And by that count, I feel as though I am barely in it, and thus not drawing on her much. It's strange.
 I guess I don't have any reason nor comfort in saying these things if there is no purpose in doing so. The reality is that I'm not drawn to her in the same way I am with Andy. With him, we talk about Baldurs Gate, and I go up to him because of that. But Bella, I'm taken by her charm, her candor, her slight depricating humour. This is no differenct than any girl we've liked. Funnily, we spoke with Kelly, but she didn't want to date someone from work. No harm no foul. But everyone else was seeing someone. Maybe I should have got to know them better. I think part of the fear in doing so, is that if your break the aura your in, which is to say the charming draw you feel, and perhaps repor you share, that you'd realize you really don't like her much. Or in another sense, we imagine they like me, whether this is based on their treatment, or because we want it to be so. Perhaps were to excited by the prospect that we just dream about it. Indeed, this causes us to struggle in moderatley treating them so.
 I would like to believe that there is some part of us which just wants to be friends. Similar to Bailey, who I just spoke with; and she had a boyfriend, so be it. Indeed, you like someone, your gonig to talk with them about the matter at hand. But during that, our in tandum, you may ask personal questions. This shows interest. Perhaps I just want to be in that matter, but more, forever. Part of my heart has always wanted more, but found grace, humour, wonder and peace in letting go, having those matters come as they be. I find this to be fitting for the nature of things, in her ups and downs, pitch of the day; and regulariuty of you being in it. It is not always sunny afterall.
 I guees where I lie is in the true matter of fraternity, love, the carnal gunk of lust, the smut in our mind, which draws ever poor want and comparison. Indeed, of all things, these matters have me ebbing to and fro. And certainties, control, comfort and focus are measures, natural in real life, but needed, or at least noticeble for their not, with respect to these waiving emotions. Emotions which I have no remorse not feeling. They are so hard to reconcile with our attempt to be ourself. Indeed, friendship come's thorough familiarity; and who you like may just be some matter of dream. As it were with Holly, I thought she'd be there with me. But not I with her. I feel in some sense this horrid conflict, that for such love and fraternity, which is not about what you get but what you give, that so am I concerned by the nature of its permission, that work feels less so, either from this or in commonality; and that as such, we need prioritize and focus less. Yet the fans which bore the flames of our life feel flat. Can these co-exist? Well truly. But your want for this, as though some added relief or gift, is not so. For only children quickly jump in; and for someone to be there, you have to show interest in them.
Boy these thoughts go on and on, and indeed, my feeling of this and that changes.
 There lies this horrid dread, as though everyone has passed me, that I've done nothing so far, that to tip toe in, would be like saying, 'hey, I know how to dribble now, pass me the ball; and their over their kicing and juggling'.
 And for true, we've gained nothing in the matter of money or friends, from the time that I put my life ahead of all things else.
 I find the root of the problem being, that if we are really fine, and unsubmitting or humouring the wish to grow, which being ourself demands, that everything being more or less present, rather small from constance, makes me feel like we can do more, and that were wrong for doing things that bring us to not; or at least pull us as we try and move into other people.
 This is the battle, this is the inquiry. Whether our approach upon complacence was right. Perhaps its been fair all along, and little problems made us go the wrong way, 'giving up who we were in that right place, only to find it hard and lonesome upon being yourself, great, but new.' Were coming over that hump now, and little things remain. As we came here with mom and dad, did not humour too much that we wouldn't move. Perhaps were holding onto the company of friends; and the idea of Bella, or anyone for that matter, seemed nice.
 One place to begin is with the question of experience, and whether what we sought as a child, or in youth, was really worthwhile, and we merely endowed to something different; seemingly out of neccessity. But, just as our way of life made our condition pallatable, was there not some quality to the experience that made you feel so? In some sense, when we would retreat from work we'd live as we did while working, but seemingly freer. Indeed, the point of our way was so that work would not seem like an influence on how we wanted to be. Indeed, you may compare what we deem credible, as a means, during work and not, and how a great deal of effort was made during the former as to make our off-time feel as though work weren't there.
 I think this is where the conundrum lies. For the reality is that you have to work. Simple. And indeed, expecation was never an absent factor, till we merely went it alone and took work by our own hands. No matter, it would seem that if flavor was all you sought, and some sort of prudence, or austerity in pleasure, that so could we engage in those green goods and those with people.
 Might we bring our senses back to reality, and lose this overpitched state. No more delirium. For the truth of the matter is that work, despite feeling ease, by which no ardor, or at least producing effort is done, doesn't mean that work isn't attended to. A general flow if you is only gained from proper sleep, which, albeit admittable to go out and stay up past 10, so this offsets the flow, which stiffens work, and requires further work. Indeed, our motive for moving was to address, or at least humour the feelings that arose in being so endowed. For mistakes brought disease upon us, and we were committed to that, rather than friends. At least in the pains we were experiencing. Now we have the advantage of knownig everything the method for friends. But we know, despite feeling that accepting our post, as was once sought unwell by our guards, that so now we feel it either smirked at, or excused, 'as those beside me pass by', that so we now know at least that this is possible, and perhaps wrong to say. The strides of our self are all thats needed. And indeed, be it with the upper tidings of might or the lower bindings of grace and melody (giddy ease and calm), that so are making for what I deem fine, but in plenty.
 So looking at our day, where people are invovled, 'which is some part of neccesity, for our whole life, be it in this spot or the other, is around some watering hole, which only children can deny, or late dependants', we have great energy with everyone. Most people at least. Some I speak little to, others I say a lot. Someone like Matin, I say little to, but there is that moment of pleasantry though. Amon, for a time, struck me, and she drew me to be invovled with her. I was hoping for some kind of admission, or sight of interest on her part. I only really talked about her pikcing, not herself. For a time, I knew her to be married, and 6 months later I find myself talking to her about UPH, and I thought she was thrown by me then. I used to be a little awkward with Bella, as though I would just walk by her, wishing to say, but not really knowing her. This was a little hard, and she is often the life of a conversation --very chatty as some call her--and I had a hard time being that. Indeed, walking by people while picking is hard, and in fact is a sign that things are well when your head is high, ready to smile and or state someone passing by. I do that with Bella now. Maybe were not there though. It depends on me a little, for I may have some sort of impressionability, or glare on my mind. Not feeling like were the center is important, but that were just being our self. A weird dynamic. Someone like CC is quite nice, and I find that she and other people are now coming up to me, about work and stuff. This is nice. I feel like were known. Bella does not though, for the most part. I just find myself wanting to talk with her. And indeed, I recall one time talking about bins being dirty; this was 6 months ago. And we just got along so well, but then kinda of looked back and forth, as if, 'I'm gonna go'. Now, this sort of happens, but its more of a fine and pleasant departure. It is usually within the conversation if I am done.
 I think the take away from all of this is that you need to learn to get to know people. People you want to know at least. For it is easier to be with them, and stay, and say hello and goodbye, if you know one another. You can ask about them, have them bring something up, and vice versa.
I think this, and will state at any rate, the indeluable demand of being me. It is one where right of reason and might are indisposable, and in some sense, neccessary to lay claim to times demand.
 For I did move one day, leaving all my friends, and my innonenct, genial, proper and beneficient life. I could not lean into the show that became clear to me. Something which I reflect on as being part of prior to the move. I only think that perhaps time had given me room above, where I could draw people, or at least be with them as I was, mighty and grown. Yet, I doubt still that I'd remain that way. It was if the Gods bore me away, for they saw me unsure and set for any coming direction. I believe I could have been as I felt right, or un-averse to, had I gone to pitt, but alas, no.
 The point, is that you don't know why you feel the way you do, and what is right to attend to, if any; and whether there may be a full willing and unformed, 'but genially adhered to, as if flowing by customs demand' whim, or oblige. Compared to our own direction, self driven, self made, born of self regard. Out of neccessity as it were; for spine and brain told me the efforts I saw were too much for the wrong thing. It is all just hard to know.
 And so it is hard to deal with emotions, dissapointment, determination and foresight, but also appreciation, subtely, waiting, and foremost, unattachment. Dreams it seem become those things, which upon certainty, fill in; as if permitted, or at least sought, within the crevices and holes that our composition unacocunts for. Yet by its sturdiness, affords some falsity. "To soften the edges."
 These dreams come pillowing out though, primarily it seems when all is seen to be so good, upon say a tragedy, where you realize what else is left, that those dreams, perhaps built of fantasy but also hurt neccessity; that so as those dreams go, where someone you imagined as there, bade for you, 'perhaps by whatever confidence your falisty gave you, as to some point where such person would be so --or fortune, fame, fortuitiy', that I fight this admittence of there not being so, as perhaps the trauma or misfortune has me needing to settle on what I truly have.
 So for you Bella, mine you are not. Nor does such hope for you come easily, or clearly. Your just a bright eye'd smile, that makes me feel warm, and wish for a hug. But you have your own life. Reducing this is from our own impurity. And indeed, as dreams fit into cracks, 'where pretense and uncertainty need formation', that so do false ideas, mistaken fears and doubt crawl through. And by this, so are we given false sight of garnishes or fortune upon you. And indeed, in my heart I'm sorry, as I am for all women I've loved, that I've hated you, brought you down, found ways to see your life for what it really is: to remove the dreams you may have, to rid myself of my own rotten imagery. "Smut has a noteworthy effect here, as my jealousy and seperation have this pungency coated over the link that I let go." And so I forgo you, not to be mine. Perhaps go, or be near, I do not know. But for me, it is I, who must be, communicate, and believe. It is our luck if time treats me to speak with you, and furthermore if I may be open. If not, this is lifes way, to bear those down their direction: which is assuredly their own, not mine.
Hey, don't know if your sleeping, but read this when you may. Just thought I'd share a little bit about me.
 I'm a little bit sad. This whole thing with Bella is got me in wraps. It's not so easy to explain, but just consider what I have to say. My writing is meant to be thoughtful and personal, but not responded to bit by bit.
 I like to imagine that someone likes me. I do this because I pay so much attention to my self; or its better to say that I need to pay so much attention to hold my life together. It gives me great things, but the energy and care I've given to this has brought me away from girls and friends. And so I rely on benig this way even more, because it's all I've got. Many mistakes I've made were unneccasary, and I realized some time ago that I need not shelter myself from people to be as I am. There just hard to mix sometimes --and I've always choosen myself.
 Anyway, I want to imagine and believe someone likes me, but it usually prompted by me liking them. They have some sort of energy, humour etc. And this sparks me, like an ember on gasoline, so ready to be lit.
 The way I'd put this, and how I'm feeling right now, is said person becomes some sort of dream, as though you could just imagine them being with you, and how amazing that would be. But when something traumatic happens, or altering --such as losing out on this freedom of dependance-- then I snap out of it, and see the reality, that my draem was false. And this crushes me, and so I feel sad. Beacuse I don't think Bella likes me, truly. And I dont sincerely like her, which is the hardest thing to admitt. I just want to live my way; but in doing so, I'm also left wanting for someone else to be there with me. There is part of me so eager for this, that I am fooled into thinking that i found someone. Like a fisherman, who is so hungry for bite, that he feels the line wiggle and he already thinks he's won.
Let us unwrap and bind these feelings for Bella.
 What comes to mind is our time with Sam, and how that fell through by my own mishap. And I recall sitting there, following the fire, feeling as though we were apart. Similarly, with Ashely, things started off fine enough, but fell apart also.
 What's worth noting is that prior to our seperations, we were more or less fine and content with the way things were. But in that, we didn't exactly put a step forward. To get more from them, or give them something; as if we had to nurture the relationship. Instead, it felt fine and novel to just do as we please, and see eachother when we will. But, most importantly, we'd gnarl like a hungry dog at the thought, and eventual reality, of them not being with me. It is as though we were a baby, fine and happy so long as our mother was there, or her seeming so at least, but upon this being not, we feel left, abandoned. Indeed, confidence comes from family, friends, and other things which are not so hoisted, but ingendered. Moreover, you may have your own character and ideals; all which is to say, that going into such bonding, or comnig out, that you have confidence and then poise respextively.
 When it comes to Bella, we've known eachother for a year. And I've always felt enthused by her, and like we have the mixings for repor. But there's been awkwardness, as though depsite our chemsitry, we weren't that close. That chemistry remains, but we have become nearly friends.
 In part, we get too enthused, and act as though were best friends; similar to me and nicole, where were just throwing ourselves into the repor. I remember though that we are in fact not so close.
 Any by those counts, it's recent familiarity or just the day that we become open with one another. But time passes, and our enthusiasm, paired with affirmation, have us feel a lowering back to reality, where I see were not so close.
 And indeed I think this is a good thing. For rather than throw yourself at her, for enthusiasm sake or waht you feel affirmed to, let us be far. Abscence makes the heart go stronger. Because indeed, circumstance can bring people close together; but then upon change bring us apart. To me, our right action is in that which brings us together. For seeing her again, we won't be so close, or electric. You'll have to gain that back. And in that, when we are not so familiar, by cirucmstance that is, here is where confidence lies, to be subtle, and bring yourself in.
 I guess the point, is that in any realtionship you ought bring the right amount of focus and energy, per the state your in. Things develop where less is required for more to be allowed. But there is a rythm. Imagine you had gone away for some time. Your resting state together would be reached. And I believe it's quite far. Maybe it is close, and were just simply friends. But I feel then as I did with Ashley, when we were with Colin and Nadia, and she was like, 'why aren't you having more fun'. If I can go and see Bella, as though our friendship remains over this time, then it is as if we are already close. At this point, I'd say, like her, and ask her what you will.
I sit here in great despair, for all the subtley I've not been.
 It is hard though for I am generally too confident in myself, ashamed almost to be as I am for it is not somehting which I try.
 Affirmation is the bane of things, though understandable. FOr in our way and joy, we feel the pressure from work, and our inconsistencies in things. Moving out is drawn to me for a smoother expeirence in this. For much of my struggle is in lacking certainty, or at least a hold, on what I'm supposed to do; and being at home makes it harder to see that. "I must be firm at home, which can be rouhg, especially when your not."
 Anyway, I feel as though we've been too apart, not really present, hiding if you will. And the truth, is that outside that full-willing experience, where nothing but the present exists, and you feel emboldened and drawn to go right in, 'and put your whole self into someone', that we actually don't like this person so much. There is like, but it is not our whole being. Perhaps that is not enough then for things to happen. But I regret not being as subtle as I am, liking as I were.
 And the irony, is that if we really liked someone, we'd need no dreasm, no affiramtion, nor fear of what we'd have to be. We'd also have no doubt. There would just be somehting we want to bring forht. And indeed, there is a garulous and affirming view of sex, and a loving one, where you don't think of the pleasure, or at least thing your recieving, but rather who your with. That fair embrace remarks real interest. But it is in fact more sublte; compared to the urge and feeling to go out there and get something. This isn't about what we get, but being who I am. It's just hard to be so confident.
 The real point is how much do you really want to be real friends. Perhaps only slightly, but there is possibility for more. I think that is the way. To let all things go, just be yourself.
It might not be overstating it to say that your place in life, be it going or in place, determines what you feel right to involve in. Right now for instance, I feel wrong, or gated, from looking at videos, movies or enjoying games, because I feel unaligned, or at least flacid in our gait regarding work. In part, this is because the news, our place at home, and how I feel harstrung to accept our place and abide the deeds around me.
 Lets take a deep breathe, and define what is really ours and of worth doing so.
 For one, we work tomorrow and the next, 6-10. My emotions are such that I feel somepart wanting, but also greeted, to be invovled with everyone, and to that extent I'd stay later, which is fine. Otherwise we'll just follow the schedule. I do hope we see Bella. I'd like to share with her how I feel about our failed move, ask her about her V2 test, and talk to her about baseball, shortshop, her's and my coaching/reffing. This is a grieving motive in part, for I want her to be close. But liberty will find its day, and we will be both more able, and thus ligthtneded, and otherwise limber and thus confident --to hold want, nicely, and move for it, gently, regretting not that such small interest is what you have, and what you can afford. It was easier when work was heavy and present, that we could believe oru wants to be more, but unavaialable. Now that we are free, seemingly, I am thrown by this. A whole discussion this is
I've always wanted to be better friends with Bella, but she's always made me a bit nervous. Early on, when I started working with her, I felt weird and awkward, like things just didn't fit; and I'd find myself turning the other way.
 But as of late things have begun to click and am glad that we've gotten a bit closer. She's someone I consider a friend.
 But we may never see one another again, or if we do, there may be no time for something personal. She may have forgotten me, or be less fazed by our recent talks.
 I am willing to let that go. For she is merely a friend to me. I am glad to know this for myself, and I need not her to know it. Personal questions are what you mean, when your talking of basebal and school, or speaking of yourself, or saying these words to her. Be it the news or want to move homes, these distractions are not a threat to me and her. I already know how I feel. If time allows I am there and open. If not, we haven't lost. It is not for me to say whether she will remember me or no.
Drip drip drip, down the chimmney we go, to no where that I know: peace seesm to allude me.
 Moving here is a dynamic of understanding, or footing at least, on what you have and for how long. And as such, were more willing to look up. But not because were free, but because we need do.
 The core of inquiry, the truth in life, is a mystery. And we in some sense are coming from states which seem sufficeint, comfortable, consuming. But are not per say reasonable.
