That she hasn't reached out is not a red flag, but only highlights our concern, for getting something done. I wish to be excited admittedly, and indeed, this step is a good one. But I am waiting for things to become clear, and am more or less ready to accept and adjust.
 I feel the core of my life, from this source or another, is in this warmth of my heart, the peace in my bones; and bringing this all through motion. To see the blue sky, clouds around, the fresh air from a scooter ride, or hearing birds and wind no a walk. Reading, be on the loue or out on the fields, oh my. Hearing Einstine, our natural sense, oor Aristotle, so speculative; Hume and inquiry, Montainge and his own route for ease, so my feeling lay. And those on youtube, from Huber and Ben, Kyle, Rich, Daily and 32. I connect with these folk; in ways that surorund the object at hand. Most people in my life lack such care, and only naturally so. They afford me strucutre, support, as Tristan has become, or even humour and understanding. It is nice to know Carry and Carly, Tara to, to tell them of my move, some struggle I have.
 Perhaps one ought be in motion to remark upon such, and not only see but elicit a lasting resolve to remain so. Were just remembering otherwise. When slow, other things come to boot; and indeed this is fine; we only feel the border of going into motion or remaining slow. At least in our furnace below.
 I cannot help but abide and enlighten the manner of duty and deed, that life is such, asked of us all, and varying only in softness and hue. What we create is not some thing comparable, but a mark of us being however settled. But such motions, are born from your open heart, liberated from concern and grief, this is literally you, in full motion. One rule is not to let old flakes of whimsy and want guide you; the reason being that your still on your feet, merely upright; while such wants, though not neccessarily poor in object, are so at heart, in that you seem to forgo the liberty which frees you, and by that count, the remnance of deed. One is free to go, or at liberty to see. And indeed, the former bears excitement, as though your unschackled legs get to run. But where will they land? Take oppertunity when you can. But might we still persist with that greatest of sense, and liken to the commission which stays with this? What of the friends and family we have now, the home at hand, the lands around? None so fashioned by their base acclaim will see such things are repativie and same-like. They vary in their own flavor and hues, but per strucutre and familiairty, a marriage being the epitomy of this, so the rock stays. It shines differently though, and catches different layers and angles of light. As do we see it glowing or when lit? What is life but this path? Ought we move forward, look back, remain and persist, endure, with the fairest of steps, as per this side of the path? For we may all take one, sittnig here, walking there? It is by my count though, that liberty, deed, ease and awe are to be our stones we stride on, and measure our posture and gait toward.
So were not moving. Frustrating and unfortunate. We knew this might happen, and were ready for it.
 Lets just breathe for a moment and keep some perspective. The truth is that the close field offered the most. Would have been nice, great. But we've not lost anything. Our time is well, we'll earn more money, and indeed, this is just something I want to do, but don't need. Were fine. The value of doing this will merely guide our able hand, and we'll appreciate it. But let it reveal what is untrue now, and worthy of address. Keep on, things like this happen. You may catch a break after all.
I'd say, with much clarity, that our ultimate thoughts, unhinged or sidetracking, are about true want, real happiness. Yet our doubt, or at least friction, is of this notion of 'things I find dear', and how these are achieved by purity and resolve; and that such is awkward and or obscure in how you communicate; which limits friends and oppertunity, the motion at least.
 Developmentally, we may just not have been in place enough, as perhaps guided by what seemed fitting for our purse resolve. Unfounded this may be, it still brought us away, to the point where, even just being here for a year, I already feel close with many, friends with a few. So much has gone on though, and were not even at a point where many years have passed. Perhaps we never had the ability for this; and indeed, our positivity does not seem to hang much on any promise from this, as we only mean not to go, rather than forever stay. Were just at peace, present and grateful. But what I may recieve seems unclear, nor gurenteed.
 Nothing distances me, or witholds me, from making friends, exciting oppertunity, attempting something professional in a manner short of excellence, 'and romance is just some mingling of the three', than our ability, want, ease, skill, and or excess of sensibility, 'whereby being protective comes from lacking, or being burdened, occupied or busy', to be all be lacking or withdrawn, as a result from me and our core ways, or some impulse away, or excuse as it were, and the balance of this in relation to how open and wanting you ought be, and your accessibility and familiairty with people. This ratio has never been so as to bring me any real lasting change. For I was so myself for such a long time, that by the time a year or so passed, despite beginning to reach that point of recptivity, I left; for I had nothing till that point, largely that is. When I went to Superstore, I found myself content, and was by those counts less wanting to move, but be. I wasn't particularly opened, but acquiesed no less. Time went on, and as though we were on an edge, I left, not realizing that I could still be me, and continue to acquiese. By those older counts we would have been happy, and at the very least not moved early. So are we the wiser. Then, moving to Vancouver, I found myself more open and amicable, getting to know people a bit more. Such a state ought not be confused as though one were some nervous lad, planning and or preparing to commune with people; as though they were sequestered, but attempted to bridge the gap. I removed the gap, just was. Indeed, we were still espoused in our pure way, but per our aura and sense, was near to everyone. I feel this way now, in the utmost. But I see, that what we lack is not means to bridge such gap. Indeed, as you learn one's name, that you are in fact, to be more akin to friends, to hear what they like and do. By those counts you may offer yourself. Asking how someones weekend was is a form of that, or how their week has been. A mistake I've made since coming back is in expecting people to ask about me, of which I was fine to respond. I never ask them enough how they are. Sometimes they don't ask me the same, but no matter. You are at least being there friend. Indeed, our embarrasment, or perhaps conservative sense, and guilt upon actions missed, brought me to feel detached from Bella; and happy when ignoring her, but not when we wished to be friends. Or feel open and amicable with her. Asking her how her week was felt off. Though, and I will take the moral, that had we just waited, and done what we did, that we would have been in that state. But alas.
 What this comes down to, is that manner of conservation, this thing your too protect. For it is one thing to say, that I have all these rules, and guided from such, and thus have interests and standards of the proceeding sort. And indeed, it is great that you feel open of these things, and learning how to ask people about themsleves, and in return share about you, is at the very least bonding, and by that nurturing for such likely companions who work at your side. It is a whole other thing as to why we have these rules, and given their difficulty in pairing with others, since your so different, that is there worth in such things? Are they needed? Moreover, do the things they provide warrant the price? In effort sure; but in the weight they produce, against other things, so we must evaluate.
 I think, perhaps, the truth of my life, is that I would have gone to Pitt, gone to school, worked out of highschool, moved locally, and stayed at work, had I been happier.
What does one say, in a perfectly parsimonious state; where there is no such bad, less something befalls him. Indeed, prior times that were otherwise lead to want, and thus grate against me as I try and accept.
 It seems worthwhile the mention the matter in which you make friends, which is all anyone really seeks. For obvious reasons. You have to be present; and indeed, asking is important, but you have to relate also. There may be nothing you do, and that's fine. But there is an aspect of oppertunity, or potential, which you must realize by being open yourself. Indeed, it seems clear to me that our feelings for others may just remark our pleasure while were there. And indeed, the regime that we share is in part how we relish them: that as we see ourselves going, and returning, so will they be there. Which is nice.
 I think philsopohy could benefit from the sensibilities that guide our common lifes, which is, that your motion, as born and bread upon propiety, is all that really tells of what is good, desirable, suggestive, and vain. What else is there to say. Indeed, we may conjure ideas, employ our skills, bear oppertunity and leverage. But our lives are the measure. And for those so well off to afford philanthropy, wonderful. But such life is still it's own; our view of it is not a relevant factor to its quality. And indeed, however shiny your steeds are, you are still included. "A grumpy king is hard to envy over a peasant at peace." This is rather divisive of me, and signals that there's room for us to more amply speak to the truth, without qualification. Simply, the life we live is subject to change; but that you were in it, however it was, is the proper matter. Indeed, whether it is in what you recieve and handle, or what you imagine and pursue, these are from right grounds. Philosophy's whole point is that such light should not be shown without connection to the matter which bears it; and that, per the nature of this matter, the mould at least which you fit, that so may this guide us, when the truth of it is considered, how not only to be to live as any given fit is deemed, but also what can be affored. "It says what is enough, in whether a position is loose or tight."
Nothing feels so intertwined with this feeling of being open, than the notion of our real selves; unadorned to the novelty of want, nor the rancour of a far world, or envy from lust. What we have is the deep roots of commradery, community, general purpose, and a sort of vital passion, for all things work and liberty. We show up, smile, am glad, listen, and share. Not everything comes up, nor is everyone so open; they are there to work after all. But our effort and gait is more or less of our own measure and determination. All that's been is further understanding of this; where it's ever clearer of what we have in being there, and over time, through motion, how it creates a just and liberal grace. Promulgation and attraction are pesky factors, but not neccessary. Let our direction guide us then, catering not to what seems nice and pleasing, and in tandum with our sense of good. Instead, let us just be with the good, do what we may, elect for what we please, but get carried away not. Our impulse, and sense of worry and shame, come from the feelings we've had, that house themselves in the part of us which is afraid to be so content. I know this almost innately, as the motion which follows is worth its own lust. But memroy sometimes is all we have, or a sliver of distance from that proper self. The un-reality of those sparks of interest, 'the embers from wants that were once sought', becomes ever more present, and in a way, stings. The effect is one where we are not sad, but rather somber about the sense that we are even the slightest bit so.
I feel quite serene. At least, notions of everlasting and expanding joy are on my pallete.
 My remark though is one of the matter of writing, or thinking at that, and what issue we have to exclaim? For i do feel as though there is some structure, or form in life, of which natural momentum oft carries, but not so entirely. I feel such sinking now. For instance, I look at our training, and indeed, the novel benefit of feeling aesthic is giving me some angst; but moreover, the actual gain from doing so is somewhat missed, yet it requires fair attention; and being where we are, I cannot give it, happily at least.
 I guess I have this gentle view of something rather fundemental, namely our indeterminate peace, of the sort that all manners of trouble and worry are ill-placed, and that perhaps only ill-wisdom brought us away from the contentment were reap. Indeed, 2020 and Vancouver on, feeling just paired nicely and fairly with happiness: wonder as it were. Just some choices brought me away, or this sense of being unlauded in our content. Some wisdom might have prevailed, in letting us nmerely step onto the channel of life, where no envy or scorn is made; nor even anything of the sort, such as flaunting or prowess, was there to be shunned off, 'or dealt with'. It is clear that the prescence of such, dealt with or not, is from a place that prudence is not. We are onnly beginning to taste this pallete, of that fine and primal sweet, in people. Indeed, the coarse memories and faith from an era ago, hold meager weight, nor much humour. And our taste of things, brings me into a fine and sweeping state within real things. Where we merely are. I carry no promise of woman and friends, as I see them there, un-siren like in their appeal or welcome. We merely are, genial and warm with all, close and someday closer with those who spark with me. Just going is enough.
It is very possible that people are familiar with one another as they have no reservation; that and they've known one another a long time. But I can't help but feel, and see, that people are in this big web, saying this or that, about work or personal, to whomever. No one is entirely special. I look at me and Tara, Carli, Andre to an extent, Tristan to, and relate that to Tristan and Angel, Bella and whomever, Tara and Carry, and the boys. I don't see Morgan for instance speaking with Tara, or Tristan even. Our buds are who they are, as are our friends.
 For so long we've wished to extract something, and by that, feel as though we were seen, from being impressive. Seeing others speak to one another in ways they don't with me makes me feel as though were not seen. But it is not some reverse, as though they find said person attratcie or impressive. These are simply not qualities. Its just that web, and your life is what it is, in terms of whose close, by a broader place in the world wide web. Think of you and Andy, talking of Baldurs Gate, or Alex and RE. These are my buds. And indeed, forgoing the mechanics, but talking with people generally creates some form of this. I'd like to talk to Andre about some shows, or ask if he's seen any.
 This brings me to this wonderful feeling, that all is fine, evened out. If favor and fortune accrue to someone, great, it is their own. But there merely part of a different point of the web, and not exactly absent from this. No one is that special. Indeed, I find myself just not terribly secure in myself, and so the motive, or at least umph, to go up to someone and ask, be it there name, or what they like, and talk about our own, is not always there. Familiarity though
Where things lie, only the Gods know. But we do have the capacity to think, or use words rather, or imagery, to remember. And by that, I see clearly that our choices have left us rather alone, and unimbued to community. As such, our frank and forward ability to meet people, and further make friends, has always come in and out of plausibility, given how familiar we are. I don't like to make points that reduce people; but I think it is a fair statement, that of a dynaic at least, that the more formed and particular you are, the harder it will be to assimilate into a group of people. Had you known them your whole life, or for an extended period of time, then sure, you could define yourself, differently even, but still remain attached. But coming in all shiny and new, but with prior definition, means you don't so elegantly and freely bond with people. A little smear I use on the boys is how easily they mesh with eachother, but from their lack of stature, and to a degree, superficial substance. They think nothing original, but are a little bull-headed. A mere smear though, from me at least. There kinder and harder working then I or any give them due.
 It was an error to approach Bella. We were not in the right mind, or place at least. For familiarity has caught up with us, and our kindness has begun to bring me closer to people, and at the very least associate easily. We spoke with Dorthy today at lenght, and gosh, she is attractive. But I see with near clarity that she is a person, and her hot face and cute ass are not things she really cares to be seen for. She is alive after all, and in some sense, almost knowingly dolls up for no good reason. Its just part of her practice, like brushing teeth. Her features just shine from such effort. But they don't matter to her.
 All that being said, we still lie here, with wants in our hearts, misgivings of our way, natural slouchness, which brings either envy or lust, or ambition and pride. We still ought be ourself; we've just never done so truly, or rightfully. But I see, the morning sun, coming to be, rising in our own west. Today we'll do our best. But we must be so as to let this grand ambition, 'for which the slightest change would equate', be out of reach. Such is the spring for grace: to feel all at your fingers yet stilll reach not.
My philosophy is one of current fit for the present direction. I see it as an angle, where some of the sides and hue are different, than what they've been or could be. "As we may imagine." Indeed, change the angle as you can, and truly, sit with the fact that justice and wonder can go together in your transition. Recognize when this hasn't been so.
 But, so much as were in our current direction, which is not to say, that in condolence, or pity, you deal with the way things are, rather, it is just a merry fact, that fitting yourself to the present is always needed. This current handle aint so bad afterall. We can work 3 days a week, which is nice. We live in a nice spot; not ideal for running, but still, it's spring, the sun is coming in the morn, we've got simple needs in regards to activity. We could use some emphasis on stretching and training. But alas, were well. It is perhaps a blessing that our change in direction was witheld, and we acted rather rashly; running on confidence, nor humouring the understanding we had. For I see how easy things are; how free we are to be ourselvrs. We truly are in kindergarten. And indeed, I live a life that returns to work and myself. Some may go out; enjoy it I say. I reckon most don't. Or if they do, it is no shade on me. I fit in the web of things, where people are on their own, with family, lovers and friends, or at work, coming and going, doing this or that for fun or propiety. I merely exist here, in a way that feels the most vibrant and forthriding. I see now, can feel, am breathing with, and happy for, this notion of things being well, and not being pulled into false or at least trying views, that really hold their value from our imbalance. Even if Bella had a boyfriend, or just novelly took to someone, Angel even as we angstly thought, oh well. This is no loss to me; for indeed, maybe we tried to be with her, but our attempt was either false, or we were foolish to believe it had worked. We can live with it as is then; and indeed, the action I wish to take does not leave me so taken by her. Like everyone else, were together, one; yet we are still ourselves. And Bella, for instance, or Amon, are not really in line with that. Simron maybe; but that's because we truly liked her. I would have spent time with her, gone to dinner. No matter, I see our childhood happening, and our ease of doing so, in leisure, as something we own. I will remark a bit of a quandery between our actual want, or care that is. For some rests on firm action, or is based on us having nothing else to do. Indeed, were just not apart of something; having only been here for a year. Barely returned as it were. We may be more present, in ability and proper stance, 'letting things flow as it were'; but time does have it's benefit hear. And indeed, Pitt meadows may not hold alot. But it has much, and there is no timer. When we leave is just a matter of somewhere ahead. Let it be when it does, retain justice with your happiness. Be taken by the company now.
 I think I lack a vision, that's all. And I feel a little too unfit and sequestered to make cheery developments in civil or natural study. I read though, our own, and write, and thats fine and plenty. For joy though, rather than growth. No matter. Diablo is coming, some 2 months away, and a new reason to start a new character on the 10th. I may just start Paladin, and leave Druid and Sorc for Hatred. Crimson Desert is the week after, and I feel enticed by it, and fairly convicned to spend the money. The combat looks fun and the exploration might be to die for. Civ is also fun, and the test realm being public has made this past week swell. There's likely a pause on things, as victroy is on its own pretty small. But that update is coming, and its a real one. Seinfeld is nice, as you can just watch it, freely, and passively. Its great to come home, or eat dinner with. Keep this in mind for the future, with other short form. True Detective was a delight, and breezing through it again is ever nicer. Season 2 will come. I must add a bit of music, as I hear the tune of Orca and Huhah, which just ligthen me, and ripen our way. I rememeber the Jurassic theme doing the same thing, where things felt swell. Oh, how justice has eluded me. And indeed, there is reason for despair and reckoning of our lonesomeness; but fear not that a) this is your nature and or sentence, and b) that you really are as such. Sometimes I feel like were as good as we need to be, and that what were missing out on is merely someone by your side, but not what encumbantly extends to that. I imagine that the people I know are in cahoots, all going to the sugar bowl, so to speak, or hanging out at the field, and were not going. Their not. Everyone has someone they may see, like Carry and Tara. Tristan didn't even know if Bella had a boyfriend or not. How personal. As for everyone's personal lives, I can't really say. I don't get the impression that they don't just go home and do as they be. I heard Joey say he goes rock climbing with Danielle, and maybe Andy. This is just something they do. What about the days not. Are they going out to dinner with their friends? Their other life, outside of work? I have not. OUr choices bore us such. But fear not that work as it is holds something your missing. We just have to bring ourselves to peace, friendly company, but elsewhere possibility, and at that content with how it is, in terms of work.
So our nature has come about on our way, and as so, we are here having been where we are, and with nothing else. A feeling for instance may come about, but the proper response is one of ceding expectation, as opposed to running towards anticipation with confidence. We may also use perspective, as not to let our view of the thing we want, upon letting go, 'or not rushing forth', as being in some way or other, that is likely exagerated; or at least a glimmering view.
 Why do we like games? Crimson Desert is coming out and it looks great; and this sparks my interest in Ark. And the memory of Diablo brings joy. It is no woman though, nor a friend, and such things embolden me, make me feel whole and strong. In the case of things I care for, we are merely throughing ourselves in, but we are not stronger. The thought that Bella, or some like me, is so drawing for it makes me feel the most. I mention our nature then, for it has only left us in this spot for a years time, and our ability to humour slight want is not so able.
I recognize that our skill in life, be either maturity, or lack there of, and our nature, which is to say ease in which our means can partake, that so are we surely short of friends, and any calm means and or experience to do so. Learning is fine; and as of late, the reality has become rough.
 But I would point out this immutable fact, that like everyone, I have a life to manage, a job to do, bills to pay, and some sense to keep me from stepping out of line with this. We lost our broad sense indeed, when we thought fame and ingenuity were around the corner. I reckon most folk I know have a more common and near view, and thus feel more stable in being where they know. Indeed, for those at school, I reckon most of their advents will be in form, but not in scope or style. Something different, better paying, perhaps more interesting, but normal.
Hey you don't have to respond to this, just wanted to share something.
 I realized today, oh so much, that me and Bella are no longer friends. She cannot even look at me, can barely mutter a word. I've been trying to move past this whole thing for 3 weeks now, and I just thought that it would smooth over. But seemingly not. I have to say this is the slightest bit dreadfull. Again, I don't need you to say anything, I just wanted to share some of these feelings I'm having. And I know they're wrong, but they're waring on me none the less. Namely, when she's talking with other people, I feel void from that conversation. Like there's this aura blokcing me from entering. And indeed, this makes me feel seperate, which deflates the sense of connection I have. As I told you, I spend a lot of time alone, and many of the choices I've made in the recent years have been made with that in mind. I never minded leaving a job, or where I lived, as I didn't appreciate the people I had. But having been back in Pitt for a year, I'm not the same. I'm trying to make friends, and appreciate the sense of community/commradery, and not to let that slip away. I get a great amount of satisfaction from being at work, and I truly care for many of the people there, you perhaps most. But this thing with Bella, is just a thorn in my side, and takes me away from all of this. Again, I thought it would sort itself out, but being together today, like holy crap it clearly hasn't. Part of me is like 'give me a break, I just asked you out'. I've been in a bit of denial that she would realize this. Maybe some day, but as of now she can't even communicate with me. And this is making me very unsettled. One way of looking at it is, when things are well, you feel good about what you have, but when things are poor, or some curve ball gets thrown your way, you loose sight of what you have. And truly, there's grief and misgivings at the core of us, in some way or the other. And these get exposed when your sense of connection and happiness are disturbed. That is how I feel.
 Read this when you can, say what you like. There's no advice I need. It was just a bit of a rough day. And I'm glad I could tell you that.
There's something to be said about things going well, and your life feeling smooth. What lies beneath, in expecatation or dissapointment, just sits tucked away.
 It is strange when this changes, as you surely feel somewhat taken from that smooth state. Yet, the truth is merely revealed.
 But this is hard, for where did I go wrong. Have we errored this whole time?
 It is true that we've lived in the window between work and school, and indeed, been very reserved in doing anything different, or what takes me out of that. In some way, it is because I see the next moment comnig, that what I care to do before is banked.
 On one hand then you have the immutable yet undetermined resolve to the basic fabric of duty, and her unending way. And then you have the matter of interest, the things you care for. Since we played Sly Cooper, I've loved games. Sly 3, lego star wars, simpsons hit and run, Revenge of the Sith, Ratchet and Clank, 2 and 3 especially. Then our ps3, tools of destruction. I think these things are immutably cool. Who wouldn't be thrilled by them. I cannot say, be it embarrasement or some other formation, but it is a mark of someone to be able to be thrown into the world, as they would with a play. Some worlds I care not to go to. Such is a mark of me.
 My point, is that in writing for instance, or reading and study, in how we watch youtube, or tv, how we go outside, how we train and practice, approach and leave work, our views on children, government, money and the gods. Indeed, there are imbalances, and that our choices have left us rather reliant on there not being so, for which troubles can tip us over. But I care for these things no matter. It is simply the fact that our life can be more stable, in place yet of motion, and in that, more malleable, open, giving and receptive. For it was not the case that we loved Bella, nor sought anything from her but this charming feeling we had. "As though we were the only one's in the world." With Simron, I would have say what things do you do or like outside of work. We could do that together, or lets do this. I would actually like to do something with her. With Bella, for instance, I only concerned myself with how she'd be with me, and that the activity was a avenue for that. We let ourself get away.
 So now, its obvious that people have no vendetta, do not judge you as I once thought they did. They also don't revere you, or think much of you outside some prompt. Nor do they committ themselves to everybody. That's socialaty your seeing, which is almost like a game, in that its fun, feels good, brought by the moment. I see ourself doing the same thing. How are you doing, I say to Angel, Mo or Danielle. Were swept by such a whim, and bear no recourse.
Bringing up Simron is a useful thing, as she fit in our life. Indeed I thought of her, in scantifull ways, but I could sit with it more. I won't claim our view wasn't from our own experience, but indeed, of our want, what we had, felt we could give, were happy to and able to go, so she struck me. She made me laugh, and I wanted to have fun with her.
 I say this, for our ways are perhaps vainly, or if that narrowly bent, as to create a similar want, but with less reason to pursue. Indeed, we let Simron go, as our feelings remarked us stepping back. But indeed, were it now, how easy it would be to ask what she liked outside of work. I've no real motive for that with Amon or Bella. I really don't care. They just compell me, in scantiful ways likely. And were a bit thrown.
 The point being, is that we are a certain way. Comparing us is hard, as so much is taken into account. But in general, we live with the utmost committment to deeds. Which is to say, that however easy they are, lacking apprehension, or fortitude even, that we don't forget them. We don't vear off, as though the path were free from them. Nor do we dream of a path without them. Nor will we retreat any long to an unjust abscence of them. As such, this way of life brings with it much self-regard and care, but also wonder and joy. And these things are so near to me, and dear in their greatest of ways. And as such, they come only as we engage with them; and this natural rythm of deeds is the limiting factor. For some, I find, forgo what is dear altogether, and more or less live in spite of their burden. I do not. I find much grace and fertility in embracing such a way, as it not only feels proper and enough, but also currates and alights that wonder and joy. Simply in how we are. And indeed, these things are the marker of something dear, and as such, comes from one being so embellished to the simplest yet deepest of ways. As regards people, they come from this. Indeed, wisdom hath availed us, in treating us to this mixture of justice and awe. Our experience has not often been so full though. Only memory can't point to slivers of such totality, that we are left somewhat wanting still, or at least hankering over an ideal. All I'll say, is that the fresh pastures of life are opening up to me, and the oppertunity someone provides is in clear access. Asking peoples name allows for us to be, and in many ways removes the wares of justice. While asking what they do, what they like, if they care for something I do, opens up a line for bonds. Which is less of a removal of friction but rather a introduction of timber, ready to be lit, 'between, as it were'. And those people who draw such do so without any requirment, or need of assassment. It is mere nature. I did so with Max. And indeed, albeit different, we still showed friendly spirit to Mo, Danielle and Angel. There may be no one who extends beyond commonality. We ought not confuse a lack of humouring of your interest, when such interest is pointed right at them. In the case of Simron, I felt only need to ask if we could do something together that we liked. Any sense of romance was brought with that, rather than elevated. So our life will be. In truth, I can't say what I want things to be. Upon reason, liberty and might, we may stay here, taking not for granted the deed in our way and our felicitous response. Such may be enough and worth no pardon. Or it may fair too much ease, and little around seems oppertune to live further. Maybe we'll move then. If we do, we'll keep our job, and thus proper way. Only, it will transfer to a different place. And we'll make way there. Indeed, it is hard to want, as it feels like you don't have enough, especially when you fail. I feel there is a center point, where you balance the novelty and apparent ease, by which your feeling and luster work towards, and the unbuttoning as it were, aversion and or restrictiness, and by that obsessive order. The point being that there may just be enough. And reason ought not give you anything else but than a ticket to agree with this. Indeed, I am struggling with the ware, misery and regret, that comes with attracting friendship and buisness, within a centered life. Ought we change, no. Merely grow.
I think of our childhood, so fine, how great it seems. Yet, I recall many weekdays, feeling bored at the sight of soccer coming, or having to wait at mom's office. Unless there was some nice dinner that night, or we knew of some shwo, I was bored indeed. I knew not envy, nor shame. Only seeking delights. And indeed there were plenty. And seeing friends was something that happened often, but not constantly; and was always special.
 Something changed during grade 5, as though we were part of a world which was not just ares, responsible, kind, pleasing as it could be. We started hanging out with Jasmine, and found ourselves with prominence in the class: around her, Melissa, Nican, Brandon and Brad. We went to the greg-moore dance, and I felt like a fish out of water. Our fist dance with this random girl was a mess, worth a laugh. But that night, and several dances more, I danced maybe 30 times, and all were well. This part of me never returned. And if it did, only was it so in our view, but not action. We felt so far from such living, and unbecomnig as it were; as though the form of such nature had changed, beyond me as it were. And so, we felt as though there was this field, a medium in the air, and we had some sort of tune to it; but it was generally on our own, without really being with anyone. That is the field you see.
 Wisdom showed her abscence one day. During grade 7, I was not very bonded with most of the class I was in. It was a split mind you. I just didn't care for everyone. There was this sense, during recess, that christina, rozanna and whomever were huddled together with Andrew, Ryan Mcwhinnie and such, and it was as though there were a dance, with everyone waiting to make the first move. That's what it seemed liked. As people try their first drink, so were the first notions of 'guys and girls' really coming to sway. And I did not like this, at all. I developed this view, deliberately as it were. It is something I had felt befoe, and was more or less aware of at Harry Hooge. In it's case, I found myself leaning into the siutation, but also feeling somewhat uppity about it. An important note is that in all these cases, till now even, that my response, prescence and ability in such scenario have always been superb. And as I was the fastest kid in my grade, so I've always felt the best. There have always been people better looking, more athletic even, smarter, harder working, earning more money. But none have been kind and confident as I. Seldom is there as smooth a leader. Anyway, it is worth a note, that despite us being well, we did not foster such circumstance. Lacking this or that, we did not procure our place in such areas. We were fine to go elsewhere. And I consider that time in grade 5, where I was becoming more colored, as those around, but upon my prowess. Oh how I would have lead.
 So strange, how things have been. Thorugh highschool, to those years following graduation, to reading the discourses, through Kong and Warm Winters. To now, where all things justice and joy are at hand: but we more feebily realize that we have not a friend. Our ability to do so is more able and with potential; but I see clearer whats not. Our lifes momentum shows this, as upon stopping, I see what we have, but what we lack, and indeed overlook what is good: what we want and are missing act liked a pinched nerve, or leak inside. This became apparent because of our relative stop, 'as though a cease upon acceleration'. Now were approaching similar pastures, but evenly and steadily, with perspective. There is room to get going truly.
When I was in grade 8, on the first day, orientation of the sort, we had this huge soccer game, and I recall coach J being there. I remember my first real day though; not so much the classes, but that we had got the planner, and for the point of this reflection, we studied it. We sat with our mom, in the living room, on the near couch, and she tested me. For whatever reason I employed myself for this. There's a few things worth mentioning. In a similar scenario, where I tried, I had just broken up with Ahsley, started the new year, and was looking for my classes. I had latched onto this resolve to meet the BCIT requirments, and thus took Calculus and Physics. The prioritiy, focus, aim, felt refreshing. And indeed, I got 96% on the first physics exam. How this compares to that first day in grade 8, is that I came in with nothing. No friends, besides Colin; though we only shared one class. It felt like all was brand new. But not in the way one feels when they enter a carnival; as though there's all these new things to try. There may have been something there; surely a balance would have been nice. But I almost, out of positivity, as if to ride above this confusion, new people and scenario, I tried ti sit on the purpose of being there. Doing so seemed to avoid, or at least overcome, any concner for anything else. I could relate this to another scenario, that albeit different in form, still compared, and that was during our PT camp, we were not endowed with any relative strenght or phsyique, but we were the smartest; and my sense was that carrying this into an atual work place scenario would bare me superiorty. As though, the common peak, at say 90%, was built in part of superficial aspects. And indeed, if I had only that to be weighed by, then I'd fail. But, knowledge reigned supreme, and it did not matter about those lesser measures. Indeed, I felt this way through the coarse, but slackened near the end; and upon studying for and passing the exam, I felt no real stance or tightness in our views and knowledge. For a test sure, but that we had a grip on it, in a way that we could stand for and employ, had fallen. I tried somewhat, having 2 interviews; one seeming alright. But I did not carry forward. That is all to say, that in our grade 8, we thought to stand above the medley of new and intimidiating things. Funnily enough, after that first day, I no longer studied the planner. In fact, I studied nothing at all. For in some sense, the actual esteem of school, which is something I also felt in college, was shown to be a farce, and that there wasn't this on-high neccessity, for you to do well. It was just a natural extension of prior years. Simple. My study habits could be better, or note-taking rather. But still, one could easily get by. I don't think I ever studied again.
 Here is the issue though. I come into school, feeling intimidated by things, similar to how one feels when they go to an interview, when their young, and to accomodate this, we act ideal. Everyone being themselves is what's imposing, as you are not so prominent in whichever way you'd be yourself, and so you sly away. I simply have never had, outside our adolesnce perhaps, any real forward motion, to stand out; at least, if so, in a compelling way. For the point I wish to raise, is that as our ideal slightneed, by virute of the calming of what seemed like this enormous task, 'as which drew out the ideal', that so were we no less confident. In effect, we still rested on the surface of the ideal, as not to dip below, 'where I just had no comfort being'. Yet, on this surface, I did not stand. Not much really stood out for me. Indeed, if I were doing it now, I'd still live mightily, unsuperficial as it were. But I'd be happy. Perhaps by understanding and ease. But I would write down what was on the board, do the simple bits of homework, and accept our fate otherwise. Truly, all marks against me in school have been from skipping simple things.
Perhaps nothing lies clearer than than our own truth, and its relation to this moment, where we sit above the norm, but otherwise don't reach for an ideal. We merely graze on the threshold between ideal and complacence, or frivolity that is.
 It seems our time after highschool, which ought have its own defintion using these terms, lead us to a real ideal, something we cared for. And upon that levellnig out, we found a new ideal in ourself. I belived in it, perhaps out of need, in that we had nothing else. Yet, the point of why we ever did this remains.
 I think that is what I wish to speak of. For indeed, it all started when we hung out with Kaelan, Austin and Nick. We made this irreverent turn away from crushing on Rozzana, and giving two fucks what Alex or Josh said. Nor was I amused by Erik. Oddly enough, we did become friends. But to no avail. It was their's and everyone's attitude. Something which I don't fail to recognize, in terms of what I like. And indeed, our confidence, and flavenous view of screwing around, makes us overlook this when we view people; and instead, we feel as though their fairer qualities are something compelling, and a motive for us to attract. Yet their attitide is nothing I acclaim, and so we are as such present.
The root of things is important. As I can see, for instance why we do what we do given how we are: but why are we such way.
 It is all a bit hard to track. For the form of things, as I see it, is one of ideals, which a good parent, or tending one, will mend you to. And then there is easy going, not future oriented, frivolous, crude, caustic etc. Which is not in fact so extreme. But you consider, kids in school and how well they do, and their manner of study. In other words, how serious are you about life and duty, or friends and fun. This is the scale.
 What I am wondering is why were we never endowed to fun? Truly, why not? There has never been an issue, has there? One element of this, is that in grade 5, where peopel naturally surrounded me, we didn't exactly become this fun loving guy, but we did start to lean into the form of it. First though, it is worth mentioning that dances for instance, were out of my wheel house, yet, in trying them, we more or less went as ourselves, but due to familiarity, could still be as we were, but in this area. This compares to working at Save On, where I was myself, but confident. Trying things unlocks the ability for you to remain as you are, but able and comfortable in something otherwise new. When we went to Marine Dr, learning everyone's name did this, and now, despite arriving a bit late, though it must be said the ask is higher, but asking what people do, or if they like this or that. Ybraj for instance, I'd like to, easily, ask him if he plays games, or what he does outside of work.
 In any case, what I'd bring up is that as I started to feel comfortable near the end of grade 5, and this kind of thing happens where the fun which you peered away from, and scorned, or felt nerved about, that you start to embrace this, whereby you may become a little mean, a little rowdy, a little excited as it were, that in this time, had we not moved, I am not sure how I would have been. For it is perhaps some strange remark of someone to remain calm and collected, yet fun. Nothing bores me more. Truly, I gleam zero wisdom nor satiety, but rather a cool acceptance of things, yet succour in the elements of frivolity and fun. "Sitting back, drinking a bear, having a smoke, listenig to music."
 I surely had no ideal, but just wanted to run. This felt better. The elements of fun are not to question, but rather the congeniality, or smoothness and fit, of said elements with a more active gait. These don't always mathc, in ability, comfort or interest. One should not be though in any apparent way. That is for culture, your parents, the esteem and sliver of guidance your teachers provdie, or a coach.
I look at our time now, and see how it was when we were in Vancouver. That in its own right was similar to now, in that we had this need of change. THough we were absent of something in that case. But I recall as work became smooth, and waking up felt nice, and we had let the idea of Simron go, letting things be, and seeing the monring sun, in the winter's gleam. So nice, and going around, west, north, and east, oh my. It just felt right. Like we were on that steady path.
 Yet, where did any of this come from. For outside of motion, you tend to notice things you lack, or could have, or may want, or feel you should. But our arrogance is so far gone, that we don't even accomdoate for this, with some sort of assurance or remedy. We sit with it so well. Its as though we were wounded, but were brought so little injury, that we had no adrenalnie or nervous response; yet we fully feel the pain.
 No matter, I look at our 2020 year, where we were so against work. But even as the year began, we felt smoother, less nerves about our speaking, and generally equiped to embrace the job. I still felt this clinging to some promise of Vancouver, or something. And I still thought highly of myself. WHich indeed, has its own source of inquiry, relating to, or comparable to that divide of seriousness and ideals. At any rate, even before Holly I began to develop the sentiment, or recgognized at least, the balance our life had. The meaning of just came to be, in that it felt that there was no alternative to this, and that this was the way everyone was suppose to be. We were either in a confident progressing state, but temporary nonetheless. Work seemed to be forever. It was just a matter if you were pleased. The fact that I was made my agreement to it all much easier. And after Holly, we doubled down, in our reflections; as though to bring some character and life to our agreement. And we just relished our gaming. Guild Wars namely was played, and Civ too. I recall Last of Us, and this just being a srouce for connection.
 I would compare this period, where we brought ourselves to formation, but to a proper state, or just one rather, of motion we'll say, compared to our stalling life during the summer of 2018, where we read Epictetus, and in away, gave form, but to a life of nothing. One which sought, naievly, to live outside; as if away from work. That felt like a life of motion, or justice, that was desired. Strange, that you recognized the need, for you knew nothing else was given. But what bore need turned out to be something less preferable. Yet, for such we adapted and formed to (in 2020).
These thoughts of mine are quite helpful. Though I don't know where to begin. We were talknig about our roots, and how we were so uncatered to groups of people, and the fashion around, that we partook in ideals, even if in a paultry way.
 What I would say, is that our ideal was never strong. For one, of school, we didn't stand by it, but merely used it as a shield. As if an excuse. I recall when we stopped playing soccer, that I ran into Lyle, and I just said I wanted to focus on coaching. That wasn't true, but I used it as an excuse for my leaving. Then, when we graduated, our ideal became of health, which was nice and I really cared for it. Formost because I could feel the effect, and not only in energy, but also fitting, we partook in study, invented sums, and overal felt great about our free time. It actually treated me well.
 I would note this, that one of my misgivings about youth, was that what I felt I had to do in order to be myself and enthused, with everyone, groups, new folk, was that I needed a job, as to afford say a car or means of doing something. I did not care for these things and thus not a job. It's odd how a job became a thing of neccessity, but for the sake of our ideal, rather than fun.
 No matter, for reference sake, when my ideal of health grew into passivity, as if it were our nature, Justin, who had done the same, yet was always employed and with Kaelen, directed himself towards work, while I went to myself.
 The place in life I saw people going was not where I wanted to go. It seemed like people went to school, for some novel ideal of 'doing your best, or making do with the promise at hand', or they got trade jobs, or worked wherever, but all so they could go and see their friends. I cared for neither of this, hovering around the latter. The oddest choice occured then, when following our time in school, and quiting smoking, we found health, and were terribly enthused. I recall that we felt something though, weird I'd say. For it still felt as though, when we were couriering, that we wanted things, or noticed that something was missing, things which were possible. We dreamed while we drove to Kelowna, and listened to I'm Not Perfect, screaming. And I made this seemingly free yet mischevous choice to leave that job, not go to Vancouver, but stay at home and focus on nutrition. I recall all things outside my floating off, and I did not care. My experience was heightened, and out of demanding effort. The year 2021 seemed so far off, but as far as I knew, I would never leave. I drifted outside any notion of the future, or friends, the people I knew. I have always struggled to return. And indeed, I felt I had, functionally that is, during 2016. Only, I was still myself. And I let our confidence lead to expectation, as though our prescence with someone were drwaing, when it clearly was not.
 What really feels strange, is in looking at those far back years, and contrasting them with now. There's something certain about our beliefs back then, as though som fear were truly guiding us. And so saying no felt fair. Indeed, we fell on rules, as though they were what justified us; like they gave room for us to be comfortable, but out of reconciliation. I guess in some sense, before I moeved, life was easy and I never struggled. I never sought friends, merely had them. And when those in that last year came around me, it was like people were growing up, in a flouncy, show-boaty way. Yet, I was all the more confident, stable, funny, and just unexuding. I think this is why Jasmine liked me. Nonetheless, when we moved, I sort of kept this appeal. In a way, our grade 6 year was like the last, but not the one to come. In that year, I saw people who I never saw again, made best friends with Colin, who took me to Disney Land. Even Gresham went Skiing with us. And when we went to Jungle Jacs, we were playing around with Kendra and her rebecca. Pretty prominent of me. Something changed though, something which I never adjusted to, but averesed myself from; but lacked any genuine alternative, be it in strenght or ideal. We whithered, passing by, watching porn and tv, eating chocolate, showing up for soccer (but with deference). I recall in grade 10 this started to feel stressfull, as nothing was so hard in the prior years. But we settled, and became very close with Colin. And in grade 11 this continuned. As though we had fit ourself right in, met Silvester, and were on the ups of what we had to do. It was my own though. And when we went over to Shawnas, it was like that first dance, where I thought no, but nonethless obliged. Such things are seldom asked of me; perhaps because it is someone you've already known, or by your relation to someone else. "Like your buddy gets you and him invited to a party." And indeed, like the dance, I fetl a little funny being there, but was enthused about it afterwards. I felt like I didn't have to try, that I could just be there. I put Colin ahead of Shawna and company though, and did not meld with their view. It's funny, when Colin was going through his thing, people start talking, and gabbing about it in the hall. Its some chatty, 'I know right, like jeesh'. No enditement, but little comments about him being as he was. And I took part of that. Perhaps this realtes to my abiilty to make fun and laugh at people who I know, especially if they fall. I don't rememeber thee exact words, but were in the group chat, and something came up about him not being invited. And I simply made the remark that we shouldn't forgo him. He was our friend, and in need. That is it you see, I never want anyone to get hurt. And if they needed something I'd be there. I just use my judgement as to whether I really need to care. When someone falls, less they need something, I'm laughing histerically. But when I really saw him getting pushed out, I felt no qualms in not humouring this. Like for real now, were not just trashing him, but saying he's not our friend. He sure as hell was mine. And there was this kind of naievity on my part, as if I said, 'come on guys, lets do something better'; and I did it with a bit of flippancy and coarseness, which they responded to horribly. I'll die on the hill that it was wrong to say he was no longer our friend. How could this be so. He was mine after all, and I would give him all credit to remain so. But they, understandibly, gave way, easier that is. I lacked the presentation, and perhaps level-headedness, to see that he was not being outlawed or damned, and that this was just how they felt, and that perhaps I was caught in the glitz and glamour of our new group, as though this meant something. That is our attiude and humor attaching itself to something which others are not so bound to. I took this for granted, and acted out of line, per what they cared for.
Something seems so distinct, or at least different, in value, reason and tone, when comparing pre covid and post. During, we went for Holley, found reason for work, then sunk into our days and activity. We'd walk down ford every day and night, read Ana karenina by the side. Things were well.
 We made the choice though to leave. The feeling was one that I comapre to now, where this way of living, which was in some way measured, but also reserved, at least from experience of what we saw as near. The difference was one, in that we did not appreciate the consquence of leaving. And on top of that, besides just pushing through, as we are sort of doing now, we also had less kowledge, of where we could go, what things could be. This realtes to Save On, where I a) happened to not like it, but b) could have moved somewhere in Pitt Meadows. We were fine to bike afterall. But wisdom had not begun to accumulate, nor sense. Looking back at that time is a bleak scenario; nothing excites me. Even though I had never really felt so liberated and free. It was perhaps maturity which had not prevailed, for we gave little resepct to neccessity. Yet, as we felt it, so did the novelty ware off. And indeed, that neccessity is where I go now. Even to that wonderous place, in Vacnouver on 49th, with Simron and all those fine folk, seeing the sun rise in the crisp morn, oh what could have been; as we were going at least. That so in this were we just appreciating the nature of finances, and how spending was not to be some temporary thing, 'at the cost of sustained debt'.
 Indeed, it speaks to where we came from prior to Covid, and how averse we were to work. Indeed, we had spent that year occupied, similar to our time in 2024. But similar to when we went to school again, where we just felt fine and able, 'as if we had room to include these; it did not seem like work, but natural want', that so we did the same thing for personal training. It's a marvel that such experience held such precious thought, yet we've not really applied it, as such a school or program has not inspired me yet. In part, I think our feelings were less privy to how much we'd really have to be present, and that perhaps, our confidence and self-esteem were leaned on, as if to hold us up in our indifference, or feeling different. That lesson of forming yourself, as to be as you feel worthy, is one I respect, but could likely use more. Consistency is all thats required for this. Though, this springs to mind that notion of an ideal, where you rest above the tippsy mixture of not being a dude, or goof, but also being comfortable, and safe; or in other words, not so open, trying not, hiding behind insecurity. As we would rest on an ideal, or at least pivot ourself against that, 'as a reason to be safe', that so we acted similarly in our PT. We employed this better and did well, but our idea of it, the end at least, was rooted in this ideal form we believed in. As our studies slackened, so this form slipped, and we could not get it back.
Maggy mentioned that Marty never knew himself, so he didn't know what he wanted. There's something also which Rust mentions, when Maggie suggests there's comfort available to him, he shuns it off; she wonders why, and he says, 'because we know what we want (ie. men)'.
 This is something to unpack. As I see in myself a development which corresponds with the latter, in that something 'I want' became known. Yet, it was built so, and rapped around, from a point where we didn't know ourself, and accordingly floundered. So what we found had a form and heart that reflected our state of lack, as did our resolve.
 As I look back on our younger years, I see the young motions which would bare us to that barren point, and thus ideal. Yet, such ideal, in all its clarity and seeming worth, does not feel like an actual insight into us. Indeed, it may be as pleasing and worthy as you esteem, but that does not mean it is true; or at least, and surely this is so, that it may simply lack the proper employment, and perspective at that. "For why, in knowing what we want, do we exclude other things. It seems, that such want is fitted, or at least made, under pressure, or rule of some sort. Hence the adaptation."
 Real knowledge though, of who we are, remarks the motive for such adaptaion, in that we see things a certain way. But why do we see that as such. What else is there to know?
I realize I have a lot of choice in the matter. Always this has been so, and indeed, this fact seems irefutable, and for all.
 Yet, and here the expeirence of others lies in a haze, but of what we are asked to do, as though without question, that so of these things are certain feelings evoked, for which we respond to in a certain way.
 I have always hated work, getting up early, going to bed on time. Nor do I love the feeling after eating a donut or pizza. Anything short of peas and carrots leaves me awry. I never liked league soccer, nor English class. If people were standing around, I'd rather be somewhere else. Odd even, if we were on our own. I knew we had to go to school, and that akin to someones inherent admission, or at least idea of god, that so did family and equity lie. Tests were always a measure, as they reflected our grade foremost; which in its own right was the real measure. If it was good, I slept easily. I loathe the feeling of wanting someone, but feeling like they don't, or that you can't. And indeed, until motion is had, I feel improper in giving some reason why we coudn't be with them. Less impmroper, but why we shouldn't pursue them is hard to sit with, as it usually comes from the reality of your relationship; that you work together, that your merely friends, that of what currates this, or brings it further, that I genuinely don't. Simron for instance had a real interest of mine, where I din't have to bring her into my world, but I sought to be apart of hers. I really do hate this feeling, or concept, that Justin and our laddy friends gave me, that getting laid was easy. I don't even wish for perspective, similar to porn. It is not a valid source; nor one which is gifted, but perhaps stuck in my own sensitive spokes. There is evidence for what they say, but no grounds for their suggestion. "They've lived, but are not wise. I envy the former, and feel poor in denying the latter: for we must admitt our standing."
So I wanted to write about two things, namely, the state of union, and a history of proceeding moments, where we emphasize how felt, valued, feared etc. at such moments.
 For the first, I'd say I'm well. I definitely feel the more or less loss of Bella, in friendship namely. I have reconciled more or less the pre-mature nature, and can sit with the reality that we thought she liked me, when we were most open and wanting to like her. She did not actually show us that: I accept the scarring. Indeed, I realize that were not together anymore, and no amount of humour will suffice. Knowing this though makes it less awkward. For there isn't this unknown, merely a small address. And this brings up our sense of people, how they are with eachother, and we with them. I forget how many times Tristan has been looked down at, by Sam, Sonal, Tara even, and how Teresa and Carry, and Carli jokingly to, remark more or less poorly of 'the boys club'. The point being, that each and all still talk to one another; it's really just a remark of how much they care. The anxiety, or rancour, is that everyone cares for one another, besides me. And the false concept is that no one cares for anybody. Let's just say this aspect of myself is fine, were quite open, learning about what people like, more than fine to say hello. The only misgiving is Bella, who in seeing her talk to people, makes me feel apart. As though were insecure, that she isn't talking to me. Our openenss just doesn't seem to click with her, and that almost makes it seem as though our common attempts are pithy. We were fine with her thoough, and acted perhaps on some ultmiate inability, to treat her as we were, let her go, and not move towards her. Such felt hard, perhaps by the feelings we were harbouring. That is the point perhaps, that when you are happy, which I'm guessing in relation to work, means your sense of job, home, family, and close friends are solid; weekends or some occasion heighten this to. But this just brings you in naturally. Its the same feeling that makes you want people, and in particular those with charm and energy, to like you, as you feel all the more secure, and more open. We exude confidence, which strenghtens our sense and expectation from writing. People are just being as they are, and so we ourselves. Our sense of ease is not always overflowing, and thus we have less nearby, nor long-prior peace, as to so merrily embrace people. Instead, were a little bit harboured, in control, tense, jerking hither and fro, away from poor things, towards good, and thus were a little kept, withdrawn, with less kindling to spark connection; yet, from porn, the sight of others, normal want, and general satisfaction, 'whereby we've either tried, or been open to it, looking at least', that so do we have a tense want; but due to the momentary divide, our view of others, as much as they caught my side, is less becoming of how they really are, or feel, and our motion towards them feels more obscure, and thus troubling. We force ourself to be confident, or treipidatiohjsly proceed, or halt, from doubt.
 This perhaps brings up our next point, which is our place in things, our time, our scenario in life. For so long, we had this dangling promsie, or back-up idea so to speak, about the prospect from online work, online dating, our abiilty to socialize and join clubs; and that Vacnouver, being the city, was a lucrativre place for this. These ideas are not lacking truth, only, as of now, they a) don't feel so exclusive to a certain environemtn, 'as though, if we just went there, all would happen', nor b) are they what I really care for. And as I'm so happy now, the need for them to dangle goes away. Indeed, as we gain more that speed but bearing, which is to stay motion so far, that what dangles are more realistic and tenuable expectations of things once thought far off. Notably, sums, and how we may email professors, and just share in general; our views on history, civil world, games and movies, the luster in life, through media. One thing that centers this, is the notion of our own stability bearing permission to both want and feel un-scrupusoly pleased, at a small comunity, a small impact, a small stake in the world; yet of a motion which is dearly my own, and thus feels in stride and flavor that it is of the whole world. "As though are heart were exuding an overflow of cream; my own, but spreading across life and time. With are indeluable strides being the lever for such." Were afraind of this normally, for it is for something so purely us; that by mitigating wants or moral decree, we escape, 'feeling it too hard to bear forth as ourself, given this mental friction'. A flaw it was indeed, to underestimate the scenario in life, 'our balance of just', and overdue it in our own fecundity, 'as if to push through the resistance'.
 It is a harsh truth, but one no less, that our biggest discontent in life is from avoiding things. The feeling comes about though, when things come your way that you are not nomrally privy to, and thus this novel prescence is not some miracle for you to connect with, but merely a passing instance of something elsewhere common: a place were not part of. This passing thing, 'as though a succuletn cake brought in front of a secluded monks eyes', has him melancholic over the fact that such cake couldn't be his; though the real insight is that he lives not near bakeries. And indeed, the moral issue, or one of complete substance, is that where one ought be, out of fear, for justice and ease, that so baked goods lie around. And indeed, being so just, makes both our wants and our senseibility, 'as if to temper, or at least position ourself according to them, but fairly', more fitting, oppertune, but unmelchonlic, or of clawing anticipation: for there is no divide, and thus no obscurity to exagerate our expecation and care.
 I believe the synopsis of our morals, our life as it were, is that living on your own, which is to say for your own control and focus, is the way for you to breathe and concieve happily, but also merrily; for the prosperity here gives a trust to your way, in that you are not afraid to lean in, lean out, admitt this or committ yourself to that, 'as those things dear don't feel that without our tight grip that they'll slip away'. Such is the condition for us to make novel but perhaps pecuniary gain, but maybe something near to us, yet worth loving. "Furthering our care for things of the world." This dependance exists in the world, and not in private. "That is for people who can afford, it having been given, or earned after a public life lived." Such a place brings you around people. Naturally by your place of employment, but also in what people do around. I think, for one, that a proper state makes you more open, and thus likely to recieve, an invitation, or otherwise be more amiable and sociable as to inquire and request.
 Indeed, life right is so, that I'm thinking of our time when we first started Superstore, and how we felt we had this identity which we could not share. Yet, our confidence shields us, and makes us feel as though an outsider, yet on the in. As if to overhwlem any sense of being differnet, or disliked. This sense of protection is the inhibator for friends; for none can really like you. We believe that if we speak to others, that they'll be so grateful and delighted. The point though, is that our shield over time lessens, and were more open. We make mistakes sure, but besides the person you error with, all are the same. You merely realize that you have not been that open.
So we may move onto the next topic, namely of our feelings through time. We'll let this be what it is for each moment, then add.
 Let us begin in youth, when we felt how we did about school, women, jobs, grades, and friends. I loved video games: ratchet and Clank, demos, modern warfare 2.
 I think actually, beginning where things took form is better. We've looked at the roots; we can further look another time.
 When we went to school, I enjoyed the study, but felt burdened by the increasing request. Besides the money, I couldn't think of a reason to do well. It seemed our natural interest was not enough. We barely squeeked by. I have this confusing memory of feeling despondant over Hannah; waiting in line for Kodiak mountain. Yet me and her spoke the summer prior. No matter, I recall missing the first 2 classes for pysche, and I was so nervous when returning. I remember being called to share my view; and the teacher was this young guy, very nice. My response was rather muted; nothing more than 'I don't know'. But, when I went to the 2nd class, I felt way more myself, askig questions, solid. It was econ which killed me. I just didn't care. Psyche was cool, but my general interest changed. I dropped out, for this or that reason; to no real backlash.
 Our life took off from here, but into our own abode. We had no forward sense. We had stopped smoking as when we came back from the states, and witcher had come out to great rejoice. I was friends with Justin, but seldom saw Colin. The job at sexy juice was nice, and my training felt good. I felt warm and comfortable, working a modest amount, only 3-4 shifts, from 2-8. Not too bad. I was a miff though. I remember driving past Colin, at the warehouse, and him standing there like 'what are you doing'. I just didn't want to be part of that. I was not in a state of life where doing what I had to do was enough for me to ponder around, smoke, stay up late, eat indiscretioantely. When I had stopped smoking I took up eating well. And I recall how well I felt coming back from the gym, having a smoothie. It was as though the monring were bright, I had this sense of the whole land, feeling the sun. This spryness, and spring to my step, gave a sense to my day, this rest, but also delude into games and reading. I felt buzzing. Truly we were more invovled, having finished school, working out and eatnig well. We meant to go to school again, and all was pretty well. Oddly enough, this moment reflected our own control, as we weren't yet employed, had stock in life, and were on the precipe of summer, but living as to appreciate the morning. We were fine, with no worry ahead. School though, and having to get a job, burdened my down. Similar in fact to the semester prior, when push came to shove, my sense of control and ease dissipated; and we had no real resolve, and thus care for discipline, 'empty fortitude at least', nor were we privy to, nor inundated to pleasure and fun. I wish I could just be happy. That moment, of may 2015, was a real delight, our first perhaps. But I compare this to the fall months, where we had left our job, were not going to school, and my life felt contained, by our surrender almost, to this private, homely, lame abode. There's a difference, even though compared to may, we were set with the same. One note is that in that end of the year, we had a deliberate acceptance of things. As though, comapred to May, where we were well, but merrily on the stream of life, and pleased to be where we were at, 'as though, akin to summer vacation, it was going, temporary, but we were not merely waiting, nor living as though it would never end, but merry and well'; that in the fall's case, the stream was over.
PLaying Civ always starts with a high, but then I get overwhelmed with all these things I want to do. One strategy is to establish yourself, within your means, in a way that is easiest, and thus smoothest, then seek out, or identify rather, places where you could extend to, or at least contest.
 Any way, we are quite well, and the weekend is coming. We see Lauri tomorrow, which is nice. We got our 600 from mom, and are pretty financially strong. About Mandi, she responds when she does; indeed, it may take time. Just double check; she doesn't seem to show annoyance at our questions; though her requests have me a little unsure how to respond. We could just send what she needs, and adjust it as we go. Let's just do that. I'll email them later.
 Anyway, I think we could update our yoga to allow thumbnails. This could be a way to idenfity relevant ones; a toggle if you will. I think we have to prioritize upper body. Warrior and what not seems unimportant, as we do this naturally. Same with lunging. Its seated, staff and upper body variants of these which are needed. I don't actually know which videos I want, or where we should focus; I just see us altering the website, to have thumbnails; and I like being able to select which ones, as if to say, 'these are what were doing now.' We can do this on VS.
 Given that, I find that sugar and pasta give me energy, but deifinelty wear me out. Its a strange balance. Eating enough grain can suffice, but a sauce is preferred. For now, we have that curry, corn and edamame, and split peas are a delight. Were having some mix of salad and lentils for lunch, potatoes for dinner, and finish with that lefotver pea soup. We don't work till 630, so we can probally benefit from going to bed a bit late. Apparently its a red moon tonight, so I'll want to go out for that. I'm gonna go walk, then come back and have lunch. Take note of these days, when the sun sets around 6. Perfect for a 4:00 lunch, and 7:00 dinner. For the weekend, I'd monitor the starch you need, and whether there's any good for sugar. Maybe we just have some tomorrow. I prefer to be in bed by 11, turning things off just after 10. The alarm helps. So I'd like to have dinner and cereal between 7-9.
 I will note the obvious and expected difficulty from doing pushups, at all. The merrit of planks seems to lessen right after you do them. But I am enjoying runnign. And I don't mean to hamper ever session with pushups, but I feel them somewhat neccessary, to be incorporated in the broader ryhtm, as it keeps us warm, limber, digesting, for sleep and pushing at work. You don't need much, and the effect lasts a while. Its not about muscles, and we should feel this important. its about how your shoulders are held, and if you are open and spry in your body. We did this on Saturday evening, and yesterday was a little tough, as I feel it in my left shoulder. But today has been fine. One trick is to simple stand, raise, to the side, and fold, while breathing 3 times each. Very nice. Don't forget corpse pose either. It helps. And so, were off the next two days, lets just do a session then. I don't mind the break, and not doing high knees, but do your warrior or something. Maybe prioritize your legs and quads. But on the day after, free range really helps. I'd almost say it is a given, short of respiratory rest. We ought not do without for fear of settling. Anyway, either tomorrow, or wedensday, given that we have thursday off, we should do some sort of session. Perhaps it will be better. I just need 2-3 sets, paired with plank, or just from and followed by a spot. Then run. I mention this because we work the two days following, and may not have monday off. And so in that case, it would not do us poor to run a little on sunday, but leave training away. Our muscles may seem the wrose, and may sulk into seemingly flat states, but the formation is there. More sculpting will be done, in stretching and recovery. And primary running helps. When were of the next week, we'll do some then. Find your moment as it were.
So, bringing things back to the present, I feel as though there is this fundemental divide, whereby you own something, and don't. Reasons and confidence tend to propell you towards it, or in keeping it, or in doubt or acceptence that it is not to stay. But truly having it is something you feel. When Bella looked at me, nearly paralyzed, and said she had plans, there was no doubt in my mnid, I knew what I'd done.
 And so, all things that have happened recently, and given what I had thought prior, I see how the real trouble in findnig a place is so, that all fluff and accessory blows away, and your left with that fundemnetal station, of which all things life and propiety orbit from.
 A tricky notion indeed, of basic need and right. Much of it is merely recognizable. In a similar light you feel this with friends; though perhaps that is a luxury that we are all to eager, or perhaps unprivy to seep. But of a home, a place to live, so we accept the one given, but realize were trapped in such, to be ourself that is. But, that we'd own it ourselves, requires that we earn it, for by definition this is so. When we were younger we thought of living outside, for I felt so called to that being all we needed. But it could not provide. Similar to eating one meal per day. Basic neccesity may leave there, but our actionable and modern stride need something else. And so for home and bread we heeded.
The simplest, yet richest of remarks, comes down to the unending rythm, or flow rather of life; the pitch and pace of which is determined foremost by our neccessity.
 Life is just weird and odd when you lack the focus to esteem and believe what you wish, and the control to abide and amend as you see fit. Resolving this matter positions you into the environment of duty. Hath the king giveth, or the teaming springs around, so we must only reach out our hand. But if property and bread are earned from coin, and only at an meager level due to the proficiency of the times, then so must we acquire such means. And such means, by virute of the coin, come by your own offering, as to recive such power. The where and whens of this are the whole matter: where you employ your effort for a path of acquisition.
 It is not useful to consider anything but the reoccuring nature of this scenario, and thus your coming and going.
 For our work, we benefit greatly from being able to push. Exercises that do this though can be waring, and need be done with form and energy, smoe of which may be lost. One reason for control is that you can perform at better times; but, in not being hasty, you have more precious moments afterward to recover.
