I wished for yoga to be like that, but I always felt, for one, that the process was harsh, but also, that it was a lot of effort, and I was really just going there for myself. Playing soccer would be a real delight. And we can try that out, enjoy it. You'll feel better going; its like meeting somonee, and your not so narrow. Love is weighed on then. Though, Epicteus and reflection have brought me to see something whole. Charging the scooter is fine, laundry will be good. Its too cold to have the window open, so there's a bit of a smell. The lavender helps. I might benefit from gettinga wicker basket. But all is good. No real issues. Getting familair and comfy; notably in coming and going for walks. Feels a bit odd to go shortly, then return only to leave. Being upstairs maybe. But its fine. I reckon we cannot live here after the summer, as its just too cold. Thankfully were on the eve of the warm stretch. Interestingly, this though, of something ahead, lightnes our need for love, as it creates a sense of certainty and excitment. Our lack of want, can bear us solem though, as we actually feel like time is not what we need; for things don't really change. When were well and things to, then more time, and a promise of something ahead, is nice. "You've got kindly to brighten to fire."
 Practice wise, the field is right next door, and going for that run, along a nice flat field was great. I am feeling the wear from having moved, being cold, not doing any training, and only today doing any free range. It helps to have some form in our motion. We've been good though, sleeping not too late. Again, its cold, so we used a blanket yesterday, which makes things rough. Our headache didn't help either, and so we passed on cereal. Brussel Sprouts were cheap, as were potatoes --they were so good. Grain wise I feel somewhat reserved, if only to make do with quinoa and lentils, then have the sprouts, maybe some potatoes. We've not toaster; air frying tastes great, but tends to want more oil than makes it light enough. But still fair. Milk+banana+cranberry is really nice, and oranges are relatviely cheap. I feel our finances are so secure, and that working 20 hours is no big deal. And per a standard we are at the peak of it. So I've no real recourse to merely be pleased right now; for things are good, with a little breathing room --from nature and our privalege- but so is this peak apt to go down. Timing is still coming together, as I feel more able and free to eat early, and there isn't a right time during the evening. Cooking though is not to hard, but preparing can feel a little crowded. Just eat enough, produce during the day, peas or veg for late lunch, then have quinoa, maybe some bread, or potatoes. We do mean to work out again. I know it will hurt, but I find myself able to sit with the incorporation required. Were still stetching and what not, walking to. And our resting is quite well. We'll be sore, I can just handle it; it doens't consume our day also, so if for an hour or tow, like yesterday you get a headache, no worry. The base should be running and activity, but having some form thorugh pushups formoest, some chinups occassionaly, and squats and or lunges as your base stabilizies --as they cost little-- that so is this enough. Training just brings everything together, and encourages you to do what is already impiortant. It procures a nature which is limber in work, bold in study, pleased in all forms of media, and rejoice in the outdoors. Our freedom serves us this, to be directed, snesible, and tuned. And as such, we are satisfied and free, but oppertune in the little things that pass by. "We've not got to study, for we have either won, or have room later."
I've got some thoughts, but the feelings are hard to state. At least, I feel a little too garky to say them. And maybe I should say nothing at all then.
 I'm crawling here, but there seems this thing of foundational reconciliation. It has to do with our way, its difference, the manner of it being more or less good and stand up. The alternative to frivolous wants and aspiration. Like why are big muscles big, if your not an athlete. Indeed, it seems fun, but it really matters not. What is hard to reconcile, is in being something I deem good, how things still seem to turn out oddly. At least our ends have been quite strange. One point made is that we never had momentum, as we did not so easily, or frivolously pursue looks, woman, or work for that sake. But we also didn't pursue careers or anything of the sort. And we either retreated home, where nothing could truly be done as you'd have to begin, or we'd leave to go somewhere, but merely float ontop of the neweness of it, only to settle into the same uncertainty, upon familiarity. There have been moments of responsability, the upwinds upon our lack. First was in 2016; but we moved to coquitlam after feeling flat early 2017. Then in fall 2018, where we walked, and tried illustrator, but to no avail. We went to Personal training soon after, but fell flat when it really counted. And we got a job, found much spirit in the basis of things, yet left; in part because we felt unsteady. I have mentioned our fear of being so cnotent, as though it were not acceptable; but this came after we left. I recall sitting at Highland park in January the following year, thinking of how nothing was so bad --as the summer/winter dynamic once seemed. I have to re-consider my thoughts for the summer of 2020. I didn't appreciate much, that we were leaving. I felt like I wanted to focus on myself, and had more or less done enough to justify that. Then we moved to Burnaby, but choose nothing after the novelty wore off. We did decide to do programming, which turned into writing, and I cared for nothing. We carried a few bad habits, and still sat in a foundation that was very much about me in the moment, rather than perpetuity. But we were well. Again though, and perhaps this is an example which highlights the key dynamics, but when we got to Vancouver I felt very free. Despite not working and feeling spry and keen for the world around, like those times before, we were more solid in ourselves. And I wrote, 'we can see things as they seem to me, and act accordingly'. This was not true though, as I found I needed to anwser to things which we were not giving due credit towards. At least, I felt like we could not live as though those questions wouldn't be raised to me. This is one effect of living with no burden, which occurs almsot purely when you are unoccupied.
 What has changed in this last 10 years, from the time where we found what was precious, and compared ourself not to those and what they found so, is that the nature of this experience became less subject to hostility, or doubt, or being but an occassion, or remedy to work. Our responsability became ever more apparent. Sometimes admittedly, as when we got employed after our second semester. But also when we moved home from Coquitlam. Something had to be done; as we had ventured to live outside, as if to thwart this basic claim. And we choose illustrator, as the approach of this felt comparable to employment. We learned an immense amount during Superstore; seeing as what it gave me, despite rough, or at the very least present, is that it gave you literally everything you needed. Indeed, our struggle with this pulled at our want to stay. But so did the words which we used to order ourself feel in conflict, if not prickled by perceived notions around. We learned a great deal about money, to a fictitous degree during our time after. And indeed, we felt the effects of this going into Vancouver. With calm and merry spirits, we got employed, endured the admittedly crooked place in our position, evened it out, felt fairly social, navigated our love, and ultiamtley sunk into the crisp and clear routine, of work and leisure. Liberty indeed. "It was as though we were doing all things we had to, while in motion." In Superstore prior, I would expeirence such motion, and became at peace with the nature of working there, as it gave such motion. It was just how much it pulled me elsewhere. And indeed, such a pull motivated our elation with Holly, or whomever. We were so focused on ourself, that it was hard to see that no one really cared. And that, in our common moments, where we indeed socialized, that this is all other people did, more or less returning to their lives. What has Joey, or Bella, or Alex, or Andy and whomever done this whole time? They must have had some dinners, gone to some parties, played some games, maybe gone on vacations. I wouldn't compare my cares with that.
What a nice day. Truly, the field around me is full of flowers, literal clouds, nice things of the whole sort. Friends to. Yet I can't quite see but what I don't have. And truly this comes from a lack; as one is literally absent of standing when their sitting. But in such a state, I see what other people have, and by that, feel myself missing.
 It occured to me that most talk between people is either bullshit, saying hi, gossiping. Seldom is it personal. Not with everyone. People talke about themselves also; yet this is not by my acocunts a esteemed thing to do.
 It just feels like were a bit to airy, unfocused, with control for sure, but unclear of ourself. All that is to say, that we came to where were at from a time where things were different, and how we were upon this new time was dealt with accordingly (with what we brought.) But this effected how things were here. And now, with the change in our stature, so are we dealing with bent items; which became so from the stature we had when we started. We literally made the motion to ignore Bella. That was how I felt. Time went on, and we had some humour, then I asked her some personal questions. But so much felt hard. And indeed, our stature was not so as to humour the possibility of how we really were; we had to beleive something was different. Now though, with that being made apparent, and now were in a new space, I see our lack of definition clearly. As though we were moving along, letting certain views and feelings come with us. Light, yet plausible. When we stopped, the plausibility of those feelings wore on me, and I could not stand them being as light as they were.
 I've just never felt so unimportant. And indeed, some sort of expectation is important. For I don't feel that many are at all frivolous. We may fear Bella doing so, out of shame and apathy. But even she has no real grounds to be seen as so. And even if someone is, this can act lile gravity, pulling your own views and feelings inward, as though anchored by comparison. This brings you out of the light of everyone else; whereby you feel absent from others, and moreso when you see them acting with other people. Most are not that weighed by but a few people. And a minor effect is for us all.
 I guess highschool is different, as that is very much our life. I don't think work is this way. Its something we show up to, rather than school, which is like a daycare, or camp. I just don't believe that there is any real consensus, nor virulent gossip. People are working after all. There may be a group dynamic, in that if multiple people are together who like one another, then the interaction is amidst them all. But this doesn't take anything away.
 Why do we feel this though? It is like highschool, where I saw people coming and going, being with this or that person, or in that group. And it meant something, and beyond what they had, it spoke to other people also, as if they were central. Characters are like this, in that they feel important. I am not sure who has a story though. I often think it is I. And indeed, you may, and your probally understimating your time and worth at work. As are you overestimating your possessive, or at least rewarded qualities. Us merely being there is something others find nice, perhaps especially so. But this is merely what it is, and something you notice. I just feel too unsure to let it be; which, like meories, happen through time, building our view. Were just to inconsistent, or riding falsity to keep us going, that enlightment is rough. For we see we have not.
All has been lost, perhaps, yet so much remains. And weeving yourself into that makes you feel like you did prior to loss.
 I look at our time and how much we under-appreciate the developmental steps we've taken. Not so much in ability but in circumstance. We moved to Pitt, and returned to Superstore, after 2 years of living at home. This was not an easy thing to do. And given the affluence of our experience in Vancouver prior, it was pretty clear that we would return. Yet we stayed. And a bonfire we've made, if not a solid camp. It's been with sticks and twigs though.
 I want to compare our starting in Vancouver with our start in Pitt. For they both seem comparable, in that we were starting a new job, had not been working prior. Yet it feels different. Maybe it was the people in Vancouver, or maybe it was just me, but I felt part of the group. I didn't notice where I was or wasn't. Maybe it was a cultural thing, and we didn't think other people had more going on. I don't think it was a view of them being lesser and worse off, as though to lift myself up; but, in contrast to how I sometimes feel at Pitt, that there was this sense of appreciation of work, like it was good and enough; like this was what life was. Being happy within that framework felt good, for it was just. I get the impression that people see work as a burden, and that not going to school or getting a job above that is somehow inferior. It's like waring ordinary clothes aint good enough. I've remarked that ethnic people, or the trait at least which from whoever I admire, is one of unassumption. And feeling that in Vancouver, I found it easier to fit in.
 It is important to note that we had been working from August through October, early November rather, when we got a real grip on things and a sort of proper well-living ensued. "Where we took refuge in the crisp morning sun." As things went, we started Superstore in Februrary last year, had a thing for Amon; we noticed at least. A month or so in I was in that state where I was paying attention to her, hearing who she spoke with, not wanting to see her go. I saw that she was married, seemingly. I'll have to check, but I made a retreat from this obsession, and let it go. From there, through the spring, we had a wonderful time. Our balance of work was nice, with 2-3 shifts. Going to Pitt Athletic after shifts was so wonderful, and we read so much as regards war and government. The Red Cross as I called it, from Loyd Georges memoir through world war 1. This all felt sublime. Through the months we found balance. I felt a little awkward with Bella, but also let her go; as we were feeling that strange seperation in seeing her leave. This helped me nicely. And we continued to regard our work and living. The news burned me though, and waking up early never seemed easy enough. One thing of note is that I often felt detached from people, and that in the morning I'd feel like there was no one around me, which had the ware from work being noticed. Contrast that with today, where we felt realtiely poor, yet due to our scenario, cared not. I felt not great, but this meant little. It meant more in those mid-year months. In many ways we just continued being me. This by our ability though, and non-expectation, as though something easy should be by our side. We did not humour, or at least take refuge in the fact that Amon, Bella or anyone was so taken by me, that we were so impressive. These thoughts came up from time to time, but we heeded them not. I found myself becoming much friendlier in the later part of the year, and this started to make work feel great. It's so funny, given whats happened in the recent month, and what we feel like we've lost, but those holiday months had me resisting working more, as it felt like it was taking away. And this is what's odd. That we had something to be weighed upon, something we cared about. Playing Ark, BF, reading this and that, writing the other. Exercise was a struggle, and by all accounts, what we cared about, 'which is simple in positions but lavish in flair', felt the effect. Only as this new year has gone on did we open ourselves up to the idea of moving, the motive being one of control. For we felt so worn by our house and family, in having to work as needed, but oblige this in any smooth or sensible way. And indeed, we let our past-time drop a little, in favor of doing things well. It remarks life in a way, that a deed lies at the floor, and you amend to it in a variety of ways, and flourish to some degree atop. I found it so exhausting to flourish, as our environment kept prodding me. So, with the intent to me, I let myself be but humble and happy, with the hope that more control would allow me to be happy and just. And we were about to, heart and all. And in a moment, snap! The scenario changed, and we were back where we started. I just simply couldn't go back to flourishing, 'as it was like having to escape the atmosphere'. But we were with nothing, nothing to look forward to, no-where to go. Just doing well as to make work easy felt like nothing, as it was for not. Work felt too little, as though our life consisted of nothing else. Indeed our entire adult life has been pivoted on this balance, whereby proper living was sacrificed for wellness ahead.
 My point, though I ought elaborate later on, is that this balance came with you from Kamloops to Pitt, was waived about upon our employment; and indeed, fostered and embellished with our acceptance of things, and rather indifferent yet pleased outlook of the time ahead. This was all very fragile though, in certain ways, and prone to exageration, self-imoprtance, shame and worry, and enviromental friction. In this very moment I feel less than I have before, in focus, dreams and prosperity. Yet, I feel I could address anything in the fullest way, and I don't feel poorly placed in doing. I just lack the energy and care, in some shape or form, for some things. I don't wish to write of the stars and their nature, nor the origin of things, nor Loyd George. It's all very strange, for such things came from some sort of neccessity, when we weren't working, or to make work better. But more often then not, through the age, these motions have been but the accumulated force upon a swing of things, into a retreat or out. "The soldifying upon adjustmnent." I think it's important to note that following our breakdown we attempted things and found social strenght. But our actual want was elsewhere, and without justification to others or fear on my part --in what we may not get-- I embellished that, greatly. My favorite writings are the beginning of Nearly Modern, and the far side of Color of Ends. Moral even, where we saw constrain ahead held much. And our time in Pitt Meadows was very much fostered by this. Perhaps paradise sat ahead, or something new and oppertune. But we were fine here, and able to just like what I saw. "We felt too little weight or reserve to not be pleased, and could enjoy the novelty." And indeed, that early part of spring, following our giving up of Amon and Bella, was so fine, it begs the question of our values, and why they're not formed after such. It's just this thing I keep hearing, 'Ryan it's easy, I fuck chicks all the time. You just say this, and they'll totally be into you'. And I can't help but comparing our acquisition of jobs and home, and how this felt far more difficult in the past, jobs especially; whereas now, you just walk in, ask for somoene, and speak as you want. Why aren't woman like that. Is there not a means? And one thing that speaks true, is that online enviroment is different, and that whatever success or ability (from trying, and learning from success what works) you find, it must come from particiapting in such environment. And by virtue of having to learn, and how distant things can be, your particiation must be active, as to develop, but also be present with your ability. And this is not something I've wanted to do. It's never been so easy as to just show up. And indeed, I've had some success from being so present. Our profile could be better, picture foremost if swiping is prevalent. Or we ought just pick a better platform, or invest in one at that --to remove limits. But I have done alright. And indeed, simply speaking with people makes me feel nice, and creates this space to breathe, in relation to whomever I may feel drawn to at work. "It makes such a place less essential or paramount, epitamous as it were."
It has never been hard to speak great words, usher up discipline and have, to quote mr robbins, 'a massive plan of action'.
 Yet I have found myself, seemingly as I beomce more free and able, asking myself what I mean to do, or ought, in terms of the actions actually taken, or would be? Sums for instance, I see it, dream of it, am empowred to make it true. Yet, all that really seems real, is that I'd write a short story about it and send it to professors, seeking feedback. Indeed, the promise of interest almost freezes me, as though our presentation ought be better. Or in some sense, that we are afraid to find out it has no interest. Yet we've never done anything with it. I believe in it anyway. Foremost, if someone could explain, simply, why the behavior occurs, I'd like to know. Perhaps everyone already knnows it; as such, I'd like to find out. This is a fairly solid thought, with real motive and sense. I can't say how other things I mean to do, or dream of, relate. For instance, we have more or less settled our media views, or leisurely passion, to the form of a news series that regularly covers certain games, movies, and government remarks. Relatively simple. Yet, this being the form of real act, does not draw me now, or sway me. Indeed, I think of how other activity can be interwined, or at least woven as to deepen the motive; yet the act in which that oppertunity makes, does not feel so compelling in it's own right.
So two things strike me in regards to health. One is that notion of action, and how ideals are not so relevant here, but what we can and are going to do. Our stabilizing muslces are all very tense, and I feel some warmth and tenderness in my neck and shoulders. This does not mean we should do pushups, as much as it seems we should. Indeed, strong moments have plenty of room on workdays, so I wouldn't weigh your choices to much as if to orbit those. Were fine. Right now I reckon we should run, as it will really ring things through. Doing chaturanga will also help, though prioritize breathing over stabilizing. General ground work always helps, in long poses, ie. knee to chest. Indeed, I don't think we need to stretch our neck and shoulders, but bring limbreness to our whole body, and thereby take the pressure of those finer muscles. Our stability needs more room to breathe and form.
 Eating wise I see that were having too little. Those red marks on your legs and proneness to bruise or cut is from you being catabolic. I reckon our musculature is part of this, as they store sugars; were likely just calling on our liver. Indeed, I suspect that part of our finer msucle pains is from this catabolism, as your muscles don't function without sugar, and so your catabolic state ignores those finer msucles. I would say the effect is strongly correlated with us eating too little. Like giving room for our posture, so we need room for eating. Everything is going to process a little flabby. We need to eat something, and indeed, a day off makes room for this. The effect of returning to proper nourishment will need seem obvious. But it is important that you eat. And indeed, I noticed that it is not so much about your overall amount, but that given what you had prior, how it was processed and stored, that what you do following bears the catabolism. For instance, we've had some sugar the past few days, and we did not eat much yesterday carb wise beside the bowl of quinoa and pasta. It fuelled me well today, but I think our use of it was overdone.
 Let us look at what we got. For one, mornings are good, with oranges, almonds, tomato and sunflower, cranberries, oat milk, bananas and bits of kale, all good. Cucumbers are on sale at Superstore also, and peanuts are very cheap. I find that having those nuts bolsters the catabolism. And our light food through the day feels great. Its the grains I struggle with. None are clear to me besides potaotes and quinoa. It's just hard to judge, as someitmes things may seem filling but from relatively small portions. And so I don't have strong wisdom here. This idea of eating enough is not very exact. We may have potatoes, some quinoa, barley, bread too. But none come together to form a large amount of food. And it's hard to tell why. For sometimes I feel like we eat enough. Other times it seems not. Off days feel a little strange, and hard to assess, given your want of finer things. But, generally, if the sun sets around 730, and your in bed around 9, then it seems you have some smaller meal leadnig into bed, with a large meal prior to sunset, and perhaps some grain or veg around 4. The ultimate fact is that eating properly is simple, yet requires regularity to reach enough. Whereas eating poorly meets your goals simply, but the effects are rough. One common element is that you don't eat the same things everyday, and if you change this or that, some things pair better or worse, yet the servings change. We are buying our own food, and not with much hesitancy mind you. Having granola and what not, or cucumber salad, or even tomato-sunflower, these are calorically rich, compared to the momentary gem of an orange. Having enough here ought alliviate the need for so much grain, or any inconsistency there. Granola is the obvious choice. And tomato-sunflower, although not terribly storage-inducing, makes for fairer eating during the day --it prevents usage at any rate. Truth be told, there is a moment in the midday where you want something more than fruit. Milk is best here, and gives us calcium. Bananas work here also is need be, and dried fruit pairs nicely --we've got some dates somewhere. But before this, the only real food is tomato-sun and oranges. Granola pairs till this, while cucumber salad bridges between milk and heart. These are pretty universal rules, and have room to faulter, realign the items. But your going to have orange, granola, milk, grain, bread and starch. Our shopping should be accordingly. Right nwo I don't feel like we have much. No bananas, some kale, full peanuts, no tomaote, plenty sunflower. Bread but no toaster yet (though air fryer works). 1 orange --though another old one. Quinoa cooked, nothing else.
 Indeed, one simple rule, or understanding, is that exercise not only promotes sleep and recovery, and general stability during work, but it draws you to eat, and properly; for you have so much of what is encouraged, that you do both what is good and sufficient. One of the hard things of eating when you don't want to, is you go for sugar, or you feel like your stuffing yourself; whereas working out makes eating needed, and so you don't mind prioritizing it, and doing little as to sit with it. This makes your day better, as time goes by where your fine doing nothing, as you either have or are going to eat. It stands as something essential and welcomed.
I don't wish to over-exert, but I've got some spunk and would like to add to our prior comment of action.
 This makes a lot of sense regarding Sums. We write the story, talking about its basic principle, and illuminate our motive as to the knowledge of this, by which I wish to understand, or if it is my honor to discover and define. Great.
 As for woman, or friends in general, there's two things. One, is being on platforms allows for emotional connection. It need not be nearby, as you can still talk and relate. When we were with Aurara, we ought a) asked her what she was looking for, if she was up for something frivolous, and b) as perhaps a earlier step, ask for a video chat. That could be real fun. Think of Emily. We went out there to hang out, expecting to fool around, and this did not really happen. We sat there for an hour before it did. But anyway, whether it is for some climax of frivolity, or actually furthering the connection, and all thats worth, I think this eligble. POF is great for this. OK is fine but not very much. Tinder is alright, though our matches are pretty poor. Which surprised me, as were using the same picture as POF and OK. Maybe the audience is differnet, but I don't imagine there being heavy discretion. I do feel a better picture or two would help. Anyway, for POF espcially but OK also, do the match thing, as you'll find people who've liked you, and you can send messages. Its pretty quick. No difference on Tinder, just smash the button. Hinge for instance, or even match --eharmony seems US based-- may be wortwhile, but in their case, I think that meeting in person needs to be on the table; at least, the effort required to make a suitable profile, and payment in matches case --though you could try free-- don't seem justified just to chat. OK is novel, and Tinder feels a little quiet, but POF has always served me here. People respond, its great to chat, there's even a few people nearby. I feel our profile could improve, but generally it feels real enough, and better than the common, 'checking things out'. The best thing about POF is that everyone there are grounded, compared to the fun surreal vibe of tinder. It feels undeniable that dating in general, from online, is more oppertune the closer you get to Vancouver. There seems warrant to invest and or pay in match in particluar, and indeed, there's more people avaialble from POF and wherever. But we are fine right now. And we've been talking to V, and just doing so gives me this sense, of what I've got, how personal something is. Do you remember the Virgin, who gets picked up by JKF. They must be so happy. Ought we even be wise about it. Its just their life. If your happy, so be it. Your inability to handle this just remarks your inability to commuincate, to be present, to be present with people and not feel your hiding.
I can speak to something that just happened, a feeling. Beside the practical moment, we worked out, and as we walked home, we had this feeling, of being sure, set, and attuned. Foremost it seems, was that this session, the recovery and regard to come, and regularity of this ahead, felt both important and demanding: worthy of priority, but also satisfaction, for the good it would bring. I felt really good. And I noticed when feeling this that all worry went away, as though we weren't missing something, no one had more than me. There may be a bit of a defense here, but at any rate, I felt that other's who go out for the day, party or what have you, are not enivable to me. I care not for that as something to do, but moreover, I tend to see it as this momentary thing, while we are more ingrained to the growing experience. Being sat in that kind of mode lets all things of the moment pass by; so I fear not.
 But I couldn't help but also notice that there was doubt, or at least a loose fit in this direction, to be so drawn, directed and deemed, to our working out, its requirmnets, and the pace and form of life that followed. It was as though we were aware of ourselves leaning in, and thereby aware of what we were steppnig away from. Bella epitomizes this for me, but usually any person can do this; at least one usually does; that they represent this kind of young free spirit, proper in their ambitions, solid in their sociality, and privy to fun. There is part of me that want's to take part in this. Indeed, I don't think anyone's particular life is like this. It is more likely that you merely wish to lean into that part of them you see, and cease this sense of avoiding that, 'as though it were saying, right here, over here, come to me'.
 I reconcile this, though with some struggle, that this part of them which you gleam is like a glow, but from a center which is not itself of interest to me, or desireable. But, it seems that we fail often to partake in that glow. Which is not so much of the activity, but just the experience of being with them. We tend not to feel sure in our own life, to feel happy in their prescence, but no less when were not. Sometimes it seems like there's nothing but that glow, and some center which we presume; cynically in the coarset of moments, as though to steady myself in thinking that glow is all there is. But we have only recently learned how to get to that core, so someone for who they are. And indeed, part of their glow is not poor or contrary to our own. It just feels hard, perhaps by time's adolescence or our own insolidity, that we don't get ourselves in there, hooked as we were, but steady. The word friend comes up, and indeed, we perhaps are too pre-mature in our understnading, or general sense, that people are somewhere and something, and thus, however you see them glow, it is not some invitation from them. They don't neccessarily want to be friends with me.
 One small part is that of age. For those who are young have time to make progress, but enjoy themselves while that is being done. While those of us who are old, are in our true employment, and thus don't feel this 'get your homework doen then go out and play'. We have work tomorrow. And indeed, on of those general truths is that kids who are in school, or are employed, likely feel that. They just may have less pressure on them for this fact to be so noticed. And indeed, if someone, as if remnisicent of their glow, works for independnace and does all they please, and that such pleasure is of the ecstatic and or occasioned sort, so be it. You perhaps don't know them enough, or yourself that is, to let things be. For even if all things for them are well, it is outside your jurisdiction. Your property is of soul concern. And indeed, the haze and or mystery of life draws comparison and or insatiety --and from there hope, for something nice you see-- but understanding helps. Perhaps a fair response given there being imperfection in your life. `
So our life has seldom been complete. For a time we would work, but usually upon a stretch where we had not. And so we would go through the process of adjusting to the labor and social element, and then upon that, find some sort of satisfaction as amidst our work. The details of that would run through for some time, till we got more or less bored. I think, for instance, of that time at Save-On, when we felt relatively dull and neutral, and decided to move out. It was as though that was an interesting and forward step. Yet when we went there, we realized there was no where left to go, except Vancouver; for which we dreamed of. But we just could not be. We had no real intent or driving factor, as if to remove any prevalance of the location you were in. We tried a whole manner of things, e.g fasting, running up the crunch, literally wanting to move to Thailand. I just couldn't be still; and felt somewhat odd, as though there was this factor that kept me from doing something so outlandish, as I sought. We wanted to walk around the world. And indeed, none of this came to pass. We quite Save On, got a new job, had some fun. But life just ran it's course, and we had no care or content in working, and we lived pretty unwell. We did better and John and Allans, but we were only the more happier, or with some peace; as though the general and required form of life, 'work being the center', was upheld, and we moulded ourselves around that. This is a bit complex, but we deiced, around April, to have no discretion in our food. And we ate pizza and pasta in huge amounts. I felt so bad, and everything was worse. I say this is complicated, for in none of this did we have any friends; and by all accounts, we were pleased but had no fun; there was none at least. From here I read Epictetus, which gave me some sense that our views prior were right; we just took a different form. In many ways, our want of vancouver, moving and being outside, were all effects of me not being happy. He showed my that such happiness was in fact the goal, but that we could approach in it a better way: as it were, as though it were in itself the goal.
 It is worth noting, though broadly, that often changes of mind and direction don't always run course as would best reflect them. In the case of Epictetus, I found my other views and ideas were humoured, namely in living outside. And indeed, when we moved home, and realized this untenuable, we accepted this indeluable current of life, where something had to be done. We elected to do illustrator, but weaned off, and merely studied histroy. We naturally grew more station, and thus were open to oppertunity. So we planned for personal training.
I would just speak of our life then. For these annals of time are of great note, but outside our capacity right now.
 We have moeved into our new place. It is nice, affordable by all common accounts. Our roomates are good, and thus pose no real aversive effect. The nieghbourhood is great, to scooter or walk, or exercise especially.
 We've started using POF, OK and tinder. The latter two aren't great, though Tinder does essentially offer means to chat. I've been on it for a few days and the interaction is low. Perhaps we must pay. I'd admitt our picture is meager and modest, yet, who is so discretionate. Your telling me, given how many people seem to use it, that only 3 people have swiped yes on me. I get likes very often on POF and OK. So whats tinder all about. No matter. POF is nice, despite having to pay to use it more than seldom. But people like to talk, and we've been talking with V, who's this nice woman from Nanaimo. I feel slightly connected and that's great. It brings me out of my shell, orients our view of the nature of these things, and thus removes jealousy and resentment. We may feel loss or hopelessness, but hearing her respond is satisfying; and we've the confidence to appreciate that, and measure our pleasure accordingly. There are many people to talk to after all, and I think there's a lot that can be done from even afar. It might not hurt to spend the 30 bucks to directly message 20 people. But right now, I'm just going to run the course with her, then we'll see anew. We do get a message a day, and there is this girl from Pitt I'd like to message. Things here are promising, in their worth at least. I was looking at the meetups for Vancouver, and there are many things. And dating is no different. I can feel seetling into the real status of my life, which brings no confidence, but also no sense of other people being any different. They may have what they have, but they only have that. Our sense of community is but a warming factor. And so for me, be it an activty or person, you just need one. At least, you need what would be gratifying, rather than the sight of everyone, 'as your absent, yet confident state seeks'.
 There is soccer at noon on mondays. There is adult soccer but its at 9, and costs 100$ upfront. The rec center is indoors, my preference, lower entry fee, closer, and just seems nice. It's one of those things that you may do once and never return, but so be it. You don't have to. Trying new things gives yuo refuge to do something else, or further bade into what you already find pleasing. This occured in the spring of 24.
 I will note also, that a very mighty yet calm approach to correspondance is accessible. We've got our discovery of sums, or at least prompt as to their being something new about it. I've got a pretty solid principle followed by the variety in it, and the notion that I seek credit and privalge to explain, or at least, wish to know. This is one of those things that takes time and effort, akin to making a real dating profile. And maybe the landscape, my interest, comapred to what else is there, and my energy at that, fashions our resolve to it. But like online dating it is there.
 What else though? I don't need promise, but I'm really removing our vainer concepts and looking at me, really. Our life, as it were, has for so long been incomplete, consistently that is. And I've said were making a bonfire with sticks and twigs. Yet, this past month, I've had those moved about, and I'm feeling the effect in our blaze.
 I will note that I watched porn momentarily, and found it awful. The impulse was there, as remarked our general excitment from chatting; which is not to say we felt sexually attracted, but had this more or less confident arousal, as though by being seen or known, that the rest of our life was on track. "And that shine from others, who seemed so appealling, and brought much ardor, worry and shame to our choice to be elsewhere, seemingly, went away." This is not so poor, and feels fine and good to exude. But porn was not great. Not awful, but near blatantly poor. Like it wasn't good. And uneeded.
I think this a nice comparison, that we've had this view for some time, that of the Dark Knight or There Will Be Blood, or star wars notably, that we once sought to make videos about these, or better put, make our views heard. This is something that has compelled us for a long time, across many things. There is this impulse, to respond and advise across a whole manner of things. I find myself talking out loud, as though to innervate such response. But I came to see that most of this want to be heard or speak came from me not being clear on what we had to say. As though we wanted to see it more. On one hand, there is the matter of importance, and how what you get from Revenge of the Sith is not so paramount,and you can just take what you get. Having more life makes this tenuable. Diablo for instance, were not so passionate about it now, just enjoying it for what it is. Our mind is elsewhere. But I allow this idea to remain open, perhaps guiding me with a steadier hand. In truth though, I consider Bella, or woman, and however well we are, if we approach it as though we weren't, which is to say with over-confidence and lack of skill or sense, that your attempt will fail. And indeed, so is this likely for all things. That even if growth has occured and your substance well, ought your actions sprout from there, 'rather than applying your sttenght in a lower way'.
 I think that akin to Simron, where we liked her, had real intent, rather than fear of not, that we were fine to let things go. Even if skill and sense could allow us to get closer with her, and steadily, we were still fine in our appraoch, as perhaps over-enthused as we were. We'd let things happen. Indeed, there is something to learn, activity to really speak to, for which skill remarks, 'that we can say this or do that. Video calls online, for instance, open up the realm of connection'. Indeed, one may have confidence in that. And truly, people are different, in that they have their own wills and lives. And similarly, if you expect that you'll just learn to make things as people would like, then your showing a similar false-confidence. But, you may trust in the thing you wish to make, and fear not of the result. Indeed, the correspondance we wish to do, alike our time on dating, may give confidence to the potential for things to be done for anything, if modest even. But we may rest on that. For life has long to go, and years to go on. And choices will make your sight of the future less glaring or wearying; but also more steadying, upright, and apt for ideas and productivity. Our whole life is coming into this mode. I felt this way when we left Vancouver. For the day to write our letter is not so. I feel not, nor am going to. But its there, ready, baring form of a known and active quanity; coming to life by the rowing prescence down the stream. That is it you see, our ideas and confidence in them come from the feeling we have in ourself. That if victory is won, then our wishes may come true.
It might serve us most to speak of things pleasing. Our solem sight of people or things' Id like, does me no favor. Reason perhaps speaks to all, and where I should be, but for another time.
 Masturbating feels really good. I've been doing it quite a bit, and our mood is not great, so it feels less than perfect. But it still remains good. And the abscence of discretion is really nice. For I don't put to much value on it, but it helps. And to do it with our toy, is pretty great.
So just as a bit of an overview of our doings, digital foundry has done some nice work on Helix and will do more for Crimson Desert. Which is itself coming in a few days. Unsure if I want to get it right away. It could be what we need to get out of this slump. I'll try and stay focused, for games, Ark is right there teatering on interest. I feel were already invested and am not so for Crimson. I just want to get into that world, get some monkeys, gather material, explore a bit of the southern map. Just hanging out in the red woods is nice, neary the rocky portion. May not last for a while but thats ok. I'd like to try it. New map for BF is coming, may be a good one. Still so intense and not sure if I like that. Would not mind going back to play some Manhattan bridge, give Mirak a chance and firestorm. Some smaller game modes could be fun, like rush, and breakthrough is a cool alternative. There changing weapon XP also, so now could be a good time. Next workshop may be coming, which really just means lots of video. Our old game is still warranted, ready to go into Modern. Just want to play really, and get high science.
 Seimfeld was great and spoke to that notion of a quick show, like New Girl, or Sunny. Web shows are like this, but are a bit different. We finished 2016, have been watching Rev thorugh E3. And my interest is off. The content is too casual and informal. I'd feel the same about Bosman. I really just want to watch Gt time. Did not plan to, but I'd be pleased to watch that. However far we go, then begin 2017, which is so fun. The current allies podcast is really good to. And over the years there are a lot of great episodes, from 2022 onward. 32 is really nice; I respect Elliot a fair bit and Kyle is a great partner. The show has no visual so it's not always an auto watch. But it comes about. And I really do enjoy Hockey Guy. Panix Indexes and power rankings are fun, and regular.
 I watched No Country and it was great. Just a nice gem. Dark Knight Rises is really bad, but the ending makes it for me. Survivor has been wonderfull. 21 is such a fav. I want to watch South Pacific, not sure about one world, but Phillipines for sure, then Fans Vs Facorite. Then the golden age, of Caguyan, second chance, gen X maybe, but San Juan Del Sir for sure. Thats exciting for me. And this new age, after Redemption, feels nice and cozy. I think something new would be nice, be it Sunny or New Girl. There's maybe something else, or something we could try. There is so much Tv its insance. We watched Scenes a while back and Mare, and True Detective. One thing I'll note is that returning to Survivor was alright given the right spot. We just started from where we paused, and we've got 2 episdoe left of True season 2. We'll finsish that then go on to 3 when were ready. Would not mind watching Star Wars. Still haven't seen Fantastic Beasts. The Superman theme is cool, could watch Man of Steel.
 Were talking with V, who is 36, lives in Nanaimo, has a daughter. She may almost trust me. She has responded and were havinga nice conversation. I feel were trying and our skills are better. To bring this to light, but the advice someone gives you calls on the situation to be a certain way to make use; if someone is advising how to fold bread dough, you actually need some dough. And I find first princples and mature, confident yet real and genuine self is better if your someone who's defined. If your not, and coallesque easily, then the tips for taking it further are only useful upon reaching that state. And indeed, I find that we need no tips, for if we've been ourself, no games are being played. Its quite easy. We just need to be ourself. Anyway, talking with V is great and says alot about you and things. But as for her, I think we may ask her some personal questions, and perhaps at that point, go on a phone date, or at least voice call. I think that'd be real cool. Part of me is just excited for the potential of someone who is in fact far away: people are in some ways indubitably close. intamcy still feels possible, but just that. Let our trust build, and make progress from a point of stationary. I like that. Very cool. And smoething like Matin knowing me a bit more, or tristan being there, and feeling the general warmth of work, is great. But this thing with V is a nice form of intimacy, really has taken my mind off of other whims or wants, that realy come from there being something not so. And indeed, fear not that anyone is held off. Have some sense of work and age. But in reality, learn to do what you want, and in that, what is reasonable
So, let us trust V. I feel vulnerable for sure, and its like weight on a loose thread, 'where were exposed'. It is just a thread though, and you can trust her. She's responded, I'd say genuinely, several times now. I want to keep hearing from her. Think of Maninder, who would reply or sometimes not, but often when she did it was later. It is monday morning, nothing to expect. Her online status does not mean she's checking. Just message her tonight. And indeed, she may not reply, or show not interest, and we'll move on. But this is good. Your part of something you want to be. And indeed, your motive is not poor. It's just whether we'll feel better.
 At any rate, I am going to make individual books for color of ends, as to see our views following our removal of grandeur, nearly modern, where we were right in, and moral, where the good from before had a engaved road ahead, while we merely were. "The world in waiting." I'm not going to be too picky or discretionate, and I just want it in book form. We can't even humour, or need not struggle to, in the full price --despite benig able to return and edit. We can pay 8$ for the book, little broken cover but so be it. I'm thinking light blue, some form of orange and red, a little green and yellow.
 Nearly Modern has Past, points which I've not read, nor tamber and to see. These could use a cursory glance, while we remove the ill-fit ones we've noticed from other things. Each piece is good as it gets.
So hearing Jeff talk about a vision, as compared to idea, in that one is about bringing it to life, I think of the motive for such creation, the principle and or reason that drives you. "Why are we making whatever it is we are?"
 And I think of the grounding factors in life, in how you have a body which may feel limbre and spry, up and ready but able also to rest. There are all these things to do, as I have, but they remark some sort of foundation which is altered from them. When we first had that smoothie in May 2015, we realized certain grounds were there. And when we read Epictetus we saw there were grounds for us. Another one is in may 2017, where we saw the basis for fasting. "That eating had a particular effect, over not."
 I struggle with being a man. Philosophy is easy. For in one you follow rules, and indeed, beyond outside pressure, it is actually quite satsifying to be this way. But your not really yourself, but rather guided, on rails, in need of control, 'rather than merely being'. Indeed, from 2020 onward, and to some extent, as our health basis was on, we simply were. Yet people compelled me, as did the wear from work have me wishing for eslewhere. And by this or that reason, grandeur and glory seemed too far away. I can't say exactly what I mean, when I consider those spring notes in 2020, and how they evened me out. Are these not rules? Perhaps we needed some. But what brings me to such direction, at least the reoccuring thread that has me go amuck, 'and in need of renewal', is the longing and or withdrawal from people, certain ones some of the time, or the group otherwise. And this is so for we lack an aim. But, the reason this occurs, is that the aim we have, which I'd say is mighty and well, holds less means and avenue, or at any rate asks you to really accept this, but thereby communicate well and vulnerable. Put another way, if we truly wish to live in this awe-stricken way, then it ought be natural; for as much as it is not, we close ourselves off, or struggle to speak as we please. THis creates grief and feeling of superiority. For you must live completely. Something that our ascetic impulse could not accomplish. Yet we still aim. The burden of things creates want, which is hard to appease, for you must slacken your temeprance, or bear friendship, which is not so easy to do. The meager yet merry lesson I've learned, is that your soul desire is to be with whom you please; but your deference or uncertainty leave you mute, for which you feel loss, which leads to expectation, 'as if to reassure or affirm', or something more or less cynical. Or your focus on your self, nurturing the rule. Some mixture of time and familiarity create all. Asking questions also. Indeed, some manner of forbitiy, as not to be thrown or elated by passion or emtion, but also strenght and solidity as to feel vulnerable and unattached, whereby innocnet questions are asked. "We often think were missing our shot, or things seem to unremarkable: dryness seems to be present, which frightnes our wanting ways." Some people say things I like, others say things in a way that interests me. For one you just need to know how, to ask and share. For the other, it is about you, for you need to be yourself in some way, as to respond to their enthusiams. "I heard you say this, or that you like that. This doesn't matter to me to much, but I like that 'you' said it, and so this part of me that relates is complled to share. Your just that interesting."
 There's just a field of norms and shrubbery, carving out a spot for you to work, eat, play and sleep. To see maybe, or venture. But that element of play at least, in a similar way that the element of work can procure more venture, that so does our eating procure a height of play. In part, we are not often with much to say to people, out of interest or commonality, in that what we love is play, and what we value is eating, not work. I've no one to really complain with, and so were just with myself. This is part of philosophy though. Normally she'd say, 'to live like this is to live honestly and true. It can be done with anybody, but there is no room for norms of the crowd, which most will not pursue'. But a man though, which is to say someone set, needs no such harbouring. We see though that choices, skill, and ill-clarirty create attachment and affirmation. Our emotional stability has not come so far as to see that our feeling of someone does not mean this; nor do we avoid this sense that were destined, or pivoting around them. Following rules is great, and when times are well this works fine. In one sense your blossomnig the substance of your care and way, 'the ideas if you will'. Indeed, for a day it's seems, we've removed our ill-wanted view of things, 'the grandiose vision for our ideas', and merely let those ideas be. This felt nice given how things happened, and that we could just be. But when the moment fell, our lack of true vision became apparent. I could have told you, and indeed gave much thought, to the fact that Bella did not like you. I was sure and strong enough to thwart, or at least remark upon such a feeling through the prior year. It did not throw me. But I found myself less committed to things, and unwanting to re-attest to rule. I felt I could give myself to something, easier and exciting. Indeed, I felt solem over the caution in my mind, as though the truth were other than what it seemed; a truth I recognized and found very little distates about prior. She seemed to like me talking with her, in that way about her test. Me being there, as I was happy with, struck me as being something she was glad; like she were happy we liked her. And despite my better judgement, which had told me all to that point, and been accepted by me, that she does not like me, gave way to the thought that she was pleased that we liked her. And so I felt almost invited to do so. Perhaps it is like me, in that I like when other people like me, but do not neccessarily like them. I wish they were just there. This is fine, truly, and remarks someone solid and true. We just weren't in a space where we coulnd;t be so clingy --which paired with bodlness kind of spoils the charade-- yet still remain ourself. That we could be present but not get sucked in, going away when the moment was over, our life carrying on. It is all about what we like. But we wanted to like her. That was the initial excitment when I spoke with Tristan. Or when I thought of Amon, just saying I like you was what I cared for the most. Hence my want for permission. And I feel like I got it.
Swashing around me are feelings of a regretable sort, of times we tried, moments we missed, chances we gave up, or could not have attempted. I see these things clearly as a result of our past, and not something to burden us now. I truly believe, as our expeirence and insight show, that all you ever wanted is avaialble. Its just a matter if there's some consistency and rule in your life, which gives some stature and ability, whereby the novelty and or pedigree of your vision comes to life, and thereby produces confidence.
 Getting a good sleep tongiht is important, as are most nights, off ones especially. I feel solid in these regards. Were up at 530, so some time around 9 is good, and I'm on par with these. No grough nor adjustment. And we've been good with our off nights. The alarm helps, to give us a reminder of the time, and start pitching our sights for turning off.
 Eating is importnat, and I know we ought eat enough. No stuffing yourself, but rather a consistency throughout the day. I won't say our balance is superb, but given the prior week and how we felt then, I feel much clearer and in control. Barely will likely help, and potatoes would just be a good mix of fair and comforting. The efect of Barley is important, and we'll take note of our steadyness tomorrow. As always, granola helps. We do need peanuts though, for having granola through the day really elevates your intake, and allows for things to be had at the right time. Forgoing granola means that the time you have milk or salad or heart, is from too empty a state.
 I know that putting yourself out there is hard. But, don't put too much weight into when someone responds, or if they don't. If they do respond that's great, but don't assume much. Consider your interest and message back when you want to. And do note that all things in life fall down this part of us that like people, but are not always sure what to do or what to say, and that, given any doubt about your way in life, that its missing this and that, that what we truly seek is just to say what we want, and avoid nothing. There is much to learn. But indeed, a more balanced heart will not deem it either easy or worthy to go out with every person you like. Being near them, honest and unworried is enough. Indeed, having something in common is more than enough, and being yourself currates that. And whatever sense there is that you want something more, and cna't ask, 'as though your revealling your cards', remarks the fact that your not sure. Not playing games is a mark of a steady self; which will not garner you wide-spread love and acclaim, but will let you be where you want, nor with grief if reason gives little room for certain things; notbaly, where there are elemants of want and excitation, but no comparability.
 I know that working out in general, and in particular when we have not done it for a while, a pose at least, that we foremost must respect this, and move or not with this in mind, aware of how we are and what our likely rest and or recovery is needed. One thing we could improve on is in long holds for upper body. One misconception is that for the sake of your neck or other delicate parts that you need to do targeted rotations and stretches, when in fact it is your broader holds that need more attention. The breathe from dandasama or forward fold are like that, or merely standing. Breathing in mountain is as such lengthening. Knee to chest has a similar effect, as does corpse or downward dog. More challenging poses are fine and has some offer, but the reality is that your breathing is what is universally needed, and that such specialized posed may not produce this. There is something to be said about being able to breathe through such things, such as in lunging, dog or squat. But practice and habit will bring those out. There is some cost to doing such poses outside the breathe, and having worked out, we can afford little.
So I had a lot of positive responses today. Starting the day off I did not run in there, ready to make a splash. I just let the day go one. In part this was due to doubt; but generally, it reflected us not being so insecure or vapid. I felt it though; as though our own substance was droping, that we did not have something so clear and defined to hold us up. But work took its way, and I found myself just being where I was, with who I was with. I've noticed this as of late, but one solid tip is that seeing people for the first time is better met with hi's and hellos. Only after that, when you have some mmoent during the day, ought you approach them. There is more for me to give at any rate.
 I felt deeply that we were not friends with many. One prevailing though of recent memory is such a notion, and the added sentiment that no one really cares for you. And when we hear them talking to other people, we feel forogtten, or just not so important as we thought, 'which weighs on our identity'. But one notion came up today, and that was that we are all on the current of responsability. None are above this. None so sunk. But saying hi or hello, talking even, comes amidst responsability. A free moment it is, or one you elect to do. But you've merely stepped out. The current keeps on though, and they return. That's the insight! I find there is some reason and likely grace in adhering to such a model, and thereby putting yourself into the ebbs of such tasks. For it brings us all in truth; we only vary in how well we apply ourselves to it, and or how steady we are, or at least consistent. For I do indeed step out sometimes. What comes to mind is speaking with Tara, Kim and Matin today. And this happens in breif moments or longer oneos throughout the day. But let there be no confusion that it is such, while work remains. It is merely for me.
 What this raises though is this fundemental concern, that above all things else, your pleasure in things is merely yours and not something whihc remarks how others feel about you. "Doth our dreams of glory and attention lay". This is good, and is liberating, for clarity of the finest things. Or peace at least in our balance of work and play. But we do feel a dissaray, at least an ill-defined foundation, for which we rest on in our work and bantering. For I get pulled too often, in both positive and negative ways. People of the exuberant sort, like Kass or Bella, evoke this in me, and for an age it seems, we've either avoided them or become obsessed. We could not just be, happily, and in some sense, make friends, 'as though we were solid enough to be so forth, but not overly drawn'. There seems to be a great movement as you go through the day, where soon you'd be off, where you ask people what there going to do. I really am interested, its great to here, and we get to share too. It is somewhat enlightening to be so forth about yourself. For it gives credit and admittence in your heart, to feel treated by such things; though not hidden or in despair. "As though such things divide you." Not everyone invites this. We were in a space where Bella did, but not as of now. Right now we merely say hello, talk about work, and say good bye. A shame really, as I did like talking with her, more than Andy perhaps. But alas. And, whatever hope there is to return, it is nice that were where we are, and not where we were. But for most I'd say, more than just some, you can ask. It's actually a great thing, and a mark of finer socializing. Quite pleased am I. What a day does give.
 I need to be a doctor though, and question this frivolous impusle I have, to imagine girls, or random woman even, as being for a split second interested in me; and splattered about in this are coarse images and or feelings of sex, as reflect porn and or domination. This is not something I'm pleased with. For years now we've been aware of the glimmering effect from porn, but only as of late have we truly backed of. Masturbating does not dissuade me, but in being non-chaste, our explicit imagery does come out. I think there are ideas which relate to our ill-view of people, or absent view, where we feel ourselves to warped in, 'as if some visitor who has to concieve who he's with'. And indeed, vanity brings expectation, as does, I'd say, a pretty insincere and jubilent confience, and arrognace if you will, to have such views as though they were reminsicnet of possibiltiy. "I am deserving of that, owed it. It is what I am worthy of." One little notion that came to mind is the glaring idea that younger people have frivilous sex, and that our current ideas may be more permissive then. THe notion though was that girls were easy back then, so much as frivolity and explicity applied. Sex is surely never detached from fun, but it is between two people, and ordained via them, rather than something you imagine. And those two people coming together for sex is not so easy, less your young; only for some even.
So being able to do free range feels important. We may not
I feel like a kid who wants candy, or at least sees someone else having candy, or imagines the possibility, but has no access to it, or feels there is this hurdle to reach, or they're just directed elsewhere; and so they feel bad, inferior, frustrated and doubtfull.
 Also, the feeling of rancour and melancholoy come up, which are forms of grief and discontent that flicker imagery and feelings of the comparable sort; where you feel either sympathy because of some idea of imperative upon someone's suffering or hurt; or you see something similar, but of the envious sort, where you feel an imperative of someone's pleasure, but also within that, a sense of how they achieved it; as though were so different, and they're in sich a way as to bring that pleasure. Hence the doubt.
 These are not the words of an adult though. Socializing is more or less done to the extent you want to. But there is an element of happiness and comfort, whereby one, for instance, who is stressed, may say less.
 And indeed, our situation is such, whereby we lack problems, but thereby a vision, and have a hard time relating to people on ethical or moral gorunds. "Where we go, what is worhthy of deed, and not." The effect of this is more of a dynamical one, ni that what spurrs socialization does not happen. "Its as if our misalignemtn of ways removes the oyxgen needed for fire."
 I'd remark then that our misalignment comes from our nature, in that we may be devout in a certain way, or reckless even. But foremost does it occur from our lack of understanding of oruselves. For it seems true that learning about people is a result of you being sure; and socializing is a result of comparability, or some sort of spark. Our nature determines who we'll click with, while our condition provides the fuel for ignition. "Sparks and kindlnig are a pair made in heaven; but you need air. Something like that at least."
 Indeed, our nature is quite severe. From an early age we made the decision to bring ourselves away from what we perceived as girls and glamour, but in doing so, made the arguably deterimental decision to change schools; which having done so in the year prior, and to one that was not nearby, we positioned ourself in a reltivley unoppertune area. By whatever motion or quality, we did not strive for anything in school, but lived as though the purpose for such excellence was unconditional. In practice, we merely got by, 'happy that we had done enough as not to be deemed unwell'. But our aim was for not.
 And all throughout that age, through our adult life even, we've either made such motions as reflect our 'getting by', or serving the moment as it were. Or, upon doing so usually, we found some enthusiasm, as if to get ourselves on track; yet the overhwelming set of these motions were motivated by the sense of direction of change made; and not to speak of the end sought, but they were not held after a time, to which we only got by upon noveltie's fade. And as it were, drift back in recession.
 Now, to be fair, there are good choices here. One's that, at any rate, despite leading to times where an aim was no longer placed, or recession occured, that we still did what was good and reasonable for that moment. Moving to Coquiltam felt like that. Moreover, in our adult life in particular, there were choices, and insights at that, which reflected a more meaningfull and lasting truth. Which is to say, that motions were made that had a lasting effect. These often reflected some new understanding and a view of some element which was essential to our life; and given the constance of it, our agreement to this was more or less a disposition to it, and thus an aim. One might even say that our aim became of virtue, in that the constance of the deed drew us to oblige, being then the aim.
 But, what stirrs me most, which leaves dread and promiscuous parsimony, is the affirmative tendency upon our excited and or retreating phases. For in doing these, much was either looked forward to or missed; and by that, a source of fear in the case of retreat, 'for the scrutiny we'd get'; but too in the case of elation, did we feel reality more sturdily built below, for which our less than realistic, or hopeful views, drew us to avoid this regard. "That our dreams may not be true." All that is to say, that we often thought of people possessively, obssessively, with frustration, and our own hatred and loating. "Something I still feel from time to time; that people are just lovely sometime, and you want to go and give them a hug, or a kiss. The esscence of love. Yet, you picture yourself as someone who is just too different, or far behind, or full of excuses to be with said person. One example that really draws shame is this feeling that you like other things, and that doing so forbids, or at least seperates you from said person in possibility. And anyone who seems even somewhat normal feels like there primed to snatch them up, or be thought of by said person."
Oh my, arrogance and self-pity, how they make my mind feel. So awkward and obstruse,, yet somehow moentarily inundated, attached.
 But these are not the motions of someone solid and true, but rather someone grapsing, 'as if lacking air'.
 For my whole life I've never had much more to say then, 'things are well and so am I'. Yet, when they weren't, it was apparent that we had no aim. And indeed, the purpose of such aim is generally towfold: neccessity and potential. For we've always known we had to work, but did not always. And we knew there was potential, but not always did we make. And what feels more apparent than ever is that life, and our thinking, 'to bare our living', is a entity of the complex sort, folded here and there, mannered in this and that way, of textures soft and dim. We cannot just speak of things as if they produce the rules we are to abide by. But we must instead use our faculty of reason to paint a picture worthy of all the edges and moods. Such a process may in fact be flat as a portrai is, or general at that. But depth, albeit an illusions, creates the experience as so; and we must consider the figure
Anyway, I wanted to ask you something. What is your aim in all of this, POF, online dating etc. I'm curious what your expectations are? I only ask because I find that the online nature of this and breaks between talking can kind of deflate interest/connection. I've enjoyed talking to you, and you've sparked something in me. Wouldn't burn down a forest, but something nonetheless. I think its important to be somewhat deliberate in trying to cultivate something. Feels like your plateauing otherwise. And I don't want that. I'd like to get to know you more, personally. I have no qualm with you living far away. Can't speak to the longevity of this, but I'd like to talk with you more, maybe video chat sometime.
 I've been trying to make a genuine effort in all of this. Meeting people is not easy, but forming something online doesn't feel any easier. Life (by it's nature) pulls my attention away, and whatever spark there is is quick to fade. Which I don't want to happen.
 I'm not trying to be dramatic but straightforward. I'm not really asking for anything. But putting yourself out there and being on this site is about matching/finding someone, and I feel you gotta make an effort, to really nourish what you find. That's my feeling
One thing I find in your social life is that you want people to like you. But you also want to like them. But if they don't respond, or you have to wait, you feel yourself getting unerved. Its as though, if I don't like you, you wont like me, but were apart.
 This may not reflect the true feelings at hand. For what you often do is give a big declaration, of intent. And this is more often than not too much; and indeed, afterwars, I find myself rather withdrawn, 'as though we've promised something which we don't actually wish to recipricate'. A similar feeling is that of hearing from work that you will get a job, or chance at least, but you just want to have that knowledge, and prefer that you didn't have to go right now. Or, when you got your place, but wanted to go double check, you preferred if you had to go later. It is important, like making a doctors appointment, to go, and to put what is good over comfort. But in the case for dating, I see that you feel yourself withdrawn and losing interest, or have doubts of hers. But ask what her expectations are. Be curious. It may be the last line. Maybe we just don't have much to talk about. Indeed, there is this odd element of want, which is very much a trajectory of the now, with little regard for whats to come. It will end certaintly. But I feel fair in bringing that up. We could just be more suttle though. In the same way that flirting is as a forward advance, to like or not, that so are your actions for your want equally oppertune, and possible driven by smarter and simpler wording. Behing honest as it were, and let her go otherwise. For I imagine certain things, things which are not poor or unbecomnig. But they reflect a certain level of trust. A level I'd like to reach. Which again, remarks expectations. Ought we try and cultivate something? I take refuge in the fact if you were just curious or liked this person, 'that you wanted to talk about something', that you wouldn't need this expectation, for you would just do.
In our soul, we find much of worth, as though some pasture were being grazed, full of sight and scenery. Yet certain livestock seems missing, and I can't help but feel poor in comparison to tohers, who by all accounts have such things, and that our own lack is from perhaps understandable choies, but some no less; moreover, our ways, in that based no choice and predilection, we feel to overwhelmed and (initially) lacknig to want such things and pursue.
 For years this has pressured me into ourself, as though we were to appreciate other things, and further concentrate on ourselves. Our sense of this changed some time ago, where we realized that doing this did not mean that we had to forgo such livestock, or better yet, that such livestock wouldn't fit.
 I have found though that such livestock is not so accessible. Certani avenues come to mind, but are not actually so simple or easy; from our experience at least. POF has often provided means for connection, talking to people with more or less similar intent. Not everynoe strikes you so much, and our loins have the tendancy to proliferate our want, and rush us forward, or feel to unerved to way. Indeed, if we really liked the persno, then this isn't there, for you feel yourself steady by you showing up.
 I had this dream last night, that we were at work, walking by Bella, looking at her, as we do now, but with no smile. It was mere recognition. As though we were hiding something, along the lines of dissapointment, and that she knew this, yet our understanding was that this was the way things were. Then, in some twist of things, we were in part of the town, talking in a group of people, of some sort of game. And Bella showed up, and we were eyeing eachother; I had a similar look of seperation. But as the game finished, she remarked how what I had said before wasn't the be all end all; it seemed as if she were saying that she was not against us going out, or that her saying no did not mean ultimately. But she did then say that she just didn't want to go out with anyone. Two feelings rose. One, is that I felt as though our body was one the verge of being given somethnig, something so pure and precious that I'd have to do nothing to live: like a nest that would allow me to forever sleep. Second, is that I felt, despite her saying her want to go out (more or less in general) was mute, that I still felt, or imagined (in my dream mind you) that she would still just go out with someone else. Someone her ambivolence felt centered around me. Perhaps trajectory was in play, where I felt that one day she may be interested, and we were not subject to that. Hence this grasping feeling, or odd wish to have what felt so near. It's like a dream of much money, in that you feel fashioned in ways completley different than you do now: flipping the peaks of work and discomfort into their finer opposites.
 I made the point shortly before that much of our desire comes from what we've already experienced. We brought some sense of belonging and playfull flirting from Harry Hooge. We had a crush on Rozzana. Soon, though in grade 9 mind you, we were introduced to the impressions and proceeding habits from porn. We looked at girls butts through grade 10. I heard Ethan got a blumpkin then too. Colin flirted with Nadia in grade 11, teasing her, and more or less cuddling --though playfully. We started going out with Ashley. And like someone who is stubborn and reserved from drugs, upon it being near you feel like having it, wanting it, but in a way near to your less-willing nature. "Come closer and do something for me: I either care not enough, nor permitt myself enough, to try."That is the mistake in all of this. We know ourself too little, or have experienced too little of the novel yet common sort, to live as we feel proper and neccessary, but not so hidden. Such a state creates a want that is almost treacherous and forbidden, yet granted by me. Akin to misheviousness. Were not there though in any whole way. And indeed, we never actually create anything sufficient for those mighty graces, of romance and relations.
