Were not there though in entirety either, and we lack the experience to know the difference, and realize what it takes, that were not on some edge; and that if someone else is, they either work for it, or are easy.
I find a useful conmparison between that of love and looking for rooms. As we felt so distraught over missing Waiserely, and 207th initially. Then our thought was, 'the details of this are not what's important, but rather the foundation in this, of having our own place." Indeed, since moving here, I've noticed nice things, but foremsot is it present that we are on our own, and have this space to think, prepare and decide. It has been so rainy that this can't help but be noticed.
 The moral though, is not that it won't be nice when we can appreciate those finer elements, nor in the case of someone's hottness or just heart-melting appeal that this wouldn't be nice to embrace. Like, 'oh, finally'. But, it is not what makes it true, or deeply meanginfull. As we have a home, so do we have relationships, with Mateen, Tristan, Andy, Joey even and Carli. It serves us nothing but ill-will and shame to think of those details were missing; and as it was with 207th initially, to get swooped up in them, at the cost of the foundation going away. That is what the stake should be. At least, that stake should hold such an according sight, that the details are not so precious, and torn from us, 'as plutarch says'.
 I see deep down that our parents, or family at any rate, have always lived with duty in mind, and expected such of me. Being frivolous was never shunned, but taht we'd do our task was paramount. And indeed, our youth, 'whereby such tasks were given weight', was what I measured myself against. It is perhaps forgetfull of me to say taht had circumstance not thrown us amuck, that our general course would have been productive of some career direction. But indeed, we held too liitle sights or firmness on such, but something akin to complacency. And so when w e dipped our toe in more playfull waters, getting thrown out left me understandibly upset, but thereby unable to so 'moderatley proceed'.
 It is hard to say, all these years ahead, through our early confidences and failure, thorugh independance and lack of purpose, through maturing and acceptance, to ability and liberty, that the root of this all comes from those turbulent years, where our natural direction held no effective effort from me. And upon trying, we felt so meek in our aim. "Nothing to affirm to us why the effort required mattered." We just could not get back on the path. And perhaps it's worth noting that such a path was never anything but serviceble, yet still decent. We cared not for school and high acclaim, but did not slump away. Were cynical in that sense.
 To perhaps put it bluntly, had we stayed course and not left Westview, or perhaps avoided the impetus all together, or had more clarity upon our transition into ease, then maybe we would have prodded along. But the question is not really on of whether I would or would not have changed? I will attest to the fact, that prior to Pitt, where enjoyment was our aim, and permitted seemingly, that we were not truly happy. Indeed, I found much comfort, and that pool of playfullness extended our scope of friends. But the pleasure I personally found, in things so pure and simple as Zack and Cody, or Bernstein bears, that these felt somewhat unsatisfying upon that break from the pool. For these things were comforts to me when we were truly with ourself. This did not get detached when we entered the pool. But when we got out, I felt, understandably perhaps, miserable being myself. And so things that comforted me in that were no longer so. I compare that to the recent misery in februrary, where I could not bring myself to eat. And my point, is that indeed, staying course may have saw me make different friends, find some advantage and or prosperity in school, even if a moderate amount. But would I be happy? Where would I go? To which life I do not know. I think there is some merrit in suggesting that upon us becoming open, and then experiencing loss, that our true care in life was revealled, and shown to be none. At least, we had not much but the enthusism from discipline and focus, as experienced when selecting grade 12 courses. And similarly, when we went to school in 2016. I think that there's some rose-tinted glasses, in thinking we would just go on our merry way. Often in those years we would dream of things, simple things. Like getting a trade, having a girlfriend, being in a friend group. Such things felt so nice. But I am not sure that once I had them that I would care when I did. And this thought did not substantaite any effort to them. But that was the quanderey, as it were. That if we did not pursue or look for those things which (as imagined) seemed so satisfying, what were we to do. Perhaps there is some similarity in that early grade 12 enthusiasm, whereupon our lack the passionate embrace of the future was reassuring, 'out of some relieved deference'. That similarly, in May of 2015, we found similar solice and enthusiasm in ourself. For our passion for the future, 'or careers and schooling so esteemed' did not motivate me anymore. "I saw the puppeterr wagging the imagery that, in form was rather drawing; but I saw the strings no less." But the actual matter of happiness, me, our experience, as felt constituted and brought by health, so this drew us.
 There is an element in this, a tricky one, where we see how these moments of passion, and as it became 'self regard', would always level off, and the concern for things real and exciting came up. There was a change though in the spring of 2020, where the somewhat insufficent duty, whereby we'd live unjust yet spiritually free, once held this unstable grip on us, that so we saw (at Superstore) a sort of true freedom offered by our efforts. Indeed, such efforts were hard
 Looking back then, that time from Superstore onward was a keystone one. For so much time prior we were trying to commadeer our time, as to give ourselves room to do both what we pleased, but also saw oppertune. "As though our genuine effort would bear something propserous." The issue though was that there was this implicite promise we were making. Of that which we had to do but weren't, and did not want to, no worries, we'll make do elsewhere. Time is fine, and we just need to be ourselves. There's just a flaw in this fundemnetally. In any case, we entered Superstore with reluctance, a wave of unconfidence and nerves, self-conscious about our annunication too. And generally we just had to affirm ourselves, feel confident, hope and or expect, to deal with the weight expeirenced. Then we met Holly, who felt like a breathe of fresh air. She would make our burdens deed like, as the ware from work would be off our mind. All I really wanted was to do as I seek; but work got in the way. Mentally foremost. Holly would remove this. But she did not. For a whole swath of reasons. But what followed was the key moment, where I found that this job, the thing which felt like a burden, a hurdle, something we had to leave, if taken seriously, was not only enough, but relatively easy. And in that, much good was found. The flow of it all and balance of music and free-thinking (while actice) struck us foremost. I enjoyed our friends, but did not appreciate them so deliberaytley. Writing came about here in it's most formal way, forming our view of why this all was enough, in terms of what we were asked and given. I rememeber so fondly walking down ford road, listening to Kong, feeling like love and beauty, wonder and awe were infront of, 'giving me breathe'.
 I've made mistakes since this, fallen into old traps, knowingly or not. Our sensibility though has grown enormously; our view of challenge and victory is vast yet delicate, with me in the most rainy and rot of days.
 Why are we still doing this so. No one is a friend to me, of a claim fairly stated as friendship. We've never found love, nor found much in our efforts but loss. Though I can't feel too bad. Aurora was very good company, and our skill seems to have come short, in what we could have asked, for something meaningfull at any rate. I made a mistake recently. I reached out, as though I had been set to release, only to have my pardon extended. Our sense was gone though and we acted confidently, only to be shown we were off. And in this, we see that our life is not so renowend and redeemable when placed outside of motion. You see the hope and nearing. You feel what we lack, and are looknig away from, or have some validified and or remedying poultrice. "I didn't realize we needed so much soothing." Yet I see what a man I am. How our dreams of conquest and fortuity are no longer detached from reality, but palpable in some real form. Our thought is there for this, the general potential is there too. Refinement is more than felicitous here. A world without fear or trouble, tasks without trepidation, love that reaches not, nor fears of meekness. "Comparably, the imagery and sensation of violence lays, in that you get swooned and grieved by the prescence of doubt. In both cases though, knowing yourself, from your own steady peace but also criteria and information, remove the impulse and or pulsation of putridity and missing out."
So I have to say that things are going well. I'm smiling. There are these obtrusive thoughts, but most of them come down to a) a similar posture amidst the flashing and or unsolid views of war, for which thinking of other peoples romance falls into, 'as though they don't also have lives, and are so unconditionally and explicitly loving to one another', and b) that knowing someone who seems to do well feels more unnerving than some stranger, for the truth is that it does not matter, nor is there any trigger for jealousy or doubt, and that someone your familiar with should not do that.
 At any rate, I find that allowing ourself to be confident and kind with people, and not be so swooned, or fretfull even, regarding objects, is overly stabilizing. For we must focus on ourself, as must we partake in the world as it is, not as we ask of it, or am enthused to see, 'as though were the center'. One thought is that there are people who are truly friends to me, people I am most comfortable with, like Tara and Carli. But there are always those kind of people, like Bella, who exhibit some sort of charm on me; or at least reservation, as though were embarrassed or hard-strung to relate. I feel the appraoch for these people is long term, whereby you socialize with them, but with indifference, 'for you ought reflect on your hesistance with them'; but over time, when familiarity and personal understanding grow, you can actually be more upfront and sociable; which is what I seek ultimately.
 There has been this notion, as it was with money, 'are we missing out?' That person over there has it, they have some method which brings success. This a) overestimates the ease, if not consistency and dedication to procure this, and b) merrits little consideration so much as our own definition is given small concenr, 'for which our interests are directed, and our commisery at that'.
 As such, there does seem this accptance of responsability, and that if these things which feel so pleasing, 'as though our growth so far can afford this', that we are in fact just feeling far enough to let ourselves go; were at least not giving firm effort and attention to our duty at hand. And indeed, as we fail or are unnerved by our open attempt, we feel the passions that get evoked upon our lack of defintiion otherwise. Indeed, sex, money, contribution and flair can be acquired, if not progressed towards, through skill and accumulation. But it seems almost certainly fair that we'd live with our present in mind, and out deference for the lurking tears that come from our negligence, btu also for the truer and prosperous effect from real adherence and liberation, so ought we be.
 The most immediate effect in all of this is that you recognize the challenge at hand, and that nothing is in fact given. "That is the definition of passion, you see, to reward yourself, or at least humour your inclinations of the immediate sort: a more or less triumphant commission, rather than something lasting and developing". Indeed, a flaw of past inconsistency, and perhaps complacent views and want, is this despair and grief from passion pulling you away. "Don't stop," it says; and indeed, stopping is what being responnsible and non-seeking feels like to it. But, given time, remarks of solid experience, and the consistent rival which our forever condition seems, so we feel the good from leaning in, and feel the weight not from being without, 'or not reaching that is'. Indeed, some things may compell us to reach, but not from a state of reaching, but stable oppertunity.
My boundries for free time usually extend to the point where sleep is affected.
 And indeed, for an age it seems, nothing has ever really been unimpactful hear, however small. One worthwhile note, as perhaps reflects our way in recent times, is that the scrupulous nature that this predicament may draw is in fact unrequired; as the particular things you do, in either avoidance or practice, do not end up being much more effective than what you'd normally do. Principle then, or primary choices fit the matter. Indeed, there is not much I wouldn't give up brushing my teeth with the lights on for. I may still turn them off, but not fretting over it, given their apparent worth.
Perhaps nothing runs clearer than the good from deeds, and abiding the basic task upon you. I prefer living this way. I contrasts our time as a child, when we'd finsih and do as we please, not looking back or forward. And the difference as an adult, is that we merely hold more duties, yet all relatively light. And as such, there isn't a moment where we are free, but rather at peace with all that's demanded. And it is hard to feel any coarseness from such demand, when the ask is relatively light and fruitfull of an exceptional life.
 Indeed, on the whole, we do things generally, be it school or work. But we tend not to do much more. And I do think there is either fun to be had, or the enjoyment which comes from lacking responsibility. I think of our youth, when an hour or so before bed, we'd dine on the finest of things; as though it were our special time. We were cowering though, letting the potential and ligthneess of duty ahead to go. We never experienced much liberty, which by all accounts, is a brought by the current of nature; of which we don't question in terms of our breathe, for it just happens, but the neccessity of food is not so easily taken for granted; so much of our efforts are done just to acquire it. And given that essential deed, 'whereupon some condition is met', so in embracing this and treating it as though you had removed the threat of a knife at your throat, so liberty lay. And the blade doesn't cease; only the condition which would trigger it, 'in say, I no longer have this job, and the effec thatt feels', that so do I have it. And indeed, the transition from youth to adulthood is one where admitting such duties are present, and you succumb to them. Oppertunity is hearlded as that which makes this surrender easier. "As not to have to settle for basic labor." The condition though remains, and beyond submitting to this, which is by all accounts essential, whe you master it, and by rule and practice demonstrate such regularly, you feel truly stupendous. For all that is needed, 'as though a literal knife to the face were known, by which requirment is in reference to', that doing this both well and with liberty, which is to say with right excess, leaves one postively well. And free, as it were, to be drawn and drive, direct and determined.
 Only, a true quandery comes up, a feeling of jealousy and remorse rather. For say things are well, as they are. Why are we not going out with friends? Well I have none. It makes sense why were not grabbing a hot meal. I need it not enough to intake its poor fit. But friends aren't poor. Sitting around with them, for dinner, would not be wrong in any way. You could go home and get a good sleep. But we have no friends. And perhaps this is just hard. Maybe the things that bring you friends is not of interest, or hard to included amidst your other duty. The idea of playnig soccer is rather parched, as it's literally training, albeit fun.
 What comes to mind is that we are in fact not pressuring ourselves, or being deliberate. Were simply not. Its as if our natural course will go, and we'll return to that more attended state. This is why I'm feeling, 'man, in this free time, why aren't we doing those things.' And this is fine, for liberty often takes this form. But the reality, is that acceptable or not, we are talking about letting ourselves go, and having no regard. Making no intentional effrot, as you may do the night before work. "The cost of sub par sleep and eating on an off day or not does not freak you out enough."
I appreciate most the fidelity on offer when living indepentantly. You just feel that upon failure you can reasonably, but not ardently resume your course, make ameds, value the attempt at new things. You retain your general view, but see where specifics are off.
 Indeed, I mentioned in Nearly Modern that I've experienced great joy, and that it is something not to be done in might over duty, but merely brightened, upon tending to duty. I feel like people have forgotten their joy. One reason is that they've never really taken too much to themselves, and seen how the simple labors they begruded are in fact so simple, and not worthy of planmning to leave; that, and all that marks the peak of joy can be expeirenced amidst what's provided. It is enough.
 I'll bring up the fact though that the nature of these labors, which, admittedly, has no nicely laid out design, 'for some may feel as such in highly esteemed things'; but at any rate, given the physical nature, and at that, manner which oft draws the sense of begrudgement, that so are we more tasked with right amends. If our time were elsewhere, than perhaps our regards would be different. Where one ends up is a whole other thing. It is merely so that given what we have, the sufficeincy of it, which is to say, satiety it provides within something neccessity, that so are the goods of this of benefit to tend to.
 What I find most in my life is that what I truly seek, a joy that is marbled out of the saps of work' excess, is that mending this takes a dedicated and limber attitude. Consistency has never done us any favors here, which is worth its own remarks. Moreover though, despite realizing and experiencing the grounds for consistent and vital conditions, circumstance, paired mind you with some insensibility, has brought us away. And so despite a decade going on, from which we've made deliberate effort of this kind, only a sparse amount of it has ever really been just. And so now, it being so, I see clearly that things like cooking and cleaning, trusting your environemnt for utility and sleep, all things such, is a pressing requirment. For if you don't do these things, than you fall. And you know this. Were drawn away, as a result of not feeling so responsible, or perhaps with the friction of needing to be responsible; for which we begrudge our ignorance of it. For life is here for us to live and be fine. But we know what is needed. And in some sense loose ourselves entirely; for cooking may just feel out of the question: when though? It is as if your committment comes and goes like the weather.
 To illustrate this, I came home yesterday; in fact I just kept going and felt great. We got popcorn and oranges, all was well. We wrote, made our book, played Civ and Crimson Desert. Very very nice; that early crisp feeling was a delight. I noticed that Bella is not very open to anything besides courtesy and 'collegue like interaction'. So be it. But I noticed though that we were unable to cook. For little reasons, in that we had too few pots, or had to clean, or were out of things that felt too much to do. That, and our idea of what is normal for food was unclear: do we have corn and edamame, or are these one off things? And oddly, this would normally not grieve me. But, I was aware of myself not able to do it, and more or less intending not to; or lacking intent at that. Taking my mind off of this, as though to embrace our missing intent, felt enticing. Yet, this drew much conflict. For what would feel nice was not something that I stood by. And the grief comes not from the nice things, but our attitude towards giving mind to them. Nice things, be it money, woman or friends, as in leisure and craft, ought come naturally, simply, withtou hesitation or remorse, for they spring amidst your bubbling state; a state which we know to come from responsibility. But when were tired, or the persistent objects, minor mind you compared to the more daunting and suitably itinerary based deed of work, feel forgotten, or almost given permission to.
 Indeed, my feeling is one of grief; but only because I see neither oppertunity or permission to ignore our minor yet neccessary duties. If I don't cook no one will, and we will have nothing fair to eat, less we have to cook it then. And this will lead to either poor eating, despondance at not eating anything special at all. Our sleep will be worse, which makes work feel more looming. And at work we'll feel it. And the crux of our life now is that too much is freedom and ability ours to ignore, or pretend at least, as though trouble is in fact really so; for which we may connive some pleasing circumstance around it. "Given the struggle with work, look at how much is comforting and agreeable to it. Let us walk this line." No I say. Your merely ignoring responsibility.
 We did this prior to Christmas, but I remarked upon the things needing doing and what was causing them to be held back. Food for instance, usually has this in our practical ability or the clarity of what we might or should eat. For one, we need more pans. We have to be able to cook things at once. And indeed, bringing clarity to mind, we are admitting corn and edamame to the standard of things. We are working 4 days a week and this removes it even from question. As inconsistent as we are there, we just got payed 1300$, have some periodic support from mom and dad, have stats, and some we work; we also get 200$ every 3 months, and an extra pay period this april and in october. Working the 20 hours is fine, and even working this half the time is generally balanced by those factors. I reckon its a 'these week I'm taking a day off', but more or less commonly working the satisfying amount. And so fruit ought have more of a budget, along with veg; I'd like to make soup. Barely, corn and edamamme is one of the most delicious things, and healthy. Adding peas to all of this, or with soup is a great affair. Corn and Edamame are the protein+starch sauce, and makes all things grain delightful. Cucumber is not always inexpensive, and we ought account for this. But still, a budget would do, for 300$ a month. I have no real intent to fried food or sweets. There is so much good food: the struggle is merely in cooking.
I've got a moment, and nothing more. Don't want to ware myself out.
 One prominent thought is that when you've done all you have to do, then all is available from there. It is merely about standards, which come by their own motion. But at any rate, the trickery in all of this is in how well you adhere to your standards. We know for instance that food is essential, and so if we don't feel like cooking, then we've nothing to eat, which feels both wrong and downing. Yet, we are not so eager or disposed to look past this, 'and let ourselves go for the moment'. The pleasure of food is not even little, and thus in need of some comfort around it. It's about the work to do so; which feels less so by clarity, for which finances are involved, as to substantiate whatever it is we choose. But so in simplicity, or rubble in the way, such as having dishes to clean prior, or not enough to cook with or store. Even our measuring causes some friction. But it is perhaps a sign of good things around, when we feel up and ready to make such a sample. A 1/4 cup if you will, for all I need right now. Such effort is available to me, no grough.
 It is nice to feel so fine and plenty. Indeed, it is about standard. I just feel it is important and in some sense, fundemental for liberty, to be so endowed to food; but not just in quality but constance to. For which our cooking out take the form of satiety, and true to our capacity. For I don't see room for the novel good of Avocadoes, or even smoothies. Ice-cream though, that is there, as is edamamme and corn, which makes all grain dleightfull, and varied also.
So I'm thinking of the fun I used to have, going out through the woods, friendly street hockey, running around really. As I got older these things went away; and from our peak of youth I just loathed standing around jestering. Driving felt fine, but only as a sort of coasting mode of that nothing. We did love gaming though and watching movies. Food was always our friend. This all may have been in part because life had split people off, to where by suitability or availability, we did not play street hockey or run around. I don't exactly knnow what other people did; as there was a change prior to everyone getting cars; which itself felt like a release from being at home doing nothing; now you could go somewhere and do nothing, with people. But I just preferred to stay home, or at any rate eat food and play games, and do this with other people. One of the things I liked about Colin was that he was adventerous, and had his own kind of comfort. These were the goods of responsibility,in that they marbled them. We walked that intended line.
 Our interests have changed though. Not so much in matter, as we still like games and movies, but the form. For life further split us, in that again, we had cars, and money to boot, which drew poor habits, or at least past-times of the feckless sort. Fun for some, but seldom for me. I just never could embrace that pleasing yet stand-still expeirence. We did not work enough to want this, nor could afford it; and held no motivation to do so.
 Our adult life began to develop here, in that we saw no place for me to be, rightfully that is. For we smoked religiously through the end of highschool, and gripped the habit as that year came to close. These felt like times where we may or may not have been with people; but we were either content because of the weed, or merely felt on our own. Going to Colin's brought this effect.
 As we turned to nutrition, and to quote, 'upon that first real smoothie, leading into and following training, I felt so sharp and light, that everything tasted and seemed better'. Timing aside, I felt this huge amount of fufillment from being so productive. Watching Thomas Frank, planning our day, skipping in the morning, having the evenings to play and see. TV, such as house, spoke to me so much, and I felt like I was watching this genuine thing; similar to how you feel, or felt at any rate, when reading a really big and esteemed book. It felt like vegetables; and this wasn't only satisfying but enjoyable. Like we were actually reading the words ,rather than glossing over them. And the nature of the show felt somewhat formative, as though we were growing. Emotionally perhaps. Study, gaming, our forray into sums, our practice of training and yoga, cardio and our sensibility through the day, all spoke to me and gave me this almost inner-well of experience. And I recall, famously at this point, reading Kirshnumurti, and his mention of our expeirence's quality, and how this seemed so important, and with repor of our current way of living. Reading Epictetus further on comapred; only in that case we grew more understanding of the work required; while this moment outside, seeing the blue sky, felt more or less liberating; as if I had accepted our condition, and worried not.
 I look at our life and consider how ceratin things were more appealling then others. For instnace, we adored P.E in school, but during lunch cared not to do anything. In Elementary we loved doing things, but not in highschool. And indeed, I recall during our second semester, and a few times when we lived in Coquitlam, playing indoor soccer, and having a real nice time. It was tiring though. And there is something to be said about going out and doing things. But I have seemningly elevated beyond this mire of depression, whereby comparions and hunger are not a thorn in my side, nor something to accomodate. It is as though we are happy, and able to more or less do as we please. If we want to watch Porn, great. It bears similar feelings to sex; the orgasm is such. I think all that it lacks is the excitment and foreplay of a real person. But it is a mere activity, readily available. Erotica as it will. Sex is not by any genuine decree a cornerstone to people dynamics, but one part of the result. And so it feels entirely seperate.
 That is what's strange though. That we almost feel open to doing bad things, or inoppertune one's, to live for ourselves primarily. One change in all of this is the malady of our intent, and how in this life of ours, unburdened by comparison or doubt, 'feeling like you could and should, like your missing out, or living in ways that our inferior to a steady state which would acquire all'; that so in our higher pace, where we don't feel so embalmed, beyond merely liking things and feeling good, 'in some sense humouring the shared, or at least uncontroversal nor weak abide to common deeds and good', that we look outwards sometimes, be it in moments where we sulk and have this outer appeal, or we feel oppertune and lucky, 'the porn of ambition and franchise'. What has changed though is that such pursuits, or ideas rather, feel similar to this common agreement, whereby luxury and nicieties, 'the icing upon normal living', that so in the case of pursuit, that so are we equally set on the common agreement, of what can be achieeved. But in that, we see that there is achievment, and all that is needed is an aim. For our view has been more or less ideal, and at that likened to a glimmer, 'whereby we see ahead, but have no foundation below'. And I recognize now, that such things are in fact quite hard to see or acquire. Yet, I find in our propserity, a humble yet formidable sense, to consider the proper path for such things, as to view them with definition, rather than dreams or appeal. That we would write of sums, fortify it's principle and descriptoion [namely that of calling the degree a range, as in the 12 hour range of a clock]; and that this being what we have, we may offer. But to whom? Well to professors, who may recieve my email. There is a connection there. Something comparable is not so seen for nature and well-being. We live in the real of promulgation, which is on it's own counts better understood, but is perhaps still afloat.
Porn is an odd thing. Different kinds effect me differently. Indeed, the nature of these effects is something of the reveallnig sort; as though we don't have, as much as there is an effect, a sure foundation.
 For I find that we hold onto things, truths if you will, certainities. The opposite of which is when we can't find a house, or are adjusting. Things going on. But when we are clear and sure, the nature of this being engaged removes us from feeling like 'this is it, what now'; it's more like we have a solid grip, but are still holding, or with rather. So we don't notice, and certain things come out.
 In the past we've taken to forming different things, as if to have them appear there. I think in most parts, the further you are from what you really need, or have a grip on rather, the more you feel like you need some broadeness and perhaps granduer to your certainty. Rounding out comes to mind. Whereas now, I see that work is well as is our home. And stabily I'll add, for we don't just feel but know. And around that is our backbone of food, the essential prep and comfort in it. That is well. Some clarity could do, but the heart of it is there, and of a full count. "No novel or lingering moments."
 But I think of porn, travel, the future, free time and writing, things of the sort. How some of these feel like neccessities to fortify little pats of me which aren't sure; or I think of how nice it would be, as if we feel good enough to dream. Yet those little parts of me, or even till this points notable modesty, has me someone conflicted, as though there are problems, or what we feel pulling us is in fact poor, or not true.
 These are good advancments. And indeed, fundementally there is good in the world. But you must play the role given, which is to work, and the job had. And from there, things of a nice or obvious sort can be strode towards, truly. They're merely ahead though. Were living now. And I feel that a whole manner of things can be said. Porn being one of them. For masturbation feels a little dry when it is done on a impulse. There's just not enough there to drive it, or carry the impulse to satisfying completion. I wouldn't say porn has any better ending, only that it carries you more. And indeed, my biggest qualm with Porn is the trust in it. Now even, I feel this cool fuzz in me, opposite, as it were, to that thick warmth that too much eating can bring. Were supple, fresh, true. But I feel that certain things can be taken advantage of. And that this cool air we feel, a mark of great days, is not so guarenteed, or absent of work tomorrow or effort to come. Something feels odd though, in feelnig as I do, so solid and true, that ignoring our want for fun which is present, somewhat dissmays me, 'as though were trepidatious'.
 Let us fear not what it may be, as in admitting, or confiding in a doctor about your doubts. We may be equally unassuming and or disdaining, so speak.
 I feel as though we don't want to indulge ourself or take our way for granted, not so much for the trouble to come, nor even the future oppertunity were missing, but the near ones. It's this notion that if we keep ourselves from such, that we will be amiable and endearing to folk at work, or wherever. It must be noted, that life has no overwhelming or obvious point for us here. Dating is rather sparse, outside of warm fufillment. Nor is there oppeprtunity, or excitement, for leisure and things of the sort. That feeling of friends. There may be some, but the entry fee is tough. And indeed, our life speaks to me here, in that we have no real sense outside of soccer, or some imagined organization that is generous and ideal, 'active if you will, for say the environment', what we would do, and thus who we might meet, in being such and going there. In part I take refuge, or rather, feel calm by the merry road in front of me. It may lack things, be it in oppertunity or just present evidence, of which your deepest self wants, namely a girlfriend, something sexual, a group of friends. Oddly though, the idea of friendship does not feel encraoched upon or in doobt, for we feel fresh in our approach to our selves and perpetuity, that such thing feels welcome, possible, and more or less attractive. Perhaps we see so much from ourselves, and on route, or with propensity, via such things, for which the merry road brings them to life, 'and shows them being so as far as the road seems, or gleems at any rate; for the gold I see draws me, nor belies me, to merely look as far as beauty feels enough; we've but an unclear nor hankering knowledge of what lies ahead'. What is near and ahead is such a treat, I have no mind for Oz. Let him be.
Oooo the green in this world, it strikes me plenty.
 Oddly though, the red in it all spirts out, perhaps only when that vast bounty feels provoking to me; as though I am moved by it to such a degree, per my stationary nature, that so we yearn for it and bear our mind and soul for such. "Dreams and aspiration are made." But so for such bounty, of a world who's columns and furnihshings are as if imbued, that so we see the parts which are red.
 But indeed, the weirdest bend in this all is from that seperation from dream and self, and how this being so in some sense invites doubt and contrition; or better put, 'and indeed I have it', it is our bias and perview to affirm what we see, 'in that vast land of gift and amnesty', that so are we tense by being so forthdrawm. Were coming up with reasons, sucumbing to what should in good sense be subject to inquiry and modesty. And indeed, it is like a reflex, perhaps by the majesty of what we seek, 'for which we conjure it with as much as we can as to make it more solid, and not so far off as it's enormity can procure'; but in that we see missing holds, loose threads and empty spots; errors, as it were, or falsity, which we can't help but give credence to.
 In some sense though, it is what we must admitt that brings perril. For it is not so much, that of vile and putrid things, that they come up as this bold and petulent truth, 'for which we'd rather just peer away and shrug off, to continue our far off plea'. But rather, our doubt of these things in some sense forgoes my ability to look past them; as though we are hardstrung to admitt. There is indeed honesty here, for I feel our dream to otherwise be selfish.
I don't feel like Nieztche, in want or attire. I've no real interest nor identity in proclaiming nor suggesting physcial views, judicial arrangment, nor the decency of men. Little is of need here, when all things are considered. And it is often us writers and inquiriers who give the harshest grade of culture and folk, 'as though they are in need of remedy: to be un-degraded'. Indeed, my father, or someone subject to the gossip and opinioons of neighbour and friend, may speak ill of people to. Indeed, old people are like this; so sure. Yet I have not personally lost. And I have no recouse to any promise nor threat of a soul. Indeed, as a philosophy, a declarative argument is perhaps needed, of your basis for lacking fear. yet, what view do I have as to demand this. I am not by any decree a heretic: only one missing oppertunity, to seem so wise, or contribute decently even. For one I'd like to do. For the other, I only feel the bellows of pillars unset. Indeed, we oft speak of things we should do, or will, or want. But what do you know? Ought this guide your way, and speak to our end's might? What will we really do?
 Well for one, communication is everyone's modality, whether it be to complaim, flirt, or promulgate. Reproach is the meekest of things seemingly, for it literally means to inquire and note. Observe as it were. And indeed, one prominent feature of life is that the appearence, or prescence at least, of some kind of people sprouting their views and ways, gives the impressions that what they speak of is in need of remedy; and that there is equal oppertunity to mend. What this view forgets though is that the views of such oracles and reformers (critics too) is not itself what mediates or determines the motions and ends of the things they're speaking. That is by common rule, natural law, the steps so far. Indeed, these are from the ground up and ought be remarked as such. For civilization, or a constituated state, 'be it for kings or confederacy', is not some god given constant. It could go away. But so the earth lay. Nothing short of supernova will erode this crust, 'to pieces that is'. And indeed, we come to the basest of remarks: the purpose of our criticsim and inquiry. Generally it is furnished with civil virtue, ie. a care for everyone's prosperity, a love that, however thin, extends to all. "The sun maketh no judgment upon her pull, she merely is; and it is by that pulling nature that those closest feel it most. Propensity thus, in proportion to distance, get's compelled the lesser, hence the wider orbit of those far away."
 It's important also to think with some excellency, which is to say robustness in your view. Principal to this is the notion of seperation-cohesion, in that, of a community we'll say, everyone lives on their own, uniquely as it will, but has in that indivudally some harmony and or fit to other folk. The community then is the summed motion of these folk. We take it for granted that we say hello to strangers in the morning, or that we stand in line, with knoweldge that we must wait. No one is confused. It is what we all do. But the we in that, is like 'we all go to school so we can get good grades: were doing it, but I have my grades. It is only we, for you also have your grades. Were the same, together, similar, but merely alike'.
 This is where the proper inquiry of nature lay; society being the constant layer of this, whereby we accept the cosmos as being oriented and true, along with the earth. Society, in this sense, is nothing but the formation and motion of humans, their grouping, and the customs and behaviour within it: laws and culture, or art and creativity rather, being the hallmark. But in this though, we are not so becoming to refer to one thing as if it is uniform in it's whole. That 'a' country was so as to entirely reflect what we say about. That China is this, Russia that, or that a kind of people are such. Think of it this way. Wages are what give people means to buy things, either for consumption or equity. The cost of things is paired with this. But each person walks a personal line, whereby, of high wages or costs, so do they fit this arrangment differently. It is as if the temperature rose and fall, but we were to say, it being cold for instance, 'that people are cold'. It may not be obvious how people might vary in their experience, or literal thermos based on the weather. But in an econmical sense, or even that of rights, the inputs that people partake in vary tremendsouly; in say my purcahse from a grocery store and yours from a resteraunt. The nataure of these things is different. The bend may be similar, in manner at least, 'more expensive things from each'. But they're different kinds. And then even, if were both at the grocery store, we buy different things. And indeed, one forgotten point, is that there is an objective ledger of items, with some naturally being less epxensive; and so when wages are low and prices high, the headline should be, 'people are being forced to buy cheaper things, as to have enough, but within their budget.' We had 100$ and could buy 50 two dollar apples. Now those apples are 3$, but we still need the same amoutn of food. We'll have to buy 50 of the now 2$ apples --which were a 1.50$. They're not as good; hence the price of peppers over carrots. So in any case, 'people are equally fed, despite the economoy, just not as pleased, as they must buy less comforting food'. Where is the perril here, by these counts at least? We must judge the comfort of people, not they're ability to survive. Again, were not making that statement, that people ought merely be frugal. It is just not a laudible, or at least worthy statement, in the world of things, to hear that people are struggling, or that things are bad and food is hard to come by. Only those who cannot afford potatoes, rice and pasta can say that. For everyone else, at a minumum, has at worst been reduced to that; which on the face of it is not something which draws empathy from me. The economy does not want to be this way; there are surely other consequences to. And so I have more regards for those, than people's comfort. "Such is not the greatest loss among other things."
We have 650$ on our credit card? that is alot, considering that mom gave us 600.
 We spent 100 on crismon, 50 on hatred, 30 on psn, once on mobile. We've spent 200 on amazon, more than half of which is going back. So thats more like 450. Which is still alot. Groceries only totalled 300.
 At any rate, we are taking off probally not much more than 50$ worht of amazon stuff, as our lube, 1 night shade and fan are a 100 together.
 This is just the way it is. 200 on ps stuff, 100 from amazon, and 300 from grocery. We had some large bills early in the month. Reality is, we have to pay off the 600. It is 300 for grocery, 200 for ps, and 100 for amazon.
 We have 4500 in cash right now.
 We were paid on the 19th for the 3 days in the first week of march, plus a sick call, then the 3 days on the week we moved in. One thing to note, is that this 35 hours, 4+3 is 600. If you add another shift, so 40, you get 1350 per month.
 We gave Mani 3200$. We have to pay for food but nothing else till June 1st. We are working 38 horus this paycheck, which is 650. The week following were working Easter, which is a 27 hour week = 460. Plus another 250 at least, so 700. We'll take one shift off this next pay period. We need to center our stretching, and emphasize running and free range, with minor inclusion of training. At any rate, we are getting paid on the 2nd, 19th and 30th, and then 2 more in May. This extra pay check essentially counts the end of march, which you don't get paid till the 2nd, then the 4 weeks of april you essentially get. So that's going to be 2000$ for april. There is indeed a stat in May. I don't have plans to leave, but our intent may change. It is a whole month away before we need to decide, as we'd have to look for June 1st. I am not sure if it is as easy as extending. More or less likely. I don't feel we've really gotten this place's potential, as its been quite raining. Its about to get nice, so we'll see. Could be nice to be here for the summer.
 At any rate, it is a bummer that we've got so much credit. Part of it is fair, but like 300$ is partially uneccssary. But no matter. My point is, that we have 4000, including the upcoming gst, no credit. Blank. We are working 20 hours more or less, which does accomodate instances of 19, to reach 1300. At any rate, given the extra pay period in april, the easter bonus, and bonus in may, we've got liberty to give up a few shifts, and get more rest. Let's take off next wedensday, and get a good recovery current going.
 We might balance things like this. Each shift is worth 80$. Stats add this, and working them adds something on top of this. Just assume that's a bonus. We have 5 pay periods till June 1st. Were at 4000 now, which is alot. Food is budgeted on 300$, so add 600 to your future expenses. Were at 3400. Everything you make these next two months is added onto that, in esscence. What a scenario. That amount of cash is pretty sizeable. We will work 3 shifts regardless, which is 10 weeks, 30 shifts, 2400$. That's 5800$. Now, add two stats, the two shifts we've worked these first 2 weeks. We'll work 4 on the fourth, as were working 3 the third week. That's 5 extra shifts as of week 4. That leaves 6 more weeks for potential bonus shifts. Were at 6300$ though.
03/23 – $16.61 Book
 03/20 – $14.55 cloth going back +15
 03/19 – $44.79 Fan
 03/14 – $11.18 Socks
 03/13 – $6.71 cltoh going back +6
 03/11 – $51.50 2 shades, 1 going back -- +25
 03/05 – $34.66 Lube
 03/05 – $20.93 Scale going back +20
Its a prevalent thought seemingly that we would expect things of the nice sort, 'things which strike our mind, fill me with fancy and thrill', but forget the requirments of this. That you could just move and travel around, or have woman and fame, these are things that, in theyre peak and thrills, sits amongst more definite parts, parts which task you with things; both in acquiistion and continuity.
 So I sit here then, relatively happy, at peace from our wants and desires, and with more or less circumpsection about our life. We heard from Justin, who is doing well; and by all accounts I am not suppose or infer that he is principally off. I am sure he is alright. There is nothing to compare to, and our life is in many ways different. It is for our own though.
 For me, it all comes down to that, however free of problems we are, there is still work to do. I reckon Justin's trouble is that he is pressured, or responding at least, to that prescence of work, in hopes of relieving it. That being the motive felt true to me, from what I heard. I on the other hand, am more or less aligned, and at any rate pitched, towards such work, as to make it less a burden but a deed, and by that, feel not restrictive or despondnent, nor grueling or grief worthy --for which passions spark from.
 The quandry for me, or at least where our view of the world, grasp of the past, and quality of current experience, 'in standard we'll say, as not to twist things around', is in the acceptance of our place, in being so well and true, but positioned. I bring up this sense, of which this morning was a great levener, that all is well, we are fine, fair pleasure and mountanous delight are present and coming. It's as if were conceding, or at least being gratefull, amidst a trying, if not demanding experience.
 When I spoke with Justin and stated, 'I want to be a remarkable teacher'. This is our simplest view. It comes from past times wanting, but remains so in this simplest form. As we had Sums, and thought of writing to Professors, 'the clarity and ease of this brought me much peace and non-haste', that so in our widest dreams, where oppertunity and reception are mixed, being a teacher sums it up. It's still out there, but more or less defined. For an age our reward was a factor, or impression at that. This view more accuratley speaks to the care in our heart. But so is it yearning. And indeed, I feel that, perhaps, what were lacking, are those small efforts and inquiries that tighten things, but also allow for us to be curious and explore. Have a list so you may note the value and or being of a piece. Organize your notepad to allow this --for an addition that is. And similarly, have a place for notes about the weather, to charter in effect. "Bring the future into harmony with the present, for the former, in it's mystery or haze, bears more general truth as the latter becomes known." Write your treatise, or about it at least. Consider the principle, consider the behavour, consider the point. Find some peace in the fact, and relief at that, of the things you want socially, and intimately moreso, that these are not called for in smitten or casual ways. And in whichever way they are, remember where, and how, say at work, it being not, removes the worry and fear that were missing so, 'as if it should be'. Further, take stock of what you really care for. Intiamte things, 'which in many ways are flings at the peak that we seek, but so as to return to our seperate base --a relief if you will, or point', that so in that mount, where like is actually found, believe in your care for a creative person, 'for in that I share', but also your love for someone who has this, but is powerful. Have standards for yourself, expectations for others, and fool yourself not into thinking you should or that others do find things easy. If it pleases you, watch porn. If it brings grief, worry or shame, determine whether it's wrong, or if a different kind would work. "false intimacy is one quality that I find erodes our sense of real intimacy. Whereas a party, or something clearly fictitous, is less so. When the fantasy is closer to real live, were touching coals."
So today was a relatively enlightening day. I saw that we had not truly moved on, and that our clutch of 'sex and attention' were not lost. Our feelings on this are quite complicated, but I see that we ought not fret or focus too hard. Generally all is well, but I feel, perhaps under the weight of work, this want to be seen, this frustration in others not seeing me (when I see them with others). And indeed, it seems like work is all that's talked about. Maybe someone brings up something on they're phone, but its just work. That is all to say, that we are with ourselves, nothing more. And indeed, I've had these insights todays, this shift of feeling, as though were not trying to get people to like me, nor are we so hastened to like them.
 I see foremost my interest was sex, or at least some sort of isolated appeal. Not to be with this person. I wish we were friends. But even then, this is still a mark of earnest confidence, but for being personal; as you either want to be able to speak to them, or know them more so you can. I don't think this neccessary though. I understand the impulse, I do. But it really comes when we feel unable to be with them; like we've nothing to say, given our vibrance. It seemed like we wanted to stair so that we could invite remarks. We preferred if they weren't there though. And oddly, we felt this nerve, or fear of looking up, for the fact that someone may not show us attention, and we accept that. There are people indeed who I don't even regard if they pass me by; or better put, notice not if they notice me not, as they pass. Other peopel aren't like that thouhg. And there is this instability in me that wishes to prompt them. We seem a bit too unfit in ourselves. And indeed, this want, this fidgeting plea to see and or speak to tohers, creates all these fears and fancies, about how they are. "Its like were hunting, and so we notice every scurry and rustle of leaves. " And indeed, one poor mark of philosophy is when it is used to justify, or describe, how other people really are, as to relieve the glaring sights we have as were looking. This is not something that should be remedied.
 But what indeed is the situation. For work is touhg, and during the middle of our shift I felt this fine updraft, where we were open. And indeed, in that state, no one could walk by without me feeling collected enough to merely acknowledge or say hello. Its as though we weren't on our toes, ready to strike a conversation, if it wasn't sparked by them. We let it happen, or create it as it's called for. Cool and breezy, like were already pleased. This is how we started speaking with Bella, we weren't trying.
Oh there is an idea, one so merry and large, that very little could compare.
 It has to do with rules in life, and how for instance, upon a mistake, it is not the bruises or frustration that matter, but your re-assessment, or refocusing, of whats right, and remaining on that. What caused the injury, or at any rate what was lost, is in it's continual threat or abscence part of the true line. We ought to have changed for sure; and indeed, we may still feel the heat from that flame. But as one who now knows not to touch it; and we need not, and can live as though we were not hurt, but with the knowledge claimed.
 There are immutable truths, but fashioned, or at least clothed in garmets, of a simpler sort than we often think. For one, the world is not some succint unit, of which you can entirely judge, nor a time for that matter, or something of a kind. Dynamics, to say the least, are what ground the reality, in that concepts are not in fact real, which is to say labels, groupings, genera, but rather, instances, species, subjects, a man. These are all that exist. And indeed, it is by their nature's that they pair and bond with others, similar or not. But extending your view outside the binding of any one particular is overly proposing. You may suppose, and indeed, in that almighty land of war, we may know that grueling pain and lareseny are stricken; all while we suppose though something broader, basic, unpresuming. Indeed, in such instance, we may overly suppose in order to forgo the belief that such extremes are more normal than is worth deeming. And indeed, ignorance exposes us to this, in that we can't sit with things, and hold the scope of truth. But so, in this exposure, are we prodeed to confirm, when we percieve, or experience even, the propositions of others.
I say this with haste, for rest would be better, but there's a fair few things in my life. Indeed, I've noticed the departure of dreams which onece were, or at least regards that seeemed to at one point be promising, if not lingering around, to not be there no longer. But for the better, as it keeps me focused, and tuned to the actual path. Indeed I was here all along. I just fell for that older notion of, 'well, when were well off things will be good, and such and such will happen'. I see now the ease in which people don't really experience things, and that we need not worry about who they're with or what they like and or wish to achieve. They're responsible. And indeed, it is clear that there is nothing easy about making friends; it's why actually liking each other, and by that having something in common, makes it easier. For we don't like complaining about work; and so much as there is trouble upon me, or at least some sort of duty, 'as to live freely as a man', so are there few who relate to me in this way. Indeed, consistency and our own posture allow for openess, and with pleasure that comes from doing that, and perhaps being oppertune; seeing at any rate, the distance required to really get close to someone; especially when they have standard. And indeed, we so are own, and center on the fact that we like people who are creative, and love those who are powerful. I think we mistake this latter with those who are strong in their responsibility. Holly was like this, and indeed Brandy to. It does not mean they're wise though, nor with anything in common with me. Indeed, if we only were ourselves around them, which is perhaps harder to do when they call on me to be ever moreso, that it is hard to reach that task, and feel that were wrong upon departure.
 I realized today that there are not too many people I care for at Superstore. None who inspire me, who give me strenght, who draw me to do better. It is not vanity, but rather petulance, and a kind of roughness that doesn't fit with our dearest ways and cares. There is some manner of culture, for every person of color I've met has been warm and inviting, but also appreciative and encouraging to. I don't get that from most folk. And truly, Joey is a sweetheart and the kind of worker who I feel the better when together. Chenoya though, doesn't elevate me. Nor does Paul. I care not for his mannerisms. In part it's me, for I have nothing against him, nor Mo nor Morgan. I perhaps underappreciate them being there. But alas. I am merely of the mind that our time here is not precious. Even though there are people I find dear. Sonal, Carli, Matin, who has become a great friend, as Tristan, Carry to some extant, and even Teresa; though her age shields her from contempt (unlike Kim who's is not old enough). Andy to and Alex are very fine, and I enjoy having the commonality with Jacob, and Oliver even. We just need to socialize more, ask questions, learn everyone's name. Its imprtant. To rule yourself you must be with such rule in this most important of lands. To rule one's self is to be in charge of they're civility; for that is formed out of nature's unweilding task: for which our power is in a free and effective response. But as such, to be so, un-qualmed, is to no everyone's name. For your a stranger, and thus oddly-pressed when unsure of that.
 I think that being well allows us to be more responsive, rather than intruding or demanding. Being at home has this effect, and I think that being in Vancouver allowed for me to be more even and well. I have often felt compressed and rushed, or devoid. And it's hard to build on that. Indeed, we had some smoothness to our pace, and naturally spoke to Bella. I recall when we spoke to Tristan that we told him about her as though to get some relief. But the idea became humoured, as though it was in fact there, waiting for me. This idea of oppertunity is a rough one, for you want to believe it's there, and not stear away from the notion that it surely is, if only for your fears and aversion. But in truth, it was not called for me to ask her out. I realize the positive thing we lost, but indeed, it was not a fair mistake to make. But alas.
So we've done well exercise wise, but our training has taken the bulk of our current iteration, and we've incorporated it, and are more or less weaning off the faitgue.
 My view then is that we need to take ourselves into formation, both from yoga, in free range and running, then slowly re-introduce some full range. If we are too reach training again, where concentration is invovled, we ought go through it with prior plumpness: as if to fill out the gains we made recently.
 For one, the manner of ouro exercise need not be permanent. It can be as we know, with some intent, or at least sense in the case of training. "Right now is good, and today is as good as any; plus we have work coming." In that sense, some light training may be fine on sunday or monday. Noe we are only working tuesday. I'd say, based on how you feel, that you may do so fuller training on that second pair of off days. But it need not lean into concentration. It depends though. I don't want to whither away. But at any rate, the second element is that we may try and rehabilitate ourselves with certain poses, or just a mindset of focus and form. Consider it like a bootcamp, where we re-initialize the strenght and habit to come. We may at least have the intent and determination now.
 In that way, I think we need some factor of warrior, paired with flow or not. Something deliberate, concise, and preferably bookended with some rise and fall. We may do chaturanga on our own; in the morning preferably. I think, actively at least, but more or less ideally, that we'd return to that manner of doing said sequence during the early day. Say were going for a walk, to read, it would do us so good to go through that chaturanga sequence. Where runner's lunge fits in is hard to say. For indeed, I find it quite rehabilative following work or training, in its upright form. But so in twists and general high lunge, where the breathe is present, this has no clear space. I just know it is well. Core training lacks the room, though, on a day where energy is present, and paired with running or trainnig, some plank, bridge and boat would migthily help. I am not enthralled by our ability to do this, but the foundation is there. Handstand is no lesser. I don't think we lack the practice, but perhaps more intention for our neck and shoulders would do fine. Simplicity fairs well here, given our current pace. Namely, childs pose is great along with tree. Some more seated in between for arms and neck would be a great addition.
 I will note that we are fatigued, and need rest. Grabbing the pillow makes for a very nice mix of recovery and sleep. Right in the middle of the day, I think this makes a great practice. Consistency with food is important. I think we overestimate the portions we have; though, it is really just consistency, or better put, regularity of food thorugh the day, whereby smaller portions work. Big ones are weighing, especially at the end; but we want to eat enough. Corn and Edamame continue to be great, with lentils, quinoa or barely. Don't forget beans and peas. Mabye on some sort of off day. A good thing to get for a afternoon chill. Popcorn feels readily avaialble, and we have not tried frying bread. Potaotes continue to be excellent; though were out. Soup is there but not overly needed. And ice-cream; we need some bananans to freeze. Lovely indeed, though the more you put in the more milk. There is one at home. Don't think twice about buyying some oranges for the day. A couple is like 2$, and were not penny pinching here. Sunflower and tomatoe remains excellent, and can't be remembered enough
So I had a very nice day at work today. For so long this thing with Bella has exhausted me. But it is clear as day that were holding hope, martrying, wishing she reconnects. This is no longer true. Instead, I simply delight in being present, for which our exhaustion was birthed, in that we felt like we had to avoid, or at least feel weary in speaking to her. The main thing that has helped is this release from feeling like we have to. For one principle fact, which can be observed, is what is called for. That base notion of 'she woudl show interest in you', is evident, and thus relatively clear as to what your to respond to. The age thing gives some understanding as to why no interest was shown; or how things were at any rate. But as things were, asking her out was not called for. So may just be.
 And indeed, I think truly, that in giving her up, which in many ways is a release of this gripping feeling, whereby we are set right now, that we re-connect with more personal matters. Something which has come to mind is this notion of content-creation. Indeed, having a clear aim, which you stand by and are unbashed to stick to, is the key. Similar to Sums, whereby it is mere effort in our way, that so for creaiton, I see that treating books/pieces is what I want to do, and that this exends to movies and shows, games even, concepts in general. The heart of it is, looking at things that bear value. And my content is about illustrating such worth. I see it quite cleanly and clearly, in that we may do 'the wonders of listening, by Plutarch', and we go over that essay. All the prep needed is to go over the piece, perhaps before hand, and look at parts you wish to speak to. It is as if you were telling a story, but thorugh commentary, and the points of interest were the plot. In the case of 'educating children' we'd mark and note the mention of erudition versus ability, and the softnes of virtues path. What we'd say is not so important, but that we'd point it out. Perhaps there is more we could do, more we could provide. But indeed, this is a treatment by me, and prioritizng that is quite a relief. You could even do, 'why some games look better than others', a sequel perhaps to the beauty of games. I think in terms of worht, I mentioned to Justin that it was not so that we were indifferent to response, and sought only to make. That is not true. We wish to be heard; or better put, we wish other's to hear it. That is our fuel more or less. And one is enough, few is many, alot is not so far. And from there, our own personal itinerary, be it leaning about me, 'elaborating on floor sitting', or outlining sums, all in this are we contained. And what is nice about this, compared to our prior sense of 'a long, bio like series', is in that case, were in some sense surrendering ourselves to the entirety of things, and soliciting only a productive response; in that there is all these things I want to do, rather than focus. Funnily, that care to make those bios reflects the wish to be heard, but perhaps our challenge in doing so without intending for all.
 I noticed today, more than ever before, walking down the main part of the floor, and similarly throughout the isles and backroom, this firm yet breezy stair; as if we weren't stiff, but could look with our eyes, gently. It felt like I could show the proper expression, as to remark admiration, familiarity, or just recognition. Being present is all I seek. And indeed, it feels as though were letting go. Rather than squabble about the folly of this and brace our feeling by shining light on someone else, we merely embrace our duty, our responsibility, our line, whereby we are present. Indeed, in the case of creation, focus is not a limit but a exhibitor; for it is fear really that keeps us from not caring for all. "What won't we get if we pass over some things." But in the case of duty, it is better and light to be so broad and humble. For duty is a downward force, a violent one in some sense. It's very prescence behooves the succeeding feeling of peace; which is a very complete and soothing effect. When we think of Bella, she is merely someone I want to talk to, to be near to and feel known. But this takes time. And the rush to go in there is so when you ignore and or relinquish the priority of duty. Which indeed, is tailored for peace; as it says, 'no longer are we reaching, but finding good in our relief of burdens. Circumstance will determine whether something exciting comes. Were bound here though, and not to that which is passing by. Nor are we going after it'. Indeed, the manajery of sex is not really a element which causes trouble amongst us. It may be a motive, but it feels more like an impulse which is clearly so, and more ardently tugging you out of your general sense. The point being, that you may be here and there in your fullest, and feel not like your being inappropriate, or that your exhibiting sexual intent. There is some sense, in that we recognize what is called for; and in our impinged moments, we don't let our sight of things get marbled with that whim of sex. But no matter, it is really up for someone whether they like you, and indeed it mostly comes naturally. Your just friends otherwise. Life has taken me many ways, and indeed, I don't feel were missing such appeal from others. I merely find it hard to be around them, as it begs me to reach not. But I find this to be both tolerable and pleasingly so. In fact, it opens me up to the possibility and potential scenarios in which friendship actually forms. Were just doing what we like otherwise. And the struggle has been that we've never been calm or able to be ourself enough, to do as we please, openly, but not get hooked in. practically, without focus or control, there were too many momnents that our ineffiecency brought such want, and thus ware from not reaching
Across the board everything is well. Our food is well kept, and agreeable to our finances. Our exercise, albeit shifting, as it often does, has room for exertion and rest; and for instance, we gave notice of our wish to reform our stretching, and how applying some concentration to that could bring out lasting habits, or tamber we'll say; and this was more or less welcomed. There's this pace to our day, particularly with activity, and this allows for chores to come when theyre needed and we feel like it, but without the feeling of delay.
 There is much I could say across the board, 'as though there were an assortment of pots, each with fair forms of their common plants', yet my experience overall feels a little bit flat. At least, I find it a bit to woven into activity; for when it's not, I think of Bella, or our zero friends. Not much else. I have no monetary nerve, nor the haste to think of, and at any rate submitt to something far down the line.
 But whereas in the past these ideas have carried me, they did so out of our natural langor, and how activity and duty had, albeit understandable, sulking moments and space; for which pleasure came in, as a short term motivator and boost; or something long term almsot relieved me of the prescence of such things, 'as though the tide were beginning to pick up, and we could more easily settle on it'.
 Whereas now, to my credit, the space is rather small, and our slowness is near abscence, or irregular in being noticble. But in that, I feel like we have nothing really to excite me or compell me. And indeed, speaking with Justin, having some reprieve and composure in our impression of people, and our general tune going through work, home and leisure, leaves me less pressed upon, nor moved, but maladoies, or even general ideas, of other peoples experience, and are seperation from it. It just brings to light what were doing, and how in part, things which are fair are missing; which is to say, they could be there. I find it somewhat pleasing to say, that we have lots of friends, and them being at work is not so poor qualifier; only there are few who I want to hang out with, and have fun. Some circumspection will show you that Bella, who's energy I like, is not always so, from the root and her attention. Moreover, when she is open, it is just for some little gabber during the day; something which, mind you, feels worse to be apart of when you feel your own place withdrawn; yet our calm eyes make this alright, for we can be even the sligthest bit present to all; and this makes richer points of energy the more hospitable. On top of that, the actual open part of her, where free time is on call, I don't suspect there's much we have in common. I've heard of her going to raves and what not. That isn't for me. And I hear she coaches young softball; don't know if she plays.
 Where my head is at is in some aside from Plutarch's sentiment of rules, and that the fairest life is one that lives in harmony with them, but thorugh time and sensibility, rises above; not away, as in detached, but of poise. As much as this is true though, we have the werid, and perhaps unexperienced part of me, that sits more or less free from time to time. The night before work does not bring me and doubt or yearning for someone or soemthing else; as going is not something we debate, and its task is a toilsome one if ignored. Yet, when the shift is over, and were off the next day, two even, I don't have much to do. Indeed, my reflection centers on the fact that going to the beach, exploring vancouver, these woudl really be fun. But less we exhert ourselves, which brings to mind the return of work, that so some natural excitment, likely from a friend, is needed. For you forget in that case. And indeed, the manner in which we oblige work is not so much in doing nothing prior, but doing certain things right. Indeed, this may mean making choices that are not poor. But for breakfast, lunch and dinner, morning and afternoon exercise, getting a fair sleep, these are needed regularly for a un-toilsome experience. And much of what we believe get's in the way of this is really just from time not procurring what could be congenial. Going to Vancouver, which mind you has us in a chair, is not somehting which un-accomodate your eatnig, stretching or sleep. Imperfections sure; but like sugar, from cereal, you may choose to. I don't feel it encumbant for us to say no.
 Indeed, I miss not recreation, nor is my leisure anything but oveflowing. But I find myself having to resort to consistency, or at least mundaneness, as I live without either access or permission for fun. And indeed, our choices in the past have given us so. For the task of work was never something which I could overlook; there never was room to have fun and live well. Surrounding this was our care for joy and craft, as perhaps relate to what extended from our dutiful oblige. It is not unimportant to note that most people, at least I don't think it uncommon, for them not to have fun. I heard that Joey goes rockclimbing with Danielle sometimes, and that seems cool. But this doesn't mean he'd walk across the land. Tristant has that appeal more. But then even, were all working, and our fun does not often extend outside these nearer types of recreation. Which I don't neccessarily care for. I already do enough. I want something exciting. I would walk to Vancouver, or ride into Langley. But less this is entirely enticing, I am going to notice the wear, and my committment comes up. "Like were pressing to hard on this chain, which then rubs against me and causes concern." Indeed, easier fun may be had, but it usually invovles drinking or indulgence. Even if we did not partake in that, the company, either as I'd expect, or as likely from such inudlgence, does not even interest me. Sitting with Joe, Scott, Connor and Foster is more or less the last thing I want to do. Because you have to be able to talk to people at this point. Perhaps in Kamloops we'd feel differentyl, but right now, we have Mom and Dad, Amon and Nev, Tristan, a history with Lori, then Carli, Andy, Sonal, Mattine, and a mix of other people. Work does have toil, in that my feet may hurt, or my left hip, or I feel tired for a moment. But I often feel right and merry. Postiviely from the work. And, mixing with the neccessary fact of this, for we are enodwed with the responsibility to it, for we must house and feed ourselves. And so having conversations with people, be it in solidarity or laughter, makes this core of us relieved and welcomed. For it is one thing, per Plutarch, to approach rules with submission, and another to see the condition that causes the rules. For treating that gives you some indulable perspective, as though some grip on gravity or respiration. Our independance reveals this fact, as we were given food and bed forever. We notice theyre need, and find peace in our own ability to satisfy it. Were in control then. But fun escapes us, both in ways that would be culpable, but also those which are denied entry. We lean on that denial sometimes, the source for at least, as if to justify why those fair pleasantries are not present. "Were to responsible, and thus have no room or fitting for such things." This used to be my way. To, "well, our choices there have brought us away from those who we may have become friends with. Were open to making them, it just hasn't been long enough, given our choices." This is where I stand now.
 There really isn't much which is absent. Regret and more or less naieve want see me imagining time with Bella, or whomever else. But these are sparks of my mind, flying off as we try and shift from our mundanity. Rememeber though that things are pitched up, and we are to keep with our duty, perhaps not with might but at least sense reserve from things unworthy of entry, and indeed, remark upon the past, the elements of the future, and inject a rythm of pleasure and activity per the current now. We've got no oranges or kale, and we've got Barley, sauce and corn. Loevely. Potatoes are cheap to. Popcorn is easy, just don't make so much. 2023 has started, and the allies are solid. Caramoan is after work Tuesday. Test serve is this week; there's new map type and UI changes, cool. We are nearly finished Nearly Modern; we'll combine Moral and 2025, with some idea of inspection and progress from our Nearly Modern foundation. The idea of speaking to worth in pieces is our dream and direction. The form and layout is not a limiting factor. Our care for the content and it's focus, will bear all satiety and impact true. Take advantage of your insight, and do things which are either accessible and creative, or oppertune and creative. The kind of people I want to be with will be there. Mattine is great. I love sharing with her. Let us rinse off this thing with Bella. Our want is not genuine or pure. Present is determined by ease and ability. Regards
So consistency feeds on simplicity, as you have less friction in your motions; moreover, there is room for moderate efforts, be it from circumstance or your state; as the simple norm of things can more or less be returned to, or kept to over a period of time.
 We have two concerns, namely, the matter of muscles and the matter of ability. In the latter's case, we find much ease from sitting and working when we've done lower and upper body training. But it is from doing it that the benefit accrues. Indeed, our fatigue does not faciliate much running, paired with training mind you. But this seems from a concentrated effort; one which, relatively speaking, was rather waring. It is crucial in these moments not to jump ahead; for during fatigue you generally want to be still, which can give you a flat feeling. It is just the matter of strenght being gained, that your not actively doing anything with it. Remaining active thugh, lightly that is, while upholing form through lifting and grounding postures, is essential, as your general lack of activity thorugh fatigue will sink you down. And given that you still sit, walk and work, these will carry you forward still, but in ill-fashion. Much of our pains come from this.
 It is a priority that you eat enough.
