We basically eat fruit and veg during the morning, incorporate bananas and milk during the day, then go to less carby grains, like lentils, and then move to full grains and starches. There are areas that don't feel so simple, namely the limit of fruit, and whether dates might assist. The mid day also, finds me, after work especially, wanting something substantive. Sardines have this effect, but so does milk.
 Food is the limiting factor, for without it you can do nothing. But, it merely allows for form to take, and indeed, that form ought permitt activity through strenghtening activity. But you must prioritize your routine of this, rather than aesthetique doubts. Indeed, I sympathize with the friction on offer, espcially after doing it. Though, it is always there, but upon abscence, we feel the need so much that we don't mind the friction.
I'll address the elephant in the room. We care about our apperance too much; though it is better to say we derive comfidence from our physqiue, and that, in noticing how we appear, so do we experience more of a function. But so in apporaching this, are we subject to the most grueling of impostures. This has been happening for about 2 years. In 2024 I began to notice this happening, though not so severely. Nor often. I might look back and reflect on our manner and consistency of training. For, since we moved to Pitt, we've experienced these impostures in tandum with training. Usually of the concentrated sort. Indeed, a month or two ago, we gave more emphasis to running, and our imopsture transitionted from our neck and head to our left shoulder. Whether the inclusion of strenght moves is fitting is hard to say. Moreover, our wish to run more than likely reflects our care to do something so much as we are or are not fatigued. Work does this indeed. And perhaps foremost in our regards should be that of stretching, or form at least. This brings all the movement and grough from work and turns it into something more suitable; both in the dispersion of energy and alignment of your parts. Work is naturally uneven and swervy, and so ironing things out, as one would in all efforts, is called for. Indeed, we have this sense that, for one, upon working out, that the effect of it lasts for some time, and that indeed, virbance of the muscle may not correlate with the strict root of it, or imbedded form; and indeed, the more consistent you are the deeper, or at least more lasting that root is. The nearby moments determine the suppleness and or spryness of your muscles, while your regularity of training creates a lasting ability for this. And our sense is that if that training is done, and your not ripe for more deepening, that being active inervates the spryness, and keeps your root afloat. This might actually be a problem; thee problem maybe.
 As it happens, we have a sort of depth, which affords firmness upon a moments call. Why is it though that we wish to be active? Is it to prevennt our roots from sinking? Perhaps there's judgement to be had, in that, if your fatigued, then the roots are, generally, sinking in, and thereby, need no innervation.
 It must be admitted that intuiting all this can be obscure and occasionally ineffective. This often leads to affirming our motion, as if out of deference for some expected sulking or idleness.
 Let us look at our key motive though, namely that of pushnig carts at work. If we played soccer then maybe our aim would be different, but indeed, having that upper body strenght creates much physcial capability. It balances out our lower body at any rate. Which, in it's own right, is equally important, for standing is also as important. Strenght is not a pure factor here. But indeed, that inward feeling of our left hip is really poor, as is our upper body stiffness; which just makes me tired. Doing pistol squats really helped, and indeed, squating always has. Lunging has always felt unstable, though it is perhaps from its isolation. And our muscles are quite tense, for such movement at least. Sitting, as we do, which can only be assessed as a form standard for actual living, benefits equally from training. In short, we experience oddities and ware in these three positions, and have expeirenced times where they were swell, and less so. Stretching does not seem to do enough. But running, it seemed, did afford a welcomed amount of structure and intensity. Strenght training affords us a desired effect in volumes, but requires that we recovery with great intent, or do nothing in the mean time; which is less preferable, nor so commiserate with work. Spots and drops, as it were, mix free range, running and small bouts of strenght exercises. This tends to create less ware, allows for more regularity; as your really limited by your room to exercise, comapred to full on training, which works the entire body and needs an according amount of rest --and were not always amiable nor sensible for that [speaking of, one tip for this, beyond proper form and eating well, is that activity is not neccessarily needed, for inervation sake. You overstimate your need to retain activity, when in fact your fatigue, as remarks the concentrated effect, carried over time by your common practice and stride, means you've not reached that calm and settled state, whereby more activity is expected. I often find that it is our home effort, work, and free range upon our fatigue/post training that causes that grievest of states].
 It is odd, that despite loosing some of that firmness, which is to say rigor to do, that when absent of training, we never feel poor. We are always doing something, from that main trio, but there is no real ware and tare, just kinks and un-firmness. As it were, stretching helps with recovery, and sleep at any rate, btu also evens you out, as to more or less make the energy you have more brim. For training is concentrated and creates much work, but so in the unformative effect of work and sitting, 'walking to', the formation is simply unconcentrated. Doing yoga though, fueled by the breathe, 'as brought upon that lighter yet all sided effort', brings more structure to your energy. Strenght concentrates, and requires dispersion, or at least proper stretching and rest as not to let weaker parts of me succumb to the emphasis elsehwere --our core is noticble here, and areas in our shoulder and legs that were not entirely worked, do to our bent nature in those areas, the left ones namely.
 I find much benefit in spots and drops, or circuit training, but with strenght being an adornment to free range and running, for in that, the energy is brought throughout your body, rather than heavily in certain parts. Doing full range, or strange, tends to increase this overall energy, as you don't often do enough to overly concentrate; moreover, the form of your moves comes from your full body firmness; whereas in strenght training your often absent in form outside the parts being focused on. Plus, the circuit nature means that, for better or worse, as you push, squat and pull, briefly we'll say, that be it for your whole body or for those general-primary parts, that then running or doing free range (lower or upper), that the energy gets distrubuted. Arms swings are a great instance of this.
 Priority number 1 is incorporation and accomodation. For exercise is in some sense a privalge. As we could simply go without, and experience the core trio as they are. But, exercise brings them out, and foremost when your training is regular and complete. Doing it poorly though, and run through those trio, can leave you uneven and rot. I experience this at least. And so, as important as it is to have sensibility in how to elevate yourself, it is a neccessity that you faciliate this growth, for the time to come. For the energy we have, when best distributed, is still left in a rather deep and heavy state. Lifting efforts and grounding ones help our musculature return to normal states, all while being free from ware and available for limbreness. Your day naturally does, so we experience its readyness almost always. But so in work, do we draw on that availability, and experience its shortcomings, or sustainaiblity at that. Food becomes important to, as eating little can cause our otherwise msucular ability to flatline.
 The soul of yoga lies, in motive at least, to form you to sit better, be still, and have posture during idle and moving matters. Full range, for instance, requires mobile stability, which comes from firm kinds of strenght. While pushing a cart, lifting a box, things of the ordinary sort, these merely require a balance and gait which is level and true. Yoga offers this. No matter, what's important is that motion is brought upon by prior motion, and exercise faciliates this. But we must rest, and are often in such a state while doing something. Liberty is fueled by this; at least it uses our resting ability as it's token of exchange. Life would be fine otherwise, if we were always moving. But we sit, on a bus, at dinner, in front of the tv. And we sleep at any rate. Though, it is not so that doing nothing is in need of fashioning, upon deeper moveemnt. That in fact comes quite naturally. Its doing things while being still; which is the matter of most leisure. Recreation is not like that though. And so, as much as we are seated, watching tv and invovled with words, so we need yoga. Indeed, we've not much to give without prior motion. All the yoga in the world does not leave me with this brightness that motion brings. But this motion needs neccessary sculpting as to allow it to properly be in seated positiones; as are we benefited from direct fashioning as to be so seated.
 Motion thus is needed for motion, be it in expelling kinds such as work or creatvity, or consumptive kinds, such as tv, or scenery even. Overall, our care for this is from the task to move without toil, and our want to move and be as were drawn. So we need exercise, as to spring the ability for strong and stable movmenet, but also yoga and or stretching, to harmonize both the practiced motion and that of work/output, with our seated and merry ways.
Whole of Me, Bees and Dreams, Greens Around, Constant Deed
So our health has returned to this dire state. And indeed, it is not simply so, that we fear a weak appearence and thus need to workout incesantly.
 Instead, we work, sit and play, and this both benefits from and or remove's toil by exercise. So we need to do some. But it is clear that the principle in which our training rests on, givne perhaps our peculiar nature, is not being met. And so we ought be practical
So lets consider our finances.
 What we have at the moment in savings is not terribly important. I am thinking moreso about our budgeting.
 For one, wage is going up, to essentially 17$ after taxes. This makes each shift worth 85$.
 It is important to note that we get 100$ every 3 months. We have to confirm this, but budgeting accounts for your 4 weeks of pay, and thus 2 pay periods. There is an extra pay period in april and october. PLus, we do get double time on stats, plus the stat itself. Which amounts to something like 14 hours for a single shift. There are 11 stats in a year, which amounts to a 1000$, if we meagerly presume working on a couple of them. Also, we got 1300$ in tax returns, from 8000$ in 2025. That was mostly working 10 hours a week. We work 15 now, someitmes 20. We may make 12000 or so. I don't know how the tax return will go, but say its 1500.
 With all of these, besides stats, there just extra. You get an extra 500 in april and october, 1500 in march, then an extra 70 in most months; an of the few you don't, you get two on some months.
Im a bit tired now, but this doesn't mean we can't put down some of the terms and ideas were thinking of.
 For one, if we are fully ourselves, it does not mean that someone likes you. It may satisfy you to be so, but it is our task to recognize whether it is really called for that we pronounce our interest. Indeed, being yourself does not equate to interest. I think it just means you want to be yourself more. Benig yourself is hard, and per our defintiion, I won't make it easier. And doing something so simple as going on a walk, may in fact make it more challenging. In such plain ways, we are only asked more to be ourself; for do we share our enthusiasm for clouds then. And if not, who are we being, what have we got. And asking then, less it comes up and you relate on that fact, 'which makes being oruselves easier, such as with Matin', is more or less a date. It just isn't called for, less your interested. Age, our work environment, it being but enthusiasm that we have, which is not limited, and can just be there. Indeed, if we remove expectation, then being ourselves is pretty satisfying. And as much as we can't, our want feels like a discomfort in merely being that. Were more or less fufilled otherwise.
 Moreover, if you think about changing yourself, it still does not mean they like you. They have theyre own likes and occupation, and care for fraterniznig, making friends at that, given age and work-situation espeically. And this comes when you are willing to give yourself up. Which I don't want to do. They may not just like you, and you can see evidence of it. And if there is nothing, no problem. It is the continuity of work and our inability to feel ourself with someone, and thereby relieved, that has me wishing they were here; in some sense, to remove this sense of works continuous prescence.
So I see a huge amount of promise ahead. Our sums is in the rooting stage, of principal and effect, sent to whomever. The care I have, in passion and all things of sort, is to teach Plutarch, the beauty of games, things of the sort. This doesn't feel like something which is meant to exhaust our interrest, and from that vantage point, finsih. It is our path, our way, to real community and contribution. I don't have similar sights on philosphy as I do sums, despite our confidence in really having one. I feel much of this can be advanced and discussed in our lectures/teaching. The point being, that were trying to learn how to think, how to view things, how to see a picture and make judgements, but rightfully. This is nice, and our interests and productivity feel nice in line. There is indeed some purpose, and I relate to when we were making BF videos and how directed we felt. I feel this is our true path, and skill may be inspired to meet it.
 I think where I struggle the most is in the constant grasp of work. For it never goes, and I don't find much promise in the notion of some future improvement. The nature of it wont go away. And even if it is less laborours, that is because it is at home. And indeed, that is a skill to be learned for employment sake. For one, I see a place that has more direct means to learn as something novel and oppertune, as you meet people, can do things in that realm, 'workshops come to mind'. And this compares to meeting people in general. What we'd do is hard to say. Some balance of life, leisure and outwardness is to consider. As I see right now that were working too much to really consider starting such a venture. I just don't have such a grasp on things. Namley the neccessary if not beneficial amount of exercise, yet the consistent tenure of it to allow its advantage to be gleamed, all while not succumbing to over-ware.
 There is something to be said about fully accepting what is available and possible, but thereby being entirely confident and hopeful for what is or could be. Work may never be somewhere you meet the love of your life, nor a friendship. And indeed, life may have to change over the coming years for the stock of people to be rotated out. Perhaps we just need to try new things. Such a time as would permitt this, or move me to actually do, is one of real stability. For I care not now outside of relief, nor do I feel so open to it. There may be workshops and things of the sort. But I anticipate them not; in part with who we are being there. Maybe if we were more settled. And indeed, this is a feeling that perhaps rests on the oddities of life, the ireconcilable ware of work, and how new and or better positioned scenarios can abate this. I think of Vancouver because it has more for me to latch onto. Yet this is only so based on how far your willing to extend yourself. There may simply be more people to date, meet, more workshops and things of the sort to attend, more in-person means for invovlement, sports, yoga, learning. This is more or less objective. But we could easily be there and feel as we do now. And indeed, if we were more stable right now, maybe we'd just begin what matter'd most. We could start lecturing as it were, and by thnose counts, maybe we just need to prioritize stability. I am not done here; in fact I"m generally pleased, and full of rigor at work, our home, family. I lack that friendship though, and depsite being above despair over dating and frivolity, I see the limit of such things. Indeed, it comes down to being yourself; which is to say responsible. Maintaining this is hard, and it is not so hard to understand why, of those things which would be welcomed and fitting to decmompress/please use in between work, that so are the accessibility of these things, or challenge so much as you can acehive universally but through your able shine, that so is it easy to say there wrong, or impractical.
 I don't wish to sink into our savings, but it feels an inevtiable truth that we cannot thrive as we seek while working as much as we do. Our training is just to weird to incorporate, and we have no recompense, given the excellecny we have, to permitt slack. I just don't wish to change as to find some groove that may be more fun and relieving, as to allow us to do less, train enough and work as needed. This makes our state feel more temporary. Which is fine, but bares some semblance of balance. Indeed, flourishing amounts to this being in steady motion. And indeed, there is always some sort of balance. Its just whether or not you gain momentum easily, or if you get dragged down from mistakes or imperfection. 3 days is so approachable. And then even, it is a matter of truth, overall perhaps. Yet again, fundmentally were given the ask of why we are this way at all. Why do we sit on the floor, avoid comfort food, making more money. We don't exactly form ourselves to be fit for somewhere. One notion I came by today was that of heterogenous difference; in that, if you had some trait that others did not, that per that part of the form or function, by which yours was merely off, that others did not surely have the same. Its as though there were a 100 kinds of claws, and you were unique in the one you had, but so did others have one of the bunch. Your just a unique part of the distribution. And indeed, I find that little of these remarks rest on some vain attempt at glory, as if to prosper amongst our struggle; nor is there much of me which is off. In fact, with stature and skill we may promulgate in a way that is both satisfying and of propsect worthy of the content. So far as the form you present it in. But little is enough some time, and our care, or satiety, bears much of the load for lecturing; for which display may advance, as our interest brings us forth.
 A closing thought is that all is pretty well, but better put, of this or that which we lack, out of meekness or practicality, prior choices and development as it were, that so is there much to like and be pleased with, some things to look forward to that mark what may come with our growing stride. Porn is problematic, so jerk off when you really want to; when it would feel nice to stroke yoour penis, tight and or with lube, or not. Not for the exhiliration of humouring that impulse --of which porn excites. Sugar is by and large forbid as it becomes such an issue. Don't worry about money too much. Were going to start running, doing some dips, and our stretching will bring more form. We've got recovery, stability/upright, and rest. These come naturally after work. Allow yourself to do things when its right, and fear nothing but living well. So train the day before work if thats right. Let form carry you and the winds of activity bring you to either aerobics or training (circuity basically). There is so much food. We must just take acocunt. We've got sauce, barley and quinoa; so we need to make corn, edmaame and lentils. Potatoes are wonderful. More running, which, better said, more activity, 'as no drain from over training', will allow to eat more. Ice crea, is great, and there is a fair amount of bread. Use Langley markey to your advantage. I want more emphasis on principle and success. The exact price of things is not too important, given our over-leverage. Bring veg, or soup, and what have you to be, so you may live more simply and effectiveyl. And indeed, for pleasure, find it. Motion must become regular though
Just a quick thought, but most people, nearly all, are not so much your friends. I grant you amnesty from the thought, or peversion, that this is some wrong thing, and that your unique in this way, and that everyone is the opposite. I think its just about how we are. In effect, going to work to see people is odd, for were not friends. And I could see Tristan, or Matin, or whomever. But theyre working. And indeed, it is an odd, 'how do you do'. There just isn't enough I feel to warrant your novel whim and desire to go and see them. Were not that close.
 On a second note, remarking upon things comes down to what we feel we should do and what we are in fact doing. For we may not need to work; but let's say we are. We surely must attend to it in the best way. And indeed, when you get down to it, the idea of work and it's continuity is not what really prompts our remarks. This is so, but again, fundementally. I think were too far off from the near and active portion of things, which we'd normally tend to in terms of some aligned address. We need less of this for the long term outlook. So much as we are compelled to do that, 'making prmoises that alleviate us now', that so we need perhaps more solidity in our understanding and directive. The oughts if you will.
 Another thing is that of learning from failure, accepting that you tried, and that indeed, other promises, prospects and anticipation are by nature oppertune. We ought not feel elated that there are so many 'fish in the sea', as it were. Them being there is not innately something to relieve and or move you, in comparison to your seperation from the thing you failed at.
 It is important to note the value and ease that comes from wanting something; knowing, as it were, what you want. For it makes things not like that unburdensome as these things which ought be oppertune --if there were no standard. And indeed, the specifics of your desire give some perspective, in that, given the standard, so is there more challenge; which alleviates this aimless yet wanting feeling for all. All seem oppertune then, for which we feel meek in not trying. Were looking for particular fish though, and they only swim in certain areas; and this challenge settles in our own view of the care and or need to fish. As far as fish are concnerned, and these one's at any rate, 'for a man hath rules but taste/interest also', that so may we be, as we are pleased and or occupied elsewhere. Such a target though creates a sense of demand, and by that udnerstanding of ability. It is not a mechanical thing, but more or less confidence in response, 'in how the sparks fly'.
 We've learned a great deal over the year, namely in how to be ourself. And indeed, being ourself gets pressed on so far as our view and according amends to things is out of wack. But generally it affords succour and might; our steps our measured this way. And such tastes come about, and we may be ourselves otherwise. And it seems but a sinking and or seeking bend to mention the lighter manner of our want, or pleasure at any rate, in simpler and common things; things that don't generally draw on one to reflect and understand, but just happen by the meager and merry ways that tending to rules and taste procur. Case in point, it brings me a nice bit of pleasure to greet someone. But this just happens. And so far as we are alright, in fortitude and steadyness, grace and succour (with sweets), then such greeting naturally comes, 'as if it were normal'. I don't wish to formalize ourselves by saying our life is in this period or stage; nor do I merely wish to ignore the form of things, and merely say were where were at, given our choice and trajectory so far. All I'll say, is that it seems that the world we live in is expanding, and resting into a fuller yet finer fashion, for which our rules ought change. But so in there lies taste. And in this time, where the world felt different, and by virtue of that, 'as we draemtn but also knew', the rules we had were adopted by me, and we held them almost too tight, 'based on their weaker nature'. This had me more or less closed off, principally occupied as it were. And those who would bear me a finer greeting, 'morgan over Bella', felt magnified. Interestingly, this was so for those positive and those negative --for those two mentioned.
We made a bit of a mistake. Indeed, it is from eating so much sugar. But prior to that, we worked out, twice, but then did nothing for a week or so, and I felt this. My neck hurt as it does, and I just felt luggardly. Work was fine, but I was not. This created that want of sugar, or at least release from duty; as we just couldn't hold it up. It is important to note that whatever we felt we had to do differently, could not just simply be done; as our effect from training maent our proposed remedy of running would still be rocky. Indeed, the point is that training leaves me rather sulking, and must be ironed out. Our time and effort to do so, confidently that is, was not enough. Our scenario comes to light, and our standard even. For I see, primarily, that much activity is sought during these times; our body calls on it. But we have little energy for that, given the heart of our food being more or less heavy. Yet, fruit is the obvious addition we need. Coming from work, eat fruit, oranges as it were. Particulalry on off days, or when your done, and you want to move. Proper sugar is fuel for this, and eating nothing is poor. Doing nothing feels terrible, and indeed slackens our incorporation of work and prior training. That being the case, fruit is expenseive; it has an expense at any rate. And my feeling is that so far as it is essential, given the nature of our free time and that of work, and how it benefits from, and makes un-toilsome at any rate, from exercise, that beyond the balance of this, where we may work more or less and train accordingly, that we still need to eat, and in union with the strides that such balance brings. I find it a forming principle that we would fashion our life around this fact, rather than change it based on our ability to meet it. Indeed, this liberal use of fruit may demand fuller income, which may compell me to find somewhere more affordable, based on how much work I find tolerable, or tasteful. Langley market has plenty of oranges. Some of them are a dollar a poound. That gives a fair bit. Even the 2$ per pound small oranges gave me 5 for less than 3$. That is a whole days worth, for an off day let's say, or at least some stretch of days. That is the neccessity, to pay 5$ for this sustained fuel.
 It is an insight of our life that, upon relief from works chain, yet maintaing our hold, that we look for things to do, as not to feel so busy, or to bridge the gap between our baser standards, 'which are often met by their rightness and simplicity, with nothing special or frequent in between'. But when we look we see not. There are friends, but merely to warm my heart, to feel somewhat seen and known, and share. A man follows his own rule, as impressed or developed, and holds tastes of a similar sort. But this motion, deep in his heart, brings worry and doubt, or in the best of times a resonance of tiredness and stiff-expectation. Sharing with people alleivates a great deal of this. And I've had this before in my life; but I feel I have it in great ways, of numnber and quality. But were not with a friend who I care to see or spend time with. People are enticing in this or that way, but were onnly seeing one side of them, or a moment. And how they are is not so encompassing perhaps, or remarking of theyre truest, or at least broader way. Take me for instance. I believe I am percieved as funny, maybe a little stirn and smitten. But who knows that I have high aspiration, or feel alone sometimes, or enjoy a privalge of pure delight. Who knows my cares and worries, my concerns? Sometimes I feel as though I have it all wrapped together, and that because of this, I have no condition or standard by which I'd relate with someone. Who is boring sparks me not, while someone who is bright and cheerful draws me in. In a way it calls on me to really be better. Our skill in speaking of our self, having done so in introductory and developing ways, and recognizing at any rate what a moment calls for, and being fit for this as not to feel oppertunity slipping, that so in our general steadyness do I feel free to be drawn to such ways of people. And I wish to be around them, similar to a scenic sky. Were free to go to that, but to them less so. And I recognize there being a manner of our draw, and that it is not so stable, per our development. For it is one thing to reach that moment where connection is made, or you feel like you've really been yourself, and another to speak to your harmony and or familiairy with said person; that you could be yourself in the deepest of ways. That kind of peace and comfort is what I seek, and indeed by circumstance or gratitude (for what could or may happen), this comes to fruition. I feel secure and safe with Matin, and she's known me a little, so there is some familiarity there and warmth, beyond what I could further do. But that sense of there being more, possibly, and at any rate, that there had been, has me feeling merry.
Financially, were in no dire straight. But, I am finding myself wanting to work 3 days a week. I just got another 650$. And indeed, our expenses aren't clear. Were not buying much, but were not buyying very smartly. Our situation though is that we gave rent up till may, and were paying for food otherwise. We have just over 4000$, minus some 600. We then have april 16th, 30th, and 2 periods of june. That's like 1500 gross including the stat. So were at like 5500. Which indeed, seems odd, given that we had a 1300$ tax retrun. I think the money is actually better than that. But still. I recognize that it is not ideal to stay here long term. I feel we will move by summers end. But, if we adopt running, stretching around this, eating more fruit, keeping our mind at bay, for we've not much to do in the way of real free time, but still maintain our enjoyment of the bounty of good things, then we may elevate to a common 20 hour week. I would like to determine our potential. But I care not really. If and when we transder, the rent will be cheaper, adn the move will cost nothing. Were getting 2000$ next year, and our fortunes are nothing but swell. I don't want to spend frivolously, nor in negligence. But im on route for something above 5000$, wiith a 500 deposit, and incuring this extra 1000$ work of rent, had we been closer to the norm. So I'm alright with that. And as many weeks as we work 20, the loss is nothing. And we may caluculate what we've given and not, and include the may stat and order of pay periods, to get some sense of our break even point. I care nothing more than that.
So I had 6k, gave 500 for deposit, then 2700 for to-may rent. We then got 1300 from t4, leaving us with 4k.
 Through february we had been getting 400$ paychekcs. From march 5th, 19th and now april 2nd, we got 740, 610 and 660 respectively. Oddly enough, we've spent some 750$ on our credit card, still have 110 -though 50 is going back. Scale must go back by saturady. At any rate, we are at 4700, including 90 from gst.
 Were up to date then. this current week we've worked 5 hours, get 4 hour from stat, plus the 10 hours from double time, then 5 on saturday. Were scheduled 15 for next week. That's 34 (570)for 2nd pay period. We are working 19 the week after.
 Whats a little strange, is that the 4700 we have right now includes the april 2nd pay period, even though we'd have had the march 5th and 19th for april 1st, and the april 16th and 30th for May 1st. June is 14th and 28th. May 18th and July 1st are stats (monday and wedesnsdat resp.) In some sense, the 1350 from march was for april, the 570 + april 30th pay period is for may 1st. Then May itself is pretty stock standard.
 Were at 113 -60 for amazon.
 11, 13, 20, 13, 3 = 60 from march 28th. Which is quite alot given that its only april 2nd. This includes 11 for ice cream. A bunch of frozen stuff, which I recount feeling expensive. Though we got oat milk too. It may be high or low. The point is that we have 4700$ cash. I don't want to dwindle it. I am adjusting, may return to a regular 20 hour per week. I might say, ideally, that we can take hours off so much as the april 2nd week provides, but work 20 hours besides this. Consider this. We have 4700, and are going to work at least 1000 worth for april and may. We will get that 1000 for these coming two paychekcs and the two in may. Plus a stat in may. That's 6700$. We need food for april and may also. That leaves us with 6000, given some -700 for food. That is not poor at all, given our 500 deposit. And this accounts for 15 hours a week, nor the stat, nor the 19 hours were working 2 weeks from now. The question is really of the time ahead. For I don't want to leave. The field is nice, but lessened because of the walls. Running can be done there, but in fact has better setup at Riversie, which is more or less free and nice. The loaction is great; though I don't find the walking to be anything unique. Going somewhere to read is great, and perhaps we'll see this more as we run. But I reckon that all roads plain or large could provide. I don't feel so drawn to go back to Pitt. I like going through maple ridge. I really like Hammond, and its hard to feel enticed by anything short of shady lane. Hospital area feels a little choked off, and west of laity is on a hill. Same thing with north of shady lane. Westview and fairview area are nice with tress, but long roads, not many fields. It's hard to say. Maybe we'll look, see if anything is near hammond. For I just need the price to come down. Maybe we can balance things out, where we can contribute a little of our own money to get through a few months, where the novelty wares off. Its nice here, and useful to have that field. We ought make use though.
 Just a little error. We work 15 this and next week. But we get another 9 from the stat -- the 4 hour plus double time. So thats 39 (650). This period were in, of this 39, plus the next on is the standard. And oddly, were not short at all. We've not in effect worked a single 3 shift week. We have the extra 600 from april 2nd, and 650 from april 16th. That leaves plenty of room to work some 3 shifts here, 4 there. Were working 19 for week 1 of april 30th, so even moreso
Twas a tough day. The insights about me were quite revealling. And oddest of all is this notion of personal want and or fear, and how these being rather unset make you compare yourself to others, and see what they want or not.
 There is a deep thought about our wish to be seen, and through confidence imagining so. It seems our want is natural, as it comes when we have some task to do. The things may be good in and of themselves, but we seek them during stress. In our case, if we lack such thing, we imagine. In part, we are sworn, if not wise to duty. And feel somewhat unincluded with things. Were often on our own. And so when i see someone else having a conversation with someone, or a laugh with a group of people, I can't help but feel were missing out, and are wrong in our ways. For why don't we have that. This is an empty thought though, or one that lives little for who we are. One notion that came to mind is the indiscernable experience of pleasure and discomfort, and how these are yours to be driven by. And indeed, independance, employment and rule are conditioned pillars to our way. For we are privaleged and thus in some form of error not to be independanat; but we must then provide; and all things fall under employment. And as such we obide rules, as to make work un-toilsome, to enjoy it even, and to ellivate our pleausre, and be steady in the wishes and or impulses that draw us away from this. I don't really care to complain about myself. If so, I'd like to do it in a genuine fashion. I find that Bella and kass just do so to vent. But not to learn and grow, or bolster themselves with strenght. Its a sort of 'ughhh', as if to endure this toiling feeling.
 It is an odd thing with Bella. My relationship with her is as dry as a desert. And I don't wish to pour water on it. If it were easy, so be it. But indeed, when we started speaking to her more personally, near the end of last year, it happened because we were unattached and indifferent. And indeed, it is critical to note the persisting nature of us. How we'd been just didn't mesh with her, and as we naturally became more familiar and solid in ourselves, we spoke as we cared to; but with more indifference. Some vulnerability, but with room to do so. There is zero room for that now. And indeed, this is from an incident, rather than the nature of things. All are viable in this sense. But I can't say I really want the same thing from everybody. And indeed, letitng ourselves imagine, is some form of remedy, to our discomfort, or something which compells me.
 I feel like we were being ourselves, and upon that, we felt free to open ourselves. For it was not easy to talk with Bella. She was bright, but the only connection was something personal. I did not care to complain, nor did I want to talk with her as she does with other people. Our steadyness produced this. But such steadyness remarked a state, an arrangement if you will. This can be judged on its, for it was some manner of alignment with rules, but perhaps folly in the custom. Upon becoming unsteady, as perhaps remarked the nature of the rule, we felt like filling in with Bella. We were excited by that prospect. For the unsteadyness came from not wanting to return to the rule; perhaps because we saw it wrong. And what happened happened.
 She is notable in terms of who I want to talk with. Given how much I want to talk with kass, how easy it is, and how well we are, the motive isn't there. Same with Allie today. I wanted to taalk with her, and upon doing so I wanted to again. But I just felt closed, as though I had to be confident in order to speak, rather than comfortable enough to do as i want. And my recourse is to more or less put Bella in that category of people I don't want to talk to. For my want should be tempered, if not proper. But the difficulty is immense, and I am not tired, nor endited, to the amount required of me in being well as to overcome this. In some sense we should let our want, or care come through. Let it be at least. In some sense we just move with indifference, understanding perhaps how removed we are from the interest we have. Loving that is, or being kind and merry to people. Yet, we have this sense as though not to expect, and by that, want little. Our soft eyes reflect this nicely.
I rememeber after Holly that we had this updraft of self-regard, and we tuned ourself to work, it's purpose, and our way of rising above it. And this felt fair and fine. Simply, the prescence of work, a deed as it were, had me feeling pleased at our ease with it. It was not as though there was nothing and so we wanted things, and felt poor and envious without. But rather felt good being in tandum with a pressing need. And indeed, good came from this. Kong comes to mind.
 But our rule changed, and not till we we went to Vancouver did things really take off. The years prior have never seemed so clear to me, but I think there is some insight. The course of 2020 was so that we just couldn't handle the requirment of work, what it needed, and also be happy. Orienting ourselves as such felt to burending, or hefty as it were. And so we left. Our happiness felt too foreign or hard to acquire, and we perhaps struggled in doing what was neccessary for things to be acceptable. We realized this, or came into a trajectory that fit to that, during our time in late 2021, where we more or less had a secure feeling of our duty, nor this enlarged sense of seeking things or verifying ourself. We were steady, and did as we pleased. Though it felt hard. Winter writings is the epitomy of that; we could barely utter a true word. And however it was, going into Vancouver I had a sure sight of what was needed but also what pleased me. I say pleased, rather than sought, for we didn't care for much. Our feeling is noted in our earliest of writings, 'we can now think and do as we deem fit, and move and adjust accordingly'. I feel we were behind the reality of work, or stability rather, and transitioning to that then adjusting were a bit of an impass. But I felt properly sentenced to work, and fit ourself into a pace of steadyness. Such strides had me more or less reasonable about our feelings for Simron; or interest at least. In short, work felt both properly seen, in that it was neccessary, but fairly so by me, in how we regarded this; but so in our outlook and experience, was it rich and brightening further, and otherwise calm and merry towards whatever could be, be it in oppertunity or passing me by, or what I'd do, solemly.
 This point in my life has remarked a kind of happiness which I like to describe as both pleasing and just. The time leading into our employment was so that what was needed of me caught up upon our move to Vancouver. And the primary nature of this gave the remedy a sense of justice, and almost validity, in being tailored and bound to it. For principal, you recognize that the primary nature of this affords you life. "As though some unconditional test, of which your fitness and response is paramount. You live if successful, and thrive if doing so is easy and sustained." I make this point for we often left times that were tough yet stable, or just rather, into moments, or periods of time rather, that felt fair and plenty, yet unbound or sturdy enough to promote what we cared for and sought in such freedom. We could not think or dream fully; and being so free lead us so. One ultimate reason was that we had no means or place to to truly be, in commisery or fidelity. For our situation was only supportive temporarily. And indeed, the heart of independance is that you don't have to bargain or beg for such state to be extended, or permanent.
 Given all of that, I've not much to say; or I can't really say much. But just to get to it, the past 3 years of my life, moreso as the months go by, have been relatively peaceful and plentiful. Yet we've become rather thin, poor in substance, missing people and friends. I see what could have happened if we stayed course in Vancouver. And indeed, where we went was not some alternative to that, but a complete switch. Morally it wasn't a regression, but in practice it was no different than fall 2018, nor fall 2020, or 2021 at that. And my choices weren't really the cause of this, but rather our sense of things, in the breakdown we had. For the whole year we had been living as we normally did, by our own rule, without precaution, or expectation at least. Normally that expectation begins to rear its head, but we had tremendous leeway. We re-wrote our website, had really good video and gaming through the spring, wrote our first book, Near Light as it were. But the summer became stale and routinely, and our exercise began to lessen. We had arranged to stay thorugh the year, and this gave me a more or less resurgence of energy. We wrote warm winters, which indeed, akin to government, god and war, saw me station our view on death; and too that of marks, or the beginnings of liberty. And we made our first productions, in BF namely, but too in the discourses. Our first 500 view video was so exciting, and brought so much passion. But I was worn. And when Bronte came early December I found myself dissapointed as she left, as the reality of her not doing as I did, or wanted to, became apparent. This is all very odd. In fact, we went to see her, and we more or less vented. She could not have been less near, or 'wanting' of me. I broke down on Christmas, crying out that I'd made a mistake. And indeed, what that was is hard to say. For on one hand, and this feels what I meant primarily, but we had this vision, and expectation, that our writing would earn us acclaim. It was our promise. Bronte coming though kind of woke me to the fact that the world, and people at that, were not in fact, nor in any reasonable sense going to, place theyre attention on you. And so I felt like we were waring ourselves thin, as if to keep this promise alive. If I could only shine brighter, I'd see it, and we'd expect this as much as we were shining. The folly in this broke our anticipation, but also relieved us of this enormous responsibility. And our coming steps saw us trying new things. We went to church, to yoga, had a coffee chat, attended mediation seminars weekly. These were great, but they made me no friends, and held little promise. And weirdly enough, I found myself not actually wanting to do them anymore, as they felt like obligations, occupations if you will, rather than fun. And I returned to our own occupations, but born and strode by our own inclination and sensibility.
 Nothing marks the grounds of these thought more than war. Governance too, but soliders being killed by blades and cannons. Such form grew in me upon reading Robert Rogers journal, or that of the civil war surgeon. "I cut off his finger, which was barely attached, and he barely noticed." Reading Spirit of the Laws was immensely satiating too. For whatever reason, our parent's viewing the news tore at me, and grappled with our manner and way. I think the reason reflects the manner were still in now. That we want things, seek them; be it out of erotic reaching or the comparison of others. But in this want, as though something naturally of inklinh, which we are fully in the manner for, that so in our fundemental way, in good and greater ways, that we expect something to come. Or, perhaps, were indifferent, ready for the future to unfold. But seeing the news just rocks that foundation. And I either need to tighten it, or let it go. Yet I don't know who I am without it. And releasing that normal grip, has me feeling erotic, or just normally endowed, yet were not there. Or, so much as we are, there's nothing oppertune.
I realize that I don't really know these people. Theyre not great friends of mine. Were not familiar, as we were with say Damian at Westview; where we knew eachother for 5 years. We've more or less known them for a year, and through work at that. Not full of time to get to know eachother. And indeed I know some. Tara knows me, as does Matin, and Tristan, Andy too and Teresa eve. Carli who I often forget. But were not overly attached to any of them. Matiin is the only one who I don't hold back from, and that is really encouraging, as it shows me the way. But I realizse I don't know anyone else that much. Not everyone cares to be knonw. Some, in fact I'd say all, only know some, or care for some.
 I'm just coming at this from my point of view in life. We had that great spot in Vancouver, similar to where we are now, steady and heads up. What could come or what we could do, felt bright, if not on the horizon. But we moved, and grew more or less a phenomanal understanding of peace and definement, and purely to. But not in harmony with anything real or functionable. Similar to when we got over the newness of Vancouver, our situation had to be amended. And indeed, that pure side of things in Kamloops saw a invincible approach to such things, while we let our time there play out; and we lived with immediacy, as the nature of our days called.
 But we then got employed, and happily to. That manner of immediacy carried over, and we never resorted to work our of deference, but with calmness, knowing we'd be fine in whatever was to come. We'd let it be and do what was fair and well now. Immediately we saw Amon, and more or less noticed our awkwardness, or woodedness with Bella. And indeed, I had those odd feelings of tiredness, struggle making eye contact, feeling somewhat wooden and unsatisifed with people; and at that, a want to be seen, a feeling of glory, 'if only they knew', for which dreams and sacrifice came to mind. But I rememeber Amon being perturbing factor, as Bella has been. And I compare it to what Cochran said, in that I felt that we could not just focus on our actions, as we were concerned with how we appeared. The idea that we were seen by her or noticed, or that we made every chance to speak with her well. This made work a chore, and brought me away from life. Perhaps the interest was there, and the motive for it being some oddity in the nature of your rules. "Temporaily complete, as it were, which brought me to find relief in something akin to fun and pleasure, but of a fair kind." And indeed, the truth in things is that our odd ruleset made it hard for me to recognize that she did not show interest in me, nor did we have sincere interest in her. Perhaps if she did, or was receptive at any rate, then this being so would fit our ruleset. But she did not, and the folly of our rules did not warrant much grief upon this.
 And so time went on. We made a more or less content committment not to prioritize her anymore. Something similar happened with Bella, whereby I saw no prospect, but still had that feelnig, and felt thrown off by it. So my resolve, or admittence rather, was to allow, as it were, these feelings and notices, of when they'd leave or moments we missed, to pass, with acceptence of the reality in which your overted from; namely, 'if they leave, or we miss this, they won't like me'. I was in agreement with that, and very quickly grew unattached. It took maybe 2 or 3 weeks with Amon. Bella quickly left our mind. In fact, I found it quite easy to think of them both, for erotic sake. For we had no definite eye, and thus remorse in hovering around amidst such motive.
 No matter, we honed in on work. I still felt tired, or at any rate detached from people. But I became more or less amiable. I think my feeling was that we were on par and friends with everyone or no one. In the spring though we read Loyd Georges memoir, which was wonderful. And our understanding of governance, and following of it from primary resources, grew deeply. Our health remained a problem, as did hearing the news, and generally work felt like this fair yet rock-solid thing. But we were strong and true, hearing the birds, the wind and moon. All was great, but under a releatively stock standard model. Our pace was good, but life was just plotting along.
 Near years end, we had essentially removed all fear of glory and touring wants. We felt calm and confident in our ability to do creative things big and small; as we dreamt, or had already. Our health was poor, but we were surpassing the worry of appearence. We soon saw the value of running, and how subssistent it was. We felt the ware of being at home, and that having more focus and control could accomodate for running well, stretching rightly, and doing the crotchety workouts in minor amounts, as to firm my body for work and leisures need. Our mind too just became well, and we found ourselves wiht merry passivity to everyone at work: no sense of ill-will or notice. And we also found deeper experiences with those we were close with. Bella namely, just became someone who I'd never talk to, but now did. We'd speak personally; whihc came from some time prior where we'd built humour and repor. The importance of this is that we don't have much in common. Were a bit to bold and confident, she is a bit younger and chatty. But I brought this together, out of our own liking, for it was nice talking to her at work: the opposite of boring, as it were. But it also just felt nice, and gave this sense that you don't need permission to love. You merely offer; and even if they left, you could still be there, yourself, with your caring mind. "You being yourself, having done nothing wrong or taken anything, allows you live without remorse." What drew me to her, and thus what I was humouring in our talking to her, was something that we approached, and did so for more than 2 months. And this was great.
 Early in the year though I found myself on a fine and steady whim, wanting to move. I looked at places, found two in Hammond, one for 700 the other for 900 but private. I orochestrated showings, saw the latter first. It smelled of smoke, but I took it. Was super excited. Told everybody. I just felt this uplifintg spirit. But when it came to move, the mess was so poor that our father insisted that we don't move. And indeed, there was some friction here a night or two before; at least the comenustation of everything was not in stone, then fell apart quickly. I would not abandon our family, and thus theyre feeling of this. And I took it upon myself to do so.
 Describing what happened is extremely hard to do. Though I can't say why. In esscence, I felt off. As though I were left with nothing but what I really had, for I was not present. And it did not feel like much. At least, I felt as though, given what I had, that I had to return, or at least re-align to our current post, which I did not want to do. Just eating felt like something in that case. But what occured, is that I spoke with Tristan, nicely I'd say, and told him I liked Amon, which was true and most important, and had a little crush on Bella. I just wanted to admitt these things. He said Amon was married, which oddly enough, I easily accepted, and have had zero mis-feelings about. But Bella, seemed like a possibility. And I got excited about, in humouring the possibility. We did not know what to do, but it seemed liked something which could happen. And I recall thinking about this, and perhaps it is best to describe it as a forward opposition to our otherwise struggling state. In that case, had we been more present, which required proper footing where we were, that so could I see what was around that. But given our lack, or un-want of such footing, we were not so present, and so things novel, or at least close but not much so, felt distant and away. The prospect of going somewhere, and in some sense, doing so by some concension as not to maintain ourselves in such a near and proper state, but rather be more aloof and thus fitting for things out and about, had me momentarily enthralled. But, for one, I did not think this meant she liked me, or would humour this. I was aware more or less, as I'd been through the year prior, that our movement forward did not constitute movement by them, to me. And we dramatically overlook and misguidinly see our advance as something which they humour and embrac, as though honrored. "By me," as it, "Like, oh my, it's you, someone so special." This comepltely undermines who they are as a person, that they a) would want something purely on your own adavnce, rather than something more naturally relating, and b) suggests that they don't have theyre own motion. As such, they don't neccessarily like you, or lack theyre own pursuit and interests. Nothing is better than the offer of a stranger. And so, on a friday, we came in, saw Bella, and immedately knew, as our prior thoughts had led us, that this would not work, and that your imaginagtion that it would was in truth brittle and off. I went through the day, more or less admitting this was so, that she was not there, and we had to deal with that. Almost certaintly our life would remain, and we'd have to refashion it, for itself, and remain appreciative of things. Funnily, it seems so clear and simple, that we'd be so grateful, and just enojy what we got. But the idea of her speaking with me, or making me glad, was something I could not handle. For I either wanted to melt and have her say more, or I had to be bolder and less wanting/needing to be speak myself. "Love comes from a place of giving, which is born from steadyness." We weren't there though, and in some sense, the somberness was in the more or less nerve at things being as they were. The reality was hard to cope with, when we weren't injecting into it the bond and care we had.
 And so at the end of that shift we had this conversation; one which in a way, showed that depsite our offness, that the prompt for you to say something, as you want, could be made, directly, by our skill and composure. And it was great. Long even. But it triggered me to change my mind, and feel as though we were being invited in. "Come forth, I want you to, I'd be glad," is what I thought. I realy did feel as though she wanted me, or at leaast would be excited, if I came forward. With smiles I approached, but got glaring eyes. As though it were in fact this fun and dandy thing, but nothing personal. At least, the second I entered that place, as though I entered her home, I was comepletely out of bounds.
So I had a pretty wonderful day. Soft eyes, as it were, lets you peer right into somebody, but without lingering, nor prying. Its just our own ease, and natural care, peering as we do.
 I realized though that fundementally we still want Bella, and that given our permission to see her, I still feel there is a want in me, for her to recognize this or that. Indeed, we expect this, or envision it. But that is generally wrong. We should be able to address her, however muted, or at least not enthralled, 'for we may not really do as we please', that so aren't we dissmayed, nor motivated as it were, to like her. And interestingly, it comes easy, as would be with anybody. I found it particularly hard, but otherwise made our advance. In part it would seem, because we had this feeling, this want; which is something I cant fully address. But it sat there in our stricken state, and was defalted at our inability to be present. Which frightened me. So we ran forth.
 I took this to mean that our happiness was the measure and key. Nothing else. We were not to exepect things for ourselves, based on how we were, but that we'd be a certain way, as would make us happy. The more open we are, the less we have this longing or wanting feeling. It is our unexpressed or muddled self which, in any sort of advance, yet incomplete, has me irking forth. This is why we often ignore people, or decide not to care, as it removes the temptation. But I sit there no longer, and am willing to abide Bella and whoever else, in ways that are timely and fitting. We can be ourself. And indeed, there is a lot of effort here, a lot of sparks flying. But so we center in, check inwardly, and see how our true happiness is forming, and where its in lack.
It wounds me greatly to have come so far, done what is seemingly right, yet get lost in our hopes and ambition. Indeed, the matters of fame and glory weigh on me not. But I expect in our freedoms and liberties to have friends; and given our lack of impressiveness, or regard rather for such being a mean, we rest on us being ourselves, open, and by that, iniating. Yet this is so hard to do; and when you cannot, it is often because of life's natural demand upon you, which has you requiring endurance, if not stability. For there is much to handle. And even those things which we take for granted, upon clarity, still require the work. And our actual quality of effort is not so shiningly consistent. For this and that reason. But we either pillary ourself as though some oppertunity has passed us by; for in being ourself, the potential is there, for anything mind you; or we feel like were missing that thing, 'as though our attention is turned back to ourself'. And indeed, all that is good and well within those respects looses it's gleam, and spot as this thing which is pleasing and worthwhile amidst our duty.
 We perhaps take too lightly how well we know ourself, or are levelled in our views and ways. Of what we have to do and what I want to do seems to get overlooked with our solemness towards Bella or whomever, or the sense that were missing something or someone, and not who we ought be to grant that. Indeed, the fault in our life is letting things come to pass, or hoping at least; as though we need only steer. But this loosens the hold we have on what we have to do, and at any rate, makes little use of those things which are near, accessible and rewarding for us. These tie together, as far as I'm concerned, to create a life of our own, which is happy and just. In this, lies prospects, in that we may do things, be it close or broad. I think we let ourself down in pursuing things that are not so easily achieved. And foremost, we do so at our own expense, as we let slip the grip we have on ourselves, and the otherwise calm and hearty strenght when were steady. It's like we have money then go out to spend it. And we underestimate the cost of things, 'less we'd buy this sports car for the hope of one night. Yet the lease lay on'.
 I see that communication and resolve lie at the tip of steadyness; for we may just be, try this, do that. In being invited to something, may we be more open, and not overt ourselves. As may we move with calm delight, in our work, where people are around us; and we may ask how they are, share how were doing. And to those we know and are closer to, may we go to them with more earnest and glee, and be cordial and as called for with everyone else. I think I just falter a little when this motions which relfect our steady state, compell me beyond what we steadily do, but for the sake of promise; for which apathy and elation drive me.
 One element is that of freedom, or liberty as we say, whereby upon our duties being met, and in right amounts, we feel as though our time is done, and we may look around, for fun. Yet the duty returns. And indeed, it may just be a matter of life that there isn't something else to do. And per our history, so is our present so. But we retain what we have. Only, it seems as though we are not so enchained, or burdened by our duties, as to need strict resolve, and by that, overtness from our fun things. Were just not near any of those, nor have had the position where oppertunity (of the such) would strike us.
 I'd remark then that life has truly set us to a point of peace and propserity. But I am struggling to use it. Do I play Civ because I like it, or because I've nothing else to do? Oddly, what is good for work and deed, sprouts me to truly care for Civ, and a host of other things. But when we question this, or feel compelled to something else, we tend to lose sight of that propiety. As though were putting something exciting and novel in our sights, and we take for granted our work and the quality of it. It is as though we are able to make a change, and by that, find fitting with someone else. Were not that way though, truly. At least, we give up, perhaps in flatter moments, who we are in trite and minor ways, all while fundementally we are the same. This creates frustration; as though we changed our wardrobe but are physically the same; for which we resent that physiciality.
 All these things though which strike you, Bella or someone sweet and pretty, or the idea of friends, whole and plenty, these are not poor or inaccessible. Its about there being people, but yours. Everyone is different; and even if they are in some ways the same, they still have significant contrasts. And circumstance too directs people to and fro, for which the best fitting folk go different paths. It is not hard to admitt that finding such path where comparability and like are present is hard to find. Indeed, someone like Bella, or Amon, or Holly, strike me, and flutter my heart. In deep ways, I see that we really just want to be present, and that in doing so, we in fact keep to our life, as though were not closed off by it. Such is the acclaim of green and vibrant pastures.
 But fundementals come to question. For why must we be as we are, but be with such liberty, or excess, as to remain who you are but assocaite with such person or thing your drawn to; where quality and tambre connect, rather than genuine comparability. At least, we come in with a mighty needle, rather than a warm embrace. Why are we so dug into being ourselves. And however so, why are we this way? Sentences of the sort I need don't seem to exist, which is why I write; in finer and blossoming moments, we make such. And what I need now is the balanced thought and connection of where we've come from and who we are because of this, and the notion that if we were different, than we'd be somewhere and in some way as to not need to or prefer being as we are now. Time has simply not given us dureation to transition. Moreover, had it, we'd be in a place where we weren't so stable and pure, but ourself, and by that, called by someone who we found appealling, even if our want was uncalled for. "Were a priest who's found liberty, but by theyre choices been dropped into a bar; and all you see is a biker's charm. But not who she is. Yet you just want to be yourself. Or better yet, find it easy to do so. Your called to at any rate. We are ourselves so much. Which does not un-inclucde the way things are."
I look at our fundementals, and the soruce for them, or philosophy at any rate. Of War, death and God, these lie as springs for our wants and whims. As if to tailor ourselves in modesty and exepectation. But it is the economoy which best characterizes our active way, our normal standing. For we need food and bed, and employment to boot. But such effort is based in the economoy. This is simply so.
 That is all to say, that we are at Superstore, and the job we have is quite flexible, pretty smooth, and generally satisfying, if not lacking much worth grieving. It is so that I accept it without remorse or somberness. It is not something I am trying to get away from. But moving on is a natrually good thing. No matter, we have the ability to transfer. The ease of this is relatively clear, though the process not so much. But I live as though our chioce could be so, and were at that point of feeling beyond confident. We can only switch once though. If we were to quite then reapply, we'd have to return to probation, which is a meager issue. And our raise is almost negligble. Our work schedule may be an issue, though I reckon this is a tolerable thing to have. And indeed, there may be oppertunity to do new things; though I prefer to say there are prospects which we can pursue. I am not sure what is going to pass us by. Though, it is not unfair to say that we may meet someone, or find some interest, that removes the care to look past our work; and that is an agreeable part of our life. For which we pursue and experience other things. No matter, as regards work, you have on one end directions, such as video editing and its relation to literary efforts; and how going to school, being near people, or anything like this, may affirm such creative pursuits, or offer us new forms of employment. I know not enough of this, in either wish or fact, to say it is some clear and definite goal or end. It is merely a loose, and in some ways fanciful view; for which the notion of direction lies. For the reality is that upon closing in on where such things would be seen, we may simply not want to. It is more like an option. Something like personal training though, equally an option, but not a direction I'm on or wish to stear towards. Then you have actually prospects and pursuits; which is to say, almost in a timely sense, that now, or at some point in the future, there is clarirty and drive for something. The glaring, or foremost one of these is Sums. Time and task may not be at our disposal, or of interest. Months may need to go by, or just changes and alterations in more primary or focused things. But so is the pursuit one of execution, rather than promise. For in one we see and know, and must merely do, while in the other, it is more like relief, or at least a spring to our current movement, 'that something good lies ahead'. "Effort to do, and the confidence that there will be effort to do, whichever it is."
 It is an interesting part of our inquiry, that we'd start with work, it's neccessity, the slight yet basic truth of economy. But where to then? People come to mind, but this feels like an odd subject. Nanely because most remarks come down to faciliation; yet the form these sentiments are about restraint and appropriateness, rathern than temperance. For we like all. And those who we don't really are those who we just gleam difficulty in. This is all to say, that people naturally draw you, but by theyre character and speal, draw you more eeasily, or perhaps cause friction and aversion. Boys, for isntance, have this kind of flamboyance and foolhardiness, but also cool and broish commradery. Who we are, as to measure as being fair in whether were fit for this or not, and thus at ease, comes down to work and our prioritiy things. But so does skill come to play, in that, so far as you are open or willing, you speak to folk, make gestures, acknowledge them. As it were, at work for instance, there are those who you speak to, or acknowledge in passing. Cresha is like this, or Morgan foremost. Then there are those you talk to about work, namely Kim or Chenoya. But then there are those who you either a) speak to about something personal, or b) speak about yourself, or just checking, as though there is this familiairty. Tristan is like this foremost, as is Andy, and Tara to. Though in one, we are more interersted, but able nonethless for the other. We have extended our type one ability in droves, as those who you merely acknowledge are not absolutely so. You may ask how theyre doing, what theyre doing after work, how theyre weekend was. You may also ask something personal, ie. what they like, if theyre in school, where theyre from. Different times call for different measures. One thought that has come up recently is that of liking someone, and how easy it is for you a) to talk with them, and b) want to see them, or ask at any rate. This is different than being personal with someone. I don't particularly want to hang out with Andy. I'm not excited at any rate. Tristan on the other hand is really into health, and so I would be up for that. Were into different kinds of health, and thus ways of living, so the space for hanging is more select. But I speak to them personally equally. Bella is a great case of something else, like Holly, or Simron to an extent. These are people, usually girls, who interest you, excite you, make you feel nervous. Perhaps it's our sexuality and we attribute too much stake and potential in such; for we often feel ashamed in being far from them, like theyre passing me by. Directing ourselves elsewhere, in life namely, cleanses me from this. But so much as we are chill and moving along, I really feel the seperation, and exhiliration upon contact. There has been a strong side of me, with Bella later last year, and Simron for the most part, where we are more or less making contact, but not incessantaly, or as though reaching some oasis. It's more like were coming together as people, together at work, and the matters of pleasure and fufillment drift around; sometimes as promising and real factors, other times as prodding intrusions, 'smearing ones as it were'. And my thinking over the recent months has formed itself into the notion that these kind of things really flourish, generally through the ways were thinking, but when there is kindling so to speak, and air, and wood. Rather than making something so roaring and fantastic out of twigs. I've submiutted the fact that people who are creative are those I who enjoy most being around, for I can be pure, honest, happy and ecstatic, and not so vain, broish, superficial, nor complain really. Easy going but in motion. Love for me is about dedication, committment, resolving yourself to be there, and follow through. This to me, is sparked and blazed by said creativity, but it is foramlized into more than fun and passion when theyre is self awareness with this, direction, fortitude: maturity and power.
 If I were God, or a canoneer, or a sailor, or a husband, father, man even, these would be soul things, of prupose and effort, end and deed. Not types, not kinds, nor genres, but things, items, monuments, for which other minor things were around. Of this, centering all efforts, is our love of literature, its enlightnment and liberty, in both ground and reward. Of all things wished for and sought, or of passion and reaching bounties, lies literature and her formative and warming ways. She has made me, treated me, and continues to waft her smells into my mind, soothing me, calming me, but baring me up, into stances of might and granduer, but also when seated, during longer moments of rest and merriment. The names of such offerings speak to me as both mentors and models, but also commrades and honored men. They sit in my heart in the form of words they've written, but in my bones also, where they sit almosmt as paintings, relics, tributes which speak to our learned motion. They bring justice to our way, sense to our dedication; reason to our plans, and heart to our strides. They've made me feel like I have all, when in truth I have not; and with even some, I glow as though I am with more than enough. And indeed, it is not theyre heradly with prommpts me, as though some familiarity I have with someone known, to which I may earn rewards via theyre notoriety. But it is moreso about me; that whether you know Plutarch's Listening or Not, I feel you ought to: and seeing as I have, so in all pedagogical, moral and impassioned ways, I mean to share. All in life, that affords pleasure or advantage, fits well if it aligns or supports this process. We've been down the most known of roads, but differing in subject and form. Though, as it happened, we were merely delayed, or at least too impassioned and thus confident, to do something different in prep for this truest kind. I see the clearer now. "But so the mountain sits; however clear in end and route, so must we climb it. And our time is spent deliberating on that, or being occupied elsewhere. But so in this spot, where in small moments we consider the peaks we'd climb, or if we'd climb at all, so are my shoes waiting."
 I look at what I want, but consider also what I need. And on one end, the subject in question is that of people and purpose. And for one, the matter is really of stability, steadyness and trust, or continuity. Our home and work reflect that. In the other though, this has the double edged sword of holding direction, and thus promise, from somewhere other than where we are. But so, in humility, do we see that such promise is only enacted one and reached through the same efforts, or behavior, as we'd demonstrate now. "One ought fish well in a lake or a pond, however promising the bounty is for those fit for getting a bite." On top of that, given the enticing nature of friends, being without out that, or feeling off about what you have, so in your proper and favored obliged, may you keep on, seeing what is head, but not holding to it, looking past where you are. The point being that you'd make do with what you have, get not false impressions oor apathy about what you could or should, and merely live in this way as stable as can be. Choices and oppertunity are for the future. We are employed now, and with friends, people at least. As do we have interests and our own regard, of primary sorts to. Not limiting ones, but differing. For we'd not go out with Bella if we were someone without regard for theyre health. We'd do it with smoeone else, likely outside of work, but your age no less. The story though is that this differing factor is naturally limiting; and indeed, I love our way; it begs me not, I am married to it, at ease to accept. But it shortens the spaces where I fit in. And although not so damning or begruding to be without friends, of the dearest or intimate sort, but it feels rot and queezy to see possibility, or at least that you could have, or want at any rate. "In those minnor monents, where occupation and standard are unkept, or at least on autopilot; passion and want come up, prompted by novel things, or old fascination --damn you porn and teenage frivolity." In any case, it is not hard to see why some other environemnt, the city namely, or where there were more people --as Pitt Meadows is entirely small and maple ridge not especially large-- is more enticing, and seemingly oppertune.
Why stay in Pitt Meadows? The simple anwser is that it is where were at, and we've made great relationships with people. I look at our friends, and I compare this with our look at work and play; it may come, and indeed, theyre may be places that bring more oppertunity. But we are where were at now. Perhaps we need to make this work, so that were not so strung by direction, and can just be. There is an odd finality, as though Vancouver, or wherever, is this end-all destination; not so much in that it is thee place to offer us what we seek, but that it is the best of places, across the board, whereby we may be steady and stable, but also make pursuit; for there is no where else to go. Pitt Meadows is small and homely, and in many ways I like this. But it has less oppertunity for work and friends, and our broader life. But such a want is perhaps the subject at hand, that were feelinga bit off, and in that way, looking ahead. Ahead will come, as time, ease and circumstance coalesque. But we are invovled now, and pleased as we are or aint, we ought make do with what we have, or at least where were at. For we have work tomorrow and routinely to come. Were running, which has shown more promise, and we may finish Moral, go through 2025, and add some novel warriora and upright sequences to follow. Our stretching in general could be advanced. Food is coming, and indeed, the expense of living where I am is not a small factor in us feeling unstable; in some way it doesn't feel worth it, as though were dragging our feet a little; so much so, that were left with more free time than good. And in that we don't have much room to afford comforts that would soften that looser edge; while we remain at heart. But we've nothing very true or novel about that edge; which on one hand brings doubts and despair, as though we should. And it makes me feel the path we've been on, and where we've come short, or that we are in some sense a failure for being where were at. I often think of being around things that this feelings remark: money, people. But when were not in such active motion, or good, we feel the lack of these things; or we stiffen, or affirm at least, inwards, as if to brace our edge. Which makes me feel closed. Liberty is on part with flourishing, whereby you are one with your deed, in both employment and the day. And by that, bear your task without toil and thus no reserve, and in that, openly and happily, 'whereupon foresight is gleamed; a view is fashioned'.
