Lets do a little reflection. Our bloodwork came back, and we were low on B12. Not surprising. We were low on Iron to. My understanding is you have ferrous reserves, meaning stored iron. And we were just under the threshold. For one, we've been eating too little, and I've noticed this becomnig a problem. Another thing, is that grains don't absort well. We are takinga B12 pill now, which is 15$ for 150 pills. This is required. Then, a vitamin C pill with some D3, for 10$, pretty much lasts the year. We'll have this with grains, and likely cut it in half and eat the rest with bread or anything besides potatoes.
 As for calories, it seems 125g cooked = 170 calories, 30g carbs. We had 300g, so 400 calories/ 72g of carbs. But 2 tablespoons maragrain was 200 calories, which amounts to alot. Our ice cream uses 1 cup, and thus is as good as oat milk; and 200g bananas. It was 70g carbs, 300 calories, which is solid. I have to calculate this, but our trailmix is easily 200 calories per jar. I have to reassess. But having 2 is quite easy, and thats 400 calories. 400+300+100 from cantaloupe+600 from 300g bowl, is 1400 calories. This leaves, excluding margarine, 500 calories for potatoes and 300 for bread. That's 2200 + some margarine. Indeed, I reckon were not starving, but given our life, eating too few carbs. Our calculation was around 380, or 400 we'll call it. This is fine and good. And indeed, running has me in great shape. I think that pushups, and pullups when your shoulders are fine, are good for scultping and giving defintoin, but running is so good. I feel the impingment in our shoulders rather than our neck. Fantastic. And I feel great, and our body is generally toned. The process is simple, march, high knees, carioca then sprint back. The field is great for this.
So I am thinking long term and where I want to be. This question though comes from the sense we have of where we are now. And truly, this 1000$ rent is pressing on me. I think it fair that we have an arbitrary value on our money, and something like lord of hatred is our own expense. Time provides this. But food and rent are renewals, and ought be judged by our provision. Food is hard to calculate, given the current scenario. We get some gifted from mom and dad, and indeed, our efficiency is solid. But we do buy alot. And not unfairly. I am not swetting over an accumulated couple hundred over the coming months. I'm really not. It's just the fact that the more or less 300$ to live is pure surplus over our comfortable 3 days a week. One notion is that I sometimes don't mind working that amount. And indeed, tracking our expenses to know how much we are in debt, or slacking, is unclear. I did do some tabulations prior and our outlook was nothing short of solid. The point, is however the crow flies, that I would like to be here through the fall. That idea delights me, for both the summer and leaves in Ridge. Things are great, work is well, food is clearer and solid, as is running fine. More stability will help, and we ought earn the right and mean to exude strenght.
 This is more or less pleasing to me, though not something decided on. It is hardly unstable, but we are on a downward slope. Indeed, I want to submitt this, but am repulsed by the notion, and am thus stirring over some conciliatory truth, of how things may be, how we may grow. Indeed, many weeks will be worked satisfyingly, and we are fine and pleased to work 3. But there is some mean loss, over time, when some weeks are the latter. It is hard to consider where we'e come from and how much we've had. Only this week are we truly working 3 days. We've earned 4s worth since the start of march. In fact we are working 4 next week. And I look onto October and how there's 2 stats and an extra payday. And whatever drop we have in rent when we move, so will this balancing effects now give us slight rises. And equally a 2000$ tax return next spring irons all out. Plus, July and October give a free shift. I am attached the the location foremost. Even if our exact spot is ideal, it is not what makes the area special. It is like Killarney, in that it is a flat area, but high. The sky seems better, in front. I love thorn road. Walker park is a great place to sit and run. These can be reached from varied angles. And surely, what compells me most is the area of Hammond. The more central the better. It is a great launching point into Maple Ridge. We may browse this whole time, looking for something perfect, or better at least. Browse at the beginning of the months, inquire as to the localles. We can't move till June 1st anyway, and were not leaving less its in the area. We'll have to confirm the manner of our comning rent. Note, that Lanea said it was almost at my leisure to transfer. I am solid. We are me. Not is worth regard accept where we are. Let us bask in the harmony of what we do for that and how easy it is. Fruits will sprout naturally upon this.
We are subject to work, foremost. All other things are unnoticable. But work, its felt. But I agree to that, and keep myself to the groove that it's set.
 I find myself seeking comfort, or recourse, as though to have something else on my mind.
I feel this fundemental point, of more or less want; but better put, at least this is one side of it, something fun and exciting to do outside or normal work and ways. Thats why we seek people at work, as it takes us out of that concetration/duty. And looking forward to somethng does so equally. And even when work is done, we feel it coming again; at least, we feel a little flat when not doing something especially fun.
 On one hand, I see that life has never been so easy. We have never given up, and made burdens into deeds, and brought duty in line with our proper way. This has made things simple. But for whatever reason, we have no friends, for fun at least. And we have little pleasure or excitment, so much as things are unclear or we are unstout. But I do see, generally, that our openenss to things is furthering; meaning, that things need be less exact for us to meld and fit with them. Our path in life seems steadily yet targeted to the city, near Vancouver. We've been there before. And indeed, we exist no different there. At least, the same things come up. I think its about application, what you can really do, seriously. Be it dating or meeting people, leisure even. I just need some firend, like Matin, like Vienna, people to go see, explore, be frivolous even. But they ought be kind, curious, un-ardently nor passively traditional, nor so emboldened or broish to be ambitious and 'cool'. Josh comes to mind. Aryn does not. This may be hard to find, but our course is there. We may even find satiety, so much as we are fine. I feel that with Matin, whereby yes, we are not given much outside of our interaction, but as light and steady as we are, we take this moment in, feel its nourishment, and keep no. It gives me hope, clarifies, which this reflection is based on. We know what we want, in proper, but can't see exactly where.
 On one hand you have our state, and if it is pleasing, prime and steady, then we have little of this seeking want, this yearning, nor the dread and doubt which plods along. Instead, were fine and happy, pitched to our deeds but also swothed in fine time and leisure. Our far sights come to be then. And indeed, stability is this odd remark, for it does not remove the attention to things, but theyre use, and by that, admittence and regard for shortcoming. "We see our faults and ill-clarity upon free time." I often think that going out or being with someone would smooth out this curve; as though things are in fact fine, moving at least, and we just need to faciliate this, with more light and free things during our abled moments. It is interesting though, that when we think of Vancouver, or better yet, the west, a sunny day, 'which we envy others so seemingly free, to be with, when were at work', that so our view of a girlfriend, in closed quarters, or being with people at the beach or park, or out on the fields; the sunset really shines then. But who is actually doing this? Andy mentioned going on some hike; it was odd and not so pleasingly illimunating that he didn't mention something to me, though he did Joey, while I was there. But I'd say no. And indeed, if he said, 'were going to the beach, wanna come', I might say yes, as that sounds fun. But indeed, if he just said, 'some of us are going to the beach'; at that point I have no interest in hanging out. Ironically that is all you seek with Bella. What do you really want to do with her?
 I should not despair too much, or pretend like our approach to life hasn't been fruitful, and isn't on par for even brighter times, and consistent too. It is, for instance, a great measure of worth in things, and need, when your living on your own, as you must work, and at the very least are not living more easily at home, and thus making judgements of things from state unequal to where you'll actually be. For we are more or less a boy, squablled, dreaming, and otherwise working thinnly and with no matter while dependant. And our transition has been fine. We are holding things well. But I see that we want pleasure; at least, it feesl like there is room for it. Though, not to be clear, out of distraction or permission, but because were merely of the way we are, and are generally open. I think that notion of matter though is importnat, as it speaks to the slices of desire we have, from a girl, some view or sunset, a plan even. And indeed, it speaks to how we are, in that we are picturing where there is more likelihood of truth coming to pass. Why aren't we doing something now? Perhaps it feels like a garden, in that if you mean to move, then why plant it. Its hard to find the effort for that; not so much because you don't know how, but because there is no apparent spot to dig. And I look at our future and consider where there are clearer spots; and where on the whole, we may be more keen and willing to dig. By our continuiation year, for years really, our grown life. But too in all the overlappings of things. I think your just not around the right people. It is not a restriction that you'd meet people at work, but they may not be the same age, or have similar values and cares. And they may have primary friendships outisde of work. Indeed, as I think of Sums and literary vids, along with running, eating earlier, not so heavily, and thus lighter in the evening, b12, no brazen masturbation, and that simple 3 day per week pace, I see other grown things, that we may truly pursue. Meet Ups, outdoor yoga, soccer, some sort of class or workshop, something creative, where you can meet people who like books, games, living well. People who you don't neccessariyl work with. I just feel in general we are not stable. I don't want to write our preface and email for sums. I just can't find the consistency to follow through, and so I don't want to begin. let life unfold, as it were, with might and reason in mind. Things are well. We have people who I care for. But I see that, perhaps in our inconstancy, or at least committment, that our life feels temporary, somewaht waiting, and so work doesn't feel like this thing entirely forboding, or at least but a wall to climb, but a chore, somehting we have to do; as though we were just going through our paces. And so we want something to spark our mind. I feel stability is possible where I am; but not of the clearest sort. Indeed, I imagine there are elemnts here that reflect our ignorance or stuborrness; ways in which we should change, things we should do. Maybe were off to not be begging (at the very least) to go on that hike. But I see that in our common honesty, that we may live in truly stable ways. It's more about our feeling when outside of this, and whether we can sustain that. In some sense, this is a leap of faith, or trusting resolve, in our near and present actions, as to at least bear us to a state which would live as though we were well. We may not follow through, but living will have been, and we'll learn and grow, and carry this to wherever time or fancy takes us. But from our reasonable pace, rather than seeking one.
We have never submitted to burden nor deed. In our youth this was a mistake, as we just went by, and all went away. But i wrote the other day, 'I seek, and am privy to joys, that are just, able to be brightened, and are those which we had when free, but kept to'. I like to say that real adulthood is like childhood, only you are able, and by that, unreaching nor marbled in your experience, for we have tasks; but also, what is near and dear, and oppertune at any rate, is heightened. For instance, as a child, we did indeed go out with friends and eat chocolate. For one, this has been brought away, as people don't go out and have fun, less it be in a gathering, or for something frivolous; and recreation is it's own past time. And chocolate does me dirty, and runs in stark contrast the effect and need of whole things. But, despite this bountiful and or gorging ways, we still liked games, tv, the outdoors. Books and reading to, if they were done for interests sake. These are our pillars now; yet they've grown mightily higher.
 What it boils down to is that I have never ceased my draw and abide from liberty. Whereas, responsibility is set upon us, for all animals need exist, by theyre own, that so upon this thrust, I never said, 'well I ought change, in aim'. The deeds never felt like burdens; and if they did, it was from our own lack, not the nature of things.
 I think the hardest thing for me is in remarking of wisdom, and whether our supposed passivity in youth really speaks to some enlightenment beyond that. Is that really so? Are we really just more understood, and by that, living as we ought to have been in youth? For in such time, where duty was upon us, we got by via our dependance, abiding for the sake of satisfying our requests. But not so we could be as we were made to, but so I could proove I was on track, doing something. Our attempt held little though, and was thrown off by turbulence. We could not return to our meandering ways, or mere placation of demand, or development rather. But my question, is had we graduated, which I nor any mean not to do, could we not have gotten some job that was agreeable, and lived on our own, in a room, playing games, and with friends mnid you?
 One immutable fact is that despite lacking our sense and position as we have now, back then, our time at worked sucked. There is an odity though, in that Mazda was actually enjoyable, and we did live rather well. I think this was because our time there was not begrudging. And so our free time afterwards was free, and we could see it for what we wanted, rather than something which would derail our work time. This happened when we switched to Kia, and later at Save-On I noticed things even more. The point though, given things now, we'd be where we are now in that time, btu perhaps well off, in that we had friends. Yet, were revising history here, for our friends were those we smoked with. This is a sort of state which seeks harmony sure, but upon acceptance, as though what were doing is what there is, and were just moving along; pleased with goods surrounding it. Have we no aim, interests, life?
For a quick tally, we had 500g of grain: 200 barley 300 lentils and corn. Then 200 calories from margarine, which ought be taken like oil, and peanuts at that. That's 700 calories. 3 of these and you'd be enough for the entire day. My thought, on one hand, is that eating out of 3 is fair, as is eating perhaps are heartiest produce going into 2. But it seems that there is room for grain to be had coming out of 3, and that lighter grains and starches can be had in the evening. Consider your potatoes. Theyre 500 or so calories plus the oil, so call it 700. Bread is about 400 including margarine. So that's 1100. A smoothie is 250, fruit is about 100, and trailmix is a couple hundred. So call it 500. That's 1500 or so hundred as it is. I can't say what our break point should be. For days change. And indeed, familiarity ought bread a general pace of eating, but sense also for what's allowed. Moreover, carbs seem the limiting factor. I'd note though that splitting your grain into 2 servings is ideal, but we ought make enough. A giant bowl of 500g is not enough to split, and you end up eating the whole thing. It'd be better to have 3-400 back to back, coming out of 3 and going into 4. This leaves our potatoes and what have you going into 7, and bread and what not going into 9. Options are imporatnt, as we may want buns, hearty bread, muffins. Or popcorn. Indeed, quinoa is part of our budget; almost as much as b12 is. That, and edamame is a fair addition, so I don't question our protein intake. By all accounts, it is our worked nature, via the floor and shifts, and our ill training. For we've literally run once. And I felt great. But the effect is waring off. By all accounts, we ought not think, 'well, if we ate today, we'd be fine, as we have some course to our time. But sugar is not it.' Indeed, going into 10 on work days we go to bed, and coming out of 10 on off nights. This is fine, and were naturally waking up leading into 7. Long ago this used to be going into 8, and for a time it's been coming out of 7. So our time of day is fine, whereby we may have some veg going into 9, fruit around 10, trail mix going into midday. And this leaves so much room and time for a smoothie or something coming out of 1. And by all accounts, coming out of 3 through going into 5, feels fine as this end point of our day, where grain is had. Not too much. As 500g is alot in one helping. But a third less, then another spaced out, your getting, nearly 1000 calories from grain, plus 2-300 from margarine. That is excellent, and a) fuels our work and running, but b) aids in recovery. Which by all acocunts fills out the effect from such efforts, but thereby aids in forming us for more demanding and or beneficial ones.
I'm still face palming about asking Bella out. It's brought so much regret and confusion.
 On one hand, I feel were overestimating the effect of this, as though were changed, and things are different. It wasn't as though we were so self-centered before. We just had this confidence, and made things about me. We didn't expect much, and checked ourself when we did. But we made it about me. And felt more or less right in doing so because we were kind about it. But, per the chore of work, we would be such, as to lift ourself up.
 We no longer do this with Bella. And if we do it is the slightest. Is it because she is especially drawing in that way? Perhaps we just feel rot in being so confident, as though we were out of line in asking her out, so we feel almost embarrassed and or peverted. Like I don't feel as comfortable talking to Ally, like I have this reputation or stink on me. That sucks, as you feel more or less shielded from people.
 I think in the depths of this lies a more or less developing but wofting reflection, of why were so confident, why work is tough, ought we have something better to do. For were being somewhat elated; not to others dissmay, but not for theyre sake. We just want something to do. Maybe this is the esscence of fun, in that you do something fun and outgoing, as either motivated or happened to alleivate the press of work.
 It became quite clear when Bella said no, that despite our openess and confidence, that others did not care. It was fine to expect, hope even, and remain checked; yet to realize that these were merely so, and not reminiscent of hwo people are, made me feel swarmy, and in some sense down, as though no one were really there, no one interested in me. And it raised the question of how they ever would. In part I had the insight that we ought not be where we are through so much confidence, but actual want and like. Creative people came to mind, or those open and positive, like Vienna or Matin. Indeed, this would be rewarding, in a light sense; which indeed overlaps with the light feeling of reward when we talk to people on a general level, like Sonal or Carli, and Tara is even more of that. But similar to Tristan, or Andy, I don't just get this slight delight, but some real satiety, for we are connected on something. I don't enjoy talking with Sonal, but appreciate it. I enjoy talking with Andy. And games have always been that thing, yet, it speaks to only one part of my life. I feel that if there were more things than games than I'd be with someone more.
 It is hard to speak to this, for it illumates the current lack of prescence, which is quite hard. I wonder why were doing what were doing, what matters to me, if we ought not change? For an age it seems, happily but also seemingly neccessary, we thought these likes of mine were all we got, or could had. And even in the recent years, we've not seen them as limiting, but perhaps qualifying, in terms of someone actually being interested in me. This sense that there is no one, in part at least, leads to us thinking others are interested in me, or would be, based on who we are; or in a regretful sense, they'd be if we were different.
 I think a healthier view is avaialble, in that we'd not feel like were subject to scorn or doubt about our lack of friends. Were hopeful sure, but moving front and head, affable to what may come and what we have. We don't feel the lack, even if we sometimes recognize it.
So our caloric needs count on two things. One, we have our early day. The trail mix is about 2-300 calories. It doesn't need to count too much towards carbs, though nuts do have some, and your getting about 20 per. Calories are odd, as we don't have this pressing value for fat. But it keeps me from dying and breaking down otherwise; losing weight as it were. And so the buff from trailmix counts. It seems that 2 servings is worth 500, and this is very cost effective. Bananas are pretty rich, and I take for granted that we have our smoothie. We may have 1 banana, some milk, some dried fruit. None of the calories here are essential, but they make up a nice initial bucket, for your latter portion of the day to more happily suffice. 1 thing of trailmix, plus milk, and some 100 calories worth of fruit, still leaves you with 500 calories. Great.
 1/4 cup of grain weighs 125 cooked, and amounts to 150+ calories. We round down due to margarine's excess/understimate. No matter, if your adding 1-200 calories from margarine each meal, then your topping off your 2/3 cup grain to be something beyond 400 calories it is. Half of cup grain is barely noticble. Its more than a snack, but its hardly a meal. And 3/4 isn't much tougher. As it were, 375g is worth 500 calories of quinoa, which is a fair amount given the size of the meal. My plan, or sight, is that we may have 2 such servings, amounting to 1000 calories of grain, plus your margarin. Were going into 2000 calories at this point.
 Food is important, and were not doing ourselves ill to be so occupied. Exercise and our natural need, and satiety, bring us away from the sense that were stuffing ourselves. Food ought be had. And by those counts, eating enough means eating timely and plenty. There is much room coming out of the evening where potatoes, bread, popcorn and what not can be had, and we're fine. By all counts we benefit tremendously by eating two servings of grain. Then having that break leading into the evening, whereby we may scooter or write, then come out of it, relaxed, eating lighter foods. This is good on all counts.
Things are easy, and at that often, taken for granted during good times and feelings. But when there's sloth, or trouble arrives, we see portions of us which are off, be it in stature or thought.
 One centering element is that of rule, and namley that, or those, which come with being an adult; which is to say, someone who is dependant on none but themselves. Per philosophy, we can say that such independance is seperate from that deepest things, but more practically, when you can partake in the methods that acquire you what you need. Doing so creates rule, in that you must follow something, so much as you have needs. Which we take for granted that we do. Indeed, there is much which we like, and be it the sun or playstation, accessibiity varies. But we require food and shelter, no question. Having these provides the point whereupon a state of excess can be reached; in that, if such things are acquired, and the means are not toilsome, and on balance, enjoyable or satisfying enough as not to cause grief or apprehension, then so may our coming strides be steady and due.
 One of the morals of adulthood is that this point of excess, whereby happiness is concerned, or well-being, is not right if the threshold for such liberty is met by some form of dependance. For as kids we are asked to do chores, upon which we may go and play. And we generally do so with full out glee, as the deeds at our base are so quick and forgettable, that we live as though there was no duty, and by that token, any to come. It is not observed that such tasks are proper, or sufficient rather, in rendering one approval, or credidation, in being themselves. Rather, they are kept to someone elses life; who in theyre ask to complete or attend something, gives yield to do as we please. But were on a rope. And morally, our point is not that such a rope is the reason to be un-dependant, as though it were something worth admitting rot. But, instead, we simply remark that you are not living as you fully and ably could, had you been the one in charge. This in many ways proposes that what our determined life may provide more pleasing qualities; moreover, it is generally encumbant. Though, why kids leave the nest rests on the benefit from doing so. I reckon it is not out of the parent's ability to afford theyre child, nor the wish to retire elsewhere --for which theyre child is an impingement. It is for theyre child's sake. In some cultures the family stay together; and theyre pursuit in life is made to embellish that. Western culture tends not to be this way, and theyre is more or less an ultimate regard for one's need to leave the home. And so much as this is true, which is to say that so far as we accept, and by that anticipate, moving out, the demands of independance hold the measure of both the timeleiness and preperation in which we reach so.
Every animal is, by nature, a beast of burden. We may consider theyre molecular nature, as to remark upon theyre place in the cosmos, or earth as it were; and how within this, the process of finding energy and reproducing is, in function and sentiment, the behavior and or motion; but otherwise, in a more literal sense, a process, be it from this or that origin, of the mechanical sort, whereby such motions occur. People are not so different, albeit altered in form. For who does not participate in the economy, neccessarily? Whether you work or run a buisness, you as an animal need satiety, and acquire this, through gathering or economic invovlement. There, you offer effort or sell things. But in the economy you are. Even those who have risen out of this demand, still gather what they seek or need from it. It is merely a matter of resources required. Inheritance is one thing, but someone had to earn that excess; for themselves and theyre progeny.
 These remarks are important, for they form the basis in which you develop, and in some sense settle. For I don't know the origin of things, but I may take for granted, or at least lean into the principle of natural cosmogyny and organic processes. And equally, whatever my observations may be, so may I fall on that to determine right and wrong, good from bad, and not so much on argument and potential fallacy.
So certain things evoke our passion, the motion of our body and mind, and give it pleausre and cheer. Be it a woman, friends, being heard, or simply speaking. A far away land even or some exotic venture or activity.
 But it is said, and I agree with this, that our rules in life, our nature, ought determine what objects are fitting for us. For indeed a candy bar would excite me, or ice cream at that, but our nature is quite forbid of that.
 I look at our life then in this very moment, and don't see it so crossed over with even a month before. You see, we are independant, holding our life. And before, we were in a state that, albeit steady, was hoisted up, or angled differently, given our situation. I often reached a point that felt as though we were only temporarily free, but thereby set to do things per our freedom. Yet it was one that was given to us. Whereas liberty, or that feeling excess, cmoes from your own dependance, yet above the deeds that remain below. In our prior state, it felt like there were no deeds. And so it felt like we were on vacation.
 The point I wish to raise is one that says, if somehting evokes your passion, and is not so off or poor to do so, then it may be that your response is off, or that you are in fact unfitted, or perhaps less free than you believe. For I often find in people that I just want to talk with them. And that perhaps, if we were confident, we'd truly just be ourself and let things go. But I recall, with Bella, that as we spoke with Tristan, even prior to bringing up Bella, we had this idea of living rule free, doing as we were drawn to, passionate about. It's that feeling as a kid where you have a car and drive about, as though you were free. This, to be clear, is the wrong state of excess; for as a teenager, you have more, but are not asked for much more. Your still given food and bed. Indeed, you may still have responsibility, but it is more allowing, rather than providing. And I felt myself imagining that state. And in tandum with this was our sight of Bella, who I'd dreamt of perhaps, in fantastical ways -no different than Amon, or Shaun for that matter. But we were just friends. We had spoken several times before, and it was as if we were unable to be ourselves so easily, as perhaps she drew me too much, and our awkward set of rules and dependance just held me too little for me to be, that we turned our eye from her. In prior times we took to fancy such folk, and acted without caution or sense, but permission, as though this passion or never meant something. At any rate, it seemed as though we became more steady we naturally opened up, and tlaked to her. For a moment we exchanged some humurous banter, but then we had some actual conversations. And I wasn't thinking that she liked me, or I her. I did admitteldy remark upon these circumstances; for we still had some obsession of the thing. But it wasn't very possessive, or rooted in promise, but rather happiness, as though we were doing what we wanted. For you see, I have rules, and by that, select my company and pursuit with respect to them. But sometimes were a bit too hardened to things, and those who naturally delight you feel too much to be with given that, and you fall in or out. Similar to the year prior, where we had lived as we sought, with the future merely coming, for which we were clear but at peace at, so not reaching, so we were in such state last fall. And this brought us to speak as we wanted. And that was nice.
 Something on my mind, is why not do what makes you passionate, so long as it doesn't produce irreversible damage, such as sugar? Why not dream of your lectures, or get excited about sums, or moving to Vancouver, thinking of our oppertunity to date? Or mom and dad's inheritance, or the general good around? For one, they are far off, and not real in theyre promise any more than the summer coming about. In principle we are far off so much as we see so far off; were not near, as it were. And indeed, the biggest issue with this, of which sums is the clearest instnace, that whatever evokes such passion in these things is not fashioned substantively, or accurately that is, for which the promise is made on. Sums, has this clear and ready form, in which we write, or edit what we have, the principle and effect of the theory, and email that to known sources. Simple. In the case of lectures, the idea is sound and our enthusiasm is there. But do we just go in, cover the piece before hand? And how long are the videos? Moreover, pipeline aside, are we really going to record and produce such videos continually? Were just not there yet, but we imagine somewhat that we are, or as though we were, what it'd mean. Such is a dream. In regards to fortune there is much, but for that which bears promise, so is it far off, and perhaps not in the form you'd think. Oddly, when I think of what is available now, say upon our move, that the aid that would be for that can almost be assumed; yet were not any clearer on that, as no promise has been made. Except, the stake in it, whereby I'd want this, is in fact absemt, and so I don't mind concieving it. 10 years down the line, whereby I may recieve some fortune upon my dad's pass, that is meaningfull; and so our want matches the far yet broad nature of this. But it being so makes the haze of it hard to agree with. I compare this to Bella and company, who in our fantasy, are permitted so for we have no stake in the game. When were speaking with Bella, or upon our conundrunm, we don't want to think of them, for we either have something of worth which we wish to maintain, and thus not think about so frolicly; or we've lost something, both good and sought, and so thinking of her feels shamefull and of remorse.
 Comparing yourself with other people is always tricky, in both character and custom. For why can't I do that? Probst asked her out? That seems common? Yet the practice feels ill-fit for someone like me, with rules, who's entertained so many odd and differing things. Who is formed differently, who seeks different things, fears differently, believes differently. Maybe there is room to ask people out on dates, or at least gesture interest in getting to know someone. Indeed, be it this, or sincere friendship, or jovial and or frolicing relations, I think there are merely dynamics invovled, which your experience and sense can recognize. For one, it is evident when these things are present. Two, is that we may have perspective as to why these things aren't happening. For instance, there is little intimate interaction for most girls I see I work with and are much younger than me. Truth be told, when I get sprung by a girl, or feel myself naturally drawn to everyone, it is an obsession, and a rather hardened yet wanting state. It is not to 'get to know someone'. We've not the steadyness nor care for that. In some weird way, it's like saying, 'if you have the confidence for it, why not go around to every gril and ask them if they want to have sex?'. Saying this is wrong is quite unsatisfying; for even if it is, the prevailing motive is not so much this rule, as if theyre is a focre against me, but that our own motions don't bring me to this; it's the fact that were not interested in doing this. And this all get's brought down to the way we've grown up, and were raised, as to be in accordance with certain things, see the world a certain waay, treat people like this or that.
 I think the moral of these recent few months is in communication, and how passively or seriously such words may be exchanged. The results, or meaning of such, are more or less evident, with rules and understanding serving as a framework, or backbone rather, as to hasten eagerness or confidence, and also dissapointment. "It says what things should not be, given the rules, and what they would not be, or ought not, given how things are. You saying hello fails not because it ellicits no want of sex on the other party." Besides this moral is what's actually happening. For our communcation is one thing, and speaks to what's possible, and how were able. But we've only got peopel at work to talk to. Mom and dad are great, as is Nev, and Matin is lovely, and Andy is one of the best, Joey is kind and hard working, and I could name a few others who are buds to me. I don't wish to share with them Austin Bats though, or to dance, nor am I keen to be curious with any of them, about the Gods, nor in ways that our provision and effort may change into industry and promulgation. Few even have rules, and find jollity and security from them -when theyre fit to them. When we are so, we don't wish to complain, nor gossip. Indeed, one element of dependance is that you give yourself leeway to step away, or lean away from your rules; as though you had saved up some cash and gave permitt to spend some. Indeed, when your financially responsible you may spend as you please; but the allowance for such is determined upon the consistent regard for saving and expenses. Within dependance though, that regard is more often than not respected, rather than demanded, and so you more eagerly or lazily loosen the thread. Indeed, growing up is odd then, though, I'd say, we need, or may find more folk like me, or who I like at any rate with more sincerity, who are grown up. Age is one thing, as is aptitude and pursuit. Creativity comes to mind, as does ideals and morality.
There is a great deal which I can say is right, a lot of wellness which is going on. But our principle, our morals, our rule, seem off, or at least perturbed by this and that. And indeed, our entire adult life has come about in trying to mend this, through affirmation, distraction, solemness or cynicism. And truly I've found some insight worth the quality of truth. But entire it is not, nor so moulded as to drive us.
 Part of the problem is in part what makes the solution, in that all is happening at once; nothing is so far off as not to be detached. One notion that speaks to this is that of insulin sensitivty, and how your choices, good as be, may not benefit you, or even be so good, given your own ability to digest sugar. And indeed, that is a sort of conudrum in our measuring of things. For we like to say that such objects are good for they create such an end; or better yet, they are good in what they are. What if your constipated though? Does the wholeness and colon-digestivity of broccoli still earn acclaim? How can something be deemed good when the quality which makes it shine is not fitting? And indeed, I like to speak of fundmenetal truths, or directives rather; as not to get stuck, or feel the insolidity of relative notions; that is, we often say that, despite all ends being positively sought, what such ends are differ based on the origin, or person in question. When speaking of this we come from the persepctive that what is good, or rather who we, is taken for granted, but that given that, we can pursue and or approve of things as such basis would postively seek. The point being that if we were thieves then we'd have our own postive end; while being men of the kind and civil sort, aims us accordingly. The motive for this notion once came from wanting to validiate non-rationale pursuits. As it felt quite unclear how we could fashion ends that weren't subject to the doubts and scorn of reason. That aspect is not decided, but merely less relevant; perhaps on the tepid grounds its warrant comes from. At any rate, we feel, in contrast, the overwhelming worth and fairness in remarking upon our actions, and things at that, based on the effects we incure, or what things cause. This is observed, rather than reckoned.
 I immediatley think of rules and what can only be compared to geogrpahy, or what in an astoronimcal sense, remarks 'what is there'. For physics, which in some sense compares to morals, or the guide of action, is different than the actions actually had. History is perhaps a better word, as it refers to what has happened. Indeed, morals, or rules, is a speculative notion, or inquiry, while history, is about the past, and events to come.
So, near history is that which reflects, more or less, how things are going, in that, what has been is still present.
 In terms of health, we had made 2 attempts to exercise a few weeks ago, and felt really good. Our concern was about effort, and whether we'd have the energy the do so. And I noticed the effect at work, where we felt quite stronger and stable. We felt terrible though after a week or so, and it feels like our inability to ocnitinue exercising, or moving at any rate, hampers the training. I tried to run to bring ourselves back, and indeed saw this as our norm moving forward, as we've experienced the rounded effect from running earlier in the year; without these awful side effects. I felt pretty good, but fell back into that poor state. Now, we've been congested, then achy. I noticed on friday that we felt like running, or doing some sort of free range for form, but this perhaps brought us over the edge. I've forgot this critical element, that 2 weeks ago we had a full ben and jerries and drumsticks, and a thing of chips. This didn't make me feel awful, but the effect on our metabolism can't be understated. I believe the result of this is a terrible one. Shocking as it were. I am thining about another bag of chips, a donut even, and even if they don't do you that bad, they surely do no good, and albeit vaired, do you a little bad.
 We spoke with our docotr the other day, and our ferrous stores were slightly below standard. I am not eager to take iron pills; a bit of pride, concern of headaches and price; so I found some promise in the notion that vitamin C with our heartier meals could help absorption. We've also just been eating less these recent period of time. And one side of this is the lateness in which we begin to eat. We've been well in eating produce during the day, but then we don't eat a huge amount in the evening. I've taken to eating earlier, which our consistent schedule has helped. I've brought this notion of 'going into, or coming out', as to make timing easier. Going in acts as a sort of limit; so for instance, going into 2, means more or less that by the time 2 arrives your done. This gives nice leeway to do something near this, as it doesn't take much time to eat. But you also get some perspective on the time before. You don't concern yourself with what comes after. Coming out on the other hand is about starting, so it can be later, but generally comes in front of this. So coming out of 3, we've been eating grains. And indeed, calories are a mishapen concept, as you get ample from oils, but not so much from carbs. And your sensitivty to these allows for higher abosoprtion, and at that, increase in activity and thereby sensitivty. Consistency is key then. But, we've taken the general stance that 125g of cooked grain is about 30g of carbs. 100g cooked is 133 calories in general. I take this numbers serioulsy, as theyre a remark of the protein and macro complex. Margarine is this added thing, that literally adds some 200 calories per 30g. We made a 700g bowl of grain, and added 100g of margarine, which is literally 600 calories. I think this has some metabolicclay filling effect. No matter, fruit always adds a little, and ignorant it may be, but I'll take a peanut over oil anytime. Proper fat as it were. It is 250 calories for our mini jar, which is nice. And smoothies, or ice-cream rather, is near 300 calories. 100g of bananas is some 30g of carbs. This all adds up nicely during the day. I just think that we need that middle portion, whereby grain can be had coming out of 3, and then our starches going into 7, with some bread or popcorn going into 9. Or, as it were, we work at 6 every day, and we go to bed going into 10; but coming out of 10 on off days. For we never go to bed at 11, but are not rushing out once 10 hits. We kind of leave naturally at 1030. So we may eat coming out of 9 on off days. I've noticed though were waking up at 7 on off days quite easily, going into 7 even. This makes our day function way better. As going into 12, we have room to eat fruit and veg and dried stuff, and then quite amply eat our smoothie and grain going into, long wise, 5.
 I'll mention that our finances are such that a lack of understanding, and at that discretion, ails me, in that we have means to spend money, but from our choosing, rather than casually. I'd note that our long-term view is that surely we will have 8000 +2000 from taxes. This brings us many months ahead, to the end of the warm months. But our budget feels off. If only because some things we buy constnatly, or in bulk; but something like brussel sprouts feels nice, but something we'd have to choose to get each time. And so it feels like it may take the place of something more annual and by that efficient. Were just not clear though. Kale for instace has lost its shine, if only because were not having so much cucumber or peanuts. Were placing more emphasis on grains. In that case, I'd add Kale to your morning salad, which is itself nice. Oranges are neccessary, and a given. Indeed, buying fruit is expensive, but worthwhile. I wouldn't confuse buying 1$ mangoes in bulk, or even the 5$ one, for proper fruit spending. You ought buy small oranges, or things on sale. Prices which you can stand by, like those 2$ cantaloupe. Those were extremeley valuable. My only concern for smoothies is that they feel almost too good that we rely on them, and by that, have no oat milk available; for we just have soy milk. Yet, we need blueberries and soy. We may choose to get some oat milk if these things are running out. Bananas are inexpensive mind you, and the milk is what we'd buy anyway. So maybe we just need to confirm the availability of berries. Barley is inexpensive, but ought be consumed with more delicay. You don't need as many grams of it as say quinoa. One important fact, which frustrates me from any esteemed judgement, is that carbs, which I consider primary energy, is not itself a marker for such energy, but rather potential. Yet, such potential is unlocked by how much you store, which is measured by your sensitivity. If your extremely sensitive, then you'll less in. If your food is less glycemic, then it will output its carbs slower, and allow you to digest more. This is a basic principle, and means that less barley is fine, for you get all of it. No matter, it is paramount that, similar to fruit, we give ourselves that grain based succour, in corn+ edamame; for protein and eatability. The expense is not small, but it is managable. Corn is 3$, sometimes 2.5, and it lasts for about a week. Soy is 3$, though it may be less elsewhere. And it lasts as long as the corn does. This is one of those things, whereby, in a more affordbale situation, these expenses would be budgeted in without concern. In fact, our full month budget is built on these being present. It's a matter of burn rate, and how much we have worked, how much were going to work, what intermedairy bonuses there are, and what our outlook of things is and what level we'd tolerate. If we have 10,000$ next spring, I'm fine with that. And indeed, we may leave this particular spot before then, surely in fact by the end of the year. But I just mean that we a) have been working alot, missing only 1 week so far of non full shifts, b) will surely, be it peridoically or for stretches of time work so fully, c) there are stats and the october bonus, d) you get some help from your parents, e) the amount we have, whereupon our situation is more affordable, is neither far, nor grieving about any expected spot. We'll have 6000$ by June 1st, we make a 1000, pluse another 1000 from bonuses, and those missing shifts for the month, amount to 350 extra, which leaves only about 300 loss, and lets say this is averaged to 200, that is perhaps, a 1000 short we'll come by the time our situation changes. Our health may change also, and our shortcoming may reduce. But having 9000$ going into the fall is not discouraging what so ever, given the abundant norm which we are heading for, and dremaing of.
So I've had some trouble titling this piece, as it is not so distinguished as the one's prior. The key difference is that those prior were about foundations, pillars, solidity. Whereas this and those to come, are about lavishing, layers, icing if you will; put on top of such foundations.
 The thing is, that these words are of a different mind, in that what preceded was a sense of problem and duty, principle and solution. Whereas now, it feels that our troubles are done: worn off if you will. And our action is all that matters. Bloom comes to mind, or that sweet succour of time; where work and leisure blend, in mentality and commission. Flourishing
 But so in all of this there lies an ambiguity, of words namely; for the basis for things is unknown. And all that I can really say, is that passion is a result, a sign if you will, to our nature being a certain way: tilted if you will. And indeed, our response to this feels just, in the same sense that we have a liking to fruit. Yet, we recognize the folly in this, the brace, as though there were something we needed to support. It all comes down to an incontinuity of action; so much so, that we find ourself inquiring into the origins of motion and matter; if only because we have stiffness in our mind. So much comes to light here, in both the quality and duration of our experience. But it is, to my point, a blending, a submission to the rythms within us. And this piece, this age, marks that point where such procession took off; where rule was not itself a measure, but something to merely direct us: to bring us back into motion. For that is the key, the answer; that our experience is all we seek. Passion is otherwise the aim: which is to say, that what's sought is not an end, but a dream.
Do you know that immutable expeirence of, 'I worked out, felt firm, then went soft'; and you feel small, yet become more firm as time goes on; as though your body can grip more. This occurs during sickness also, where you notice yourself becoming soft. And indeed, we've made many mistakes in trying to reaffirm, upon the dis-ease of being soft. On one hand, rule is not established; but still, as confident as we are, the forces of vanity and shame crawl in.
 No matter, I mean to say that mistakes have been made, and our manner of constance has altered. To a wonderful degree, as principle takes route. In a way we are sage like rather than mere philosophers; as we've turned rule into pracitce. Yet were perhaps impure in our rules, and makes errors and misjudgedments because of this. All I can say, is that running, vinyasa and eating regularly through the day is essential.
 Our finances need tenuring too. For one, I am not saving for a child, just as I am not saving for myself. I am against financing our inertial aims, but for the sake of constancy, and if principal is kept, so can I bend and tribute. For our employment is foremost, and the steadyness at that. Our bedrock is one of foundational wellness, rather than dire need. For if we were fired we'd move home. If we could not do that, than our judgement would truly be personal and self-reliant, and worthy of no shame. The matter of children has it's own simplicity and faith; but such is a far off dream. At any rate, we are employed, able to transfer, and with sights of somewhere long term, rather than novel. "Vancouver and its density of people and activity, rather than abbatsford and it's novel views." Given that, and our norm here even, we'll be spending 700 on rent, 300 on food, not much else. We'll work 3 days a week, which is near 1050, plus 800 in stats, 400 in gst, a few sick days, and 2000 from taxes. This is what we expect. And in calmer moments the future gleams and fortune sparkles. As does our time with a friend, or a feeling of control give way to avoiding responsibility. I feel accordingly. For our current place is at a premium; and a month in what sticks to me is Maple Ridge, the look of Hammond, the pretty nice field, and utility in being close to one. Being close to Langley Market is nice to. But none of it is essential. And all roads come to be our home. I wish to be me in motion. I give no permitt to born or sugar, nor dreams and fancy. We just fall in or out of line. And sometimes I feel the pressure, which brings on passion or steadyness; or it comes short, and we turn to trickery or excess.
 I am struggling with our health, and for the moment, working 4 days feels exhausting. Though, wokrnig at all feels that way; it's just that 3 is mentally agreeable, as were unsure else. When were moving 4 is fine, but were not so now. The premium is killing me then. One friction point is our obscurity in spending; for we have no budget; as our movement is more willing and forecful rather than constant and of momentum. Our steps are a bit heavy going into work, and a bit to soft and 'let go' when out. So I am buying food. Not inordinately, but more than I need. Some aspect of this is fair as we settle, but other parts feel off, and feasibly managed. We have 325$ on our credit card. Our 45$ fan is going back, we returned 40$ worth of Amazon things. We bought 40$ worth of vitamins, and a more expensive fan. And the rest is from food. We've only spent 100 or so this month, a bit less in fact, so even our less than managed spending is not so far off. We worked 600+ for march and all but this past week of April. And we called in tomorrow and are working 14 hours on top. So we are making money. It's just the case that if we don't work that 4th ship, were out 80 bucks. We have 4600 now, 200 to pay off on credit, 600+ coming this thursday, 600- on the 30th. But were left with -600 for credit on June 1st, leaving us with 5000 plus whatever we make in May, 1000 min. So, say we may 1000 in June, then minus another 300 and minus a 1000 rent. Were at 5700. This means we'll loose 300 each month, from July 5400, August 5100, September 4800, October 4500. So we can take all things for granted, work our common 3 day, and have 4500 going into Novemeber 1st. For one, I rest on the fact that our expected expense, at 800 even, in Vancouver guarenteed, will leave so much room for spending and saving, that I can't wince at this depleting reality. Plus, you have May, July, Septemeber and double cotober stat, which is another 400, so call it 4900, plus an extra 500 in october, so up to 5500. That's with us burning June-October (5 months) 1500$. This is a premium thought, and not a poor one. So much as were playing with house money, this is more than tolerable. We just need a bit of management, so we know what were spending. All may fall in line, for moments or stretches. We may not even stay here till that time. I have no priority time to move to Vancouver. It just feels promising of friends, women, productivity and ambition, recreation and stability: all of which light our mind and leisure. That is our bonfie now, and compared to the twigs at home, we have some logs now, but the fuel feels pricey. Vancouver is a forest to burn down.
I see this most tender of points, that of our fun, our money, our relations or friends, there is nothign wrong or shame worthy about lacking them, nor much which I find disagreeable. Our point, in theyre abscence, is only the clearer. And what I've found since returning to work, and our life even, is that we are merely perturbed at our inability to be ourselvres, to speak as we want, ask and or listen. For in that, we do feel drawn, overly perhaps, with garulous motive. Those who beckon me to relax and be, and make our point in things all the lighter, calm me not; as our deepest compuslion triggers. Our expeirence and sense rear us off this track, but we turn away; and often, find ourself saying things such as 'we don't need them, or theyre poor, or our kind of life is not meant for this'. This comes along, and in some places us as though we were not with such sight and friends; for which our point is sharpened. But it is a little compressed; and upon life's slip, we feel the beckon; or better put, we feel the effect of us merely turning our head, and not being ourselves. "For life ought be lived with point and purpose indeeed, but in the open; for which much that reflects your more ardent and hid desires gets is spoken to." The result is the opposite of stearing off but barreling forward. For it feels so off to return to principle; for in that we've in truth had our head low.
I look at our spring writings from the prior year, and what stands out is this common ntoion of, 'were free, but know not what to do'. And our resolve, or what we take notice of, is a contrary notion, of progress, whereby the source for our 'unsure' remarks, comes from a state where were not moving. And progress then becomes this counteraction to that; and something we more or less have faith in, as if to swart our concerns during stillness. I noted the cost of merriment, in that, right action produces fair results; but if we do this so often, we may find ourselves refuting resolutions born upon our mistakes, when inaction occurs. If these happen so irregularly, it is not even a memory which keeps us from turning direction, or bearing down, 'as if to enliven our stillness'. Were always moving. Our mistakes merely make us feel flat.
When I looked at our place, my strongest thought was, 'the bathroom seperates us from the other two rooms', we'll be fine. This has reigned true. But, admittedly, it has not been needed for anything but our own privacy. We never her Amon or Situ. It is Awyn who can be loud and annoying, and the little guy downstairs. I don't know where the other tenant went, but he was annoying too.
 This new place is right on Laity and the four way stop near Shady lane. It is quite nice from a walking perspective, has lions and volker park, which is itself quite a gem. And it's overall nice. The price is 650 with internet and laundry. The room was musty, had the slightest unvevenness under the window, but was otherwise a fair size, similar to our prior room. There was no closet from what I remember. This may be a deal breaker, or at least something in need of treatment. Also, our room is right next to the other tenants, who is a younger indian girl, who works at Superstore, and lives there too with a male friend. This may be an improvement over our current scenario. Theyre bed is on the far side of the room, from mine, which is good. And I don't expect they will be loud; nor will we reach a point of being too loud until late. And were in bed around 10 anyway, so I don't think this poses too much an issue. And having rooms next to one another isn't something unfamililar, nor unlikely too happen. It was this way with Arryn, to no ill effect. And on Canada Way too. Whatever sound we hear, so our fan lay. And it's a basement, so everything is a bit more closed off and thick.
 The window was small, but fit just right, like a little hobbit hole. With some dusting and wiping, the cleanliness and smell would be above suitable. The kitchen wasn't as big as I thought, but had nice appliances. And again, this is all for 650$. That's crazy. And it's in a perfect spot. Just going there as it was I could see every loop and straight was nice. And very open, yet full of trees and brush. Better than where we are right now. Imagine the morning, the birds. Laity is itself a great street.
 So forget the closet space or layout. Considering our prior room, the size doesn't matter, and of all things noise and 'closet space', we'll figure out. The price is an enourmous factor. If it was run down though and had no value, I'd see the issue. But I'd say it's not. The space was good, a little small, but nothing more than I need. The living area leaves something to be desired, but no issue. Once you meet the other person, then it's no different than now. It's all on one floor, despite the 4 steps to get into the place. And it is new, and will have to reach a point of comfort, as canada way did and 49th. This isn't jumping at me, if it weren't for the location and price. But those are the important elements. And I don't think the place as it is speaks of an issue. Not ideal or overwhelmingly good. But is our current one? It has nice closet space, but used in no genuine way. We should inquire about the laundry, the nature of the other tenant? The closet?

 I understand you have to think about/discuss, but I did have a few questions.
 1. What is our access to laundry? I only ask because in my current place we only have limited use of it
 2. I don't remember if theyre was a closet in the room? Is there any place for storage?
 3. I can't spell her name, but the girl said she lived with another person. Are they boy or girl? It's an odd question, but I'm just curious if I'd be living with a couple?
 4. I suppose that sound isn't too big an issue because were in the basement? I'm just wondering about a) my privacy, and b) the privacy of others, noise wise, given that my room would be next to the others, and people above? Can you say who is right above me -- is it the kitchen, or someone elses bedroom?
 Odd questions I know, but just wanted to get all the information I could. I really like the place, but I do have to leave my current one, and these were some things I was unclear about.
So we may not get this place. I am alright without the cupboards. I don't anticipate our roomates being loud; and our own volume does not seem an issue. Our scooter will fit, there's laundry, internet --the quality is not an issue, it really isn't. We'll scrub the room, and all other smells are fine, as they permitt our own use.
 I can't tell you how much I've sought to return to our forward motion. It is our way, and we've spent too much time getting by it, only to live as though it weren't there. Indeed, even our simplicity has room for error; but that has been foremost because we can't buy food, nor really see or anticipate our schedule, for which yoga and training suffer. We are working tomorrow till 11 and next til1 10. We work Tuesday and Wed til 11, then friday to 11, and were off for 3 days. These should be but the next ones, of which tues and wed follow. But we may fill this time with right and pleasing things. Truly. I wish to accept our matters, but aslo agree with them, in that they are enough and just. For in one we are tasked, by economic need; but in the other we are satisfied, in having what we seek. We can maintain our pace, in both liberal and dutiful ways. But not feel that our excess or freedom is to be hidden, as though it weren't right. I feel that way now, that what we have is not sustainable. It is hard to enjoy the fact that were off at 10 on Saturday.
Being friends with people is this common norm, a status if you will. No different than being amiable to a co-worker, or polite to a boss. It's how you conduct yourself.
 It has to be made though; not so much earned, as if you must appeal to them, but rather time must be put in. And it's this nurturing process that speaks to the possibility for friendship with any; for you must merely show the time. But, given this fact, and how it is not there to begin, it is not something to be wholly expected or sought after. Confidence should helm you, with fair reason, motive and ability. Little things can be said, and our relationship is such.
 I think of Dorthy, and how I expect little, but through confidence. With Bella, or Amon at one momen, or Simron, or Baley, Nicole, Holley, Barbi, Sinem, Joshes friend, Heather, Adara, even the bakery girl; these draw my mind. And we've known this for an age seemingly, but in part we feel this way because it seems Destiny. Truly a childish form of love. For our want may be unbridled, but so are we unformed then. Or we may be like a boy, who lusts, cooly, but has no real formation beyond this. Those who are confident and lust, are formed, and merely do as they please. Were just made a different way. Had we been, we'd maybe find people our age, and not so based on work.
 In part, our fitting for someone, based on what they want, and who they are (age), orients what is possible. Love may come, but so may people just go theyre own way. But relations are possible with anyone. And I often relish the fairness of who we speak to, in our warm yet calm way. Its as though we look for not, but accept the chemistry possible, and let it be. Parts of me are not whole though, in hope and expectation. And this makes such things hard to achieve sometimes; for were often now satisfied with mere warmth, or were hard strung to be, and go right in. Prior to asking Bella out we were fine; but we had the possibility in mind. We just didn't bind ourself to it, and let the warmth be. We thought there was promise here, but were fine to let things go. Often my greatest grief comes from thinking it should be, and worse, that it could have been upon us trying. This feeling comes in some form regardless, for we do have some intent in mind. But in the case with Bella, and Simron to, we just accepted that it may or may not happen, while life went on. One way of looking at it, was that the warmth already there was good and enough, and it was fine to remain so. Or deepen even, but in such warm ways. This felt fine and fair, we didn't have to beg for it; we just were ourselves, and made friendship happen. We merely were, giving, requiring not. So time could go by.
 I think we've learned, in our abscence of this, what real relationships are made by; namely that you actually like someone, and form a relationship, in that you share things, do things, have something in common. That you want to do things together; romance being an extension of that, whereby you want to be together. It is nice to have learned this; or better yet, given that, what I see in others that would make this so; that they were creative, funny, hard working, original, with spirit and cheer, but mature also. Some blend of those things. People who I'd want to share that I write, and do yoga, and try in life, and my music, the dance, the clouds.
 Normally were quite content. But we expect things from people; as though we are seen and impressive. And I think alot of people go through life thinking this; but our error with Bella revealled that this is not so; and that people are into those they actually like, and have but friends otherwise; those they talk to or chat, but mean not a whole lot. We are not special. And again, this was fine, for I felt that despite what we expected, I still got what I want. The moment prior to us asking, we were friends. I miss this, and am begrudged by the fact that it seems tough to return. The ambihiuity of this is rough also; and our understandable reservation, in not trying to make this into something, when it is likely that only I care. She only saw you as a friend, and now your barely that. She has her own friends, were away. But the warmth there is something I miss and feel regret about. Alas.
 I see that we don't have many friends of our own. Few who excite me at any rate. And indeed, there types here a man, who is not bound by much other than common rule. And in that, we are aimed at liberty and cheer, fine peace and fairer pleasure. This makes such warmth a desired thing; while heat and passion, or enthrallment, would be a dream. Such is not the reality. At least, it is hard to make so. Were lost sometimes in feeling so absent from what would perhaps bring us such. Were so different. And indeed, it is but a fancy which your hoping for, rather than the real relations at had. Which, by our own admittence, in our full and more or less kind, is for such warmth and nothing more. I only see, in all of this, how there may be something deeper, with heat and passion of an according kind. But you must really be with that person; and thus they are more likely than not to be a person who warrants this.
 I find myself lacking that now, and seeing little space for it to happen. My impression is that Dorothy does not see me this way. Indeed, being impressive and funny is not what this is about, for that has nothing to do with you; at least, it's about what they give and you get. And much of our offer to her is based on what we would like to recieve. That, or our view is of frivolity. Merely, we may be friends, and I'll ask for that. Such is the way though. Perhaps all relationships are hard, so meaningfull as they are. And they say, that the right relationships come more or less easily; for what made them meaningfull was there and a spark occured. Simron remains this way for me. Perhaps not our ultimate person, our soul mate. But not our muse either. For that, warmth has always laid. And indeed, Bellas fire is out; may we not succumb to remorese or jealousy. But if it be someone else, or the ability for all in general, that such embers may burn. You never know which one's you'll want to tend to and blow on. There is a heartier comparison to this also, in say the friendship we have with Matin, or Andy or Tristan, Alex even or Joey, or Carli and Tara. This is a denser, baser kind of company. The fruit and vegetables of our world. We are grain.
